In a recent talk delivered before an audience of a16z speedrun founders from the class of SR005, a16z cofounder Ben Horowitz warned there are no easy shortcuts to understanding the task that awaits them as they lead their companies. 
“Nobody’s gonna train you to be a CEO,” Horowitz said. “There are no five steps for being a CEO.” Instead, the problems founders are set to encounter are overwhelmingly likely to be situation-specific. So founders and new CEOs often need  people they can trust and discuss their problems with, preferably experienced operators who “know what they’re talking about,” in Ben’s words.
With that caveat in mind, Horowitz offered the founders some of his hardest-won lessons. Below are five that stood out.
1) Do the thing you can do, even when all the options look bad
When Ben took his company Loudcloud public in 2001, he saw it as the least bad choice out of many very bad options. The move was, he remembers, “clearly a bad idea.” But the only other option, after having spoken with “literally every investor in the private markets, was bankruptcy.” 
Horowitz says that at moments like these, he often sees founders and CEOs hesitating to make a hard choice. They know they need to do the hard thing: to fire that underperforming sales lead, or re-architect the product. Faced with a seemingly impossible decision, they make the mistake of biding for time, which only compounds the pain they’re facing.
In Loudcloud’s case, Horowitz says, bankruptcy “was obviously a worse choice than going public.” So he made the painful decision, and took Loudcloud to the public markets. Horowitz says if he’d thought about “all the things that were gonna happen to me when I went public—and they all did happen to me, by the way—I would’ve just shot myself in the head.”
But hesitating would have been even worse. Ultimately, Horowitz was able to hold the company together and sold it to HP for $1.65 billion in cash in 2007, a journey he’s written about in detail in his bestselling book The Hard Thing About Hard Things [ https://substack.com/redirect/d99a9cda-702d-4c67-8cbf-2f70ec06045b?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ].
When it’s all on the line, decisiveness is key to survival. As a founder or CEO, your job is to act under uncertainty. 
“I always come back to: ‘what can I do? And I better do that right now.’ And that’s a lot how you work your way through those very bad [choices].”
“The worst thing you can do is think about ‘what are all the implications of it?’ That doesn’t matter. Is that the best choice I have? If that’s it, I’m doing it… You’re not a great entrepreneur unless you feel like you don’t have a choice but to build a company. Because otherwise you’re gonna quit.”
2) Choose investors who help you build momentum and confidence as a CEO
Andrew Chen asked Ben: For founders seeking investors particularly at the earliest stages, how do you evaluate who the best partner might be? 
Ben said to focus on two questions: Can the investor help create momentum (e.g. through support with hiring, follow-on, and customers)? And can they raise your confidence and capability as a CEO so you’re more able to make timely, high-quality decisions despite uncertainty? 
“The thing you don’t have,” Horowitz said to the audience of speedrun founders, “is you don’t know how to do that CEO job. No founders we work with who haven’t already been CEOs of giant companies know how to do that. And it turns out to be a really complicated skillset.”
Ben gave examples like selecting talent for jobs you’ve never done (“I had never been a CFO, I didn’t know what the hell that was”), or building a control structure, or talking to Wall Street. This uncertainty slows down decision-making and causes founders to hesitate. “When I watch founders who fail at doing [the CEO job], it always comes down to a crisis in confidence,” Horowitz says. “You know you don’t know what you’re doing. You know it’s hurting the company. But yet you still have to make a decision.” 
So it’s critical to work with investors who can help you get to a higher confidence level to make decisions more quickly. For this reason, Horowitz says, he favors investors who have been CEOs. “Try to mostly get investors that have done something.”
Otherwise, you might end up with a team of investors who give you trite and nearly useless advice: 
One, my favorite pieces of advice I got… and I got it like 27 times: They’d say, “look, here’s the key: Hire A players.” And I was like, okay... yeah, my plan was to hire a bunch of f***ing morons, but now I’m gonna hire A players.”
3) Focus on strengths more than weaknesses
Using the original iPhone as a reference point, Horowitz argues that when it comes to evaluating technologies, or products, or even talent in individuals, you should focus more on strengths over weaknesses. 
“If you look at the 2007 reviews of the iPhone, they’re saying it’s a bad phone,” Horowitz says, “It’s a way worse computer than a normal computer. The keyboard is much worse than a Blackberry’s.” 
But focusing on the iPhone’s weaknesses missed its strengths, including the possibilities unlocked by the device’s camera and the ability to have a GPS in your pocket. Breakthroughs like these are what allowed for entire new companies like Uber to be built. “It just has to be unbelievable at something,” Horowitz says. “It doesn’t have to be better than the last thing at everything.”
The same logic applies to how founders should think about their own capabilities. When speaking about how a16z evaluates founders, Horowitz drew an analogy to how he thinks about new technologies like LLMs:
“I always say it’s like we’re not looking for lack of weakness. There’s something wrong with every entrepreneur, including all you—including me… but that’s not the question. The question is, are they world class at a thing? Are they the best in the world at something? Do they have a secret that nobody else has? Do they have something really special? What’s the magnitude of the strength? And I think the future is a lot like that. How strong is the strength? How different is this technology? We now have a computer that you can talk to in English. That’s kind of important. That’s a hell of a thing.”
4) You have to be much better than the competition, but winning comes with its own advantages
One of the first challenges founders face is acquiring early customers. Here Ben warns against taking a cavalier attitude toward competitors who are already in the market.
“You don’t take out a competitor with an equally good product or a slightly better product,” he says. “It’s gotta be a 10 times better product. And then of course with AI, if you take the market you get more data about your particular kind of user.”
On this last point, Ben highlights a unique opportunity in the age of AI. By building something that’s good enough to attract an initial batch of users, you’re then in a position to learn about them—and train new models—to serve that particular audience even better than before. Learn from every interaction, ship improvements, and hold the account. That’s the secret to defensibility. 
“If you’re talking to super high-end programmers all day about how to design their program, that’s a lot of information into your system and into your model that nobody else has. And so that becomes part of your differentiation. And then possession is nine tenths of the law when competing on software.”
5) Always believe in yourself
Asked if he had any final words of wisdom for the founders in the audience, Ben had this to say:
“I’ll just say this: Congratulations on betting on yourself, because that’s what this is all about. Your job is to make your bet pay off. You believed in yourself enough to do it. There are gonna be many times when you question that bet and you question that belief, but that gets you nothing. Don’t worry about it. You’ve just gotta do your thing and keep going.”
“Focus on what you can do and don’t worry about the doubts. The doubts are gonna be there. And the worst doubts aren’t the ones people put on you. It’s the ones that you put on yourself. Every entrepreneur has them. I spent a lot of time with Mark Zuckerberg when he was like 22 years old and he had tremendous doubt about whether he was gonna make it. Everybody has that. It’s not about what you feel. It’s about what you do. So just focus on what you can do. You’re gonna feel scared. If your guts aren’t boiling, you’re not even trying at this thing.”

RE:[CASE 174824875600936] Unable to find detailed information about
 Llama cost estimation
body_text:
Hi Meghdad,

Thank you for your thoughtful feedback—I completely understand your concerns.

To address your question about deployment options: Currently, Provisioned Throughput is the only available method for deploying fine-tuned models in Amazon Bedrock. This approach is necessary because private (fine-tuned) models require dedicated resources for both security and performance, while base models can be served with a shared-resource, on-demand strategy. The reason is that fine-tuned models need their own isolated computing environment and cannot share resources in the same way that base models can in a multi-tenant setup.

If you are exploring alternatives outside of Bedrock, AWS offers several flexible services. I would like to provide some examples:

    - AWS SageMaker Endpoint: Offers some no-code capabilities (e.g., with JumpStart), but deploying custom fine-tuned models generally requires code or scripting [1, 2, 3]
    - AWS EC2: Provides full control, but requires manual configuration and coding at every step [4]
    - AWS Fargate / ECS: Serverless for containers, but model deployment and scaling require code and setup [5]

However, all of these would require some degree of coding or manual orchestration, and you'd still need to manage cold starts (ranging from 1 to 5 minutes depending on the service) when scaling up from zero.

If you'd like, I'm happy to discuss further or help design an architecture that meets your needs. My regular working hours are 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM, Monday to Friday, AEST. You can reply to this case through the web interface during these hours for a prompt response.

Reference:
[1] https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/serverless-endpoints.html
[2] https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/machine-learning/llm-experimentation-at-scale-using-amazon-sagemaker-pipelines-and-mlflow/
[3] https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/machine-learning/learn-how-to-build-and-deploy-tool-using-llm-agents-using-aws-sagemaker-jumpstart-foundation-models/
[4] https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/machine-learning/serving-llms-using-vllm-and-amazon-ec2-instances-with-aws-ai-chips/
[5] https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/machine-learning/building-an-efficient-mlops-platform-with-oss-tools-on-amazon-ecs-with-aws-fargate/

We value your feedback. Please share your experience by rating this and other correspondences in the AWS Support Center. You can rate a correspondence by selecting the stars in the top right corner of the correspondence.

Best regards,
Michael L.

subject:
RE:[CASE 174824875600936] Unable to find detailed information about
 Llama cost estimation
body_text:
Hi Meghdad,

Thank you for reaching out to AWS Premium Support. My name is Michael, and I am a member of the AI/ML team. I will be assisting you with your case.

Before we proceed, we would like to sincerely apologize for the long delay in our response. 

I understand you have reviewed the AWS Bedrock pricing page [1] and are seeking further clarification. Allow me to explain model pricing in three key points for better clarity:

1. Bedrock Foundation Models Charging Modes

    The foundation models in Bedrock have two charging modes:

        - On-demand (Charged by Token Amount): This is a fully serverless architecture where AWS manages all infrastructure. It supports the base model [2] and allows vector-level customizations such as knowledge base ( AWS built-in Retrieval-Augmented Generation) [3]. 
        - Provisioned Throughput (Charged by Time): This mode uses dedicated infrastructure, and you control when to start and stop the infrastructure. In addition to the base model and vector-level customizations, it supports model-level customizations such as AWS built-in fine-tuning and continued pre-training [4].

    Since you mentioned you plan to fine-tune a model, please note that only the Provisioned Throughput charge mode supports this option.

2. Provisioned Throughput Commitment Options
     
    Provisioned Throughput can be purchased with no commitment, a one-month commitment, or a six-month commitment. For example, on the pricing page under the Meta - Provisioned Throughput section, you will see three hourly prices per model unit for Llama 3.1 Instruct (70B): 

        - $24.00 per hour with no commitment;
        - $21.18 per hour with a one-month commitment;
        - $13.08 per hour with a six-month commitment. 

    AWS offers discounted rates for longer commitments, so the hourly rate is lowest with a six-month commitment.

3. Llama 3.1 Instruct (70B) Customization (Fine-tuning) Monthly Pricing as [1]

    The monthly pricing for Llama 3.1 Instruct (70B) customization consists of three parts:

        - Fine-tuning training, which is $0.00799 multiplied by the number of tokens. For example, if you fine-tuned the model with a total of 1,000 tokens, the charge would be $7.99.
        - Custom model storage per month, which is $1.95, covering storage in your AWS S3 bucket.
        - Hourly custom model inference, which is $23.50 per hour for the Provisioned Throughput rate with no commitment. The hourly rate decreases to $21.18 per hour if you select a one-month commitment.

If you have further questions, please feel free to ask. My regular working hours are from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM, Monday to Friday, AEST. If you need to contact me during these hours, simply reply to this case through the web interface, and I will respond promptly.

I look forward to our continued correspondence and to resolving your concerns to your utmost satisfaction.

subject:
RE:[CASE 174824875600936] Unable to find detailed information about
 Llama cost estimation
body_text:
Hello Meghdad,
 
Thank you for contacting AWS Premium Support. My name is Rushali, and I will be assisting you with this case.
 
>From the case notes, I understand that you want to know if there is a way to do fine-tuning with Amazon Bedrock and its associated costs within the service. Please feel free to let me know if I misunderstood anything.
 
I would like to inform you that with Amazon Bedrock, you can customize Foundation Models with your data to deliver tailored responses for specific tasks and your business context. You can fine-tune models with labeled data or using continued pre-training with unlabeled data.
 
For customization of a text generation model, you are charged for the model training based on the total number of tokens processed by the model (number of tokens in the training data corpus times the number of epochs) and for model storage charged per month per model [1]. 

Inferences using customized models are charged under the Provisioned Throughput plan and require you to purchase Provisioned Throughput. One model unit is made available with no commitment term for inference on a customized model. You will be charged for the number of hours that the first model unit you use for custom model inference takes. If you want to increase your throughput beyond one model unit, then you must purchase a 1-month or 6-month commitment term.
 
Implications for Your Use Case:

You said: “…make it available as an API, over an unlimited period of time, in which our users will prompt the fine-tuned model.”

So, your cost model will look like this:

Cost Category	                    How You’re Billed
Training Cost	One-time            based on tokens × epochs
Model Storage Cost	            Monthly, per fine-tuned model stored
Inference Cost	                    Hourly, via provisioned model units — even if no traffic occurs
Additional Throughput	            Optional, but billed in 1- or 6-month blocks if needed

So yes — you are billed for availability (hourly), not just actual usage.

This is not like the serverless public models on Bedrock (e.g., Claude, LLaMA without fine-tuning), which charge per token.

This documentation here includes all pricing breakdown for models in Bedrock, specified by regions [1]. Considering your use case, you might want to take a closer look at the “Pricing for model customization (fine-tuning and continued pretraining)” section for whatever model you want in the Model pricing details . In addition, you can see some pricing examples on how to calculate the total costs of using Bedrock model for customization at the bottom of the page.

For more detailed information on how to fine-tune your models using Amazon Bedrock, kindly review the referenced links [2], [3], and [4].
 
Thank you for contacting AWS Premium Support once again. I hope you found the above-provided documentation helpful. If you require any further clarifications or have other questions, please do not hesitate to reply to this case, and I would be happy to assist.
 
Have a AWSome day ahead and Stay Safe! 
 
References:
[1] https://aws.amazon.com/bedrock/pricing/
[2] https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/customize-models-in-amazon-bedrock-with-your-own-data-using-fine-tuning-and-continued-pre-training/
[3] https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/custom-models.html
[4] https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SdTWlQPy1jE
 

We value your feedback. Please share your experience by rating this and other correspondences in the AWS Support Center. You can rate a correspondence by selecting the stars in the top right corner of the correspondence.

Best regards,
Rushali D.
Amazon Web Services

subject: GitHub is updating the Actions pricing structure
body_text:
‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌

( https://github.com )

You are receiving this email because your usage of GitHub Actions may be impacted by upcoming changes to GitHub Actions pricing ( https://resources.github.com/actions/2026-pricing-changes-for-github-actions ).

*What’s changing, when*

* On *January 1, 2026* , all customers *will receive up to a 39% reduction in the net price of GitHub-hosted runners* , depending on the machine type used.
* On *March 1, 2026* , we are introducing a new $0.002 per-minute GitHub Actions cloud platform charge that *will apply to self-hosted runner usage*. Any usage subject to this charge will count toward the minutes included in your plan ( https://docs.github.com/billing/concepts/product-billing/github-actions#free-use-of-github-actions ).

No action is required on your part.

We’re excited to say that as a whole this means GitHub will be charging less than ever for Actions. 96% of customers will receive a lower bill or see no change.

Please note the price for runner usage in public repositories will remain free, and there will be no changes in price structure for GitHub Enterprise Server customers.

For more details, please visit our posts on GitHub’s Executive Insights page ( https://resources.github.com/actions/2026-pricing-changes-for-github-actions ) and the GitHub Changelog ( https://github.blog/changelog/2025-12-16-coming-soon-simpler-pricing-and-a-better-experience-for-github-actions/ ).

*Why we’re making this change*

Actions usage has grown significantly, across both CI/CD and agentic workloads. This update provides lower costs for most Actions users, aligns pricing with actual consumption patterns, and helps us continue investing in improvements to the Actions platform for the benefit of all customers.

*Recommended resources*

To help you prepare for this change, we’ve published several updated tools and guides:

* For answers to common questions about this change, see the FAQ in our post on GitHub’s Executive Insights page ( https://resources.github.com/actions/2026-pricing-changes-for-github-actions ).
* See the GitHub Actions runner pricing documentation ( https://docs.github.com/billing/reference/actions-runner-pricing ) for the new GitHub-hosted runner rates effective January 1, 2026.
* For more details on upcoming GitHub Actions releases, see the GitHub public roadmap ( https://github.com/orgs/github/projects/4247 ).
* For help estimating your expected Actions usage cost, use the newly updated Actions pricing calculator ( https://github.com/pricing/calculator#actions ).
* If you are interested in moving existing self-hosted runner usage to GitHub-hosted runners, see the SHR to GHR migration guide ( http://docs.github.com/actions/tutorials/migrate-to-github-runners ) in our documentation.

You can find more information on GitHub’s Executive Insights page ( https://resources.github.com/actions/2026-pricing-changes-for-github-actions ) and the GitHub Changelog ( https://github.blog/changelog/2025-12-16-coming-soon-simpler-pricing-and-a-better-experience-for-github-actions/ ).

Privacy policy ( https://help.github.com/articles/github-privacy-policy/ ) • Terms of use ( https://help.github.com/articles/github-terms-of-service/ ) • Contact us ( https://docs.github.com/en/support/contacting-github-support ) • Unsubscribe ( https://mailing.github.com/asm/unsubscribe/?user_id=15762298&data=irVYJytH20p5hnx1luIWpfwWTnCIA1CbWln7RlO6oppoMDAwdTAwMILTLgKlicnbi3iH9XXTP90A8avbnfXPnzWwontnxB1RhRVq7FTygzyvuTy6-RrphqjeOH1lHQ9ewnPsHp9Anagcd269T3Eh57uGDJL8EneHDWoTZBP26ROQWbU1loIQyhRmy2umlLwubMp1gzH-kWi-s1wEzxyK2sU5MuBBrRMWL-vfgZDBb-vAJJ_smCpFCyeJjKCArrkSiyGHtKfj3QukgLJFS5nN8pcoFICxzF7AAapKDRlO71NJJ-MQCKrHio_NUpCHrpL8xmHwlQxH-yKj_bDccmW55oWMhXuCDWEIFDNhVtSTqKVEOh_AvBdStHmlHeHRwtsdbpjkUrcmWuiwum3P68QfDxler2NcxqGOIBwlbZ8Dfnz9EVKgQevQp_OBhxJnN3-PWdUIcYGsgCNuW6N58ZUBWx1m5f_6GJ-gO-kHf_tLGElYNVUhmktgXqJWyZt1C9UI41FZc6d9zDyaFhzLttLYDwSpQrgUVUi5AUlcL2Qyr1SrCB5D74htbX6j95_bvpoPpbbr3FtoVXxk4ftHRQIL5kEVAUK7BzYKKpE96dYlzik9qOrgPPdGHez06x_9S1Muk_EzebYZ9WVZzFTEvbCHISVelK-AwFA0dpuCfGrRUbncIm46AD5Z9NtHOb-VHk6D7GRo6Z5YUBcFjSL1vYXB0E5w1gYDo7AGarUgM09x3paWxe3w2R2wc6vNC7f1fNZdpG7qgolYgoTzGPs5_Nv3Wh2J4bGopb0kwtxOHY1Tv7MfigoH03lHGCRVeVqQRKA6xj7AmX8foDq8hck9ci0zKG1o-n3oSWhLA8vQd-0iu4wyAoWLUbk40rMLSw9Je_I0hniDflpNydqqg4oeZrtGNVFYfA5N0MxJDhtPoGk2OWCoYQR6Lpj5-XICVNCT2V4RmFApzZ36X38V1kmqhx7LTjdmMoYjekLLZLPl5tvSV5OhZIn9xaPrZTW18O4OVT4eMfN2BbDg4F5iOF0Ofkx_9NuuM5PrNAr57zgami0vpaF3FtfZSebIMzUH-vw2QQo0zzUXR-3V52yX1Uu4ZAwR9AtQtR28d4MJSm_krqMmHVSZpoNt15FjTjBnXPJEODLxchoRfymi_U0= )

subject: RE: small product teams
body_text:
Hi Meghdad,

Is your capacity for product development aligned to your roadmap?

The key’s to focus on effective & agile toolstacks for faster traction vs feature-heavy and time-consuming solutions.

I’d love to tell you how ListedKit onboarded us to build their MVP in just 7 weeks.

Got 20 minutes next week?

Victoria Barouskaya, 
Chief Growth Officer at Paralect

subject: You just made a payment
Quick Follow-Up – Calibrion AI Seed Round
body_text:
Dear team at Volution Ventures,

I hope you’re doing well.

I wanted to briefly follow up on my previous message regarding Calibrion AI and our EUR 3 million seed round. We’ve continued to progress since my last email, with strong pilot interest from both European and US companies confirming our approach to enabling enterprises to unlock the true value of their proprietary data with LLMs.

Given Volution Ventures’ track record of supporting early-stage deep tech startups, I believe our vision for a market-leading LLM customization platform aligns well with your investment focus.

I’d love to share our pitch deck and arrange a short call to walk you through the product, our traction so far, and how we plan to scale this opportunity.

Could we find a 20–30 minute slot next week to connect? I’d be glad to adapt to your schedule.

Looking forward to your feedback.

Best regards,
Nils

--
Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 ・ nils@calibrion.ai <>
Französische Str. 20・10117 Berlin・Germany
www.calibrion.ai <http://www.calibrion.ai/>
￼￼

> On 22. Jul 2025, at 19:33, Nils Erbach <nils@calibrion.ai> wrote:
> 
> Dear team at Volution Ventures,
> 
> I am reaching out to introduce Calibrion AI, a data annotation platform enabling enterprises and organisations to fine-tune LLMs to their proprietary data. We built a working product and already reached pilot-stage interest from European and US companies in the eCom vertical.
> 
> While most LLM infra and dev platforms focus on model architecture and LLMOps, we focus on unlocking enterprise adoption through (proprietary) data and fine-tuning workflows.
> 
> Calibrion envisions a future where businesses harness their proprietary data and domain expertise to build specialized LLMs, turning generative AI from a threat into a strategic asset for both internal and market-facing applications.
> 
> Our mission is to become a market-leading platform for LLM customization and domain adaptation, setting the standard for performance and usability in the enterprise AI space.
> 
> To underscore the scale of this opportunity and the importance of what Calibrion does: In June 2025, Meta acquired a 49% stake in Scale AI (a company also focused on LLM data annotation and founded in 2016) for $14.8 billion at a $30 billion valuation. 
> 
> We are currently offering subscription for our EUR 3 m seed investment round to: 
> - expand our engineering and data science team
> - Accelerate go-to-market efforts across verticals 
> - Further develop our AI capabilities and integrate new LLM-driven features.
> 
> To accelerate the company's development, we assigned significant equity to an option pool for key employees and aim to be cash-flow positive within 18 months.
> 
> We would be happy to share our pitch deck and schedule a call to walk you through the live product. The product is fully developed and currently undergoing regular testing and stabilization.
> 
> Please find attached a letter outlining our fund-raising goals as well as a one-pager on Calibrion.
> 
> We look forward to hearing from you and would be glad to schedule a conversation at your convenience.
> 
> Best regards,
> Nils
> 
> -- 
> Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
> Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 <tel:+49+(0)30+478+662+25> ・ nils@calibrion.ai <mailto:meghdad@calibrion.ai>
> Französische Str. 20・10117 Berlin・Germany
> www.calibrion.ai <http://www.calibrion.ai/>
>

subject: B2B SaaS – Product ready - Zalando SE paid pilot secured – Seed Round USD 1.2M; Calibrion.ai - AI Infrastructure / Data annotation
body_text:
Dear Sunil,

We are building Calibrion, an agent-native data annotation platform adressing one of the largest barriers to organisational GenAI adoption: enabling proprietary LLM training through high-quality, rapid annotation. 

Calibrion delivers: 
- Agentic data annotation that accelerates annotation up to 100x (from months to hours)
- Cost savings of around 90%
- Built-in proprietary quality control to ensure accuracy at scale 
- 35% faster human-in-the-loop through research driven optimized workflows

We recently signed a 6 months paid pilot with Zalando SE, secured a verbal commitment from Siemens Energy AG E-Commerce division, and are negotiating pilots with Cotopaxi (US sustainable outdoor brand) and LILT (AI-driven translation leader). 

We are seeking USD 1.2M in seed funding to expand our team, onboard additional clients, and implement product-led-growth.

Our founder and CEO, Meghdad, Holds a PhD in Computer Science with a specialization in Natural Language Processing, and has over eight years of applied AI product experience. 

Given your investment focus, we think Calibrion could be a strong fit. You’ll find our one-pager, pitch-deck, and product demos attached. We’d be glad to arrange a 30-minute call to walk you through the product and discuss and our upcoming milestones.

Best regards,
Nils 


Nils Erbach

Finance, Legal & Business Development ・ Calibrion AI
Tel: +49 1 <tel:+49+(0)30+478+662+25>7662205065 ・ nils@calibrion.ai <mailto:meghdad@calibrion.ai>
Kolonnenstr. 8・10827 Berlin・Germany
www.calibrion.ai <https://www.calibrion.ai/>
￼
￼
￼

Logo_CT (https://dkcS3h04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/OV+113/dkcS3h04/VWgmzy6gwD3BN3Cvs4vXdgDcW3mxgk35CMSP6N1k24Y23lcq-W6N1vHY6lZ3nSW81fX2s59QGKRW8Lbnhs5TRDnlW5G262s5bQtGpW8-M1sk5JYJ68W7R-dnL8N-J16MnYwC23syJvW4SncMc6cKXk4W2GCR3l1v1PvNW2bL9yh1bQf0hW6YX-cs1qtnmZW3WmNBC7DCdX0W27v__m4dGJmhVNmRvM1HTWrsW2P3ZTl3KzDVzW31Z_mK7sF6FwW4JxV-j6BN7VGW5wXYlZ8qvrysW1QDnjp8twmy7W6cHPRT1cnfr6W3zsGfS5M5b0JW36CRnH7sc6_DW2q4KCt4LCjhxdH6VTC04 )

Hallo Meghdad,

am 09. Oktober 2025 ist es soweit: Wir eröffnen das Incubator Village Beeskow – ein neuer inspirierender Treffpunkt für Unternehmergeist, zukunftsweisende Geschäftsideen und regionale Entwicklung.

Nach unserer ersten Ankündigung freuen wir uns, Dir nun das offizielle Programm vorzustellen. Im Austausch mit Start-ups, Netzwerkpartner:innen und politischen Akteur:innen möchten wir diesen Tag nutzen, um kreative Impulse für eine lebendige Gründungskultur im ländlichen Raum zu setzen.

📍 Ort: Alte Tischlerei, Spreeinsel 6, 15848 Beeskow

🕒 Einlass: ab 15:30 Uhr

Unser vorläufiges Programm:

15:30 – 16:00 Uhr

Check-in & Welcome Drink

16:00 – 16:30 Uhr

Begrüßung & Auftakt

• Eröffnungspräsentation durch das Incubator Village-Team

• Grußworte durch

• Robert Czaplinski - Bürgermeister der Stadt Beeskow,

• Frank Steffen - Landrat des Landkreises Oder-Spree und

• Kerstin Jöntgen - Vorstandsmitglied der Investitionsbank des Landes Brandenburg

16:30 – 17:00 Uhr

Impulsvorträge

• Colin Berr, Founder Mentorlane - Netzwerkpartner

• Tim Jaudszims, Co-Founder Tiny Space - Expertenbeirat

17:00 – 17:30 Uhr

Start-up Pitches

ab 17:30 Uhr

Networking, Flying Buffet & Drinks, Start-up Lucky Box

subject: Andrew Ng Announces Agentic Document Extraction,Google’s Transformer 2.0 Fixes Memory, and GPT 4.5 versus Sonnet 3.7
body_text:
Welcome (back) to AI Minds, a newsletter about the brainy and sometimes zany world of AI, brought to you by the Deepgram editorial team.

### In this edition:

* 🎥 Attention isn’t all you need: Google’s “Transformer 2.0” and AI Memory Recall

* 🧠 Researchers use deep learning to detect mental illness

* 🦊 Stereotyping animals with vision-language models

* 💻 Deepgram & Vonage Technical Webinar: How to build responsive voice agents

* 🛣️ Meet Deepgram at HumanX & NVIDIA GTC!

* 📲 Three new, trending AI apps for you!

* 📄 Andrew Ng announces Agentic Document Extraction

* 🐦 Social Media Buzz: Best code embedding model in the market

* 🚁 Drone uses machine learning to track its subjects with a camera

* 🎙️ AI Minds Podcast with Pablo Palafox, Co-Founder and CEO at HappyRobot

* 🤖 Bonus Video - Two AI agents play game OVER SOUND: Sonnet 3.7 vs GPT 4.5

Thanks for letting us crash your inbox; let’s party. 🎉

----------
**We coded with the brand-new Whisper-v3 over the past week, and the results were **_**not**_** what we expected. Check it out ****[here](https://deepgram.com/learn/whisper-v3-results)****!**


--------------------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/18c135d7-112b-4588-8b9f-659e512b4066/div-2.gif?t=1700246279)
Caption: 


----------# 🎥 Attention isn’t all you need: Google’s “Transformer 2.0” and AI Memory Recall

Youtube: How Google's "Transformer 2.0" Might Be The AI Breakthrough We Need (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UMkCmOTX5Ow)

_**Video description**_: “In this video, [Bycloud] will be sharing the research that aims to solve the problem of context window, kv-cache, and memory recall efficiency. Even though the title only mentions Google's research, [Bycloud] also included research from Meta and Sakana AI. They paved a good way to introduce the idea of AI memory.”

**Papers mentioned in the video:**

* [An Evolved Universal Transformer Memory](https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.13166)

* [Memory Layers at Scale](https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.09764)

* [Titans: Learning to Memorize at Test Time](https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.00663)

----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/18c135d7-112b-4588-8b9f-659e512b4066/div-2.gif?t=1700246279)
Caption: 


----------# 🔍 Detecting Mental Illness with Deep Learning and Stereotyping Animals with vision-language AI

View image: (https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXe3sSHXcYM7XqGfnk8hmlbCebsM9xtJQu2UYkcU7UcQjHaBvOJsHEG-rHqA1s6FM1GqlyDOWYTCFF6hRvQdkH3_cYMmL2P86kqGcrScBd7VuCDEdkPtfXP8SXYTcxtWpWoeZKojeA?key=9ins5EzfjGO-dXh_GvI9NV24)
Follow image link: (https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.04342)
Caption: 

[Tutorial on Using Machine Learning and Deep Learning Models for Mental Illness Detection](https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.04342) - This tutorial provides practical guidance to address common challenges in applying machine learning and deep learning methods for mental health detection on platforms like social media. It focuses on strategies for working with diverse datasets, improving text preprocessing, and addressing issues such as imbalanced data and model evaluation.

———————————————————————————

View image: (https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXd-k2IH7pPP-yxF3LtxZD4Q1ajxWdl8qlPAu4fM7y7afBamMfCl0hYk-bYpBqRPV6_vJm-f5g8Hjz41slepKadyUzFRQRtoBix5TfKTKTky0jXhNqoNBOChgscSKclBlKVWKu5MSA?key=9ins5EzfjGO-dXh_GvI9NV24)
Follow image link: (https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.12433)
Caption: 

[Owls are wise and foxes are unfaithful: Uncovering animal stereotypes in vision-language models](https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.12433) - This study investigates how animal stereotypes manifest in vision-language models during the task of image generation. Through targeted prompts, the authors explore whether DALL-E perpetuates stereotypical representations of animals, such as "owls as wise," "foxes as unfaithful," etc.

----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/18c135d7-112b-4588-8b9f-659e512b4066/div-2.gif?t=1700246279)
Caption: 


----------# ⚡ Technical Deep Dive: How to Build Responsive Voice Agents with Vonage & Deepgram

View image: (https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXfvhJ20dfmdt2xb5gEabQPGPxF2NspapSOG2ZT61PirK89A9xj1fe2IkF6j_2CHcyj4g7KXLNmJtGg47Ku6oGgnYVjR0xWHCm4Mx0Rtmukavm4tWDiFMJBMZfJxaZgKm4PfL59b6g?key=9ins5EzfjGO-dXh_GvI9NV24)
Follow image link: (https://www.crowdcast.io/c/vonagedeepgram?utm_source=deepgram&utm_medium=deepgram&utm_campaign=deepgram)
Caption: 

Learn how to build human-like voice agents for customer support, appointment scheduling and more in our March 26th technical webinar with Vonage.

**When**: Wednesday 26th March 2025, 10:00 PT / 12:00 ET / 17:00 GMT

**Where**: Online

⭐️  [Save your spot here!](https://www.crowdcast.io/c/vonagedeepgram?utm_source=deepgram&utm_medium=deepgram&utm_campaign=deepgram) ⭐️ 

**_Hosted by_****:**

* Benjamin Aronov, Developer Advocate at Vonage

* Tony Chan, Senior Solutions Engineer at Vonage

* Damien Murphy, Applied Engineer at Deepgram

----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/18c135d7-112b-4588-8b9f-659e512b4066/div-2.gif?t=1700246279)
Caption: 


----------# 🔊 Deepgram is Hitting the Road: HumanX & NVIDIA GTC

View image: (https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXdjjcpSfy7XwwAXl-ndq_SNLV_6vEplxa0PozTIy8Of3_NIzpxSS5avOwJk-ybRxk_tpBhnwxVbuZUegsdl7qurcpYeXqSnqcC3IDHqVKrprjb1U7RSHCV22A1LWfzJp3nAYH23FQ?key=9ins5EzfjGO-dXh_GvI9NV24)
Follow image link: (https://deepgram.com/contact-us)
Caption: 

We’re gearing up for HumanX & NVIDIA GTC — two of the biggest AI events of the year. If you’re building with voice AI, stop by to see how our APIs can power real-time, scalable speech applications with low latency and high accuracy.

📍_**Find us here**_:

* 🚀 HumanX – Booth 825

* 🚀NVIDIA GTC – Booth 1709

Let’s meet onsite—[grab time with our team!](https://deepgram.com/contact-us)

----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/18c135d7-112b-4588-8b9f-659e512b4066/div-2.gif?t=1700246279)
Caption: 


----------# 🐝 Social Media Buzz: Ng announces Agentic Document Extraction

Tweet:  (https://twitter.com/svpino/status/1896555790066196805)

———————————————————————————

Tweet:  (https://twitter.com/AndrewYNg/status/1895183929977843970)

———————————————————————————

Tweet:  (https://twitter.com/1Marc/status/1446878170242748423)

----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/18c135d7-112b-4588-8b9f-659e512b4066/div-2.gif?t=1700246279)
Caption: 


----------# 📲 Three new, trending AI Apps for you!

View image: (https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXcUgZvCY3w33NUsrXyoTzY9xAKZw1IlJ3_TbtdxW0yX7knYz0qp9LhoCMUHpIYqPFwg0YlLXtdmNQdzbE9e7qDMAlz4MRSiZxBsjbfTov2CpGqGm9hEjXLjoNUNEULRon9nECcMcg?key=9ins5EzfjGO-dXh_GvI9NV24)
Follow image link: (https://deepgram.com/ai-apps/snapvid)
Caption: 

[Snapvid AI](https://deepgram.com/ai-apps/snapvid) helps save time in the video editing process by adding subtitles and emojis in seconds. Additionally, you can insert video footage, transitions, and sound effects with just one click.

———————————————————————————

View image: (https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXf-ygzLkhSFb4dd0tjvXaxE4EWxl22ekBqhFTR4F43DYfJ4oKhVwA_3TbQoE4g-KMCWkespeQyaL07KMgTp9UikKKKriJpoO28zoTjvpiBs6lXz6HbVOeuMTb7dTIaJ-0mxqu1_aw?key=9ins5EzfjGO-dXh_GvI9NV24)
Follow image link: (https://deepgram.com/ai-apps/lenso-ai)
Caption: 

[Lenso](https://deepgram.com/ai-apps/lenso-ai) is a cutting-edge application designed to enhance productivity and streamline workflows. By leveraging advanced technology, Lenso offers users a seamless experience that caters to a wide array of needs. Whether you’re a professional looking to optimize your daily tasks or a team in need of effective collaboration tools, Lenso provides a comprehensive solution.

———————————————————————————

View image: (https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXf1EhztTmzYJQ7WRtcri-pPBwwnxBER_hNVDVfLZMDhlugLkKa_gMz1IC-81VQqWp8suoy3tJGRRoWXhFrBsBiQ-efeXvZ2IlzEOVkl9o8dj_J-IHAShYZEn2lMCVsn_H_dx80x1w?key=9ins5EzfjGO-dXh_GvI9NV24)
Follow image link: (https://deepgram.com/ai-apps/mix-check-studio)
Caption: 

[Mix Check Studio](https://deepgram.com/ai-apps/mix-check-studio), powered by RoEx, is a cutting-edge platform designed to provide precise feedback and enhancement for audio mixes and masters. Utilizing advanced AI technology, Mix Check Studio aims to streamline the audio production process by offering users the ability to upload their tracks and receive detailed analysis and improvements. This tool is especially beneficial for musicians, producers, and audio engineers.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/18c135d7-112b-4588-8b9f-659e512b4066/div-2.gif?t=1700246279)
Caption: 

# 🎤 The AI Minds Podcast

Youtube: Pablo Palafox, Co-Founder and CEO at HappyRobot | AI Minds #051 (https://youtu.be/7-BrT6ac6ac?si=gFwc3zoD4XQGbwyO)

This episode of the AI Minds Podcast features Pablo Palafox, Co-Founder and CEO at HappyRobot. HappyRobot automates communication across channels with AI workers that integrate with your systems, manage conversations, & log data. 

He emphasizes the customer-centric approach they’ve taken, continuously refining their platform based on feedback from the logistics sector to ensure real-world value and address genuine business pain points.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/18c135d7-112b-4588-8b9f-659e512b4066/div-2.gif?t=1700246279)
Caption: 

# 🤖 Bonus Video: Two AI agents play game OVER SOUND: Sonnet 3.7 vs GPT 4.5

Youtube: Two AI agents play game OVER SOUND: Sonnet 3.7 vs GPT 4.5 (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=umNu2gg7Hr0)

_**Video Description**_: “All the communication is happening exclusively by sound, using Gibberlink (powered by ggwave protocol). The bots were not programmed to play this game. One of the bots simply had an objective "play and win tic tac toe" in system prompt.”


———

You are reading a plain text version of this post. For the best experience, copy and paste this link in your browser to view the post online:
https://www.aiminds.com/p/ng-announces-agentic-document-extraction-google-s-transformer-2-0-fixes-memory

Nils

🍂 Red Sift Quarterly Vol. 6: Fall into Q4's biggest updates
body_text:
Leaf it to us: Q4's top product updates

Red Sift Quarterly vol 6 ( https://redsift.com/?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol6&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing )

---------------------------------------
Leaf it to us: Q4's top product updates
---------------------------------------

Welcome to Volume 6 of the latest updates and innovations from Red Sift. Each quarter, we share the latest on our products, customer stories, and upcoming Sifter sightings. Check out what's new:

* Brand Trust expands protection from domains to social platforms ( https://blog.redsift.com/product-release/red-sifts-quarterly-product-release-fall-2025/?utm_content=intro-bullet&utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol6&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ).
* Certificates Lite ( https://blog.redsift.com/certificates/active-certificate-scanning-and-smarter-expiry-alerts/?utm_content=intro-bullet&utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol6&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ) gets a major update: Active certificate scanning & smarter alerts.
* Red Sift partners with GuidePoint Security ( https://blog.redsift.com/news/red-sift-now-offered-through-guidepoint-security-in-new-partnership/?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol6&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ) to protect over a third of the Fortune 500.
* Get ready for DMARCbis ( https://redsift.com/guides/dmarcbis-guide?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol6&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ): Understand what's changing and how to prepare.

⭐️ What's new with us?
--------------------------

Social Media Monitoring for company and executive impersonation in Brand Trust
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Impersonation threats don't stop at domains. Businesses are increasingly facing fake profiles that misuse their brand or executives' identities to deceive customers and partners. Social Media Monitoring extends protection beyond domains, detecting company and executive impersonation across Facebook, Instagram, X, TikTok, YouTube, and LinkedIn. See the full announcement ( https://blog.redsift.com/product-release/red-sifts-quarterly-product-release-fall-2025/?utm_content=first-para&utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol6&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ).

Certificates Lite gets "Active certificate" scanning & configurable alerts
--------------------------------------------------------------------------

Certificates Lite, the free expiry monitoring service recommended by Let's Encrypt, now runs daily scans of your endpoints and only alerts you on certificates that are actually in use and close to expiry. That means fewer noisy warnings and clearer visibility to keep your domains secure. Read more about the update ( https://blog.redsift.com/certificates/active-certificate-scanning-and-smarter-expiry-alerts/?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol6&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ).

Quarterly Product Release: Fall Edition
---------------------------------------

Updates to Brand Trust and Certificates Lite are just a glimpse of what's new. This quarter also brings enhancements to OnDMARC reporting, improved alert customization across products, and performance optimizations. For a full roundup of every Red Sift update this quarter, check out the Fall Product Release ( https://blog.redsift.com/product-release/red-sifts-quarterly-product-release-fall-2025/?utm_content=full-roundup&utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol6&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ).

Leading US retailer switches from Agari to Red Sift OnDMARC
-----------------------------------------------------------

One of the largest U.S. retailers replaced Agari with Red Sift OnDMARC to gain the domain-level visibility, usability, and hands-on support they needed to stay secure and compliant across 80+ domains. Read the full story ( https://redsift.com/resource-center/case-study/leading-us-retailer?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol6&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ) to see how Red Sift delivered a seamless migration with zero downtime.

🧠 thought leadership
-----------------------

* Red Sift's CEO, Rahul Powar, speaks with Mark Thompson on AI's Shadow of the Algorithm. Watch here ( https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UMYFjsLH3ZI&utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol6&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ).
* From compliance to confidence ( https://app.livestorm.co/redsift/from-compliance-to-confidence-advancing-your-email-authentication-strategy?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol6&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ): Advancing your email authentication strategy.
* Protecting patients in healthcare ( https://drive.google.com/file/d/1GVYHepHAQv-67KCwivhgM2J46gHzEs8c/view?usp=sharing&utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol6&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ): Why DMARC is a critical defense against email spoofing.

🗞️ In the news
-------------------

* Associate Press ( https://apnews.com/press-release/ein-presswire-newsmatics/red-sift-expands-brand-trust-with-social-media-monitoring-to-stop-impersonation-threats-ff833e85365a325a6b4fff045e38367a?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol6&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ): Red Sift Expands Brand Trust with Social Media Monitoring
* Boston Business Journal ( https://www.bizjournals.com/boston/news/2025/07/31/red-sift-bank-vulnerability-phishing.html?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol6&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ): Over 50% of US banks are vulnerable to phishing scams
* ChannelE2E ( https://www.channele2e.com/news/red-sift-adds-email-brand-protection-tools-to-guidepoint-services?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol6&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ): Red Sift joins GuidePoint Security's lineup of reseller offerings
* CU Daily ( https://thecudaily.com/cybersecurity-firm-says-its-found-75-of-cus-vulnerable-to-email-spoofing-attacks/?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol6&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ): Cybersecurity firm says it found 75% of CUs vulnerable to email spoofing
* Downtown with Rich Kimball ( https://downtownwithrichkimball.com/2025/09/18/rahul-powar-9-18-25/?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol6&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ): Interview with Rahul Powar
* Mornings with Pat Kreitlow ( https://civicmedia.us/shows/mornings-with-pat-kreitlow/2025/09/29/deep-concerns-about-deep-fakes-hour-2?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol6&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ): Deep concerns about deep fakes with Rahul Powar
* Waste360 ( https://www.waste360.com/sustainability/red-sift-releases-annual-sustainability-report-showcasing-companywide-esg-progress?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol6&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ): Red Sift releases annual sustainability report

📅 Connect with Red Sift
--------------------------

Catch the Red Sift team in person or online:

* IDC IT Strategy & Cybersecurity Summit ( https://my.idc.com/eu/events/72077-idc-it-strategy-cybersecurity-summit-austria?_gl=1*v2rb6o*_up*MQ..*_ga*MTYyMjgzMzkzMy4xNzYxNjgyNjAw*_ga_Y7CNRMFF6J*czE3NjE2ODI1OTkkbzEkZzEkdDE3NjE2ODI2MDckajUyJGwwJGgw*_ga_541ENG1F9X*czE3NjE2ODI1OTkkbzEkZzEkdDE3NjE2ODI2MDckajUyJGwwJGgw&utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol6&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ) - November 11 | Vienna, Austria
* Defending against multi-channel brand impersonation ( https://app.livestorm.co/redsift/defending-against-multi-channel-brand-impersonation?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol6&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ) - November 13 | Virtual
* ISMS Forum ( https://www.ismsforum.es/?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol6&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ) - November 13 | Madrid, Spain
* Closing the gaps: Multi-channel brand impersonation in healthcare ( https://app.livestorm.co/redsift/multi-channel-brand-impersonation-in-healthcare?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol6&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ) - November 18 | Virtual
* Durbury Tech Days 2025 ( https://core-ict.be/durbuy-tech-days-2025/?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol6&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ) - November | Ardennes, France
* Cisco Connect Sweden - December 2 | Stockholm, Sweden
* Advancing your email authentication strategy for 2026 ( https://app.livestorm.co/redsift/advancing-your-email-security-strategy-for-2026?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol6&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ) - December 2 | Virtual (Australia and New Zealand)

We hope this edition of Red Sift Quarterly brings you fresh insights and even fresher reasons to keep your cybersecurity posture strong.

Until next time,
Team Red Sift

 

Red Sift helps 1,200+ organizations build proactive security.

Ready to speak to our team of experts?
Contact us ( marketing@redsift.io?subject=Question for an expert&utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol6&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ) 💌

💰  Troy Kirwin shares a common mistake founders make when fundraising [ https://substack.com/redirect/e7895e06-6216-4584-b51d-2f03e4814a9e?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]…
📢  Jordan Mazer argues that >4 interviews is “TOO MANY INTERVIEWS” [ https://substack.com/redirect/3d150497-867d-4d28-aec4-65033055f8c9?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
💼  Join our talent network [ https://substack.com/redirect/e7975a0d-6c82-4cab-92fa-0c307bb56008?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] for more opportunities. If we see a fit for you, we'll intro you to relevant founders in the portfolio.
Recently we asked a handful of speedrun alumni: “What’s the most expensive lesson you’ve had to learn as a founder?”
Each replied [ https://substack.com/redirect/55fd0732-e976-40e4-8a36-4cb8e42092f4?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] with minor variations on the same answer: hiring mistakes.
Hiring is among the most consequential decisions you’ll make as a founder, especially in the earliest stages of your company’s life. It is also one of the areas where founders come to us the most often asking for advice.
Today, we asked a16z speedrun’s Head of Talent Jordan Mazer [ https://substack.com/redirect/a4003414-3071-4a1e-9c67-c433cee757db?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] and Head of People Tom Hammer [ https://substack.com/redirect/55346cc0-b300-4bf7-9650-145e913709bf?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] for the advice they most often find themselves giving to founders making their first hires. Here’s what they shared:
1) Know thyself, and where you’re headed
The first maxim for hiring well is, as with the ancient Greeks [ https://substack.com/redirect/d9f6986d-e4b5-481b-b18c-8987049a8586?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ], to know yourself and your goals.
Jordan Mazer says that many founders struggle with hiring people who are a good fit, in part because they aren’t yet clear enough on what their objective is. “Do you actually know what you’re trying to accomplish?” he asks. “If you don’t, you certainly don’t want to bring on a ton of people, because they can take you in the wrong direction.”
Jordan uses the metaphor of a rowboat to describe early stage teams. For new hires to add value and momentum, everyone has to be rowing in the same direction. And the stakes are very, very high:
The automatic assumption that many founders have is that by adding someone to the team, they’re going to have a positive impact on their velocity. And, they assume, the question is just how positive? They imagine it might be a 2x speed increase or maybe 3x increase.
But sometimes the person you’re hiring—once they’re in the boat with you—is rowing the opposite way. So either they’re neutralizing you or they’re pulling you backward. That’s a totally possible outcome.
There are so many things that can go wrong with new hires. They can make other people upset. They can quit. Any number of things can happen with the wrong person on your team. So if you’re small, and the impact of a single person can literally double your velocity or halve it, or even take you backwards, that’s a big deal.
So goals matter, but so does your own leadership style. You have to ask yourself: What sort of people do you work best with?
“The first thing is acknowledging who you are and not just who you want to be,” Jordan says. “Then you have to hire people who fit well with who you actually are.”
Maybe you prefer working with more social types who you can bounce ideas off of and who make the workplace more fun. Or maybe you just want people who lock in and focus entirely on getting the job done. There’s nothing wrong with either approach—but you have to be intentional about selecting people who match your preferred style.
Because the stakes are so high here, Jordan says he often tells founders that “you probably shouldn’t be hiring until it’s excruciatingly painful on your own or without the additional resources.” Because the risk of a misaligned hire is so high, “You probably shouldn’t be doing opportunistic hires at all.” Instead, each early hire has to be extremely carefully considered.
2) Lean on your network
So what are the odds that you successfully bring on a new hire that ends up being a fit for your team? Jordan estimates that “even the most effective hiring machines are only about 80% accurate.” But that figure represents the best-case scenario:
80%. That’s your odds when you know the exact job you’re hiring for, and you’ve interviewed 10,000 people before, and you’ve seen exactly what works and what doesn’t. But any normal person or organization probably has more like a 1 in 2 shot of succeeding on any given hire.
For early stage founders, then, Jordan often advises leaning toward in-network referrals for your first hires. “If you’re hiring people that you know and have worked with, or who are only one-degree separated, the chance that it’s gonna go well is much higher.”
One upside of hiring from within a closer circle is that you’ll know where to look to get reliable referrals when doing diligence on candidates. Even if the candidate isn’t someone you’ve worked with directly, if you have trusted mutual connections you’ll have a better chance of getting honest feedback about the person, which can inform your hiring decision. Even this approach isn’t a guarantor of success, of course, but it lowers the odds that you’ll be shocked by something that only comes up after they’ve started the job.
The problem with this approach, Jordan admits, is that it doesn’t scale. “You cannot scale that as a hiring mechanism for long,” he says, “but you can certainly do it for the first two or three people.”
And those very earliest hires are often among the most impactful.
3) Decide on your compensation framework early
One of the toughest challenges for new founders is the question of how to compensate early hires. What’s reasonable? What’s not?
Tom Hammer points out that even founders who’ve been a hiring manager at a bigger company can feel out of their depth on this topic.
“If you’ve ever touched compensation for other humans at a bigger company, you know that there was actually a whole bunch of work that went into building the infrastructure that you used,” Tom says. “It’s sort of like the invisible water around you at a big company. But somebody had to build that.”
For this reason, Tom says he advises founders to think about their compensation philosophy early:
Every hire you make without building some basic infrastructure potentially represents backwards compatibility complexity that you’re creating in your organization.
For the first ten or so hires, you’re likely hiring a lot out of existing networks, and you’d anticipate a much higher degree of flexibility to get the early team built right. But sometimes you see companies go for years without putting real structure in place, where they’re just making up salaries and titles for basically everybody in the organization. As a much larger team, cracks start to form and they have to retrofit into some rationalized compensation system. Every single one of those decisions is now a potential renegotiation with somebody.
This is one of those things where you can start feeling the pain of not having a framework long after it’s become a problem. So being thoughtful about how you make compensation and title decisions is one of those hygiene-improving things that I like to pull forward. I encourage founders to build it earlier than is intuitive.
All that said, how do you determine what the right market comp for any given job opening is? Ultimately, you want the people on your team to feel they’ve been treated consistently and thoughtfully.
Having some sort of structured way for determining compensation, Tom argues, makes comp conversations feel less arbitrary. Normally, this means leaning on high quality third party compensation data. “And if I’m a candidate, it makes me feel more confident that the team members I’m going to go work alongside had the same conversation that I did. A simple framework for compensation also allows you to tell a candidate “this is my best offer” in a way that ensures compensation at your company doesn’t become a product of who negotiated the hardest. This is 100% achievable for even very early stage founders, and surprisingly differentiating because many don’t do it.”
4) When making exceptions on comp, know your reasons why
The obvious pushback founders often give, when urged to settle on a compensation framework, is that it feels premature, or even harmful to their chances to land exceptional candidates.
Shouldn’t founders make exceptions for the best talent? Tom tackles this one:
The argument people make is: “We should just go pay whatever it takes to get the people that we need.” And, while there is a logical limit to that point because obviously you also have to care about dilution and affordability and runway impact, I’m not totally opposed to that logic at the very earliest stages.
If you need to reach for somebody who’s going to be pivotal to your business, you should know how much of an exception you’re making. Ben Horowitz wrote over a decade ago [ https://substack.com/redirect/2051befc-e92a-4787-a727-93d039d068f1?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]  [ https://substack.com/redirect/2051befc-e92a-4787-a727-93d039d068f1?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]about how you can only hold the bus for one player—there’s only room for one Dennis Rodman on your team. Arm yourself with the knowledge that comes from having quality data. You should know how your offer (or your candidate’s ask) stacks up to the market for similarly capitalized startups.
Then, once you know how much of an exception you’re making, you can do something that I have found to be very, very compelling for early stage candidates, which is you can walk them through how you got to your offer. You can help them add context to the numbers you’re talking. A candidate might be comparing you to a public company salary, not realizing you’re paying generously relative to other early stage startups.
When it comes to early stage startups, most halfway sophisticated candidates who are knocking on your door aren’t going to be maximizing for cash. They could go almost anywhere else and make more cash than what you’re going to offer them. What they do want is a sense of consistency, a sense of a real process. And of course, a founder who can tell the story of “why us,” which ultimately informs the value of your equity.
Jordan Mazer points out that this part of the conversation actually is still often challenging, when you’re interviewing candidates new to the world of early stage startups. When hiring, Jordan says, you will often encounter people who aren’t prepared for the sort of salary cut that’s often required to join a pre-revenue company.
Most people like to imagine all the benefits of going to work for a startup.
They might say “I’m going to have agency and there’s going to be no red tape” and they’ll name 15 reasons they’re excited about a startup. And I go, “What if you had to give up 60% of your salary?” They often respond with some version of “Well, I have a mortgage and three kids and they’re all in private school.” Okay. You have to be prepared to make big changes if you don’t have enough savings to continue living your lifestyle. A lot of those people end up acknowledging startups aren’t a fit for them. They have the realization of “this is not a welcome change” at this point in their lives. I don’t know that all of them understand that right away.
I end up explaining to a lot of people, the reality is that you want to go work for a business that has the financial facility to meet its goals. And if your cash compensation is too high, you’re going to become an extractive force on the business such that you actually might stymie their ability to be successful.
Tom says when it comes to this topic, he likes the bring back the rowboat metaphor:
It’s like if you’re on that rowboat, and you’re stripping wood off of the hull to make a fire to keep warm. As an employee joining a startup, every dollar of salary you take is a dollar you can’t spend on growing the business into something as valuable as possible for you and all your fellow owners. So this is why a candidate should care about the business having, as Jordan says previously, the “financial facility to meet its goals.” How cold are you willing to stay to get as far across the ocean as possible?
Jordan makes the case that making too large of an exception for a candidate is bad for them as well as for the business:
As a candidate, it’s not good for you either. With early stage startups, these are businesses that have almost no money. So if you meet a founder who’s willing to pay you equivalent to a big Fortune 500 company, I recommend you reflect on whether that bodes well for you or for the company.
If you’re a founder, you have to be able to tell some candidates that they might be better off at a series C company. I tell many people to consider going to a growth stage business. They’re going to earn a stronger base salary  and have a more certain outcome.
5) Correct mistakes quickly
Hiring misfires are inevitable. But Jordan and Tom agree early stage founders should in many cases be spending an outsized portion of their time on building their teams.
Tom says that the most common trap he sees founders falling into with bad fits is simply failing to act quickly. Many founders adopt wishful thinking, or hesitate because they’re worried how they’ll be perceived by their team:
I get founders telling me “someone on my team isn’t cutting it, they’re six months in and it’s not working.” For some founders it seems to come down to the difficulty of acknowledging that you made a mistake when hiring the candidate in the first place.
The first mistake you made was hiring them, but that’s a forgivable mistake, because you can and should adjust your assessment process. The second mistake is the one you’re making every single day you don’t do something about it. And for early stage teams, maintaining a high performance bar and strong talent density is so incredibly important, as it’s one of the big competitive advantages of small teams.
Jordan Mazer says that this is one of the toughest lessons founders can learn.
Because the people on your team are so important to your company’s future, quickly accepting responsibility for mistakes is one of the most important skills any leader can have: “The faster you’re able to accept that you’ve made a mistake and then do something about it quickly, the less hiring is a risk to your organization.”
That’s it for this week! Thanks again to Jordan Mazer [ https://substack.com/redirect/a4003414-3071-4a1e-9c67-c433cee757db?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] and Tom Hammer [ https://substack.com/redirect/55346cc0-b300-4bf7-9650-145e913709bf?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] for sharing their hard-earned hiring wisdom with us. Be sure to follow both on LinkedIn for more tips like these.
Like this post? Forward it to your team! For more weekly dives into the world of early stage startups, subscribe to our newsletter below.

subject: Calibrion x b2venture – Follow-up on seed round
body_text:
Dear Andreas,
Thank you again for taking the time to meet on November 20th and for the thoughtful discussion about Calibrion and the future of AI data infrastructure. It was a pleasure to explore how our agent-native annotation platform fits into your view of AI as a new coordination and automation layer in software.​ <https://www.b2venture.vc/stories/part-ii-beyond-productivity-how-ai-is-becoming-the-new-coordination-layer>​

As shared, Calibrion enables AI agents and human experts to collaboratively generate and validate fine-tuning datasets for LLMs and diffusion models, delivering data up to 100x faster and at a fraction of today’s cost while maintaining rigorous, scientific quality controls. With early traction from enterprise customers such as Zalando, Siemens Energy, Cotopaxi, and Lilt, we see strong validation that high-quality, scalable data pipelines are becoming a critical bottleneck for AI-first organizations.​

We are currently raising a EUR 0.6M seed round to expand the team, scale pilots into production, and move towards a product-led growth motion, and your suggested minimum ticket size of EUR 500k would fit very well into this round. We would be excited to have you and b2venture as the lead investor or to match your preferred ticket alongside a small group of complementary co-investors. <https://swissstartupassociation.ch/2024/04/03/meet-our-investor-member-andreas-goldi-partner-at-b2venture/>​​

If helpful, we would be happy to share a brief data room (financial model, product roadmap, and pilot pipeline) and to walk you through it in a short follow-up call over the next one to two weeks. Please let us know if you would like to proceed to a deeper dive, or if there are specific questions or areas of concern we can address ahead of your internal discussion.​

Best regards,
Nils

Nils Erbach


Calibrion AI
Tel: +49 1 <tel:+49+(0)30+478+662+25>7662205065 ・ nils@calibrion.ai <mailto:meghdad@calibrion.ai>
Kolonnenstr. 8・10827 Berlin・Germany
www.calibrion.ai <https://www.calibrion.ai/>

subject: B2B SaaS – Product ready - Zalando SE paid pilot secured – Seed Round kEUR 600; Calibrion.ai - AI Infrastructure / Data annotation
body_text:
Dear Andreas,

We are building Calibrion, an agent-native data annotation platform adressing one of the largest barriers to organisational GenAI adoption: enabling proprietary LLM training through high-quality, rapid annotation. 

Calibrion delivers: 
- Agentic data annotation that accelerates annotation up to 100x (from months to hours)
- Cost savings of around 90%
- Built-in proprietary quality control to ensure accuracy at scale 
- 35% faster human-in-the-loop through research driven optimized workflows

We recently signed a 6 months paid pilot with Zalando SE, secured a verbal commitment from Siemens Energy AG E-Commerce division, and are negotiating pilots with Cotopaxi (US sustainable outdoor brand) and LILT (AI-driven translation leader). 

We are seeking EUR 600,000 in seed funding to expand our team, onboard additional clients, and implement product-led-growth.

Our founder and CEO, Meghdad, Holds a PhD in Computer Science with a specialization in Natural Language Processing, and has over eight years of applied AI product experience. 

Given your investment focus, we think Calibrion could be a strong fit. You’ll find our one-pager, pitch-deck, and product demos attached. We’d be glad to arrange a 30-minute call to walk you through the product and discuss and our upcoming milestones.

Best regards,
Nils 


Nils Erbach

Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI

Thanks!
Nikras
