body_text:
Google Workspace logo



Data Regions in your Google Workspace Service Specific Terms now cover  
additional products and data types.



Dear administrator,

We're writing to inform you about recent updates to the Google Workspace  
Service Specific Terms, effective immediately. These updates are part of  
our ongoing efforts to refine and clarify the terms under which our  
services are provided, including covering new features that have been added  
to Google Workspace.

We've provided additional information to help you understand the changes.

What this means for your organization

Key changes:


We now support regional data processing for certain editions of Google  
Workspace
The Workspace Service Specific Terms have been updated with details on:

Functionality enabling in-use data processing within certain Workspace  
Services and editions
An expanded list of datasets covered by certain data regions policies
Additional information regarding the Assured Controls products and storage  
limits policy


What you need to do

Action advised:


Review the updated terms. You can see how we have changed our terms over  
time by reviewing previous versions at the bottom of the relevant terms  
pages.
No action is required from you for these changes to take effect. We have  
published the updates in the following sections of the Google Workspace  
Service Specific Terms:

Section 1 (Data Regions)
Section 16 (Assured Controls)
Section 17 (Storage Limits)


The terms are effective in accordance with your Workspace agreement.

We're here to help


Find details on in-use processing and covered datasets in the help center  
article, Data covered by data regions
Learn more about Assured Controls and Assured Controls Plus in this help  
center article
Refer to the Google Workspace storage FAQs for admins page for information  
on the storage limits policy

Thanks for choosing Google Workspace.

– The Google Workspace Team




Was this information helpful?

Perplexity is Getting Sued... Again.
body_text:
### Find customers on Roku this holiday season

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/9fbb57f7-7cd9-415d-8b68-c1569fa4d99b/1200x600_Holiday_General.png?t=1764725436)
Follow image link: (https://advertising.roku.com/ads-manager-holidays?utm_medium=paid_newsletter&utm_source=beehiiv&utm_campaign=pem-us-ads-manager-beehiiv-cpc-q42025&utm_content=BFCM_LP_cpc&utm_term=VPSLCCALRN&_bhiiv=opp_e3911536-e69a-4721-9f39-243c74240b8e_a420a27c&bhcl_id=a3b23be4-0268-4444-9c5d-6d7e1e89191e_bd74a657-278f-4ddb-833c-61e078d26306_70dc14dc-168a-41ff-81d7-6d2f0075867e)
Caption: 

Now through the end of the year is prime streaming time on Roku, with viewers spending 3.5 hours each day streaming content and shopping online. [Roku Ads Manager](https://advertising.roku.com/ads-manager-holidays?utm_medium=paid_newsletter&utm_source=beehiiv&utm_campaign=pem-us-ads-manager-beehiiv-cpc-q42025&utm_content=BFCM_LP_cpc&utm_term=VPSLCCALRN&_bhiiv=opp_e3911536-e69a-4721-9f39-243c74240b8e_a420a27c&bhcl_id=a3b23be4-0268-4444-9c5d-6d7e1e89191e_bd74a657-278f-4ddb-833c-61e078d26306_70dc14dc-168a-41ff-81d7-6d2f0075867e) simplifies campaign setup, lets you segment audiences, and provides real-time reporting. And, you can test creative variants and run shoppable ads to drive purchases directly on-screen.

Bonus: we’re gifting you $5K in ad credits when you spend your first $5K on Roku Ads Manager. Just sign up and use code GET5K. Terms apply.

[Use code GET5K now](https://advertising.roku.com/ads-manager-holidays?utm_medium=paid_newsletter&utm_source=beehiiv&utm_campaign=pem-us-ads-manager-beehiiv-cpc-q42025&utm_content=BFCM_LP_cpc&utm_term=VPSLCCALRN&_bhiiv=opp_e3911536-e69a-4721-9f39-243c74240b8e_a420a27c&bhcl_id=a3b23be4-0268-4444-9c5d-6d7e1e89191e_bd74a657-278f-4ddb-833c-61e078d26306_70dc14dc-168a-41ff-81d7-6d2f0075867e)

# Perplexity's AI Problem: Is "Helpful" Becoming Theft? 

Here's what's happening: The New York Times just sued Perplexity AI for massive copyright infringement. But here's the kicker—they're just the latest in a growing dogpile that includes The Wall Street Journal, Reddit, Amazon, Japanese newspapers, and even the Encyclopedia Britannica.

What is going on?

## The Business Model Nobody Asked For

Perplexity's pitch sounds innocent enough: it's an AI-powered answer engine that searches the web and gives you direct answers instead of links. Skip the clicks, get straight to the facts. Sounds helpful, right?

The thing is, those "facts" come from somewhere. And according to publishers, that somewhere is their paywalled content—copied without permission, stripped of context, and served up as if Perplexity did the work.

The Times alleges Perplexity scraped millions of articles, including premium subscriber-only content, and reproduced them in AI-generated summaries. Sometimes verbatim. Sometimes with fabricated details ("hallucinations") that still get attributed to The Times, damaging their reputation while Perplexity profits.

## Why This Matters

Listen, this isn't just about one lawsuit. Since August, Perplexity has been sued by:

* **Japanese publishers** seeking $30 million in damages

* **The Wall Street Journal and New York Post** for "massive illegal copying"

* **Chicago Tribune** over real-time paywall circumvention

* **Reddit** for scraping billions of user posts despite cease-and-desist letters

* **Amazon** for its shopping bot masquerading as human users

That's a $20 billion startup facing existential legal threats from multiple continents.

## The Core Tension

Perplexity's defense? They compare themselves to past innovations—radio, TV, the internet—that publishers initially resisted. "Publishers have been suing new tech companies for a hundred years," their spokesperson said. "Fortunately it's never worked, or we'd all be talking about this by telegraph."

But there's a difference between indexing content (what Google does) and replacing it entirely. The Times struck a licensing deal with Amazon for its AI. OpenAI has agreements with AP and Vox Media. A legal market exists—Perplexity just chose not to participate.

Instead, they launched a revenue-sharing program _after_ the backlash, offering publishers scraps while building their entire business on content they never paid for.

## What Happens Next

If publishers win, AI companies will need licenses to summarize paywalled content. If Perplexity wins, the subscription model for journalism collapses—why pay The Times when an AI gives you their reporting for free?

The Chicago Tribune case is especially interesting because it targets real-time content retrieval, not just training data. If courts rule that even _accessing_ articles to generate answers violates copyright, every AI answer engine—from ChatGPT to Google's Gemini—will need to rethink how they operate.

## The Deeper Question

You know what's fascinating? Perplexity isn't wrong that their product is useful. Users love getting answers without clicking through. But "useful" and "legal" aren't the same thing.

We're watching courts decide whether AI companies can build billion-dollar businesses on other people's work, or whether innovation requires actually negotiating with the people whose labor you're monetizing.

The answer will define the next decade of AI development.


———

You are reading a plain text version of this post. For the best experience, copy and paste this link in your browser to view the post online:
https://www.aiweekly.com/p/perplexity-is-getting-sued-again

subject: B2B SaaS – Product ready - Zalando SE paid pilot secured – Seed Round kEUR 600; Calibrion.ai - AI Infrastructure / Data annotation
body_text:
Dear Pawel,

We are building Calibrion, an agent-native data annotation platform adressing one of the largest barriers to organisational GenAI adoption: enabling proprietary LLM training through high-quality, rapid annotation. 

Calibrion delivers: 
- Agentic data annotation that accelerates annotation up to 100x (from months to hours)
- Cost savings of around 90%
- Built-in proprietary quality control to ensure accuracy at scale 
- 35% faster human-in-the-loop through research driven optimized workflows

We recently signed a 6 months paid pilot with Zalando SE, secured a verbal commitment from Siemens Energy AG E-Commerce division, and are negotiating pilots with Cotopaxi (US sustainable outdoor brand) and LILT (AI-driven translation leader). 

We are seeking EUR 600,000 in seed funding to expand our team, onboard additional clients, and implement product-led-growth.

Our founder and CEO, Meghdad, Holds a PhD in Computer Science with a specialization in Natural Language Processing, and has over eight years of applied AI product experience. 

Given your investment focus, we think Calibrion could be a strong fit. You’ll find our one-pager, pitch-deck, and product demos attached. We’d be glad to arrange a 30-minute call to walk you through the product and discuss and our upcoming milestones.

Best regards,
Nils 


Nils Erbach

Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
Tel: +49 1 <tel:+49+(0)30+478+662+25>7662205065 ・ nils@calibrion.ai <mailto:meghdad@calibrion.ai>
Kolonnenstr. 8・10827 Berlin・Germany
www.calibrion.ai <https://www.calibrion.ai/>
￼
￼
￼

subject: Commerce Cockpit - 1st Draft

Will Calculators Outlast ChatGPT: Three Wild AI Stories You Need to
 Know
body_text:
### Find customers on Roku this holiday season

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/9fbb57f7-7cd9-415d-8b68-c1569fa4d99b/1200x600_Holiday_General.png?t=1764725436)
Follow image link: (https://advertising.roku.com/ads-manager-holidays?utm_medium=paid_newsletter&utm_source=beehiiv&utm_campaign=pem-us-ads-manager-beehiiv-cpm-q42025&utm_content=BFCM_LP_cpm&utm_term=VPSLCCALRN&_bhiiv=opp_2256d33e-a7b5-47f9-b0d9-6adf11bf4865_a420a27c&bhcl_id=66b4979e-05ff-4dc8-a5df-7209082aa884_bd74a657-278f-4ddb-833c-61e078d26306_70dc14dc-168a-41ff-81d7-6d2f0075867e)
Caption: 

Now through the end of the year is prime streaming time on Roku, with viewers spending 3.5 hours each day streaming content and shopping online. [Roku Ads Manager](https://advertising.roku.com/ads-manager-holidays?utm_medium=paid_newsletter&utm_source=beehiiv&utm_campaign=pem-us-ads-manager-beehiiv-cpm-q42025&utm_content=BFCM_LP_cpm&utm_term=VPSLCCALRN&_bhiiv=opp_2256d33e-a7b5-47f9-b0d9-6adf11bf4865_a420a27c&bhcl_id=66b4979e-05ff-4dc8-a5df-7209082aa884_bd74a657-278f-4ddb-833c-61e078d26306_70dc14dc-168a-41ff-81d7-6d2f0075867e) simplifies campaign setup, lets you segment audiences, and provides real-time reporting. And, you can test creative variants and run shoppable ads to drive purchases directly on-screen.

Bonus: we’re gifting you $5K in ad credits when you spend your first $5K on Roku Ads Manager. Just sign up and use code GET5K. Terms apply.

[Use code GET5K now](https://advertising.roku.com/ads-manager-holidays?utm_medium=paid_newsletter&utm_source=beehiiv&utm_campaign=pem-us-ads-manager-beehiiv-cpm-q42025&utm_content=BFCM_LP_cpm&utm_term=VPSLCCALRN&_bhiiv=opp_2256d33e-a7b5-47f9-b0d9-6adf11bf4865_a420a27c&bhcl_id=66b4979e-05ff-4dc8-a5df-7209082aa884_bd74a657-278f-4ddb-833c-61e078d26306_70dc14dc-168a-41ff-81d7-6d2f0075867e)

# 

Here's a fun paradox for you: In December 2025, while AI models are crushing gold-medal math problems at the International Mathematical Olympiad, Casio is still selling 39 million calculators a year. Why? Because as one Casio exec put it perfectly: "Calculators always give the correct answer."

And honestly, that's the most 2025 sentence I've ever heard.

Let me walk you through three absolutely fascinating AI stories that just collided this month, each revealing something crucial about where we're actually heading with this technology—spoiler alert: it's weirder and more nuanced than Silicon Valley wants you to believe.

## The Governor vs. The Tech Bros

First up: Ron DeSantis just threw down the most comprehensive AI regulation package in America, and it's causing chaos. Florida's proposed "AI Bill of Rights" directly contradicts Trump's hands-off approach, creating a rare public split on tech policy within conservative politics.

The emotional center of this proposal? Megan Garcia, whose 14-year-old son Sewell died by suicide after months of increasingly intense conversations with a [Character.AI](https://Character.AI) chatbot. The final message from the bot: "come home to me as soon as possible." Minutes later, he was gone.

DeSantis isn't playing around. His package includes parental access to kids' AI conversations, mandatory disclosure when you're talking to AI instead of humans, and—here's where it gets spicy—prohibitions on using AI as the sole basis for denying insurance claims. He's also blocking utilities from charging residents more to power Big Tech's hyperscale data centers, which is brilliant politics given that nobody wants their electric bill subsidizing ChatGPT's training runs.

The federalism fight brewing here is fascinating: Trump wants a ten-year moratorium preventing states from regulating AI independently, while DeSantis is basically saying "over my dead body" and asserting Florida's right to protect its citizens. This isn't just about AI—it's about whether states can govern emerging technologies at all.

## The Calculator That Could

Now for my favorite story: the revenge of the humble calculator. While ChatGPT can write poetry and code, it still sometimes screws up basic multiplication. Ask it to multiply 4,596 by 4,859, and you might get the wrong answer. Your $10 solar-powered Casio? Correct every single time.

This isn't about AI being bad—it's about different tools serving different purposes. AI systems are phenomenal at creative reasoning and complex problem-solving, but they hallucinate. They make confident mistakes. In developing countries without reliable smartphones or internet, that $5 calculator with a 10-year battery life is simply superior for what it does.

The parallel to the abacus is striking: it took 10-15 years for calculators to displace it in developed economies after 1957, and it lingered for another generation in Asia. Calculators might follow the same pattern—gradually fading from developed markets while remaining essential elsewhere. Perfect accuracy in a specific domain can outlast sophisticated but fallible generalization.

## Big Brother Gets a Body Cam Upgrade

Finally, Edmonton just became the first city globally to deploy facial recognition on police body cameras, and the privacy community is losing its mind—rightfully so.

Here's the tension: In 2019, Axon explicitly refused to add facial recognition because the technology was too biased, particularly against darker-skinned individuals. Six years later, they're testing it anyway, claiming the tech has improved. Except studies still show error rates up to 100 times higher for people of color versus white faces.

Edmonton's police argue they've added "guardrails"—four-meter detection radius, good lighting only, human verification required. Alberta's Privacy Commissioner is essentially saying "that's not how privacy law works," but the pilot is proceeding anyway.

The core question law professor Gideon Christian raised cuts deep: "Body-worn cameras were originally a tool for police transparency and accountability. This tool is basically now being thrown to mass surveillance."

## The Path Forward

So where does this leave us? These three stories sketch a future that's less "AI takeover" and more "messy negotiation between capabilities and values."

We're likely heading toward a patchwork regulatory landscape where states like Florida set their own rules, creating compliance headaches for tech companies but also laboratories for what actually works. Perfect-accuracy tools will coexist with sophisticated-but-fallible AI systems, each serving different needs. And surveillance technology will advance faster than privacy protections, forcing uncomfortable democratic debates about where we draw lines.

The optimistic read? We're finally having grown-up conversations about AI governance. The concerns aren't hypothetical anymore—they're about real kids, real privacy violations, and real mathematical errors. That's uncomfortable, but it's also how democratic societies figure things out.

The calculator teaches us something important: just because AI can do something impressive doesn't mean it replaces everything before it. Sometimes reliable beats sophisticated. Sometimes simple wins.

And sometimes, the future looks a lot like 39 million Casio calculators, still giving the right answer.


———

You are reading a plain text version of this post. For the best experience, copy and paste this link in your browser to view the post online:
https://www.aiweekly.com/p/will-calculators-outlast-chatgpt-three-wild-ai-stories-you-need-to-know

subject:
[Product Update] Gmail emoji reactions will be enabled by default
 starting Feb 9, 2026
body_text:
Google Workspace logo



Users will be able to react to Gmail messages on web, Android, and iOS.



Dear administrator,

We're writing to let you know that starting February 9, 2026, Gmail emoji  
reactions will be enabled by default for all existing and new customers.

We've provided additional information below to guide you through this  
update.

What this means for your organization

Key changes:

The Gmail emoji reactions feature will be turned on for all customers by  
default, unless admins in the organization have previously changed the  
feature setting or updated the setting before February 9, 2026.

Potential impact:

Gmail emoji reactions let you react to Gmail messages on the web, Android,  
and iOS. The feature was previously launched to Google Workspace customers  
in an opt-in phase effective April 2025 (with the default admin setting  
disabled), and has been available for free Gmail accounts since 2023.

Note that in some cases users cannot react with emojis, for example:


Email thread contains a Google Group
User is blind carbon copied (BCC'd)
Email is client-side encrypted

For more information on the feature, review the Reply to emails with emoji  
reactions help center article.

What you need to do

Action advised:


If you want users in your organization to use emoji reactions, there's no  
action required from you since the feature will be enabled by default  
starting February 9, 2026
If you want to disable emoji reactions for your organization, you can  
disable the feature for either the whole organization or specific users or  
user groups by completing the following steps:

Go to the Google Admin console and find this setting: Gmail > End User  
Access > Emoji reactions
Make sure the setting is unchecked and then click Save


For more information, review the Manage Gmail settings for your users help  
center article.

Thanks for choosing Google Workspace.

– The Google Workspace Team




Was this information helpful?

A smiling face A neutral face A sad face


© 2025 Google LLC 1600 Amphitheatre Parkway, Mountain View, CA 94043

You have received this important update about your Google Workspace account  
because you designated this email address as a primary or secondary contact  
for mandatory service communications in your Google Admin console profile.

subject:
Zalando DPA - Bold sections to be technically reviewed / Main
 Contract GDPR compliant / ANNEX 1 filled / ANNEX 2 needs work / ANNEX 3/4 not
 applicable
body_text:
Subject: Zalando DPA - Bold sections to be technically reviewed / Main Contract GDPR compliant / ANNEX 1 filled / ANNEX 2 needs work / ANNEX 3/4 not applicable


Notes on the DPA Agreement [ Bold sections need to be reviewed ] :


i.) Main contract: 
- Put software / intellectual property into the Legal Entity [ it needs to own the software, which you own personally right now ] 
- Who is the Controller of the Data? - Joint? Only Zalando? - I think, default Zalando is the Controller, which can only change by Court order 



ii.) Annex 1 - Description of Transfer 

pages 6 / 7:
- Does Calibrion use personal data [ of Subjects? ] - in case, which type? / I assume no personal data will be used! 
- How long is any data stored in the Cloud for Calibrion processing purposes? / I assume it will be deleted, as soon, as the data is processed and annotated 

page 7 / 8:
- SCC [ Standard Contractual Clause ] / I assume the forementioned contract 
- Calibrion uses Cloud inside the European Economic Area 
- Assumption: Data is encrypted and will be deleted immediately after being shipped back to the customer in an annotated form


iii.) Annex 2 - Technical and Organizational Measures / Technical stuff to be answered by Meghdad 

- Calibrion needs to make sure, to process data according to GDPR 

iii.A) Pseudonymization measures

- Assume, Calibrion encrypts all data, such that pseudonymization does not have to take place

iii.B) Encryption measures


iii.C) Measures to ensure confidentiality [ Does it apply, data itself is encrypted and can not be accessed by Calibrion or third party ] 

1. Physical access control measures on own premises to answer 
2. Logical access control 
3. Data access control [ page 13 ] - Access design for individual users 
4. Separation rule [ page 13 ] - I assume software-based customer separation 
5. Further measures [ page 13 ] - Calibrion employees are not able and not allowed to download and process Zalando data; Also, data would be useless, since encrypted


iii.D) Measures to ensure integrity

1. Data Integrity [ page 13 / 14 ] - Making sure stored data in the system cannot be corrupted by malfunctioning
2. Transmission control [ page 14 ] - Measures taken to ensure verification and establishment transmission control 
3. Transport control [ page 14 ] - Measures taken to ensure proteciton of confidentiality and integrity of data during transmission 
4. Input control [ page 14 / 15 ] - Measures taken to enable verification on whether and by whom personal data has been entered and modified in data processing systems 

iii.E) Measures to ensure availability and resilience

1. Availability Control [ page 15 ] - Measures taken to ensure personal data protection against accidental detruction and loss
2. Quick recovery [ page 15 ] - Measures taken to ensure the ability to quickly restore the avauilability of / access to personal data and used systems in the event of a physical or technical incident  
3. Reliability [ page 15 ] - Measures taken to ensure functioning of the system and reporting of malfunction 

iii.F) Measure for the regulär Testing and Evaluation of the security data processing 

1. Verification process [ page 16 ] - Make or buy Internat assessment process development [ annually ] ; If third parties involved, hold them accountable 
2. Order control [ page 16 ] - Making sure, that Zalando / Controller data is only processed in accordaüce with Controller instructions [ Reference to ANNEX 1 ]

iii.G) Measures for limiting data retention [ NEEDS TO BE FILLED IN ]



iv.) Annex 3 - Permitted Sub-Processors [ not applicable, since Calibrion does not make use of Sub-Contractors processing data ] - NOT APPLICABLE / Relevant data is filled in

- Question open, if Iranian Team-Members are considered Sub-Processors? 
- To Date, Calibrion is the last member in the chain of data processors, to not applicable / Calibrion does not use Sub-Contractors who process any data from the Controller 

v.) Annex 4 - Accession to the DPA [ Not applicable, since no acceding company ]  - NOT APPLICABLE / Relevant data is filled in

￼

subject: Test - New Approach to Investors
body_text:
Dear Team at Target Partners,

We are building Calibrion, an agent-native data annotation platform adressing one of the largest barriers to organisational GenAI adoption: enabling proprietary LLM training through high-quality, rapid annotation. 

Calibrion delivers: 
- Agentic data annotation that accelerates annotation up to 100x (from months to hours)
- Cost savings of around 90%
- Built-in proprietary quality control to ensure accuracy at scale 
- 35% faster human-in-the-loop through research driven optimized workflows

Our moat compounds across years of world-class AI, LLM, and agentic expertise; patentable processes; the inherent complexity of the problem we solve; a fast, high-performing team; and early distribution into some of the world’s leading enterprises.

We recently signed a 6 months paid pilot with Zalando SE, secured a verbal commitment from Siemens Energy AG E-Commerce division, and are negotiating pilots with Cotopaxi (US sustainable outdoor brand) and LILT (AI-driven translation leader). 

We are seeking EUR 1,000,000 in seed funding to expand our team, onboard additional clients, and implement product-led-growth.

Our founder and CEO, Meghdad, Holds a PhD in Computer Science with a specialization in Natural Language Processing, and has over eight years of applied AI product experience. 

Given your investment focus, we think Calibrion could be a strong fit. You’ll find our one-pager, pitch-deck, and product demos attached. We’d be glad to arrange a 30-minute call to walk you through the product and discuss and our upcoming milestones.

Best regards,
Nils 


Nils Erbach

Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
Tel: +49 1 <tel:+49+(0)30+478+662+25>7662205065 ・ nils@calibrion.ai <mailto:meghdad@calibrion.ai>
Kolonnenstr. 8・10827 Berlin・Germany
www.calibrion.ai <https://www.calibrion.ai/>
￼
￼
￼

subject: Re: Calibrion Pitch - Follow-up and Materials
body_text:
Hi Meghdad,

Thanks again for the documents and the interesting conversations!

We have discussed with the broader team and decided to pass the current round of investment. It was clear from the beginning that it would be an early investment for us and this manifested over the process. At the core, we find the thesis of reducing the friction for companies to deploy there models interesting. However, we’d have more comfort having first proof points on a customer’s value / ROI perspective.

That being said, I’d love to stay in touch and I wish you great holidays!

Best,
Matteo


Matteo Stegner



Senior Associate



Email:      mstegner@cuspcapital.com<mailto:mstegner@cuspcapital.com>

Mobile:   +49 176 11808248

We've heard from many of you that even small customizations can
make a big difference in your meetings. For example, Calendly
users who personalize their Event Types and meeting
communications have seen a 20% increase in scheduled meetings!

Customizations also add a professional touch and show you care
about providing the best meeting experience possible, helping you
build stronger customer relationships.

Read on to learn about our most popular Calendly customizations.

subject: Re: B2B SaaS – Lead Secured – Seed; Calibrion.ai - AI Infrastructure / Data annotation
body_text:
We are building Calibrion, an agent-native data annotation platform solving
a critical bottleneck in organisational GenAI adoption: enabling
proprietary LLM training through high-quality, rapid annotation. Calibrion
delivers: - Agentic annotation to increase speed ~170x (from months to
hours) - Cost reduction of ~90% - Proprietary innovative built-in quality
control to maintain quality at this scale - 35% faster human intervention
through optimized human-in-the-loop workflows We recently signed a 6-month
paid pilot with a Zalando, verbally closed a pilot with Siemens Energy
E-com department, and are in negotiating with Cotopaxi (a leading
sustainable outdoor brand) and LILT (a San Francisco company pioneering
AI-driven translation) for two other pilots. We are seeking €600,000 in
seed funding to expand our team, onboard additional clients, and implement
product-led growth.
Calibrion's founder and CEO, Meghdad, holds a PhD in Computer Science
specializing in Natural Language Processing, enriched with 8+ years of
applied experience in shipping AI products.

We believe Calibrion fits closely with your investment focus. Attached you
can find our one-pager, our pitch deck, and some of our product demos.


On Fri, Oct 10, 2025 at 10:05 PM Nils Erbach <nils@calibrion.ai> wrote:

> Dear Team at [ … ], We are building Calibrion, an agent-native data
> annotation platform solving a critical bottleneck in organisational GenAI
> adoption: enabling proprietary LLM training through high-quality, rapid
> annotation. Calibrion delivers: - Annotation speed increased ~170x (from
> months to hours) - Cost reduction of ~90% - Built-in quality control
> improving accuracy - 35% less human intervention, reducing resource needs We
> recently signed a 6-month paid pilot with a large listed e-commerce
> retailer in Germany. We are seeking €600,000 in seed funding to expand our
> team, onboard additional clients, and accelerate product-led growth.
> Given [ … ]’s track record in early-stage deep tech and AI ventures, we
> believe Calibrion fits closely with your investment focus. Attached are our
> pitch deck and one-pager. We would value the opportunity to schedule a
> 20–30 min call with a product walk-through at your convenience. Best
> regards, Nils
>
>

subject: B2B SaaS – Lead Secured – Seed; Calibrion.ai - AI Infrastructure / Data annotation
body_text:
Dear Team at [ … ],

We are building Calibrion, an agent-native data annotation platform solving a critical bottleneck in organisational GenAI adoption: enabling proprietary LLM training through high-quality, rapid annotation.

Calibrion delivers:
- Annotation speed increased ~170x (from months to hours)
- Cost reduction of ~90%
- Built-in quality control improving accuracy
- 35% less human intervention, reducing resource needs

We recently signed a 6-month paid pilot with a large listed e-commerce retailer in Germany. We are seeking €600,000 in seed funding to expand our team, onboard additional clients, and accelerate product-led growth.

Given [ … ]’s track record in early-stage deep tech and AI ventures, we believe Calibrion fits closely with your investment focus. Attached are our pitch deck and one-pager. We would value the opportunity to schedule a 20–30 min call with a product walk-through at your convenience.

Best regards,  
Nils

subject: New features and webinar with Clay & ColdIQ - October 2025
body_text:
Margaret Sikora [https://woodpecker.co/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/margaret_sikora_photo.png] 
Margaret Sikora
CEO @ Woodpecker.co
LinkedIn [https://www.linkedin.com/in/margaretsikora/] 
Woodpecker Logo [https://woodpecker.co/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/wp_logo.png] [https://woodpecker.co?utm_source=newsletter&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=monthly_update_1] 



In this newsletter:
 * Join me for a webinar on multichannel outreach we’re hosting with Clay and ColdIQ
   
 * Check the new Woodpecker features that help increase deliverability and conversion rates
   
 * Get two cold email tips at the end
   
   



Clay + ColdIQ + Woodpecker webinar: Find out what effective LinkedIn + email sequencing looks like


Join Owen from Clay, Harry Mason from ColdIQ and me for a webinar where we’ll show you:
 * How to identify who you should be connecting on LinkedIn (and who to skip)
   
 * Smart lead routing strategies based on account scores
   
 * How to build familiarity before the ask + why LinkedIn-first outreach works
   
 * Live Clay table demos showing multichannel workflows in action
   

Perfect for: lead gen agencies, growth-focus teams and anyone tired of spray-and-pray outreach.


🔷 6th November at 12pm ET (6pm CET)
🔷 30 minutes
🔷 Save your spot now [https://app.getresponse.com/click.html?x=a62b&co=Itcat2&lc=hb63iM&mc=r2&s=BosBXMv&u=CI4vv&z=EIVkJcP&]




Woodpecker’s new features:


1. RevReply + Woodpecker integration [https://app.getresponse.com/click.html?x=a62b&co=Itcat2&lc=hb63Xs&mc=r2&s=BosBXMv&u=CI4vv&z=EJdZQ9d&]: Increase conversions by quick response management powered by AI


🔷 RevReply acts as your inbox manager that helps you with speedy replies, so you can close deals when your prospect is still in a buying mindset



🔷 Benefits:
- Faster response times → higher conversion rates
- Automated handling of responses frees up your team’s time
- Helps you scale - close more deals quicker
- The prospect’s experience with your company is great from the start



🔷 You can use triggers phrases for auto-responses, get notified on Slack when a new reply comes in, and get suggested auto-generated responses, and more.



What you need:
 * The Integrations add-on in Woodpecker ($20/mo)
   
 * Any RevReply plan
   



[https://us-ms.gr-cdn.com/getresponse-CI4vv/photos/f1170a9a-6915-4ddd-8a0a-4f4029aa7d41.png]


2. LinkedIn API endpoints [https://app.getresponse.com/click.html?x=a62b&co=Itcat2&lc=hb63Ki&mc=r2&s=BosBXMv&u=CI4vv&z=E9Y7rJ&]: Manage your LinkedIn campaigns without logging into Woodpecker


🔷 You can build LinkedIn campaigns from scratch, run and modify them while they’re running.



🔷 Examples of how you can use it:
 * Create and run LinkedIn or email + LinkedIn campaigns with personalized messages and customizable delivery settings (you can use different LinkedIn accounts for different steps in one campaign)
   
 * Pause, stop or edit your LinkedIn campaigns
   
 * Add and delete email and LinkedIn steps
   
 * Update the content of your LinkedIn messages
   
 * Get a list of campaigns
   
 * Update campaign-wide settings
   



[https://us-ms.gr-cdn.com/getresponse-CI4vv/photos/fdbffa2a-005f-471c-bd67-3e7f759f279d.png]



3. Email copy warm-up [https://app.getresponse.com/click.html?x=a62b&co=Itcat2&lc=hb6326&mc=r2&s=BosBXMv&u=CI4vv&z=EIUyI8F&]: Warm up your email copy to build trust with email providers and boost deliverability


🔷 Benefits:
 * Warming up your email copy teaches email provider algorithms to associate it with positive engagement
   
 * As a result, you get more emails delivered to primary inboxes instead of spam folders
   
 * You don’t need to do any manual work to keep copy in sync (but still have full control over it on a per-campaign basis)
   

🔷 Email copy warm-up can be active only when at least one of your mailboxes is being actively warmed up in Woodpecker.


🔷 It’s included in the email warm-up at no extra cost




[https://us-ms.gr-cdn.com/getresponse-CI4vv/photos/dfedb8c1-dc0e-412f-a44b-4f29c6026fa7.png]


Cold email tip #1 [https://app.getresponse.com/click.html?x=a62b&co=Itcat2&lc=hb63ej&mc=r2&s=BosBXMv&u=CI4vv&z=EBSYtCM&]: How much do you need to pay to contact 10k prospects?


The same cold email set-up that cost you $5,500 in 2021, now costs around $400.


In 2021, to contact 10,000 prospects you needed to pay $5,500 in total:
- $500 for a sequencer with 10 mailboxes connected
- $5,000 for 10,000 prospect emails



Today, to contact 10,000 prospects with Woodpecker, you pay just over $400:
- $188 to send to 10,000 prospects - you can connect unlimited mailboxes
- $219 to find 10,000 prospect emails (already verified)



In both cases you would also need to get mailboxes - in Woodpecker you can get a mailbox for as low as $4 per month.


Not to mention that the cost of running a cold email campaign is quite stable. And a more stable acquisition cost = better predictability of your revenue.

Cold email tip #2 [https://app.getresponse.com/click.html?x=a62b&co=Itcat2&lc=hb63LV&mc=r2&s=BosBXMv&u=CI4vv&z=EhSQths&]: Emails between 74-100 words get the highest response rates


Why shorter works:
 * Mobile-first world - Most emails are read on phones
   
 * Attention spans - You have 3-5 seconds to hook them
   
 * Cognitive load - Busy executives scan, they don't read
   
 * Easier decisions - Less content = faster yes/no
   

The editing process:
 1. Write your first draft
    
 2. Cut everything that isn't essential
    
 3. Count words
    
 4. Cut 25% more
    
 5. Read it out loud
    
 6. Cut again
    

Example:


[https://us-ms.gr-cdn.com/getresponse-CI4vv/photos/93d5a5cf-f8e2-4db8-90d9-940f4477c2ae.png]


Thanks for reading and see you next time!



Profile photo [https://ci3.googleusercontent.com/meips/ADKq_NZEbjt1hLHRpJUm-T03r9RSh0pwx0-vUDdosLWVAC69H2jIjrgMytC4Kslr2Uk4rI0Ukx8mixCWrEHpRLnwbN4SiDXZcZ90QdmtPtQboLN18kRmrqArVzSKbnOYqhiJ=s0-d-e1-ft#https://i.woodpeckerhq.com/sign/c316b4b4-504a-42e6-b119-b8facaa7b4cc.jpeg]
Best Regards,
Margaret Sikora
CEO @Woodpecker.co



LinkedIn [https://i.woodpeckerhq.com/sign/linkedin.png] [https://www.linkedin.com/in/margaretsikora/].


You may unsubscribe [https://app.getresponse.com/unsubscribe.html?x=a62b&co=Itcat2&m=BjsKLD&mc=r2&s=BosBXMv&u=CI4vv&z=EChM9SV&] or change your contact details [https://app.getresponse.com/change_details.html?x=a62b&co=Itcat2&m=BjsKLD&s=BosBXMv&u=CI4vv&z=EhSVMZN&] at any time.

subject: 🚨 When AI Gets Uncomfortable: Process Servers, Free Giveaways, and the Surveillance State Goes Public 🎭⚖️
body_text:
View this post on the web at https://aibuzz.news/p/when-ai-gets-uncomfortable-process

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
Would you like to be featured in our newsletter🔥 and get noticed, QUICKLY 🚀 (56,000+ subscribers)? Simply reply to this email or send an email to editor@digitalhealthbuzz.com, and we can take it from there.
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
This week, the polished veneer of AI’s public narrative cracked wide open in three spectacular ways. While AI companies usually control their messaging with surgical precision, we witnessed something different: Sam Altman getting ambushed on stage with legal papers while a crowd booed, Silicon Valley’s biggest players flooding India with free AI tools in a barely-disguised land grab, and Palantir’s CEO openly admitting his company’s surveillance ambitions on national television. These aren’t carefully orchestrated product launches or curated keynote moments—they’re the messy, uncomfortable realities of an industry expanding faster than it can manage its own contradictions. The glossy future of AI is colliding with courtrooms, geopolitics, and ethical questions that won’t stay buried in press releases. Let’s examine what happens when the AI industry’s most awkward moments become impossible to ignore.
The Most Awkward Moment in AI: Sam Altman Gets Served Papers While the Crowd Boos [ https://substack.com/redirect/c2f1d154-14f8-46cc-811a-8fee2830bc1c?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
You can plan for technical difficulties or tough questions when giving a public talk. What you can’t plan for is having a process server storm the stage and hand you legal documents while a thousand people watch. That’s exactly what happened to OpenAI CEO Sam Altman this week at San Francisco’s Sydney Goldstein Theater. Mid-conversation with Golden State Warriors coach Steve Kerr, a man climbed on stage announcing, “I have a subpoena for Sam Altman.” The crowd erupted in boos. Altman didn’t take the document. Security escorted the intruder away while the moderator tried awkwardly to continue. The first assumption was publicity stunt—except this wasn’t performance art, it was actual legal procedure playing out in the most public way possible. This moment captures something crucial about where AI has arrived: the industry’s biggest players are no longer operating in the protective bubble of tech conferences and friendly media. The courtroom is catching up to the keynote stage, and the collision is spectacularly uncomfortable.
Thanks for reading! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work.
The Great Indian AI Giveaway: Why Silicon Valley is Betting Big on Bangalore (and Beyond) [ https://substack.com/redirect/1a419469-8b33-446b-a07c-ceea8c0713f9?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
Something extraordinary is happening in India’s digital marketplace right now: while the rest of the world debates paywalls and premium subscriptions, tech titans are giving away their most sophisticated AI products to hundreds of millions of Indians for free. This isn’t charity—it’s calculated strategy. The dominoes fell in quick succession: Perplexity AI partnered with Airtel to bundle free access with phone plans, Google teamed up with Reliance Jio to sweeten data packages with complimentary AI tools, and now OpenAI is offering millions of Indians a full year of free ChatGPT Go access. As analyst Tarun Pathak explains, the plan is simple: get Indians hooked on AI services now, then monetize later once habits are formed and switching costs are high. With India’s massive population of English speakers and rapidly expanding digital infrastructure, whoever dominates this market early could control the next generation of AI users globally. This is the land grab phase happening in real time, dressed up as generosity, and the stakes couldn’t be higher for reshaping the global AI landscape.
“Pick Your Dystopia”: Palantir’s CEO Just Said the Quiet Part Out Loud About AI and Surveillance [ https://substack.com/redirect/1c63f74b-6f0f-45b0-bdd4-44f80785955a?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
Most CEOs carefully manage their public image with practiced corporate speak. Palantir’s Alex Karp is not most CEOs. During recent appearances on Axios and CNBC, Karp didn’t just defend his controversial data analytics company—he laid out a vision of the future that’s equal parts Silicon Valley manifesto and geopolitical ultimatum. When asked what Palantir actually does, his answer was both grandiose and vague: “We are growing the GDP of the US. We are the part of the AI economy where things are useful.” That GDP obsession has become his favorite talking point, suggesting that investors betting against Palantir are essentially betting against American economic growth itself. What makes Karp’s comments remarkable isn’t just the boldness—it’s the transparency. While other tech executives dance around surveillance concerns with careful language about privacy and safeguards, Karp openly frames his company’s work as essential infrastructure for maintaining American power. He’s saying the quiet part out loud: surveillance capitalism isn’t a bug, it’s the feature, and you need to pick which version of that future you want to live in.
Thanks for reading! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work.

Unsubscribe https://substack..cOTKDxKOvi694FrUu9LcH_xCsl93WAa7kbhuiSp9Qwo?

subject: 🌞 Red Sift Quarterly Vol. 5
body_text:
Red Sift Quarterly vol 5 ( https://redsift.com/?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol5&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing )

-----------------------------------------------
Sizzling into Q3 with hot-off-the-press updates
-----------------------------------------------

Summer’s here, and we’ve been busy expanding our product suite, earning accolades, and sharing fresh insights to keep your security posture at its peak. From a fresh look to launches and summer events.

Red Sift OnDMARC: #1 in Europe in G2's summer report
----------------------------------------------------

Our customers are pretty awesome, placing Red Sift OnDMARC at the top of the charts again ( https://blog.redsift.com/news/europes-1-for-dmarc-red-sift-ondmarc-does-it-again/?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol5&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ), this time as the #1 Regional Leader for Europe.

Stream Red Sift telemetry to Sentinel, Splunk, and more with Event Hub
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

In July we released Event Hub, a new capability that streams real-time, structured security events from Red Sift products into the platforms security teams already use. See how you can stream your real-time events ( https://blog.redsift.com/product-release/stream-red-sift-telemetry-to-sentinel-splunk-and-more-with-event-hub/?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol5&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ).

latest thought leadership
-------------------------

More than 50% of US banks remain vulnerable to phishing attacks
---------------------------------------------------------------

50% of the top 500+ US commercial banks don't enforce a strict DMARC policy (p=reject), in fact 40% only sit at p=none or no DMARC policy at all. See the results in full ( https://blog.redsift.com/research/more-than-50-of-us-banks-remain-vulnerable-to-phishing-attacks/?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol5&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ).

Forbes: Getting Outlook.com ready for bulk email compliance
-----------------------------------------------------------

Technical lead Faisal Misle walks through the must-meet requirements. From configuring DMARC, SPF and DKIM to enabling one click unsubscribes, keeping complaint rates below 0.3% and using TLS 1.2 or higher. Read the full article ( https://www.darkreading.com/cloud-security/getting-outlook-com-ready-bulk-email-compliance?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol5&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ).

Healthcare and cybersecurity: 73% of breaches lack DMARC enforcement
---------------------------------------------------------------------

Our new research indicates that 73% of recently breached healthcare organizations lack a DMARC policy of p=reject (enforcement), making them susceptible to BEC, email spoofing, and domain impersonation. Dive in ( https://blog.redsift.com/healthcare/healthcare-and-cybersecurity-a-landscape-of-escalating-risk/?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol5&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ).

Connect with Red Sift
---------------------

Catch the Red Sift team in Q3
-----------------------------

Attackers don’t stop at DMARC, and neither should you ( https://app.livestorm.co/redsift/bulk-up-or-bust-meeting-microsofts-new-requirements-for-high-volume-senders?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol5&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ) - On demand | Virtual
Black Hat USA ( https://blackhat.com/us-25?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol5&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ) - August 2-7 | Las Vegas, Nevada - Reserve time ( https://drift.me/jonathanmateas/meeting/?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol5&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing )
The next layer of defence: DMARC, DNS, and Brand Trust ( https://www.e92plus.com/events/detail/25-08-13/The%20next%20layer%20of%20defence:%20DMARC,%20DNS,%20and%20brand%20trust?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol5&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing )- August 13 | Virtual
Cisco GSX 2025 - August 26-28 | Las Vegas, Nevada
MSP Summit ( https://themspsummit.com/?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol5&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ) - September 15-17 | Orlando, Florida

Hot off the press
-----------------

* Red Sift joins the Jisc Chest platform to strengthen email security in the education sector ( https://blog.redsift.com/news/red-sift-ondmarc-joins-the-jisc-chest-platform/?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol5&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing )
* Mailgun and Red Sift partner to boost email programs with stronger authentication ( https://blog.redsift.com/email/mailgun-and-red-sift-partner-to-boost-email-programs-with-stronger-authentication/?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol5&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing )
* Red Sift named a Top 50 company in 2025 Emerging Stars Awards ( https://blog.redsift.com/awards/red-sift-named-a-top-50-company-in-2025-emerging-stars-awards/?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol5&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing )
* Red Sift wins 2025 Cybersecurity Excellence Award for OnDMARC ( https://blog.redsift.com/awards/red-sift-wins-2025-cybersecurity-excellence-award-for-ondmarc/?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol5&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing )
* Why DMARC and BIMI are a business priority ( https://blog.redsift.com/bimi/why-dmarc-and-bimi-are-a-business-priority/?utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol5&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing )

We hope this edition of Red Sift Quarterly brings you fresh insights and even fresher reasons to keep your cybersecurity posture strong.

Until next time,
Team Red Sift

 

Red Sift helps 1,200+ organizations build proactive security.

Ready to speak to our team of experts? Contact us ( marketing@redsift.io?subject=Question for an expert&utm_campaign=RedSiftQuarterlyVol5&utm_content=Red+Sift+Quarterly+Vol+5&utm_medium=email_action&utm_source=email_marketing ) 💌

21A Noel Street, Soho, London, W1F 8GR

subject: Antler - Invite to Meet an Antler Partner
body_text:
Hi Meghdad,

We are writing to remind you to join one of our office hours on Thursday at 13:00.

We usually run office hours every week for founders interested in joining Antler.

In our office hours, we give a lot of detailed information about what to expect when joining Antler, what type of founders join Antler, how the cohort runs, what is expected of founders, commercial terms, and a chance to answer any additional questions.

Alan, partner at Antler and founder of WestwingNow, will be hosting weekly office hours every Thursday at 13:00. This is an excellent opportunity to understand more about how we support founders and ask any questions you might have.

- You can register to the session via the following link (https://content.antler.co/e3t/Ctc/2P+113/djW2rd04/VX88k76TR__BW7NzsT65Gb4FKVcSh175tfB3KN4Yn9N43l5QzW69sMD-6lZ3pqW8xZHcw1HhxDvW5jwg903pKbcZV_GRlJ2bp9rMW2p3H2l2C5Fm0W4mk33k6CPWC9W2_8kWb4n_4GkN8h4XtjtX0CVW5rLP3s1rLKgFW3_zRHd5KhnHYW7Pyrs8700YLkW14Gb7l1zwHdyW1Yddy84rfyfqW6_zCvX6nv6KjV3hgg84m0XW3W6j_5XP28v8tPV1BPsx8KHCwZW4kjd3k258vxPW8WpCYj4Q8JdhW5LJPQy8g19BwW30Zckd2RtD-Jf7FBF9P04 ) .
- To add a reminder to your calendar please click here (https://content.antler.co/e3t/Ctc/2P+113/djW2rd04/VX88k76TR__BW7NzsT65Gb4FKVcSh175tfB3KN4Yn9Pz3l5QzW95jsWP6lZ3msW8YXcC23mMBZ4W1STGtT1N3hk4W2LgNy95R-PF-W8f5Zl_2sCXxYW5cDMPF8yqVwmN284bbCyp3LQW5r-fwg8Z5tFhW1MLm_L1Y118kW4G721d35S0K1W8s3BNk51XS_lVpb0HS4JSWqBW810lY-2DN7kSW34bKR26lr01GN3rZChkTTpQLW8sY3cF8gvmPPW8wgPWW1M0z7TW1mJCzW6qMkg-W38cm-54Fggz_V92C0-1jcN0LW69k5Z25ZR54GW7kPCp18HJ3JmW15fnFH3551XhW2ksxM-7ZmyHxW6tSLky4XBX8-N3CCRL0Y_S-lW75qnWj4JcJY6W7chqt98dXjpNW65QJLd3N5ScwVs1Hqb4x1x4nW4MnHfQ3__085d7gvQ604 ) . We hope to see you there!

It is highly recommended to join this session before your interview, it will serve as a way of getting to know Antler better and also prepare you for your interview.

We hope to see you there!

Best Regards,

Matteo Fortunato

>>

>> sharing the example vendor datasheets.
>>
>> Below is a summary of the items we discussed, separated into what we
>> agreed is feasible for the upcoming demo and what will be addressed
>> afterward.
>>
>> *INCLUDED IN THE DEMO*
>> 1. Technical Drawings classification
>> (Final decision is pending on testing how well the OCR works on
>> extracting the table and nameplate from the drawings.
>> - Does the filename match the product name?
>> - Name plate attributes should match the same attributes on the product
>> technical details
>> - Attribute customized device must match drawing number in the table
>> right of the slash sign (0 to _) in the customized device number
>> - Type field in the table, the code part, e.g., 3EL1 must be in the
>> product title or attribute product type
>> 2. Price Anomaly Detection
>> The goal is to create an anomaly detector agent/ML model.
>> The Input is a list of prices, output is the anomalous prices
>> Max would send a GraphQL output to be checked to see if it can be used by
>> this module
>> 3. Checking the price validity field on the product page
>> Also comes from the same GraphQL output
>> 4. Is the picture attached?
>> 5. Are all the relevant data there to request the order
>>
>>    - Can be checked rule-based
>>    - A set of attributes must be there.
>>    - Checking for the presence
>>    - Checking for them not to be vague (e.g. see frame agreement or
>>    similar)
>>
>> *NOT INCLUDED IN THE DEMO*
>> General Price validity
>> Expired Frame Agreement
>> Does the technical data sheet make sense?
>> Does technical data sheet has all the attributes?
>> Does the customer see what he's supposed to see, or not.
>> Do all orders have the requested delivery date
>> Is the correct currency being used
>> Are there different prices between similar products
>>
>> For each of the above items, we require either API access to the
>> corresponding data or an offline/downloaded version provided in a shared
>> storage location to proceed with the POC.

>>

wrote:

> Dear all,
>
> Thank you for the great meeting on Friday, and thank you, Ole, for sharing
> the example vendor datasheets.
>
> Below is a summary of the items we discussed, separated into what we
> agreed is feasible for the upcoming demo and what will be addressed

>
> *INCLUDED IN THE DEMO*
> 1. Technical Drawings classification
> (Final decision is pending on testing how well the OCR works on extracting

> - Does the filename match the product name?
> - Name plate attributes should match the same attributes on the product

subject: Re: Calibrion: Guardrail Agent
body_text:
Dear all,

Thank you for the great meeting on Friday, and thank you, Ole, for sharing
the example vendor datasheets.

Below is a summary of the items we discussed, separated into what we agreed
is feasible for the upcoming demo and what will be addressed afterward.

*INCLUDED IN THE DEMO*
1. Technical Drawings classification
(Final decision is pending on testing how well the OCR works on extracting
the table and nameplate from the drawings.
- Does the filename match the product name?
- Name plate attributes should match the same attributes on the product
technical details
- Attribute customized device must match drawing number in the table right
of the slash sign (0 to _) in the customized device number
- Type field in the table, the code part, e.g., 3EL1 must be in the product
title or attribute product type
2. Price Anomaly Detection
The goal is to create an anomaly detector agent/ML model.
The Input is a list of prices, output is the anomalous prices
Max would send a GraphQL output to be checked to see if it can be used by
this module
3. Checking the price validity field on the product page
Also comes from the same GraphQL output
4. Is the picture attached?
5. Are all the relevant data there to request the order

   - Can be checked rule-based
   - A set of attributes must be there.
   - Checking for the presence
   - Checking for them not to be vague (e.g. see frame agreement or similar)

*NOT INCLUDED IN THE DEMO*
General Price validity
Expired Frame Agreement
Does the technical data sheet make sense?
Does technical data sheet has all the attributes?
Does the customer see what he's supposed to see, or not.
Do all orders have the requested delivery date
Is the correct currency being used
Are there different prices between similar products

For each of the above items, we require either API access to the
corresponding data or an offline/downloaded version provided in a shared
storage location to proceed with the POC.

Let's schedule a follow-up call to discuss details.

Best regards,
Meghdad

subject: Calibrion: Guardrail Agent
body_text:
Chat GPT:


Here’s a compact example like you’d see in a vendor datasheet (values are realistic but generic), for a 145 kV system station-class MO surge arrester:





General



  *   Type: Metal-oxide surge arrester without gaps (MO)
  *   Application: Station class, outdoor
  *   Duty class: SH (High lightning duty), Line discharge class 3
  *   Nominal discharge current In: 10 kA (8/20 µs)
  *   System frequency: 50 / 60 Hz






Ratings



  *   Highest system voltage Um: 145 kV
  *   Rated voltage Ur: 120 kV (r.m.s.)
  *   Continuous operating voltage Uc: 96 kV (r.m.s.)
  *   Power-frequency withstand (1 min, wet): 325 kV (r.m.s.)
  *   Lightning impulse withstand (1.2/50 µs): 650 kV (peak)






Protective Characteristics



(Values at 20 °C, after pre-conditioning, unless stated otherwise)


  *   Reference voltage (1 mA d.c.): 150 kV (d.c.)
  *   Leakage current at Uc: ≤ 1.0 mA (r.m.s.)



Residual / protective voltages


  *   Lightning impulse 8/20 µs
     *   5 kA: 310 kV (peak)
     *   10 kA (In): 330 kV (peak)
     *   20 kA: 360 kV (peak)
     *   Lightning impulse protective level Upl: 330 kV (peak at In)
  *
  *   Steep current impulse (1/20 µs)
     *   10 kA: 360 kV (peak)
  *
  *   Switching impulse (30/60 µs)
     *   0.5 kA: 260 kV (peak)
     *   1.0 kA: 275 kV (peak)
     *   Switching impulse protective level Ups: 275 kV (peak at 1 kA)
  *






Energy / Charge Capability



  *   Thermal energy rating Wth: 10 kJ/kV Ur
  *   Repetitive charge transfer rating Qrs: 3.0 C
  *   Long-duration current impulse: 600 A, 2000 µs
  *   High-current impulse: 65 kA, 4/10 µs (single shot)






Temporary Overvoltage (TOV) Capability (phase-to-earth)



Without prior duty, at 60 Hz:


  *   1.4 · Uc (134 kV) for 10 s
  *   1.3 · Uc (125 kV) for 60 s
  *   1.25 · Uc (120 kV) for 10 min



With prior duty (energy equal to Wth):


  *   1.3 · Uc (125 kV) for 10 s
  *   1.2 · Uc (115 kV) for 60 s






Housing, Insulation & Creepage



  *   Housing type: Composite silicone rubber, grey
  *   Creepage distance: 4 350 mm
  *   Specific creepage: 30 mm/kV (Um)
  *   Degree of protection: IP54 (terminals), IP65 (housing)
  *   Recommended phase-to-earth clearance: ≥ 1 600 mm






Mechanical Data



  *   Specified long-term load (SLL): 800 Nm
  *   Specified short-term load (SSL): 2 000 Nm
  *   Seismic capability: 0.5 g horizontal, 0.3 g vertical
  *   Short-circuit / pressure relief rating: 63 kA r.m.s., 0.2 s






Service Conditions



  *   Ambient temperature: −40 °C … +50 °C
  *   Altitude: ≤ 1 000 m (standard design)
  *   Installation: Outdoor, vertical; indoor on request
  *   Pollution level: up to “heavy”; “very heavy” on request (increased creepage)






Dimensions & Weight



  *   Overall height H: 1 850 mm
  *   Minimum flashover distance (line–earth): 1 450 mm
  *   Base fixing: 4 × M12, PCD 300 mm
  *   Top terminal: M16 stud with flat terminal pad
  *   Net weight: 55 kg






Accessories / Options



  *   Line terminal: stud or clamp connector
  *   Base: standard galvanized steel base or insulated base
  *   Grading rings: upper / lower for Um ≥ 170 kV (optional here)
  *   Ground lead disconnector (GLD)
  *   Surge counter with leakage current meter






Standards & Tests



  *   Design & testing: IEC 60099-4
  *   Application: IEC 60099-5
  *   Quality system: ISO 9001 / ISO 14001





If you like, I can now adapt this to another voltage level (e.g. 36 kV, 420 kV) or to distribution-class with a much shorter spec block.

[Case 174824875600936] Customer Added Communication: Unable to find
 detailed information about Llama cost estimation
body_text:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you can confirm the sender and know the content is safe.

  Hi Michael,

Thank you for your detailed and complete answer. 

So to confirm, the Provisioned Throughput where we can fine-tune and run continous pre-training is not available serverless and we have to start and stop the server ourselves?
Is there an option to simulate serverless or similar (make the model available only when requests are coming in)? Otherwise, the costs for idle hours can add up really fast.

If this is not the case, just a feedback from my end as an engineer who truly enjoys working with AWS and appricaites everything you do in Bedrock: I regularly recommend Bedrock to my clients who want to fine-tune. However, this very point 
is the reason that many are scared off. While they have no problem paying for hours of use, or per token and let it scale as the usage increases, they are highly concerned about
high costs of idle fine-tuned models specially during the early stages of their launch. So unfortunately they go with other providers. 

Best regards,
Meghdad

Hi Meghdad,

this E-Mail serves as a working document to clear any open questions on the Cap-raise: 

Software development cost [ OPEX / CAPEX ] : 

i) Low budget scenario [ Angel Invest @ EUR 250,000 ] 

3 x 50% Freelance Developer [ EUR 30 / hr ] - Front - End / Back - End / Data / Dev Ops 

ii) Normal budget scenario [ Seed Round ] 

ii.a) Company Full Time Team [ To be build up / Hiring Strategy? ] 

2 x Front-End Software Engineer [ FTE ] - EUR 70,000 EUR / a [ plus 22% Social Security Costs ] / Direct Cost
1 x back-End Software Engineer [ FTE ] - EUR 70,000 / a [ plus 22% Social Security Costs ] / Direct Cost
1 x Data Engineer [ FTE ] - EUR 70,000 / a [ plus 22% Social Security Costs ] / Direct Cost
1 x Dev Ops / ML Ops [ FTE ] - EUR 70,000 / a [ plus 22% Social Security Costs ] / Direct Cost 
1x  Engineering Lead [ FTE ] - EUR 100,000 / a [ plus 22% Social Security Costs ] / Direct Cost
2 x Product Manager [ FTE ] - EUR 70,000 / a [ plus 22% Social Security Costs ] / Direct Cost
1 x CEO / CTO [ FTE ] - EUR 120,000 / a [ plus 22% Social Security Costs ] / Indirect Cost
1 x CFO / Finance Lead [ FTE ] - EUR 100,000 / a [ plus 22% Social Security Costs ] / Indirect Cost
2 x Senior Sales [ FTE ] - EUR 70,000 / a [ plus 22% Social Security Costs ] - Design Incentive System / SG&A 
1 x UI / UX Designer [ FTE ] - EUR 65,000 / a [ plus 22% Social Security Costs ] / Direct Cost 
2 x Customer Success [ FTE ] - EUR 65,000 / a [ plus 22% Social Security Costs ] / Indirect Cost / SG&A
1 x Assistant [ FTE ] - EUR 65,000 / a [ plus 22% Social Security Costs ] / Indirect Cost / SG&A

ii.b) External Services 

Books / Accounting / Payroll: EUR 20,000 / a / Indirect Cost / SG&A
Website & SEO: EUR 5,000 / a / Indirect Cost / SG&A
Legal: EUR 25,000 / a / Indirect Cost / SG&A
Coaching & Consulting: EUR 50,000 / a / Indirect Cost / SG&A

ii.c) Office & Equipment: 

Office & furniture: EUR 48,000 / a / Indirect Cash-Out / CAPEX
Subscriptions: EUR 24,000 / a / Indirect Cost / SG&A
Hardware: EUR 32,000 / a / Indirect Cost / CAPEX 


iii) Revenue Build-Up [ To to Roll- and built up Revenue fast? Simple, but effective Steps ] : 

iii.a) pricing structure [ How many customers is Calibrion Platfrom able to handle right now? ] 

Team Subscription: EUR / USD 999 / month
Business Subscription: EUR / USD 1,999 / month
Enterprise Subscription: EUR / USD 3,999 / month

iii.b) Customer attraction / onboarding / Retention strategy / milestones [ Sales & Roll-Out Strategy ]: 

- Journey and milestones: pilots / closing enterprise clients 
- Which cost / service is Calibrion disrupting? 
- potential leads and verticals: i) eCom; Fin-Tech; 

iv) Runway, growth & valuation - medium term strategy: 

v) Financial Statement development / Forecast 

Open questions: 
- Go-To-Market Strategy 
- Sales Ramp-Up & Roll-Out Strategy [ Top-Line Development ] 
- Equity Story [ What will the capital raised by used for? Future company development ] 
- Deep understanding for the product [ Short-term call and product walk-through ] 

Open / to finalize documents: 
- Investor letter [ 80% ] 
- Teaser / OnePager [ 70% ] 
- Pitch-Deck [ 90% ] 
- Financial & Business Model [ 30% ] 
- Short-List [ Folder for each VC so far on g-drive ]

[Reminder] Enable default Smart feature and personalization settings
 for Google Workspace users
body_text:
Google Workspace logo



We implemented changes to the Smart feature and personalization settings in  
Q1 2025.



Dear administrator,

We're writing to remind you of changes to the Google Workspace Smart  
feature and personalization settings. These two settings allow users to  
control how their Gmail, Meet, and Chat data is used to provide Smart  
features and personalized experiences within these applications, and across  
Google Workspace and other Google services.

We've provided additional information below to guide you through the  
upcoming changes.

What this means for your organization

Changes to features

In Q1 2025, we launched updated “Smart features for Google Workspace”  
settings page that resulted in the following updates:


More specific control: The previous setting for sharing Gmail, Chat, and  
Meet data to provide smart features in other Google products was split to  
provide you with individual controls for sharing across Google Workspace  
and/or sharing across Google services.
Improved data controls: You have broader control over how your Workspace  
data is used for smart features and personalization across Google Workspace  
services. The previous setting only controlled Gmail, Meet, and Chat data.
Additional user journeys: You are able to control whether Google Workspace  
content and activity is used to personalize additional Gemini for Workspace  
features.

What's not changing

The updated privacy settings do not impact existing controls over smart  
features in Gmail, Meet, and Chat. These changes do not alter our  
underlying data handling practices or our robust privacy commitments.

What you need to do

You should see the new settings in your Admin console and have the ability  
to set the default settings for your users.

Action required for users in Europe and Japan only

To give your end users access to Smart features using Google Workspace  
data, enable the default smart feature settings by following the steps  
provided below:


Open the Admin console.
Navigate to the left-side panel, and click Account.
Click Account settings.
Click Smart features for Google Workspace.
Select Set a default value for users.
Check the box below to turn on the setting.
Note: If the settings are left unchecked after selecting “Set a default  
value for users”, it will be default disabled for your users.
Click Save.

Note: You can set the user default settings for the three settings controls  
below:


Smart features in Gmail, Meet, and Chat (existing setting)
Smart features in other Google Workspace products (updated setting)
Smart features in other Google products (updated setting)

Requirement for re-consent

Starting late Q2 2025, if the updated admin settings are not set to a user  
default, your users will receive a prompt in Gmail to re-consent to the  
updated settings. If they choose not to opt-in at that time, they have the  
option to enable the updated settings through their Settings pages in  
Gmail, Drive, Chat, and Meet.

To re-consent to the new settings via the prompt, your users can complete  
the following steps:


Click the smart features prompt when it appears in Gmail.
Select Turn on smart features in Workspace.
Click Next.
Select Turn on smart features in other Google products.
Click Save.

We're here to help

We understand that this update may require that you make adjustments on  
your end, but we're here to support you. If you have any issues or  
questions, please review the Google Workspace Admin Help Center article.

Thanks for choosing Google Workspace.

– The Google Workspace Team




Was this information helpful?

A smiling face A neutral face A sad face


© 2025 Google LLC 1600 Amphitheatre Parkway, Mountain View, CA 94043

You have received this important update about your Google Workspace account  
because you designated this email address as a primary or secondary contact  
for mandatory service communications in your Google Admin Console profile.
