Tailored VC Approaching - Point Nice Example
body_text:
Dear Team at Point Nine Capital,

We are building Calibrion, an agent-native data annotation platform which solves the bottleneck in organisational GenAI adaptation: Data annotation to enable organisations to train LLMs [ and further level GenAI models ] on their proprietary data. 

Compared to previous solutions in this space, Calibrion reduces annotation time by ~50%; annotation cost by ~65% and increases annotation quality with built-in quality control by ~3x. 

We were able to sign a 6 month paid pilot with a large listed e-commerce retail company in Germany and seek EUR 300,000 - 500,000 in seed financing to build a team supporting onboarding of further clients to speed up time towards product led growth.

Given Point Nine’s strong track record of backing early-stage deep tech ventures, we believe Calibrion aligns closely with your Investment thesis. Please find attached our pitch deck. We would greatly apprechiate some 20-30 mins for a call including a product-walk-through according to your schedule.

Thanks for getting back to us and best,
Nils 

--
Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 ・ nils@calibrion.ai <mailto:nils@calibrion.ai>
Französische Str. 20・10117 Berlin・Germany
www.calibrion.ai <http://www.calibrion.ai/>

subject: 14 Big Ideas for 2026
body_text:
View this post on the web at https://speedrun.substack.com/p/14-big-ideas-for-2026

💡  This Week’s Big Ideas
💰  SR001 alumni Runware announced a $50m Series A [ https://substack.com/redirect/1f6a851b-ffb7-4a59-8c4b-e6a10284a8fb?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] to bring inference closer than ever to users
💪  SR005 alumni First Voyage announced a $2.5m seed [ https://substack.com/redirect/e4b35814-0b37-4c3c-97a7-ccc1c64de41c?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] to help users build healthy habits
📺  Don’t miss our interview with legendary consumer investor Kirsten Green [ https://substack.com/redirect/fbb162e2-e9a9-419c-b4b1-ef8fff14cab0?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] of Forerunner Ventures
💼  Let us help you find your next job! Join our talent network [ https://substack.com/redirect/e1816f75-9d35-4ff2-a915-b860cff1e55d?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] to get access to world-class opportunities. We'll intro you to relevant founders in the portfolio.
Each year, a16z partners publish their “Big Ideas” for the next 12 months, calling shots on where tech founders will take us next. 
Our friends across the firm have already published a three-part series featuring Big Ideas from partners on Bio + Health [ https://substack.com/redirect/a7e3d323-17fc-4e89-a1d7-0bded3497883?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ], American Dynamism [ https://substack.com/redirect/d8d41b9b-c6f3-4a95-b9bd-d8d8df88f97f?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ], and Crypto [ https://substack.com/redirect/d1c633d8-877f-4569-9fd9-46de5a8d629e?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]. 
Today, 14 investors and operating partners on the a16z speedrun team weigh in with their own Big Ideas for 2026.
1) AI world models will take the spotlight in storytelling
By Jonathan Lai - X [ https://substack.com/redirect/e24eca03-62e5-4839-b59f-409e449e1dd3?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] | LinkedIn [ https://substack.com/redirect/f64d351a-e4a1-44ea-b36c-5a9ff92b56f8?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
World models will also likely give rise to not just a single game, but an entire new category of generative world experiences. You could have a horror experience where you’re hiding from generated monsters, or a D&D experience where you’re roaming an infinite fantasy world with friends.
2) Venture capital will eat private equity
By Troy Kirwin - X [ https://substack.com/redirect/fb015422-c4c6-4eb6-88cc-83bd0e92541e?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] | LinkedIn [ https://substack.com/redirect/ff91fe76-c703-416e-b749-56aadc24da22?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
While the west coast, Patagonia-wearing VCs and the east coast, PE suits used to live in different universes… In 2026 with AI, I believe, those worlds converge.
3) Multi-player AI will eat single-player AI
By Fareed Mosavat - X [ https://substack.com/redirect/5eebc058-6a5a-4dd7-9813-7af84249fd4c?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] | LinkedIn [ https://substack.com/redirect/ef7634d7-2414-4132-9dd2-389e566c21fd?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
Right now, most AI tools are built for one human + one model in a private workspace. ChatGPT, Cursor, Claude. Incredibly powerful, but currently optimized for individuals. The impact is massive: drafts, code, specs, campaigns, workflows. But almost none of it is shared, aligned, or contextualized across a team.
4) Pricing will become earned, not negotiated
By Macy Mills - X [ https://substack.com/redirect/44be1091-da03-4724-8b2d-395bea40b5d5?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] | LinkedIn [ https://substack.com/redirect/6127b4a6-dfcb-4926-ac2f-039b4830aa61?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
Say you need 50 qualified sales meetings. Instead of buying another AI tool, you post a bounty: “$500 per meeting booked.” AI agents compete. Whoever performs best gets paid. We already do this with bug bounties, Kaggle, hackathons. Why not for AI agents going after real business outcomes?
5) The first AI-native university
By Emily Bennett - X [ https://substack.com/redirect/7d63c850-eca8-4e52-ba90-8d8722943dd5?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] | LinkedIn [ https://substack.com/redirect/65a71e96-a2a1-4ca9-a2ac-6ff0003a2e70?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
Picture an institution where courses, advising, research collaboration, and even building operations continuously adapt based on data feedback loops. Schedules optimize themselves. Reading lists evolve nightly and rewrite themselves as new research appears. Learning paths shift in real time to meet each student’s pace and context.
6) The world will begin to optimize for ME
By Josh Lu - X [ https://substack.com/redirect/f7157008-bb22-4a3d-8b82-102d2d90896c?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] | LinkedIn [ https://substack.com/redirect/8054c5ae-ed05-490e-b85b-ba29fcecf7ca?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
For over two centuries, our world has been shaped by one idea: economies of scale. This mindset delivered extraordinary progress: affordable/accessible nutrition, reliable housing, global transportation (and the accompanying shrinking of supply lines and cultural divides), and more. But in 2026, I believe this paradigm flips.
7) The hacker house will professionalize
By Tom Hammer - X [ https://substack.com/redirect/336db154-dbf3-4eac-84ab-3d51cdb36e97?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] | LinkedIn [ https://substack.com/redirect/8fe3696c-8dff-4f48-b587-9c1c189dc1cb?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
Ambition breeds ambition, and society needs ways to get the smartest and hungriest young people in a single room together. Residencies are one of the cheapest and most effective ways to create this clustering / cohesion—the expansion of solo founding / building only heightens the need for cohesive communities
8) The return of well-paid writers
By Ryan Rigney - X [ https://substack.com/redirect/c4ddbba2-886e-4b52-b3f6-ff19e24c7e2b?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] | LinkedIn [ https://substack.com/redirect/f75a55fa-e2c8-4f34-bac3-311a501884eb?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
The word on the street is that the kids don’t read anymore. Everybody overdosed on YouTube Shorts and Bang energy drinks and forgot how to flip a page. So the story goes. But something strange is starting to happen. Startups are talking about wanting to hire “storytellers.”
9) Curation, not content, will be king
By Lester Chen - X [ https://substack.com/redirect/bfe2295d-c0a4-4854-9d31-89eab65d7cde?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] | LinkedIn [ https://substack.com/redirect/992ea327-1e34-49c6-927a-5b624707d8aa?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
The best movie/game/book recos I’ve gotten came from word of mouth or short-form videos, which led me to YouTube, which I then consumed while then going to Reddit to discuss. As cliche as it sounds, can AI solve this? An ‘agent of my tastes’ recording the things i consume and suggesting the most relevant pieces of media worth spending my time on. 
10) AI-native marketplaces will work for the buyer
By Sam Shank - X [ https://substack.com/redirect/4be7d65d-8d66-4c97-a4e3-0abe88dec9e9?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] | LinkedIn [ https://substack.com/redirect/0866d52f-a1cf-43c4-b463-e750b17d6327?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
This past year, I’ve had a front-row seat watching teams at a16z speedrun build AI agent companies across legal, logistics, manufacturing, and other complex, operational domains. What’s striking isn’t just how capable these agents are, it’s that they already take responsibility for outcomes, not just information. Seeing that up close has changed how I think about e-commerce.
I’ve spent much of my career around marketplaces and the classic playbook was powerful: bring fragmented supply online, aggregate it in one place, make it easy to search, and reduce friction at checkout. 
11) Fat AI startups will own the slowest markets
By Andrew Lee - X [ https://substack.com/redirect/e112ad30-9a86-4940-be6e-1efee883c9eb?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] | LinkedIn [ https://substack.com/redirect/17b1a1e8-ac3d-4a3d-a883-28f8ff832165?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
Back in 2010, Ben Horowitz said some startups should go fat—build the full stack, own distribution, and move fast—while Fred Wilson preached lean. Fifteen years later, the debate’s settled: in the AI era, fat startups win. A fat startup ships outcomes, not features. It bundles software, data, hardware, and human ops into one integrated product that actually gets the job done.
12) AI will become a “civilization” level technology
By Marcus Segal - X [ https://substack.com/redirect/121fa404-45af-48c7-8e80-6cb123f50dfd?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] | LinkedIn [ https://substack.com/redirect/ae719734-b900-4f41-8305-0d4e2f57b2f8?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
Predictive health could transform the health insurance industry from a cost of treatment model to a cost of prevention model. Smart cities powered by AI that integrates traffic, utilities, school and safety data to create better urban environments. Natural disaster prediction and remediation planning to save lives and trillions of dollars.
13) We’ll develop tools that help us stay human
By Lejla Johnsen - X [ https://substack.com/redirect/949c3fc8-9c25-4b49-a258-536f67bde2a7?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] | LinkedIn [ https://substack.com/redirect/cccfbf3c-1cce-4a8f-b476-7106dec03114?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
AI is doing the grunt work so we can finally master human work. This speed of change is pushing us toward what truly matters: self mastery. We can’t control the external, but we can strengthen our internal operating systems by using tools that scale emotional intelligence, foster faith, and bridge the isolation AI risks creating.
14) The coming consumer AI renaissance
By Kenan Saleh - X [ https://substack.com/redirect/550d622f-033b-476b-8c01-3f91a5b6b651?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] | LinkedIn [ https://substack.com/redirect/a19122e6-87bd-4914-ad25-dd623776027b?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
I’m especially excited about products that use AI to make previously expensive services cheaper and more accessible, sometimes using human-in-the-loop to start. This includes things like AI travel agents, personal assistants, matchmakers, therapists, tutors, and more.
That’s it for our roundup of the a16z speedrun team’s Big Ideas for 2026. Have thoughts you’d like to share? Hit us in the comments below.

subject: New Event: Nils Erbach  - 10:00 Tue, 18 Mar 2025 - 45 Minute Meeting
Remember to check your organization information
Seed Round Investment Opportunity Calibrion AI
body_text:
Dear team at Lunar Ventures,

I am reaching out to introduce Calibrion AI, a data annotation platform enabling enterprises and organisations to fine-tune LLMs to their proprietary data. We built a working product and already reached pilot-stage interest from European and US companies in the eCom vertical. 

We are currently raising our Seed round to: 
- expand our engineering and data science team
- Accelerate go-to-market efforts across verticals 
- Further develop our AI capabilities and integrate new LLM-driven features.

We would be happy to send over our pitch-deck and arrange a call where a walk-through of the actual product can be arranged. 

Please find attached a letter outlining our fund-raising goals as well as a one-pager on Calibrion.

We would be pleased to hear from you and would welcome any additional feedback.

Kind regards, 
Nils 

-- 
Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 <tel:+49+(0)30+478+662+25> ・ nils@calibrion.ai <mailto:meghdad@calibrion.ai>
Französische Str. 20・10117 Berlin・Germany
www.calibrion.ai <http://www.calibrion.ai/>

subject:
Trial Next Steps: Improve candidate engagement with your technical
 assessments
body_text:
Using pre-written questions in your interviews will reduce the work you have to put into each interview and reduce bias by allowing you to judge each candidate on the same criteria.

Step 1: Create your question

Use the question wizard to select the coding language, write candidate instructions, set up starter code, and customize the question as needed for your purposes. At the end of the question wizard you’ll publish the question so you can use it in your interviews. Create a question (https://d353Nv04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/W4+113/d353Nv04/VWSxYF5VgcNWW1jkC6q7TdL28W1TDTHt5ssPsNN7Y8_3s3pyd0W8wLKSR6lZ3nmW39L8d49j1cCxW6t6PKM8Y0LByW4ZVlp58YvVy3N7vnyYsSx-rTW3w_ZWm3JTVt8W4_X7YH5-GMD8W3XY0Y71x_DHNW3Y2r1T3-QZTjW8Y8xF85T4yYfVNH5KZ8ZmgDNW3jjsYL273B9zW8tqTWx8RjwdWW3dz6T_2vbqVGW3lShqQ7Q8gVMW2f05xf6kmblZW3TsDcR2vy5FNTlCnj7sSnbRW28K9_t5BS9sHW1GnLYT8mF2S5W8RFMdK2x2650N2znwR1LP39DW6rSW5Q3l_pjwW14qJ_D1rd2JBW1WlTTS5MKwG-W8ZKhb-7dt1FMN8jwDVfrfkZ_W3zw84Z68nc6FVHj8MF154mQrf1Tm46H04 ) .

Step 2: Create a pad from the question for each candidate

Once your question has been published, you’ll navigate to the Questions dashboard and click Create Pad. In the pad you’ll change the title of the pad; we recommend using the name of the candidate, the role they're interviewing for, and the date.

Step 3: Invite the candidate when you’re ready

In the bottom left of the screen, you’ll find the Invite button. Grab the URL from here and send it to the candidate.

🚦 If you have the waiting room enabled, you’ll have to click either the Open Pad to Everyone or Admit button to allow the candidate to enter the pad.

Once the candidate is in the pad, click on Start Call to initiate video and audio with them to answer any questions they have and work collaboratively with them through the tasks.

Step 4: End the interview and watch playback as needed

Once the interview is complete, make sure you click the End Interview button in the bottom right of the screen. This will ensure the candidate can no longer make changes to the pad, as well as give you access to the interview playback.

You can also add to any private interviewer notes to ensure any follow-up thoughts or questions are documented for future reference.

Step 5: Repeat steps 2-5 for each candidate

No need to set-up a brand new pad from scratch for each interview – just create a pad from the question dashboard, invite the candidate, and conduct the interview.

Create an interview question
(https://d353Nv04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/W4+113/d353Nv04/VWSxYF5VgcNWW1jkC6q7TdL28W1TDTHt5ssPsNN7Y8_3s3pyd0W8wLKSR6lZ3nmW39L8d49j1cCxW6t6PKM8Y0LByW4ZVlp58YvVy3N7vnyYsSx-rTW3w_ZWm3JTVt8W4_X7YH5-GMD8W3XY0Y71x_DHNW3Y2r1T3-QZTjW8Y8xF85T4yYfVNH5KZ8ZmgDNW3jjsYL273B9zW8tqTWx8RjwdWW3dz6T_2vbqVGW3lShqQ7Q8gVMW2f05xf6kmblZW3TsDcR2vy5FNTlCnj7sSnbRW28K9_t5BS9sHW1GnLYT8mF2S5W8RFMdK2x2650N2znwR1LP39DW6rSW5Q3l_pjwW14qJ_D1rd2JBW1WlTTS5MKwG-W8ZKhb-7dt1FMN8jwDVfrfkZ_W3zw84Z68nc6FVHj8MF154mQrf1Tm46H04 )

subject: Antler - A reminder for our upcoming meeting
Re: Your Qonto account

subject: Re: Your Qonto account
body_text:
Hi Beshoy,

Thanks for reaching out.
I went through your questionnaire, and unfortunately, it is not directly
applicable to us.

We (StemNLP GmbH) offer SaaS subscriptions and AI development services.
Although we are negotiating several deals, we are still pre-revenue. The
deals are expected to
close soon and start in early 2026.

StemNLP made a small revenue (I think around 2K) in Q1 2025 from a small AI
development project. For that, I can send you the invoices. Note that of
that invoice partially went into our Wise account from which I made
transfers into Qonto account.

Our brand name is Calibrion, and our website is www.calibrion.ai
In the imprint, you can find all the StemNLP legal details.

Let me know if you have any other questions.

Best regards,
Meghdad

subject: You just made a payment
[Billing and Service Notice] Google Workspace service and pricing updates
body_text:
Google Workspace logo



Review the updates to AI features and pricing for your Google Workspace  
subscription(s).



Dear administrator,

We recently added the best of Google AI to Workspace plans to help your  
teams accomplish more, faster. In addition, we added new, simple to use  
security insights and controls to help you keep your business data safe.

We also announced updated subscription pricing. Your subscription will be  
subject to this updated pricing starting July 7, 2025*.

We've provided additional information below to guide you through this  
change.

What this means for your business

New Workspace features

Your updated pricing reflects the many new features now included in your  
Google Workspace edition. With these changes, you can:


Summarize long email threads, draft replies, and compose professional  
emails faster and easier with Help me write in Gmail
Quickly format and organize data with tables in Google Sheets
Improve file sharing and collaboration with shared drives
Get AI assistance in writing, planning, and learning with access to the  
Gemini app with data protections
Accelerate learning by uploading your docs, PDFs, videos, websites, and  
more to get instant insights and podcast-like Audio Overviews with  
NotebookLM
Access tailored security insights, actionable guidance in the admin  
console, and simplified data protection controls with security advisor

Updated subscription pricing

Starting as early as July 7, 2025*, your Google Workspace Business Starter  
subscription price will be automatically updated to $7.00** per user, per  
month with an Annual/Fixed-Term Plan (or $8.40 if you have a monthly  
Flexible Plan).

*The specific date that your subscription price will increase depends on  
your plan type, number of user licenses, and other factors.

**Prices will be updated in all local payment currencies.

If you have an Annual/Fixed-Term Plan, your subscription will be subject to  
updated pricing on your next plan renewal date, no earlier than July 7,  
2025. We will provide you with more specific information at least 30 days  
before updates to your Google Workspace plan pricing are made.

What you need to do

No action is required from you. Features have already rolled out to Google  
Workspace Business Starter subscriptions, including AI features in many  
additional languages. Subscription prices will be updated automatically  
starting July 7, 2025.

We know that data security and compliance are top priorities for business  
leaders when adopting AI, and we are committed to helping you keep your  
data safe. You can understand how to effectively utilize generative AI in  
your organization, and learn how to keep your data confidential and  
protected.

We're here to help

If you wish to make changes to your subscription or payment plan, please  
visit the Admin console. Find which edition and payment plan you have on  
Google Workspace Admin Help.

Refer to the Help Center for details regarding the AI features and price  
updates, including updated local currency pricing.

Thanks for choosing Google Workspace.

–The Google Workspace Team




Was this information helpful?

A smiling face A neutral face A sad face


© 2025 Google LLC 1600 Amphitheatre Parkway, Mountain View, CA 94043

You have received this important update about your Google Workspace account  
because you designated this email address as a primary or secondary contact  
for mandatory service communications in your Google Admin Console profile.

>>>>>>>> Hi Meghdad,
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> Thanks for your questions, happy to walk you through everything!
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> To create something similar to the *Synthetic Intelligence* video,
>>>>>>>> here’s a quick overview of our production process:
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> *Step-by-Step Process:*
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    1.
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    *Creative Brief & Discovery*
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    We start with a short questionnaire to understand your goals,
>>>>>>>>    audience, and product workflow.
>>>>>>>>    2.
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    *Scriptwriting*
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    Our writers craft a compelling and technically accurate script
>>>>>>>>    based on your input. If you have existing materials (pitch decks, web copy,
>>>>>>>>    product docs), feel free to share — we’ll distill the best story from them.
>>>>>>>>    3.
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    *Voiceover*
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    Once the script is finalized, we record a professional
>>>>>>>>    voiceover (you can choose the voice, accent, tone, etc.).
>>>>>>>>    4.
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    *Storyboard & Styleframes*
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    We visualize the narrative with static frames showing the look,
>>>>>>>>    feel, and flow of the animation. You’ll be able to review and approve
>>>>>>>>    before we animate.
>>>>>>>>    5.
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    *Full Animation*
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    We bring everything to life with motion design, sound effects,
>>>>>>>>    and music, ensuring the final piece is smooth and visually engaging.
>>>>>>>>    6.
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    *Revisions & Delivery*
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    You get unlimited rounds of revision at every stage — we only
>>>>>>>>    move forward when you’re 100% happy.
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> *What We Need From You:*
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    Any existing documents that explain your product/process
>>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    Your input on goals, target audience, and any must-have visuals
>>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    Optional: Your brand assets (logo, font, colors)
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> If you’d rather let us handle everything end-to-end, we’re happy
>>>>>>>> to, most of our clients do just that.
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> *Pricing:*
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> For a 60–90 second custom animated explainer like the one you
>>>>>>>> referenced, our pricing starts at *$1,500 USD*. This includes:
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    Scriptwriting
>>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    Voiceover
>>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    Storyboarding
>>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    Custom illustration
>>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    Full animation
>>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    Music & sound design
>>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    Unlimited revisions
>>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    Full commercial rights
>>>>>>>>    -
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>    HD/4K delivery + source files
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> Let me know if you’d like to schedule a call with our CEO, he’s
>>>>>>>> worked with several AI companies and could share ideas tailored to your
>>>>>>>> product. You can book a time here:
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> 👉 *10 Studio Introduction Call*
>>>>>>>> <https://10.studio/introduction-call/>
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>> Looking forward to hearing your thoughts!
>>>>>>>> Best Regards,
>>>>>>>> Team 10 Studio <https://10.studio/>
>>>>>>>> Book a Meeting <https://10.studio/introduction-call/>
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>

subject: Re: Animated Explainer for Calibrion AI?
body_text:
Hi Meghdad,

This is Alex, Client Success Lead at 10 Studio.

We understand that budget can sometimes be a concern. If that’s the case,
please let us know. I’d be happy to find a middle ground to see how we can
provide the best possible results within your budget, making sure you’re
fully satisfied with the outcome.

Alternatively, if you need some time before starting the project, please
let me know when you’re looking to begin. This will allow us to prepare
some initial planning for your project, which we can share with you if
you’d like.

Could you kindly share your thoughts on this? We’re looking forward to
hearing from you.

Best Regards,
Team 10 Studio <https://10.studio/>
Book a Meeting <https://10.studio/introduction-call/>

subject: Re: Animated Explainer for Calibrion AI?
body_text:
Hi Meghdad,

Thanks for your questions, happy to walk you through everything!

To create something similar to the *Synthetic Intelligence* video, here’s a
quick overview of our production process:

*Step-by-Step Process:*


   1.

   *Creative Brief & Discovery*

   We start with a short questionnaire to understand your goals, audience,
   and product workflow.
   2.

   *Scriptwriting*

   Our writers craft a compelling and technically accurate script based on
   your input. If you have existing materials (pitch decks, web copy, product
   docs), feel free to share — we’ll distill the best story from them.
   3.

   *Voiceover*

   Once the script is finalized, we record a professional voiceover (you
   can choose the voice, accent, tone, etc.).
   4.

   *Storyboard & Styleframes*

   We visualize the narrative with static frames showing the look, feel,
   and flow of the animation. You’ll be able to review and approve before we
   animate.
   5.

   *Full Animation*

   We bring everything to life with motion design, sound effects, and
   music, ensuring the final piece is smooth and visually engaging.
   6.

   *Revisions & Delivery*

   You get unlimited rounds of revision at every stage — we only move
   forward when you’re 100% happy.

*What We Need From You:*


   -

   Any existing documents that explain your product/process
   -

   Your input on goals, target audience, and any must-have visuals
   -

   Optional: Your brand assets (logo, font, colors)

If you’d rather let us handle everything end-to-end, we’re happy to, most
of our clients do just that.

*Pricing:*

For a 60–90 second custom animated explainer like the one you referenced,
our pricing starts at *$1,500 USD*. This includes:


   -

   Scriptwriting
   -

   Voiceover
   -

   Storyboarding
   -

   Custom illustration
   -

   Full animation
   -

   Music & sound design
   -

   Unlimited revisions
   -

   Full commercial rights
   -

   HD/4K delivery + source files

Let me know if you’d like to schedule a call with our CEO, he’s worked with
several AI companies and could share ideas tailored to your product. You
can book a time here:

👉 *10 Studio Introduction Call* <https://10.studio/introduction-call/>

Looking forward to hearing your thoughts!
Best Regards,
Team 10 Studio <https://10.studio/>
Book a Meeting <https://10.studio/introduction-call/>

a16z Ranks Top 100 GenAI Products, 2025 State of Voice Report
 Released, Hassabis speaks on Path To AGI
body_text:
Welcome (back) to AI Minds, a newsletter about the brainy and sometimes zany world of AI, brought to you by the Deepgram editorial team.

### In this edition:

* 🎥 a16z Ranks the Top 100 GenAI Products

* 💸 The best (and worst) AI models for personal finance

* 🔍 Can LLMs detect bias in marketing?

* ⚡ State of Voice Report 2025: The Year of Human-Like Voice AI Agents

* 🐦 Social Media Buzz: Discussions break out over Altman’s upcoming Reasoning model

* 🎵 New Musical Production AI model garners hype

* 💻 Karpathy compares LLMs to command terminals on X

* 📲 Three new, trending AI apps for you!

* 🎙️ The AI Minds Podcast with Jonathan Hunt, Co-Founder & CTO at Elefant AI

* 🎤 Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis speaks on The Path To AGI

* 💾 The Beginner’s Guide to Machine Learning with Rust

* 🔊 Voice AI Agents Exposed: What is Conversational AI?

* 🧭 Graph Neural Networks (GNNs): A Deep Dive

Thanks for letting us crash your inbox; let’s party. 🎉

----------
**Looking for a cutting-edge AI medical transcription model? Click **[here](https://deepgram.com/learn/introducing-nova-3-medical-speech-to-text-api)**. **🥳


--------------------

Caption: 


----------# 🎥 _a16z_ Ranks the Top 100 GenAI Products

Youtube: The Top 100 GenAI Products, Ranked and Explained (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=c0_7ffZte80)

Video description: “This month, a16z’s Consumer team released the fourth edition of the GenAI 100 — a data-driven ranking of the top 50 AI-first web products and mobile apps, based on unique monthly visits and active users.”

----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/18c135d7-112b-4588-8b9f-659e512b4066/div-2.gif?t=1700246279)
Caption: 


----------# 🔍  Best (and worst) AI models for personal finance and How LLMs detect biases in marketing

View image: (https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXeGlFWwb8VBAwmJpOKXOlN3DLjhayw6E9BiZRUqoYoT4xUz6gbjlxd_9eo5vT5i6y6ztz10a9K_corYYPBeVmubzSDW7FeBMa4HLSNs8cUrlnmpXih-htfKp1SBye-iuuc8KE32tg?key=GKuHC4BGODv_JBUasKo_nY_B)
Caption: 

[Can AI Help with Your Personal Finances?](https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.19784?) - This paper explores the potential of LLMs to address key challenges in personal finance, focusing on the United States. It evaluates several leading LLMs, including ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Llama, to assess their effectiveness in providing accurate advice on topics such as mortgages, taxes, and investments.

———————————————————————————

View image: (https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXebf_-IZZjw6V_mOmqaarwbXPrj5Ntbfhj_DEV48EJJ1eFD_-ydSUI4fHqLObywjVVk9_ZWYdw-mWXp2ko5ZaBuVrQQgMOrspfNiQLBhBSRYQHvt_0xG_468RnD0NClbqwW7M-C?key=GKuHC4BGODv_JBUasKo_nY_B)
Caption: 

[Towards Equitable AI: Detecting Bias in Using Large Language Models for Marketing](https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.12838) - This study examined bias in finance-related marketing slogans generated by LLMs (i.e., ChatGPT) by prompting tailored ads targeting five demographic categories: gender, marital status, age, income level, and education level.

----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/18c135d7-112b-4588-8b9f-659e512b4066/div-2.gif?t=1700246279)
Caption:

Long story short, 2025 seems to be the year of human-like voice AI agents. If you’d like to take a look at the data, statistics, and analyses yourself, you can check out [the full report here](https://deepgram.com/2025-state-of-voice-ai-report).

[Berries AI](https://deepgram.com/ai-apps/berries) is a cutting-edge AI scribe designed explicitly for mental health professionals. Trusted by over 1,000 clinicians, Berries AI offers an affordable, personalized, and HIPAA-compliant solution for mental health notes.

Caption: 

[Boo.ai](https://Boo.ai) is an AI-powered writing assistant that helps you write faster and better. It uses advanced natural language processing to generate text, make suggestions, and provide feedback to improve your writing.

———————————————————————————

View image: (https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXeUf-8Tv8ZF33ED7w5CJLBVNJnCPMUWCnZXEUi3ZyQBTnuvsbwfs-CazUvCGNB35S2awnjbQGFJlshw2w1nFTCzK7Id3wDuWNNIjv8RhH_OXB2hQ8hSePMAvjKD51BwPmpRAm4r?key=GKuHC4BGODv_JBUasKo_nY_B)
Caption: 

[PandaChat-AI](https://deepgram.com/ai-apps/pandachat) is an AI-powered platform that allows users to upload any data like documents, presentations, spreadsheets, images, websites etc. and ask questions about the content. subject: On Dealing With Rejection as a Founder
body_text:
View this post on the web at https://speedrun.substack.com/p/on-dealing-with-rejection

Engineers, come meet us! During next week’s Tech Week SF we’ll host a breakfast for engineers who are in school (or within 5 years of graduating). Come meet the a16z speedrun team, including GP Andrew Chen, and learn what it takes to get funded by a16z speedrun while enjoying some waffles and açaí bowls. Apply here [ https://substack.com/redirect/a7b1c12c-d45f-45b6-9add-c8edd4c02ee3?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ].
💡  This Week’s Big Ideas
🧊  Come meet our team at Tech Week SF (Oct 6–10) and LA (Oct 13–17)! See the full list of a16z speedrun events for San Francisco [ https://substack.com/redirect/391f0d08-77eb-4bac-9644-a1b8f8c6c11c?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] and Los Angeles [ https://substack.com/redirect/9303cc21-1735-4679-a722-f2437050c16c?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ].
💫  Some amazing startups from the class of SR005 launched this week. Check out these viral launch videos from Taya [ https://substack.com/redirect/8e9931b5-0fb9-4457-88e4-f2a483048e63?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ], High Agency [ https://substack.com/redirect/2bc9f07b-17d2-4472-994b-33720b05836c?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ], and OpenSesame [ https://substack.com/redirect/db9dbd21-b1c0-422e-ae07-e22f41c63d11?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ].
🐦  “The best advice I got early on was to play positive sum games,” writes Robin Guo [ https://substack.com/redirect/3ada03f5-a0d2-44b1-bd9a-9be74a7fcc7c?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]. “People will naturally help people they like.”
🎉  On Oct 9th Bella Nazzari is throwing a party for people who’ve recently moved to San Francisco. Details in thread [ https://substack.com/redirect/e4fb86c4-c45f-4bf3-a025-598e4c93ee89?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ].
💼  Join our talent network [ https://substack.com/redirect/bb64f974-2576-4d97-91cb-2fad1570f16d?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] for more opportunities. If we see a fit for you, we'll intro you to relevant founders in the portfolio.
Natan Voitenkov [ https://substack.com/redirect/f7e47ddf-ec4d-4ba5-8aaf-99728bb995e0?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] walked into the meeting feeling confident. He and his cofounders were set to pitch to an investor that had been described by others as “friendly.” And Voitenkov had plenty of reasons to feel optimistic: His startup, Genway AI, had already leapfrogged many other barriers in their way.
But once the meeting started, the mood soured. “The entire meeting was spent with him scowling at me, lecturing us on CAC, LTV, and ACV,” Voitenkov says. Worse, he felt he’d been embarrassed in front of his team. “I left convinced I had failed,” he says, “certain my co-founders would lose trust in me.”
Instead, Voitenkov’s cofounders encouraged him. “They told me they’d never work with someone like that, and that they admired how I handled the situation,” he says. “That’s when I realized the true value of cofounders.”
Three Ways to Handle Rejection as a Founder
Rejection is something you face constantly when building a startup. As Hedra CEO Michael Lingelbach put it  [ https://substack.com/redirect/f8c71a12-3cbe-42f3-a58f-0982d73b858a?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]in this newsletter two weeks ago [ https://substack.com/redirect/f8c71a12-3cbe-42f3-a58f-0982d73b858a?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]: “You face rejection when recruiting, you face rejection when fundraising. Even if a team seems successful, they’ve all gone through a lot of rejection.”
Rejection is simply an inevitable part of the process of building a startup, even for the best teams. With applications for SR006 officially closed as of this past Sunday, our team of investors is now hard at work reviewing applications and selecting the teams for the next cohort of speedrun. But for every offer we extend, we’ll also be sending out around 200 rejection notes. A little over 14,000 teams applied for SR005, and fewer than 0.5% were ultimately accepted. That means we’re necessarily passing on an enormous number of talented people with strong ideas.
1. Prep for the Next Application Window
The good news for speedrun applicants is that there really is no downside to just applying again to the next cohort when applications re-open in ~5-6 months. In fact, a16z speedrun investor  [ https://substack.com/redirect/97b28f28-d3e1-4364-95ff-36389b414a6f?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]Troy Kirwin [ https://substack.com/redirect/97b28f28-d3e1-4364-95ff-36389b414a6f?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] estimates that about 30% of the teams in his cohort for SR005 had at least one founder that previously applied—and was rejected—for an earlier batch.
These stories naturally tend to get edited out of the narratives that founders tell online. When we spoke with a couple of the repeat applicants in Kirwin’s cohort, more than one downplayed their previous efforts. “Oh, maybe I applied a year ago,” one mused. “We applied to a lot of things back then.”
So it goes. But it raises the question: what makes an application more likely to draw investor interest on the second try? This is something we’ve addressed in detail here in the following post about what the a16z speedrun investors look for in applications:
TL;DR: Traction with customers is the most powerful signal we’re interested in, along with the pedigree of the founding team and the relative opportunity size of the idea being pursued.
Given this, it follows that if you have an idea you really believe in, doubling down on proving your idea’s merits by delivering something of value for real, paying customers.
2. Focus on Customers and Revenue
When investors talk about “traction,” it refers to several possible metrics. For pre-revenue startups, it might include a growing email waitlist, viral tweets, or fanatic engagement data from early testers.
But for companies with a product in the wild, we believe one metric is king: revenue.
Genway’s Natan Voitenkov puts this point neatly:
“Throughout my journey from inception to seed, I have encountered three major types of rejection: from investors, from customers, and from potential hires. Putting these experiences into perspective is essential. While investor rejection can feel intimidating, it is ultimately customer rejection that matters most—because, as the saying goes, revenue solves all problems.”
Getting revenue traction isn’t just one of the best ways to draw investor interest—it’s also often the best way to extend your runway. And in the process of pursuing paying customers, you may even discover new problems that clarify your startup idea or lead to a more fruitful pivot.
3. Ignore the Haters and Lock In
This is perhaps the most ironic possible advice to give within the context of a newsletter with “a16z” branding, but the simple truth is that investors can and often do get it wrong, and sometimes you should ignore our advice. No investor is likely to know as much as you do about the market you’re building for, so it’s possible that getting rejected by investors—including when applying for a program like a16z speedrun—isn’t actually that useful of a signal.
One last quote from Voitenkov illustrates this point nicely:
In the context of raising capital, I often remind myself that even the best in class investors are wrong nearly 90% of the time. Ask yourself: how many dates did you go on before meeting your significant other? Fundraising follows the same principle. To raise capital, one must be prepared to face numerous rejections. Ultimately, it is a numbers game—much like dating.
Like this post? Forward it to your team! And thanks to Natan Voitenkov for speaking with us for this one. Be sure to follow him on X [ https://substack.com/redirect/5a155356-10bc-4890-89a1-bf3545bc9779?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ].
For more weekly dives into the world of early stage startups, subscribe to our own newsletter below.
Get the a16z speedrun newsletter weekly 👇
You are receiving this newsletter since you opted in earlier; if you would like to opt out of future newsletters, you can unsubscribe immediately.

This newsletter may link to other websites and certain information contained herein has been obtained from third-party sources. While taken from sources believed to be reliable, a16z has not independently verified such information and makes no representations about the enduring accuracy of the information or its appropriateness for a given situation.
References to any companies, securities, or digital assets are for illustrative purposes only and do not constitute an investment recommendation or offer to provide investment advisory services. Any references to companies are for illustrative purposes only; please see a16z.com/investments [ https://substack.com/redirect/7c7405e2-96c9-4d0f-a17b-1ef7c8322342?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]. Furthermore, this content is not directed at nor intended for use by any investors or prospective investors, and may not under any circumstances be relied upon when making a decision to invest in any fund managed by a16z. (An offering to invest in an a16z fund will be made only by the private placement memorandum, subscription agreement, and other relevant documentation of any such fund which should be read in their entirety.) Past performance is not indicative of future results.
Charts and graphs provided within are for informational purposes solely and should not be relied upon when making any investment decision. Content in this newsletter speaks only as of the date indicated. Any projections, estimates, forecasts, targets, prospects and/or opinions expressed in these materials are subject to change without notice and may differ or be contrary to opinions expressed by others. Please see a16z.com/disclosures [ https://substack.com/redirect/acca7e3e-c5ad-46e4-8d9d-e91a766bc866?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] for additional important details.

Unsubscribe https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9zcGVlZHJ1bi5zdWJzdGFjay5jb20vYWN0aW9uL2Rpc2FibGVfZW1haWw_.1GeXlqGmkV_xsMozFc-v5MTzyg2JA4MbnheaTue9AVU?

subject: B2B SaaS – Product ready - Zalando SE paid pilot secured – Seed Round EUR 600K; Calibrion.ai - AI Infrastructure / Data annotation
body_text:
Dear Sven, dear Peter,

We are building Calibrion, an agent-native data annotation platform adressing one of the largest barriers to organisational GenAI adoption: enabling proprietary LLM training through high-quality, rapid annotation. 

Calibrion delivers: 
- Agentic data annotation that accelerates annotation up to 100x (from months to hours)
- Cost savings of around 90%
- Built-in proprietary quality control to ensure accuracy at scale 
- 35% faster human-in-the-loop through research driven optimized workflows

We recently signed a 6 months paid pilot with Zalando SE, secured a verbal commitment from Siemens Energy AG E-Commerce division, and are negotiating pilots with Cotopaxi (US sustainable outdoor brand) and LILT (AI-driven translation leader). 

We are seeking EUR 600,000 in seed funding to expand our team, onboard additional clients, and implement product-led-growth.

Our founder and CEO, Meghdad, holds a PhD in Computer Science with a specialization in Natural Language Processing, and has over eight years of applied AI product experience. 

Given your investment focus, we think Calibrion could be a strong fit. You’ll find our one-pager, pitch-deck, and product demos attached. We’d be glad to arrange a 30-minute call to walk you through the product and discuss and our upcoming milestones or just connect via phone (number attached).

I called you and left a message in the voicemail.

Best regards,
Nils 


Nils Erbach

Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
Tel: +49 1 <tel:+49+(0)30+478+662+25>7662205065 ・ nils@calibrion.ai <mailto:meghdad@calibrion.ai>
Kolonnenstr. 8・10827 Berlin・Germany
www.calibrion.ai <https://www.calibrion.ai/>
￼
￼
￼

subject: [Action Advised] Review Gemini app conversation history admin settings before General Availability
body_text:
Google Workspace logo



New settings will go into effect by the end of May 2025.



Dear administrator,

We're writing to inform you about new admin controls for Gemini app  
(gemini.google.com) conversation history, which became available for  
pre-configuration starting on May 5, 2025.

We have provided additional information below to guide you through this  
change.

What this means for your organization

What's changing

You can now:


Turn conversation history on or off for users
Set a specific retention period for conversations

Key details


Pre-configuration: Starting May 5, 2025, you can pre-configure the new  
Gemini conversation history settings.

These settings are expected to take effect for users by the end of May 2025.


Default settings: Gemini conversation history will be ON by default. The  
default conversation retention period will be 18 months. This is consistent  
with current behavior.

Potential impact


If you make no changes, the following default settings will apply:

Gemini conversation history: ON
Conversation retention: 18 months
Activity older than 18 months will be automatically deleted.

These admin settings will override any individual user changes previously  
made to their Gemini Apps Activity settings (gemini.google.com or Gemini  
mobile app)
Users will not be able to override the Gemini conversation history settings  
configured by their admin

Note: This update will not affect your current Gemini app service settings.

What you need to do

If you want to keep the default conversation history and retention  
settings, no action is required from you.

However, we recommend you to review and update the Gemini conversation  
history settings before the settings take effect.

Here is a reference image of the new setting to help you familiarize  
yourself with the update:


We're here to help

For more information on the Gemini conversation history Google Admin  
console settings, please refer to the documentation in our Help Center.

Thanks for choosing Google Workspace.

– The Google Workspace Team

subject: [Important] Your Workspace has exceeded storage limits
Payment Confirmation
B2B SaaS – AI Infrastructure / Data - Early Stage Product Ready - Early Enterprise Traction – Seed Round - Calibrion AI
body_text:
Dear Team at Project A Ventures,

We are building Calibrion, an agent-native data annotation platform that removes one of the largest barriers to building AI models at the scale and speed required today: creating high-quality training datasets.

Current data annotation market dynamics enable rapid revenue growth (€100M - 1B ARR reported in 2024/2025 within 12–24 months) for data annotation platforms with strong technology and product–market fit, underscoring the current demand for high-quality AI data.

Calibrion positions itself as the first human-agent-native solution, approaching the problem with a new perspective and value proposition, delivering:
- Agentic data annotation that accelerates annotation up to ~100x (from months to hours)
- Radical cost savings
- Proprietary three-layer quality control to ensure accuracy at scale
- 35% faster human-in-the-loop through research driven optimized workflows

Our moat compounds across:
- Years of world-class AI, LLM, and agentic expertise
- Patentable processes
- Early distribution into some of the world’s leading enterprises.
- The inherent complexity of the problem we solve
- A fast, high-performing team

We recently signed paid pilots with Zalando, Siemens Energy, and are negotiating additional deals with Cotopaxi (US sustainable outdoor brand) and LILT (US AI-driven enterprise translation leader). 

We are seeking EUR 1,200,000 in seed funding to expand our team, onboard additional clients, and implement product-led-growth within the next 9 - 12 months.

Our founder and CEO, Meghdad, holds a PhD in CS with a specialization in NLP, and has over nine years of applied AI product experience.

Given your investment focus, we believe Calibrion could be a strong fit. I’ve attached our one-pager and pitch deck, and included a link to our product demos here <https://docs.google.com/document/d/1bMC1AW6Wu-eTxOIhEkro05LAr8QYiMcfU7WgXCZe4vQ/edit?usp=sharing>.

We’d be happy to walk you through the product and share what we’re building toward.

Nils Erbach

Calibrion AI
Tel: +49 1 <tel:+49+(0)30+478+662+25>7662205065 ・ nils@calibrion.ai <mailto:meghdad@calibrion.ai>
Kolonnenstr. 8・10827 Berlin・Germany
www.calibrion.ai <https://www.calibrion.ai/>


￼￼

subject: B2B SaaS – Product ready - Zalando SE paid pilot secured – Seed Round kEUR 600; Calibrion.ai - AI Infrastructure / Data annotation
body_text:
Dear Gabriel,

We are building Calibrion, an agent-native data annotation platform adressing one of the largest barriers to organisational GenAI adoption: enabling proprietary LLM training through high-quality, rapid annotation. 

Calibrion delivers: 
- Agentic data annotation that accelerates annotation up to 100x (from months to hours)
- Cost savings of around 90%
- Built-in proprietary quality control to ensure accuracy at scale 
- 35% faster human-in-the-loop through research driven optimized workflows

We recently signed a 6 months paid pilot with Zalando SE, secured a verbal commitment from Siemens Energy AG E-Commerce division, and are negotiating pilots with Cotopaxi (US sustainable outdoor brand) and LILT (AI-driven translation leader). 

We are seeking EUR 600,000 in seed funding to expand our team, onboard additional clients, and implement product-led-growth.

Our founder and CEO, Meghdad, Holds a PhD in Computer Science with a specialization in Natural Language Processing, and has over eight years of applied AI product experience. 

Given your investment focus, we think Calibrion could be a strong fit. You’ll find our one-pager, pitch-deck, and product demos attached. We’d be glad to arrange a 30-minute call to walk you through the product and discuss and our upcoming milestones.

Best regards,
Nils 


Nils Erbach

Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
Tel: +49 1 <tel:+49+(0)30+478+662+25>7662205065 ・ nils@calibrion.ai <mailto:meghdad@calibrion.ai>
Kolonnenstr. 8・10827 Berlin・Germany
www.calibrion.ai <https://www.calibrion.ai/>

Let’s fight fraud together

Hello Meghdad,	

The holiday season is a peak time for cyber threats, so we want to share some helpful tips to keep your account secure.

One of the most common techniques used by fraudsters to steal confidential personal or professional information such as user IDs, passwords, and card details is phishing. This is why we encourage you to be extra cautious with unexpected emails, links, or requests for information. 

Our advice to help you stay protected:

*
Review notifications carefully. Always read approval notifications and SMS texts carefully to confirm that you initiated the action.
*
Keep your credentials private. Never share your sign-in credentials, bank details, or Qonto card numbers with anyone.
*
Activate SCA (Strong Customer Authentication). This extra layer of protection ensures that only you can perform sensitive actions on your Qonto account, like making transfers or adding new members.

👉 At Qonto, protecting your data is our priority. You're always in control of your Qonto experience, including how we communicate with you. To receive only what matters most to you, you can now manage your messaging preferences at any time in your Account settings > Marketing notifications.

subject:
[Case 174824875600936] Customer Added Communication: Unable to find
 detailed information about Llama cost estimation
body_text:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you can confirm the sender and know the content is safe.

  Hi,

Thanks for getting back to me!

This contradicts what I have learned from the automated support response, as well as what I have seen on the latest pricing documentation:

>From your pricing page under Meta: 
Customization (fine-tuning) pricing
An application developer customizes the Llama 2 Pretrained (70B) model using 1000 tokens of data. After training, uses custom model provisioned throughput for 1 hour to evaluate the performance of the model. The fine-tuned model is stored for 1 month. After evaluation, the developer uses provisioned throughput (1mo commit) to host the customized model.

Monthly cost incurred for fine-tuning = Fine tuning training ($0.00799 * 1000) + custom model storage per month ($1.95) + 1 hour of custom model inference ($23.50) = $33.44

Monthly cost incurred for provisioned throughput (a 1-month commit) of custom model = $21.18

Could you please clarify?

Thank you!

  =======================================

  To contact us again about this case, please return to the AWS Support Center using the following URL:

  https://console.aws.amazon.com/support/home#/case/?displayId=174824875600936&language=en

  (If you will connect by federation, log in before following the link.)

subject: You just made a payment
The Growth Meta: X Frees the Links
body_text:
View this post on the web at https://speedrun.substack.com/p/the-growth-meta-x-frees-the-links

Tech Week returns next year, and we’re coming to Boston!
The founder-favorite series is back next year: kicking off with Boston’s first Tech Week, May 26–31, and then heading to New York, June 1–7, before wrapping up on the West Coast with San Francisco and Los Angeles in October.
For more info, check out tech-week.com [ https://substack.com/redirect/f1c81f95-d799-4ed3-9cb1-10e54d4fe322?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
💡  This Week’s Big Ideas
🧊  Would you give up a “dream job” to chase your childhood dream? Elena Wagenmans of Taya did. This is her story. [ https://substack.com/redirect/d2a65eb5-1240-4144-9d1e-ee2f55266529?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
🔨  Tom Hammer on why founders should be spending more time recruiting [ https://substack.com/redirect/02131d3c-2d44-4fdd-9cb5-de4b30efdcca?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
💫  Bella Nazzari wants you to know about job openings at Taya [ https://substack.com/redirect/339114a2-11e8-4882-8112-085c8b0fff47?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
🤝  Macy Mills swears she’s super busy at work rn [ https://substack.com/redirect/07efe86f-99a8-4bf4-a50f-8b9532e0bb2e?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
💼  Join our talent network [ https://substack.com/redirect/112778c7-379c-46ca-a1e0-97c9f19489af?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] for more opportunities. If we see a fit for you, we'll intro you to relevant founders in the portfolio.
Marketing is hard for early stage startups. This is not 2015, where you could throw up some half baked ad on Facebook and have a 3x return on ad spend with relatively little effort. Nowadays growth is brutal, cutthroat, and constantly shifting.
To assist founders in our network with growth, we’re starting a new mini-series we call “The Growth Meta.” In these editions, we will highlight some trends in tech marketing/growth that we hope give you an edge. As always, the best compounding advantage in startups is building a  kick-ass product, but using the newest growth strategies can act as a multiplier.
Think of this advice like reading the patch notes for your favorite video game—there are always balance updates that give some new marketing weapon a temporary advantage. Here’s what our team is seeing working in the meta right now.
X has freed the links
For many years, we advised startups to not bother posting direct URL links on X. Due to algorithmic suppression, those posts never got enough eyeballs to be worth the effort. With this latest update, it seems X’s leadership team is opening a window for external links to work. Now, when a user clicks on a link, it opens up a minibrowser within the app, where users can still scroll, but on the bottom, they can like, retweet, etc.
After talking with a few founders and experimenting with it ourselves, we’ve found that just posting the link still doesn’t really lead to clicks. Instead, what’s most effective is having a short post, accompanied by a small media asset, which when combined, leads to a curiosity gap.
We’ll define all those terms in a sec, but an example is the most helpful way to show this. Take for example this post by Dan Shipper [ https://substack.com/redirect/c156697d-fcaa-4c91-a442-51e47958c466?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ], where he uses a pithy hook accompanied by an image to make people want to click.
In total this drove 401K views on X, which translated to over 11K clicks [ https://substack.com/redirect/c156697d-fcaa-4c91-a442-51e47958c466?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] on the article for at least a 2.75% click through rate. Not bad! Especially considering that this post likely took 30 seconds to put together and cost nothing.
To replicate this sort of result for yourself, you’ll want to add a couple of ingredients to your poasting:
Punchy copy: Keep your text concise to hook users without overwhelming them. Please, please, please do not use an LLM to write this for you. You can use it to iterate, but you always want to edit it to feel natural and non-performative. The only goal of this copy should be to grab users’ attention and focus it on the core value or hook. For instance, Deji Ajetomobi launched YesCheff with a brief announcement [ https://substack.com/redirect/e8b07f18-95fc-4042-92eb-037d18796183?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]. When paired with a simple video, the post garnered over 1,100 likes and 80,000 views despite a following count under 2k. 
Small media asset: Include a lightweight visual like an image or short video to complement your text. You can spend lots on high-end production, or you can just do a Loom with a product demo, the important thing is that it is authentic to your company and the customer set you are targeting. For a fun example, Raul Dronca, a design engineer, shared [ https://substack.com/redirect/d1306a57-a191-4f81-b878-76782004afb7?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]: “Built this add-to-cart animation with @motiondotdev Demo…” alongside a quick demo video, resulting in over 850 likes and nearly 200,000 views from his 1,100-follower base. It works because the video is eye-catching—and that’s pretty much it. The bar isn’t that high here here, people! Just post authentic stuff that’s fun to look at consistently, and it’ll eventually hit. 
Curiosity gap: This is the most important dimension. You have to give people a reason to click. Tease just enough information to create an informational void that users feel compelled to fill by clicking the link, often by hinting at benefits or exclusives without full reveals. Ayush Agarwal announced [ https://substack.com/redirect/bb09efe9-62cb-43a0-8bd2-c37a8a7e6490?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] Dodo Payments with a fun GIF, achieving over 250 likes and over 34,000 views from fewer than 5,000 followers. By promising SEO juice and priority features without spoiling details, it enticed startup founders to submit, turning views into traffic.
The beauty of this strategy is its accessibility. You don’t need a massive following or advertising budget to see meaningful results. As these examples show, accounts with under 5,000 followers are regularly achieving tens of thousands or even 6-figure view counts by combining these three elements effectively.
Now, a word of caution. Like all marketing channels, this window won’t stay open forever. X could change the algorithm tomorrow, or everyone could start using this tactic and flood the zone. That’s why we call this series “The Growth Meta.” These strategies work now, but the meta is always shifting. 
Got a growth tactic that’s working for you right now? Reply and let us know.
Like this post? Forward it to your team! And for more weekly dives into the world of early stage startups, subscribe to our own newsletter below.
Get the a16z speedrun newsletter weekly 👇
You are receiving this newsletter since you opted in earlier; if you would like to opt out of future newsletters, you can unsubscribe immediately.

This newsletter may link to other websites and certain information contained herein has been obtained from third-party sources. While taken from sources believed to be reliable, a16z has not independently verified such information and makes no representations about the enduring accuracy of the information or its appropriateness for a given situation.
References to any companies, securities, or digital assets are for illustrative purposes only and do not constitute an investment recommendation or offer to provide investment advisory services. Furthermore, this content is not directed at nor intended for use by any investors or prospective investors, and may not under any circumstances be relied upon when making a decision to invest in any fund managed by a16z. subject: [New] Your payments are getting extra protection
body_text:
Qonto ( https://qonto.com/en?utm_source=qonto_internal&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=header&utm_content=website&utm_position=block_header_logo )

Your payments, protected

VoP (Verification of Payee) automatically checks beneficiaries’ details, giving you confidence with every transfer

Learn about VoP ( https://support-de.qonto.com/hc/en-us/articles/39130581174545-What-is-Verification-of-Payee-and-how-does-it-protect-my-payments?utm_source=qonto_internal&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=security&utm_content=faq-en&utm_position=block_0_cta )

Learn about VoP ( https://support-de.qonto.com/hc/en-us/articles/39130581174545-What-is-Verification-of-Payee-and-how-does-it-protect-my-payments?utm_source=qonto_internal&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=security&utm_content=faq-en&utm_position=block_1_img )

Hello Meghdad,

The end-of-year period is a rush of activity for all of us. But for fraudsters and scammers, it’s absolutely peak season. A fake invoice or phishing email can be costly in both time and money, so let’s double-down on payment protection.

>From October 9, all SEPA transfers will include real‑time Verification of Payee (VoP) of beneficiaries. This extra layer of security runs quietly in the background to help prevent errors and fraud, so you can focus on growing your business.

When you're about to validate a transfer, VoP will automatically give you one of the four following instant alerts:

*

✅

Full Match: details match — you can validate your transfer.
*

⚠️

Close Match: review a suggested correction.
*

❌

No Match: stop and verify before sending.
*

❓

Verification not possible: the other bank can't perform the check right now.

You stay in control and can complete a payment regardless of the alert you receive. In the first few days after launch, you may see more "verification not possible" alerts, as banks adapt. This won't block payments - it simply means the check couldn't be performed.

⚠️ Please stay vigilant with your payments: Qonto can’t be held liable if you proceed with a transfer after being informed of a name mismatch.

🔒 Reminder: Qonto will never ask you to transfer funds or share card details with third parties. Find more tips in our blog article.

Discover VoP ( https://support-de.qonto.com/hc/en-us/articles/39130581174545-What-is-Verification-of-Payee-and-how-does-it-protect-my-payments?utm_source=qonto_internal&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=security&utm_content=faq-en&utm_position=block_1_cta )

Learn to prevent fraud ( https://qonto.com/en/blog/business-management/security/phishing-how-to-detect-and-avoid-online-fraud?utm_source=qonto_internal&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=security&utm_content=blog-article&utm_position=block_1_cta )

Tips

Make sure your clients have your exact Qonto account name so they can pay you confidently with VoP. Find it in your app from Settings > Organization details.

Help center ( https://support-de.qonto.com/hc/en-us?utm_source=qonto_internal&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=signature&utm_content=FAQ_en&utm_position=block_signature_img )

Take a look at our help center or contact us by chat from your account.

subject: B2B SaaS – Product ready - Zalando SE paid pilot secured – Seed Round kEUR 600; Calibrion.ai - AI Infrastructure / Data annotation
body_text:
Dear Thomas,

We are building Calibrion, an agent-native data annotation platform adressing one of the largest barriers to organisational GenAI adoption: enabling proprietary LLM training through high-quality, rapid annotation. 

Calibrion delivers: 
- Agentic data annotation that accelerates annotation up to 100x (from months to hours)
- Cost savings of around 90%
- Built-in proprietary quality control to ensure accuracy at scale 
- 35% faster human-in-the-loop through research driven optimized workflows

We recently signed a 6 months paid pilot with Zalando SE, secured a verbal commitment from Siemens Energy AG E-Commerce division, and are negotiating pilots with Cotopaxi (US sustainable outdoor brand) and LILT (AI-driven translation leader). 

We are seeking EUR 600,000 in seed funding to expand our team, onboard additional clients, and implement product-led-growth.

Our founder and CEO, Meghdad, holds a PhD in Computer Science with a specialization in Natural Language Processing, and has over eight years of applied AI product experience. 

Given your investment focus, we think Calibrion could be a strong fit. You’ll find our one-pager, pitch-deck, and product demos attached. We’d be glad to arrange a 30-minute call to walk you through the product and discuss and our upcoming milestones or just connect via phone (number attached).

Best regards,
Nils 


Nils Erbach

Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
Tel: +49 1 <tel:+49+(0)30+478+662+25>7662205065 ・ nils@calibrion.ai <mailto:meghdad@calibrion.ai>
Kolonnenstr. 8・10827 Berlin・Germany
www.calibrion.ai <https://www.calibrion.ai/>
￼
￼
￼

subject: Following up on Calibrion AI – Seed Round & Live Demo
body_text:
Dear team at QuantumLight Capital,

I hope you are doing well.

I wanted to follow up on my recent introduction of Calibrion AI and our EUR 3 million seed round. We’re excited by the strong early traction we’ve seen and would love to show you how our platform is helping enterprises turn their proprietary data into a strategic advantage for LLM adoption.

Given QuantumLight’s focus on backing breakthrough technologies at the intersection of AI and enterprise infrastructure, we believe Calibrion could be a compelling fit for your portfolio.

If you’re interested, I’d be glad to share our detailed pitch deck and arrange a short call or live demo to walk you through the product and discuss our next steps.

Would you have time next week for a quick conversation? I’m happy to adapt to your availability.

Looking forward to your thoughts and hoping we can connect soon.

Best regards,
Nils

--
Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 ・ nils@calibrion.ai <>
Französische Str. 20・10117 Berlin・Germany
www.calibrion.ai <http://www.calibrion.ai/>
￼￼


> On 22. Jul 2025, at 19:16, Nils Erbach <nils@calibrion.ai> wrote:
> 
> Dear team at QuantumLight Capital,
> 
> I am reaching out to introduce Calibrion AI, a data annotation platform enabling enterprises and organisations to fine-tune LLMs to their proprietary data. We built a working product and already reached pilot-stage interest from European and US companies in the eCom vertical.
> 
> While most LLM infra and dev platforms focus on model architecture and LLMOps, we focus on unlocking enterprise adoption through (proprietary) data and fine-tuning workflows.
> 
> Calibrion envisions a future where businesses harness their proprietary data and domain expertise to build specialized LLMs, turning generative AI from a threat into a strategic asset for both internal and market-facing applications.
> 
> Our mission is to become a market-leading platform for LLM customization and domain adaptation, setting the standard for performance and usability in the enterprise AI space.
> 
> To underscore the scale of this opportunity and the importance of what Calibrion does: In June 2025, Meta acquired a 49% stake in Scale AI (a company also focused on LLM data annotation and founded in 2016) for $14.8 billion at a $30 billion valuation. 
> 
> We are currently offering subscription for our EUR 3 m seed investment round to: 
> - expand our engineering and data science team
> - Accelerate go-to-market efforts across verticals 
> - Further develop our AI capabilities and integrate new LLM-driven features.
> 
> To accelerate the company's development, we assigned significant equity to an option pool for key employees and aim to be cash-flow positive within 18 months.
> 
> We would be happy to share our pitch deck and schedule a call to walk you through the live product. The product is fully developed and currently undergoing regular testing and stabilization.
> 
> Please find attached a letter outlining our fund-raising goals as well as a one-pager on Calibrion.
> 
> We look forward to hearing from you and would be glad to schedule a conversation at your convenience.
> 
> Best regards,
> Nils
> 
> -- 
> Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
> Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 <tel:+49+(0)30+478+662+25> ・ nils@calibrion.ai <mailto:meghdad@calibrion.ai>

subject: Wir aktualisieren unsere Vertragsbedingungen
body_text:
Qonto ( https://qonto.com/de?utm_source=qonto_internal&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=header&utm_content=website&utm_position=block_header_logo )

Wir aktualisieren unsere Vertragsbedingungen

Hallo Meghdad,

ab dem 19. März 2025 können Sie Überweisungen in Fremdwährungen nicht nur einzeln ausführen, sondern auch im Voraus planen oder als wiederkehrende Zahlungen einrichten – genau wie bei SEPA-Überweisungen.

Diese Erweiterung gibt Ihnen mehr Flexibilität bei internationalen Zahlungen. Sie ist automatisch in Ihrem Plan enthalten, ohne Auswirkungen auf Ihre aktuellen Konditionen. Die Gebühren bleiben unverändert und entsprechen denen für einmalige Fremdwährungsüberweisungen. Wenn Sie die Funktion nicht nutzen, entstehen Ihnen keine zusätzlichen Kosten.

Was ändert sich in den Vertragsbedingungen?

Um diese Funktion bereitzustellen, haben wir die folgenden Anpassungen an unseren Vertragsbedingungen vorgenommen:

*
Widerrufsrecht (Artikel 2.14a)

Überweisungsaufträge für planmäßige oder wiederkehrende Fremdwährungsüberweisungen können bis spätestens zum Ende des vorherigen Werktages widerrufen werden.
*
Hinweis zu Wechselkursschwankungen (Artikel 2.14c)

Der Wechselkurs kann sich zwischen Auftragserteilung und Ausführung ändern. Dadurch kann der endgültige Überweisungsbetrag abweichen.

Wie geht es weiter?

Die aktualisierten Vertragsbedingungen treten am 19. März 2025 automatisch in Kraft.

👉 Alle Einzelheiten finden Sie über den unten stehenden Link. Bitte nehmen Sie sich einen Moment Zeit, um die Änderungen zu prüfen.

Bei Fragen steht Ihnen unser Kundenservice gerne zur Verfügung.

Ihr Qonto Team

Aktualisierte Vertragsbedingungen lesen ( https://legal.qonto.com/de#template-goi-lzmgb )

Hilfe-Center ( https://support-de.qonto.com/hc/de?utm_source=qonto_internal&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=signature&utm_content=FAQ_de&utm_position=block_signature_img )

[Case 174824875600936] Customer Added Communication: Unable to find
 detailed information about Llama cost estimation
body_text:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you can confirm the sender and know the content is safe.

  Hi, 
I would like to learn more about fine-tuned Llama model cost estimation.

In particular, I would like to know if we fine-tuned a model and make it available as an API, over an unlimited period of time, in which our users will prompt the fine-tuned model. 

Will we be charged for those seconds that the model was used? Or will we be charged for the entire persion when the model was available? 

I assume since Bedrock is serverless, we must be charged for the seconds the model was used, but I have not found this explicitly in the pricing documentation.

Thank you in advance
Meghdad

AWS Region: eu-central-1

subject: Re: Foresight AI Safety Grant: Thank you for your Submission!
body_text:
Hi,

I was wondering if you have made decisions about AI Safety grants from
September deadline. We have applied under Calibrion AI.

Thanks in advance!

Best,
Meghdad Farahmand

On Tue, Sep 30, 2025 at 15:51 Foresight Institute <grants@foresight.org>
wrote:

> Dear Meghdad Farahmand,
>
> Thank you for applying to Foresight Institute's AI Safety Grant.
>
> We will evaluate your proposal when our next application deadline has
> passed. When that deadline has passed, we aim to make a decision on your
> application within 8 weeks.
>
> Please monitor this email as we will be in contact with you should we
> require additional information. Please also feel free to respond with any
> questions you may have.
>
> Thank you for taking the time to submit, and we look forward to learning
> more about you and your work.
>
> Best wishes,
>
> Amy Jehan
>
> The Foresight Institute
>

subject: Foresight AI Safety Grant: Thank you for your Submission!
body_text:
Dear Meghdad Farahmand,

Thank you for applying to Foresight Institute's AI Safety Grant.

We will evaluate your proposal when our next application deadline has
passed. When that deadline has passed, we aim to make a decision on your
application within 8 weeks.

Please monitor this email as we will be in contact with you should we
require additional information. Please also feel free to respond with any
questions you may have.

Thank you for taking the time to submit, and we look forward to learning
more about you and your work.

Best wishes,

Amy Jehan

The Foresight Institute

subject: Re: Exploring Workshop Collaboration Opportunity – Calibrion AI x BLISS Berlin
body_text:
Hello Meghdad,

I hope you’ve had a good start to the year.

I wanted to briefly follow up on your message from November regarding the
BLISS workshop. You mentioned that you would like to work with us in early
2026, so I thought now would be a good moment to reconnect.
We’d be happy to schedule a short call to discuss possibilities for 2026.
Feel free to let me know what would work best on your side.

Looking forward to hearing from you.

Best regards,
Ardian

BLISS Partners Team
Berlin Learning & Intelligent Systems Society (BLISS e.V.)
partners@bliss.berlin
https://bliss.berlin/

>
> Best regards,
> Meghdad

subject: Re: Exploring Workshop Collaboration Opportunity – Calibrion AI x BLISS Berlin
body_text:
Hi Ardian,

Thank you so much for reaching out.

November/December are much busier than I expected, but I am excited about
the BLISS workshop and looking forward to organizing it with you.
However, I don't think it's feasible in 2025.
I will get back to you in early 2026.

Best regards,
Meghdad
