subject: Re: Exploring Workshop Collaboration Opportunity – Calibrion AI x BLISS Berlin
body_text:
Hello Meghdad,

I hope you’ve been doing well and that the October deadline went smoothly.

I am Ardian, and I am also part of BLISS Partners Team. I’ve been working
closely with Nina, and I’m reaching out to follow up on the workshop idea
you discussed together.

As we are now in November, I wanted to briefly follow up on your
conversation regarding the potential workshop for BLISS. We would still be
very happy to organize this with you and align it with your schedule.

Would you have time for a short call in the next week to discuss the topic,
format, and timeline?

Best regards,
Ardian

Ardian Begisholli
BLISS Partners Team
Berlin Learning & Intelligent Systems Society (BLISS e.V.)
partners@bliss.berlin
https://bliss.berlin/

'Meghdad Farahmand' via Partners & Sponsors <partners@bliss.berlin> schrieb
am Fr., 22. Aug. 2025, 15:22:

> Dear BLISS Team,
>
> I was recently in touch with Justus regarding the BLISS AI Speaker Meetup
> Series, and he suggested I reach out to you about the possibility of
> hosting a workshop together.
>
> I’m the founder of Calibrion AI, a Berlin-based company building a
> next-generation data annotation platform tailored specifically for LLMs.
>
> I hold a PhD in Computer Science with a focus on NLP and have over 8 years
> of applied AI experience across startups and enterprise settings.I’d be
> glad to co-design a workshop with you, drawing on Calibrion’s core
> expertise and its impact on LLM adoption. Possible themes include
> fine-tuning, evaluation, data quality, and LLM-as-a-judge—always grounded
> in real-world use cases and client experiences.
>
> If this aligns with your vision at BLISS, I’d be happy to share a more
> detailed outline and adapt the format to fit your community’s interests.
>
> Looking forward to your thoughts.
>
> Best regards,
> Meghdad
> --
>
> Meghdad Farahmand PhD
>
> Founder ・ Calibrion AI
>
> Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 <+49+(0)30+478+662+25> ・ meghdad@calibrion.ai
>
> Kolonnenstr. 8・10827 Berlin・Germany
>
> www.calibrion.ai
>

I’m following up on my email from 29 Aug (it may have landed in spam). We’d
love to explore your idea and discuss how we could collaborate on a
workshop together.
Would you have 20–30 minutes next week to chat?

Best regards,
Nina

Nina Zukowska
BLISS Partners Team
Berlin Learning & Intelligent Systems Society (BLISS e.V.)
partners@bliss.berlin

subject: Re: Exploring Workshop Collaboration Opportunity – Calibrion AI x BLISS Berlin
body_text:
Dear Meghdad,

Thank you for reaching out and connecting with us. Your idea sounds
excellent, and we believe it could be a very valuable event for the BLISS
community. As we are currently expanding our workshop program, the timing
works out perfectly.

I've attached our information brochure for workshop partners, which
provides more details about the format, audience, and pricing of our
workshops.

Would you be available to meet next week to discuss the details further?
We’d be glad to explore how we could shape this workshop together.

Best regards,
Nina

Nina Zukowska
BLISS Partners Team
Berlin Learning & Intelligent Systems Society (BLISS e.V.)
partners@bliss.berlin
https://bliss.berlin/

subject: Exploring Workshop Collaboration Opportunity – Calibrion AI x BLISS Berlin
body_text:
Dear BLISS Team,

I was recently in touch with Justus regarding the BLISS AI Speaker Meetup
Series, and he suggested I reach out to you about the possibility of
hosting a workshop together.

I’m the founder of Calibrion AI, a Berlin-based company building a
next-generation data annotation platform tailored specifically for LLMs.

I hold a PhD in Computer Science with a focus on NLP and have over 8 years
of applied AI experience across startups and enterprise settings.I’d be
glad to co-design a workshop with you, drawing on Calibrion’s core
expertise and its impact on LLM adoption. Possible themes include
fine-tuning, evaluation, data quality, and LLM-as-a-judge—always grounded
in real-world use cases and client experiences.

If this aligns with your vision at BLISS, I’d be happy to share a more
detailed outline and adapt the format to fit your community’s interests.

Looking forward to your thoughts.

Best regards,
Meghdad
-- 

Meghdad Farahmand PhD

Founder ・ Calibrion AI

Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 <+49+(0)30+478+662+25> ・ meghdad@calibrion.ai

Kolonnenstr. 8・10827 Berlin・Germany

www.calibrion.ai

subject: You just made a payment
Follow-up: Calibrion AI – Seed Round & Product Demo
body_text:
Dear team at Volution Ventures,

I hope you’re doing well. I’m following up on my earlier note regarding Calibrion AI and our ongoing EUR 3M seed round.

We’re building the first data annotation platform designed natively for LLMs, aimed at solving the critical bottleneck in enterprise AI adoption: unlocking the value of proprietary data for fine‑tuned, domain‑specific models. Calibrion enables enterprises to move beyond general-purpose LLMs and achieve production‑grade performance — while delivering:

Up to 35% cost savings and weeks faster time‑to‑delivery

Superior data quality, powered by proprietary QC and automation workflows

Early traction – with pilots underway in both Europe and the US (incl. a publicly traded German retailer and a $150M‑revenue US brand)

A clear role in a $1–1.4B serviceable obtainable market by 2028 for LLM‑specific tooling

With generative AI adoption accelerating and the recent Meta–Scale AI $14.3B deal underscoring how critical this data layer is, we see significant momentum ahead.

Given Volution Ventures’ strong track record of backing early‑stage deep tech startups, we believe Calibrion aligns closely with your investment thesis. I’d love to share our deck and arrange a short product walkthrough. Would you have 20–30 minutes next week to connect? I’ll gladly adapt to your schedule.

Looking forward to your thoughts.

Best regards,
Nils

--
Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 ・ nils@calibrion.ai <mailto:nils@calibrion.ai>
Französische Str. 20・10117 Berlin・Germany
www.calibrion.ai <http://www.calibrion.ai/>
￼
￼￼

> On 1. Aug 2025, at 09:59, Nils Erbach <nils@calibrion.ai> wrote:
> 
> Dear team at Volution Ventures,
> 
> I hope you’re doing well.
> 
> I wanted to briefly follow up on my previous message regarding Calibrion AI and our EUR 3 million seed round. We’ve continued to progress since my last email, with strong pilot interest from both European and US companies confirming our approach to enabling enterprises to unlock the true value of their proprietary data with LLMs.
> 
> Given Volution Ventures’ track record of supporting early-stage deep tech startups, I believe our vision for a market-leading LLM customization platform aligns well with your investment focus.
> 
> I’d love to share our pitch deck and arrange a short call to walk you through the product, our traction so far, and how we plan to scale this opportunity.
> 
> Could we find a 20–30 minute slot next week to connect? I’d be glad to adapt to your schedule.
> 
> Looking forward to your feedback.
> 
> Best regards,
> Nils
> 
>

subject: B2B SaaS – AI Infrastructure / Data - Early Stage Product Ready - Early Enterprise Traction – Seed Round - Calibrion AI
body_text:
Hi Cecilie

We are building Calibrion, an agent-native data annotation platform that
removes one of the largest barriers to building AI models at the scale and
speed required today: creating high-quality training datasets.

Current data annotation market dynamics enable rapid revenue growth (€100M
- 1B ARR reported in 2024/2025 within 12–24 months) for data annotation
platforms with strong technology and product–market fit, underscoring the
current demand for high-quality AI data.

Calibrion positions itself as the first human-agent-native solution,
approaching the problem with a new perspective and value proposition,
delivering:
- Agentic data annotation that accelerates annotation up to ~100x (from
months to hours)
- Radical cost savings
- Proprietary three-layer quality control to ensure accuracy at scale
- 35% faster human-in-the-loop through research driven optimized workflows

Our moat compounds across:
- Years of world-class AI, LLM, and agentic expertise
- Patentable processes
- Early distribution into some of the world’s leading enterprises.
- The inherent complexity of the problem we solve
- A fast, high-performing team

We recently signed paid pilots with Zalando, Siemens Energy, and are
negotiating additional deals with Cotopaxi (US sustainable outdoor brand)
and LILT (US AI-driven enterprise translation leader).

We are seeking EUR 1,200,000 in seed funding to expand our team, onboard
additional clients, and implement product-led-growth within the next 9 - 12
months.

As Calibrion founder and CEO, I hold a PhD in CS with a specialization in
NLP, and have over nine years of applied AI product experience.

Given your investment focus, we believe Calibrion could be a strong fit.
I’ve attached our one-pager and pitch deck, and included a link to our
product demos here
<https://docs.google.com/document/d/1bMC1AW6Wu-eTxOIhEkro05LAr8QYiMcfU7WgXCZe4vQ/edit?usp=sharing>
.

We’d be happy to walk you through the product and share what we’re building
toward.

Best,
Meghdad
-- 

Meghdad Farahmand PhD

Founder ・ Calibrion AI

Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 <+49+(0)30+478+662+25> ・ meghdad@calibrion.ai

Kolonnenstr. 8・10827 Berlin・Germany

www.calibrion.ai

body_text:
JUNE 9–12, 2025

SAN FRANCISCO + VIRTUAL

[Dark teal design with overlapping circles and angled lines.]

Hi Meghdad,

Thank you again for joining the Data + AI Summit Virtual Experience! Here are a few final reminders to ensure you get the most from the event.

Complete the survey
Your feedback helps us improve next year’s Data + AI Summit — if you haven’t already, please take a minute to complete the [survey](https://dataaisummit.databricks.com/flow/db/dais2025/surveys/login?scid=701Vp00000DUbu0IAD&utm_source=databricks&utm_medium=email).

Starting today, you can stream content on demand. Visit the [Sessions page](https://www.databricks.com/dataaisummit/agenda?scid=701Vp00000DUbu0IAD&utm_source=databricks&utm_medium=email#session-previews), log in and click on Session Details to access:

- Keynotes by data and AI visionaries, including Jamie Dimon / Chairman and CEO, JPMorgan Chase; Dario Amodei / Co-founder and CEO, Anthropic; Ali Ghodsi / Co-founder and CEO, Databricks, and more.
- Hundreds of sessions and technical deep dives covering the latest advances in leading open source projects and industry technologies like Delta Lake, Apache Iceberg™ and more. You can also hear from Databricks customers such as Mastercard, Meta, T-Mobile, 7-Eleven, and plenty of others.

Save the date
Save the date for next year’s Data + AI Summit, June 15–18, 2026, in San Francisco. And come see us in person at the [Data + AI World Tour](https://www.databricks.com/dataaisummit/worldtour?scid=701Vp00000DUbu0IAD&utm_source=databricks&utm_medium=email), starting this August, with 19+ stops across the globe.

Data + AI Summit Team


160 Spear Street 13th floor, San Francisco CA 94105

© Databricks 2025. All rights reserved.

subject: Willkommen zu unserem ersten Newsletter!
body_text:
Lockout_CBT-1 (https://dkcS3h04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/OV+113/dkcS3h04/VWkk-P5cW4N6W4GX7LL1pj0fCW7RdXLG5GhSD1N6TZ7sb3lcq-W6N1vHY6lZ3q2W9ddbJl7vyj0XW3VkRpg147rTpW55lBmF5h0617W6SZDv-6tkvgXW7P1Y9l8R_WZFW8P28Lp569Gf_W1Z6ZtM15cMB4W197h0n23tSp0N2cKR8THvsZHW8BKDXl6PwjSCN4SS4mdzYrKFMT77J4bZGnFW2xNFx54SfhjFW1G2VBK56KcF2W5w8n794H6xmkW4jlxcM85GT5TW64j2xG72mmx3W5tZ44j553cX6N2hC3zGQl-KyW6Y7-JB66Y93qW3bWbSK2zQhXlN16ydbdm7VQFf6VPD-404 )

Hallo Meghdad,

ab sofort möchten wir dich einmal im Monat über Neuigkeiten rund um das Incubator Village Beeskow informieren.

Hier erfährst du alles Wissenswerte rund um unser Start-up-Zentrum: Wir stellen dir unsere Unternehmen, Partner und unser Team vor und zeigen dir die aktuellen Entwicklungen und Highlights unserer Community.

In unserer ersten Ausgabe erhältst du Einblicke in eines unserer Start-ups und erfährst mehr über unser Accelerator-Programm. Außerdem teilen wir praktische Tipps für Gründer:innen und schauen auf die feierliche Eröffnung des Incubator Village zurück.

Viel Spaß beim Lesen!

Im Startup-Spotlight: Entenkoje Campingboote

Fotos: Entenkoje

Wir stellen euch regelmäßig unsere Start-ups vor. Den Anfang macht ein Unternehmen aus dem Tourismusbereich: Entenkoje. Gründer Jonathan Schöppe bietet ein einzigartiges Campingboot-Erlebnis in der malerischen Märkischen Schweiz an.

Das Konzept verbindet die Freiheit des Campens mit dem Naturerlebnis auf dem Wasser und schafft so ein nachhaltiges Mini-Retreat für Gäste. Jonathan meldete sich frühzeitig im Incubator Village Beeskow an. Er sah, wie gut unser Projekt angenommen wird, und die konkreten Möglichkeiten, um sein junges Unternehmen schnell voranzubringen:

„Was ich hier im Village besonders schätze, ist die zielgerichtete Starthilfe und das starke Netzwerk. Mit der Unterstützung des Teams vor Ort kann ich die Gründungsphase viel schneller und sicherer durchlaufen. Das ist der Turbo, den mein Start-up braucht.“

Mehr erfahren
(https://dkcS3h04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/OV+113/dkcS3h04/VWkk-P5cW4N6W4GX7LL1pj0fCW7RdXLG5GhSD1N6TZ7rW3lcq-W69sMD-6lZ3ljN2DMfVj2JYxfVWBG8d16sNfgW8XgHBN6BbnLtW69Y7NR25QXMVW7xhlgD387Zq5W1yQ9t410l-6LW3ls0t11dWVdnW7H-Vvn2KFh3jW3mkmph7nc6knW4RvN4M204lCjW1nfHSf76hm3_W3KHmKt6WHC9JW71ck3g6wXBb-N8NVnmNWLyf5N4PLgvcrjT4FVrw9sk9kGYbyW7k10x28bNqrfW6VZ7DN4zThv-W25jFJv1WF6LyW2ndQWx6gWckhf6mQRX204 )

Bewirb dich als Gründer:in

Zweimal jährlich bieten wir 20 Start-ups einen begehrten Platz in unserer einzigartigen Gründer-Community. Besonders internationale Gründer:innen sowie Remote-Teams finden hier ideale Bedingungen vor, abseits des Großstadthypes, aber mit globaler Perspektive. Euch erwarten: Sechs Monate intensives Mentoring und Coaching - von Unternehmer:innen für Unternehmer:innen, Zugang zu Start-up Investor:innen und Business Angels sowie ein vielfältiges und internationales Netzwerk.

Sichere dir deinen Platz: Die Bewerbungsfrist für das erste Semester 2026 des Incubator Village Beeskow endet am 15.12.! Du kannst dich aber auch schon für die darauf folgenden Semester bewerben.

Jetzt kostenlos anmelden
(https://dkcS3h04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/OV+113/dkcS3h04/VWkk-P5cW4N6W4GX7LL1pj0fCW7RdXLG5GhSD1N6TZ7sb3lcq-W6N1vHY6lZ3q2W9ddbJl7vyj0XW3VkRpg147rTpW55lBmF5h0617W6SZDv-6tkvgXW7P1Y9l8R_WZFW8P28Lp569Gf_W1Z6ZtM15cMB4W197h0n23tSp0N2cKR8THvsZHW8BKDXl6PwjSCN4SS4mdzYrKFMT77J4bZGnFW2xNFx54SfhjFW1G2VBK56KcF2W5w8n794H6xmkW4jlxcM85GT5TW64j2xG72mmx3W5tZ44j553cX6N2hC3zGQl-KyW6Y7-JB66Y93qW3bWbSK2zQhXlN16ydbdm7VQFf6VPD-404 )

Praxiswissen und Gratisvorlage für dein Business

Gute Ideen sind der Anfang. Jetzt geht es ans Umsetzen: Auf unserem Blog findest du jede Menge Inspiration und praktische Tipps für Gründer:innen.

Zum Blog
(https://dkcS3h04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/OV+113/dkcS3h04/VWkk-P5cW4N6W4GX7LL1pj0fCW7RdXLG5GhSD1N6TZ7sb3lcq-W6N1vHY6lZ3p_W8D3lQc6wvs3HW6mrxKk5kb_FSVZDWSy8yZnYHW7G6RPm4tH2GbW8NQcS17594z5W5DpvpC3Sx4ZgW8D32TL29NHx2W1bl4yG1YyPzrW7wSk1L6fJDQTW1lKWc96l5mBPW8h8lvb5XShgCW2tHlc65BBkm4W41BCTf769jrKW55KYN96mMXqPW34Cx4H5r5_q2W4NLJwD61zhPGW28nQWs6HC3P7V7HtlG1sbRNmW75tjND985NxkW1sl-zP5Y0M9VW24Z0m4874v14N2gzXC0mzpb6f18sZ4x04 )

Damit du sofort loslegen kannst, bekommst du von uns ein Business Model Canvas: die moderne Ergänzung zum klassischen Businessplan. Mit diesem übersichtlichen Template kannst du dein Geschäftsmodell klar strukturieren.

Hier gratis herunterladen
(https://dkcS3h04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/OV+113/dkcS3h04/VWkk-P5cW4N6W4GX7LL1pj0fCW7RdXLG5GhSD1N6TZ7t43pyd0W8wLKSR6lZ3kDW68TkPP8ljgt6W50xXRp1WsDwzN2hhY_ZZtS45N5rrRtv7JxKRW14PDk43mnG0WN55rcSQhb76DN1BZ4gtk2PwZW150zm077g13nW2WbSJL724HcyW8cpcHm6gyR6cW8LR5hy6RW_jNN5X38j3DgbBlW5S_nPk4bh-PpW17SlGl1qlDhvN4NkL0zcbRYrW5b68dY2n6LJ0W1_RV0g6xnV76W43tQbL2Lrsv9W8K_vyz6XF2tLW7fRP7J8R96tXW3Xt_8_22453FW6f_JH22NbYjMW8R9dNV2dLCVJVT3czQ6rhv1GW7_T9V74z0CVZW6QRpW046XF2RN5Mqtx_4vqdrW32pk8d701Fpkf56yGnH04 )

Rückblick auf unsere Eröffnung

In der ersten Reihe sitzen (von links): Landrat Frank Steffen, Bürgermeister Robert Czaplinski, Wirtschaftsminister Daniel Keller, ILB-Vorständin Kerstin Jöntgen.

Die Eröffnung des Incubator Village Beeskow (https://dkcS3h04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/OV+113/dkcS3h04/VWkk-P5cW4N6W4GX7LL1pj0fCW7RdXLG5GhSD1N6TZ7tn3lcq-W95jsWP6lZ3nZN2gkJ1fYgP6pW4vRsjL7w-2_BW1J2-JR545NxFN4T8gY33PrCPW7ZnTGz2Qjy0ZW1Pcv172jJ1YfW7j8C415gchxNW7tL7NK44X6ldVKJ_wF8pPrT2W54D6_C7yHvD3VBsRHm1BcyBJW4W7G6d2mvCYDW87wR5L64LsJcN5rDZHGNcWZjW26-vtB4f0yTjW32WVVW8xM8XvVPj1wK11m_BSV9DYpF8pBDKyW7Tl_Zn1M33yhW1XLKlw7Xh0tSW8V_Ggn6r0NGPW59P1SN1C8wmkW8fK0cz7tRHHdVGhM_V7Pv0J2W6nHQj-83Rzv-W6Nybqj7hv8ldW745W6b27_J5kW8PCc4r8THLBsN72qj3XkDQq2W4pGbQY2z4Khwf2tCw5q04 ) im Oktober war ein voller Erfolg. Lockout_CBT-1 (https://dkcS3h04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/OV+113/dkcS3h04/VW_29k1rPndQN2V5rfQhVDdzVSnzBh5H8VZGN31NNBM3lcq-W6N1vHY6lZ3kZVDLg_r3Mj8byW4tNYjP3Fq8r1W1-lLl01d8VHwW7LRGLZ4JXXdFW1RNZPT62F9kgW4fKGpv3GGwQjW8klhvb1K2KRgVXPkPl5TLckLW4mD-Pp8Qtp3zW2kNg4G5kwKc5W5Dg4DB8P7zcwW2yT2W55LG9yVW7WQWq94F4n-RN6r40__ld4VQVmzyvC5-PhdyV_f28J5-DKyZW4mzb4y4BGrxpN8Lk2h5t_8srW2fj8KN6JdqzJVxcFf242hB90W1XZz1B2yzgDlW9l0Sr44M_cScf7t12V604 )

Hallo Meghdad,

die ersten Kick-off-Meetings waren ein voller Erfolg! Die Gründer:innen zeigten sich begeistert von den individuellen 1:1-Onboarding-Sessions – drei intensive Stunden voller Coaching, Orientierung und klarer Impulse für ihren Start. Schon bald beginnt unser Accelerator-Programm, und wir freuen uns darauf, die Start-ups mit gezieltem Mentoring und wertvollen Kontakten voranzubringen. In dieser Ausgabe stellen wir dir den Leiter unserer Gründerprogramme vor und geben Einblicke in eines unserer Start-ups. Außerdem werfen wir einen Blick auf unser großes Januar-Event und teilen praxisnahes Wissen für Gründer:innen.

Wir wünschen von Herzen frohe Weihnachten und schöne Feiertage. Auf ein gesundes Wiedersehen im neuen Jahr!

Termin-Highlight: Erstes Retreat-Wochenende in Beeskow

Vom 23. bis 25. Januar 2026 veranstalten wir unser erstes Retreat! Die Gründer:innen erwartet ein abwechslungsreiches Programm mit praxisnahen Workshops und interaktiven Formaten. Expert:innen wie Tim Jaudszims, der selbst mehrere erfolgreiche Start-ups gegründet hat, sind vor Ort, um ihre Erfahrungen zu teilen, Impulse zu geben und auf individuelle Fragen einzugehen. Gemeinsame Aktivitäten, Sessions und Abendveranstaltungen schaffen eine vertrauensvolle Atmosphäre, in der nachhaltige Beziehungen und Kooperationen entstehen können. Die Teilnehmenden haben die Chance, wertvolle Kontakte zu knüpfen, sich auszutauschen und ein starkes Netzwerk aufzubauen – ein wichtiger Grundstein für den langfristigen Erfolg ihrer Start-ups.

Im Start-up-Spotlight: ZUKUNFT-WALD-LOS

Teil des Teams "ZUKUNFT-WALD-LOS": Waldarbeiter Max Gräf, Försterin Monique Müller und Axel Becker, Leiter des Forstamtes Oder-Spree (Bild: ZUKUNFT-WALD-LOS)

Unsere Wälder brauchen Schutz und Pflege - gerade angesichts der Herausforderungen durch den Klimawandel. Heute möchten wir euch eines unserer Startups vorstellen, das sich diesem wichtigen Thema widmet: ZUKUNFT-WALD-LOS. Die Initiative von Försterin Monique Müller und ihrem Team unterstützt private Waldbesitzer:innen im Landkreis Oder-Spree beim Umbau ihrer Wälder und vernetzt sie mit passenden Partnern. Das Projekt wird aktuell noch ehrenamtlich betrieben. Mit unserer Unterstützung möchte das Team noch mehr Aufmerksamkeit, finanzielle Mittel und Personal gewinnen, um seine bedeutsame Arbeit auszubauen.

„Die Zusammenarbeit ist für uns ein echter Gewinn! Das Team hier in Beeskow hilft uns dabei, die Initiative sichtbar zu machen und unsere Reichweite zu erhöhen.“

— Monique Müller, Projektleitung ZUKUNFT-WALD-LOS

Mehr erfahren
(https://dkcS3h04.eu1.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/OV+113/dkcS3h04/VW_29k1rPndQN2V5rfQhVDdzVSnzBh5H8VZGN31NNBs3lcq-W69sMD-6lZ3nBW3GMv4h6yd4XgV2-f592k1g7NW7Kp3y81lmNyHVJ9W_N5vjdYTW85hRbJ5BXtvnW48CPWv1mpBT4W1qJMhq6z48KSW6t9WWF92qr0nLtSWysfTDTW2214Cx1PhC2jW3vbcbS7BLsMVW3rzPB-2dXVF3W64gj9c98LhFMW6Vpr7P8Vr9TPW1pVdzt8l2M8dW44nP_87TT-zMW3Jw_wb84dNbGMxYq9y_dLZrW95sb8l4Dy5F1W8r80q99d9q2Wf912p0R04 )

Meet the Team: Juan Manuel Pons

Juan ist der strategische Kopf hinter unseren Gründerprogrammen. Er verantwortet als Head of Accelerator Programs die Betreuung der Community, die Gestaltung der Lernangebote und die strategische Weiterentwicklung unseres Incubator Village. Mit seiner Kombination aus akademischer Erfahrung, internationaler Projektarbeit und Start-up Know-how bringt er ideale Voraussetzungen mit, um unser Gründerzentrum auszubauen und nachhaltig zu stärken.

„Nach den ersten erfolgreichen Kick-off-Sessions können wir es kaum erwarten, mit den Teams in das Accelerator-Programm zu starten. Packen wir es gemeinsam an und nutzen wir die einzigartigen Chancen, die Beeskow bietet!“

Praxiswissen: Die 10 größten Mythen über Businesspläne

„Businesspläne sind überbewertet!“ Manche halten Businesspläne für altmodisch, bürokratisch oder einfach unnötig. Doch genau diese Denkweise verhindert Wachstum, Fokus und langfristigen Erfolg. Welche die 10 größten Mythen über Businesspläne sind und wie du sie erfolgreich für dich nutzen kannst, erfährst du jetzt.

subject: Andreas' Questions
body_text:
1.) Technical Differentiation: 

- What is your core technical moat? - Agent native 
- How are your handling the inevitable evolution of foundation  models?
- That’s your data advantage? - Human error? 
- Can you show me your code architecture? 

2.) Market reality and Customer Validation: 

- Let’s talk about your Enterprise pilots in detail: What are the actual pilot terms? How much are they paying? what specific KPIs deine success? When do pilots convert to full contracts? 
- What are you actually charging today? - Current MRR? How many paying customers? Bridge between projection and reality?
- How does your pricing actually work in practice? - Have customers actually accepted the pricing? Typical contract value? How does this compare to Snorkel / Scale AI? 
- What is your true ICP? Specific definition since you mentioned companies across AI Teams / E-commerce brands? Who has budget and urgency to buy now? 

3.) Competitive Landscape & Defensability?

- Why won’t Scale AI and Snorkel AI just add agentic abilities? They are well funded and placed in the market? Could do it within 6-12 months
- What happens when your competitors realize agents are the future? - Sustainable moats beyond being early? 
- How do you compete in Enterprise sales cycles? - How do you break into established contracts by incumbents?

4.) Business Model & Unit Economics: 

- Walk me through the unit economics
- How does your 12-month profitability projection account for CAC? 
- What is your churn assumption? 
- Explain your usage-based revenue scaling

5.) Team and execution: 

- Why is Meghdad the right founder? 
- Who else is in the founding team? 
- What’s your plan for hiring key roles? 
- Have you personally experienced the pain point as buyer or seller?

6.) Go-to-Market Strategy: 

- Why is PLG Realistin for Enterprise annotation? 
- That’s your customer acquisition strategy for the next 12 months? 
- How do you Bridge from pilots to revenue? 

7.) Market timing and risks: 

- Is the market timing actually perfect, or are we early? 
- How do you think about AI model commoditization risk? 
- What's your regularoy risk? Eypecially in Europe? - EU AI Act / GDPR compliance

8.) Funding and Capital efficiency: 

- Why EUR 600K and why now? - Low number for AI infrastructure; How did you arrive there? What milestones will this capital achieve? Why not raise more to accelerate growth? 
- What’s your capital efficiency plan? How do you want to reach profitability within 12 months while schling meaningful ARR? What are your assumptions about burn rate? 
- What is your next round Strategy? - When Series A/B? Have you talked to potential investors? Assumptions about burn rate? 
- How do you compete for talent in the expensive AI hiring market? Limited capital vs. world-class engineers

9.) Reality Check Questions: 

- What is the most likely reason Calibrion fails? - Competitive response; execution; market timing; technical challenges? 
- What are you not telling me? - Whats your biggest concern keeping you up at night? 
- If I check references with your pilot customers, what will they say? Real Status of the relationship? 
- Evidence for the 100x improvement? - Extraordinary Claims require extraordinary evidence … Live demonstration? Actual real data from bevore and after?

On September 9, 2025, Trusted Advisor will remove ten (10) Trusted Advisor checks. Newer, more accurate checks are currently available that replace these checks.

The following is the list of legacy Trusted Advisor checks to be removed on September 9, 2025:

Legacy Trusted Advisor Check Name and (Check ID)

8. Amazon Redshift Reserved Node Optimization: (Check ID- 1qw23er45t)
9. Amazon ElastiCache Reserved Node Optimization: (Check ID - h3L1otH3re)
10. Amazon OpenSearch Service Reserved Instance Optimization: (Check ID - 7ujm6yhn5t)

These legacy checks are replaced by the following newer checks:

Check Name and (Check ID)

1. Amazon EC2 Cost Optimization recommendations for instances: (Check ID - c1z7kmr00n)
2. Amazon EBS cost optimization recommendations for volumes: (Check ID - c1z7kmr02n)
3. Amazon RDS cost optimization recommendations for DB instances: (Check ID - c1z7kmr03n)
4. AWS Lambda cost optimization recommendations for functions: (Check ID - c1z7kmr05n)
5. AWS Savings Plan purchase recommendations for compute: (Check ID - c1z7kmr09n)
6. Amazon RDS Reserved Instance purchase recommendations: (Check ID - c1z7kmr11n)

We recommend Trusted Advisor customers start utilizing the newer checks and recommendations as soon as possible, because they offer our customers better and more contextual recommendations on cost optimization opportunities in their AWS account.

What Do You Need To Do To Receive Recommendations from the Newer Checks?

In order to receive the full set of recommendations from these newer Trusted Advisor checks, you will need to:

Opt In to Cost Optimization Hub [1].
Opt In to Compute Optimizer [2].

Opting in to Cost Optimization Hub (COH) provides some cost savings recommendations, but full access to all recommendation types requires opting in to both COH and Compute Optimizer. While COH alone offers savings plans and Reserved Instance recommendations, idle and rightsizing recommendations are only available through Compute Optimizer, which analyzes resource usage to provide these additional insights.

If you have already opted in to Cost Optimization Hub, you are already receiving recommendations for these new checks. If you have not opted in to Cost Optimization Hub, it will take 24-48 hours for the new recommendations to arrive in your AWS account. 

For more information, refer to AWS documentation on Cost Optimization Hub and Compute Optimizer [1][2].

For additional information on the legacy and replacement Trusted Advisor checks, refer to AWS documentation on Trusted Advisor checks [3].

subject: Your transfer to Arsham Mikaeili was sent
B2B SaaS – Product ready - Zalando SE paid pilot secured – Seed Round EUR 600K; Calibrion.ai - AI Infrastructure / Data annotation
body_text:
Dear Team at Target Partners,

We are building Calibrion, an agent-native data annotation platform adressing one of the largest barriers to organisational GenAI adoption: enabling proprietary LLM training through high-quality, rapid annotation. 

Calibrion delivers: 
- Agentic data annotation that accelerates annotation up to 100x (from months to hours)
- Cost savings of around 90%
- Built-in proprietary quality control to ensure accuracy at scale 
- 35% faster human-in-the-loop through research driven optimized workflows

We recently signed a 6 months paid pilot with Zalando SE, secured a verbal commitment from Siemens Energy AG E-Commerce division, and are negotiating pilots with Cotopaxi (US sustainable outdoor brand) and LILT (AI-driven translation leader). 

We are seeking EUR 600,000 in seed funding to expand our team, onboard additional clients, and implement product-led-growth.

Our founder and CEO, Meghdad, Holds a PhD in Computer Science with a specialization in Natural Language Processing, and has over eight years of applied AI product experience. 

Given your investment focus, we think Calibrion could be a strong fit. You’ll find our one-pager, pitch-deck, and product demos attached. We’d be glad to arrange a 30-minute call to walk you through the product and discuss and our upcoming milestones.

Best regards,
Nils 


Nils Erbach

Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
Tel: +49 1 <tel:+49+(0)30+478+662+25>7662205065 ・ nils@calibrion.ai <mailto:meghdad@calibrion.ai>
Kolonnenstr. 8・10827 Berlin・Germany
www.calibrion.ai <https://www.calibrion.ai/>
￼
￼
￼

subject: Hiring for: Director of AI & Automation
body_text:
Hi Meghdad,

Hope you are well.

I wanted to reach out to discuss a potentially interesting opportunity with
you for a Director of AI & Automation.

My client is an iconic marketplace redefining how consumers discover and
engage with local experiences, and they are jumping into a new chapter.
Through a recent private equity investment and a clear transformation
roadmap, they're rebuilding the platform from the ground up.

They are looking for a hands-on AI leader who can bridge vision with
execution, driving the design, build, and scaling of real-world AI
applications that boost customer and merchant engagement while embedding
automation throughout the business.

You’d own delivery of AI-powered solutions end-to-end: from NLP and
automated recommendations to workflow automation and agent orchestration,
partnering closely with Product, Engineering, and Data teams to turn ideas
into shipped solutions. It’s a high-visibility, high-impact position with
the freedom to move at scale-up speed.

Based on your experience and expertise, I believe this role might be a

How to Pitch Your Startup In Under 2 Minutes
body_text:
View this post on the web at https://speedrun.substack.com/p/how-to-pitch-in-under-2-min

The SF/LA Tech Week calendar just went live with 1,500+ events, more than double from last year! It’s a packed lineup built for founders with investor events, GTM deep dives, founder salons, and more. Explore it all here: tech-week.com/calendar [ https://substack.com/redirect/439e3a86-7bc1-494d-a335-f0d909fc542d?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
💡  This Week’s Big Ideas
🧊  Remember, applications for SR006 are open now through September 28th! Secure up to $1M for your startup by applying today [ https://substack.com/redirect/8ca140bd-3985-456d-93a0-926aedd3597a?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ].
🔨  For founders based outside of the US, getting here for speedrun can feel like such a daunting process. In this video, Tom Hammer explains the Global Founders Program [ https://substack.com/redirect/b0ae78fd-e5d8-44f0-9a2b-b4cb2d8caff3?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ], our solution for helping international founders make the move. 
⚡  This Friday evening Robin Guo and Ryan Rigney are hosting a live Q&A Office Hours with Super.com’s Henry Shi. Sign up to join the call [ https://substack.com/redirect/e78d10fa-96d9-4b18-8849-82c3ab77aa23?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] and ask Henry your questions about the lessons he learned building a $200M/year business.
💼  Join our talent network [ https://substack.com/redirect/116be063-2efa-4dbe-ba12-a565f8745727?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] for more opportunities. If we see a fit for you, we'll intro you to relevant founders in the portfolio.
At its core, speedrun is a program designed to help early stage founders reach their next stage: nabbing that lighthouse customer, hiring the right team, raising the next round. And one of the big, milestone moments in the 12-week program comes at the halfway mark. We call it Progress Day.
On Progress Day, each founder gets just two minutes on stage to pitch their startup to a room full of their peers. After each pitch, our team of investors call out feedback from the front row—loudly enough for every other founder watching to take notes and apply lessons to their own slides. 
To get a feel for what this is like, we’re sharing footage of the Progress Day pitch from Fearn AI [ https://substack.com/redirect/2bb47ff9-c816-40d5-b8a4-153409bf71fd?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] co-founder and CEO Han Kim [ https://substack.com/redirect/4fcd8d5d-dbf2-4353-bbd4-83c203e1d404?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ], who earned high marks from the investors with his pitch for an AI-powered assistant for drafting intellectual property. This is the kind of pitch that could easily bore an audience to tears, but Kim won over the crowd with his cogent breakdown of the problem space, a brisk summary of his team’s technical edge, and a touch of humor. Watch Kim’s pitch in full below: 
Progress Day Pitch - Han Kim (Fearn AI)
Kim says that he and his co-founder, Angela Gao [ https://substack.com/redirect/1632b6e5-336d-4d53-b28a-12abf04f6b17?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ], first watched Demo Day pitches from previous speedrun cohorts, then consulted advice from the investing team. Then they began to piece together a narrative. 
“For us, it felt straightforward,” Kim says. “We started Fearn because of the problems I experienced in my past job, and we built our team and our tech to address that problem. In terms of practice: It’s a short pitch, so we figured we couldn’t freeform it. We wrote out a script. We then practiced it several times the day before, until it felt approximately memorized.”
The night before Progress Day, Kim says, he and Gao got together with a group of other speedrun founders to practice and give each other feedback as well. “One of the best parts of the speedrun community is being surrounded by other founders you trust and admire,” he says. “We felt that made the biggest impact.”
We asked Kim: Why do you think your pitch was so well-received?
His response:
In general, we did our best to convey a personalized story. So a heuristic might be: if someone else pitched our pitch, would it make any sense at all? Ideally, the answer should be no. So our thinking there was to try and emphasize things that were the natural results of our strong founder-market fit. For example, we talk about us building our own hyper-compact foundation models (something that only people like ourselves can do, from our time as Caltech PhD students), and we explain why we built our own foundation models for our industry (an insight that can be recognized only after working as a Big Law patent prosecutor with a strong technical background). We wanted every statement in our pitch to feel justified by our own talents and experiences.
—Han Kim, co-founder and CEO of Fearn AI
In all, over 60 startups in the class of SR005 presented on-stage at Progress Day. Each went through a grueling effort to prepare their own presentations. We wondered: what advice might they have for other early stage founders?
So we asked a handful of founders with particularly well-received pitches three questions: how they prepped for their presentation, what advice they’d give to other founders, and what their goals are as they enter the final weeks of the program and look toward Demo Day.
Question 1: How did you prep for your Progress Day pitch?
>From Kevin Jiang [ https://substack.com/redirect/b67a0e1c-cea1-4053-b438-d5bcab447c67?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ], co-founder and CEO of Azimov [ https://substack.com/redirect/9dbbd3b2-4187-48f3-8f70-ea73ac973c8c?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]:
I consumed all prior Demo Day pitches and marked down the ones that stood out to me. I broke down the structure of those pitches. Based on the learnings there, I wrote a script for my pitch. I sent the pitch to previous speedrunners and got feedback. I refined my script. Then I created a wireframe of the pitch and imagined myself speaking as I went through the wireframe. I imagined the slides that would capture people’s attention and left out the slides where I couldn’t imagine people being captivated. I then turned the wireframe into a proper deck.
I watched Ted talks or podcasts where someone was speaking in a way that captivated me. I broke down the way they speak, their facial and body gestures. I then imagined myself almost embodying the way they speak and move.
I filmed myself doing the pitch (this is something I do for all pitches) until I nailed the tempo, content, pronunciations, and my body gestures.
>From Alexis Sursock [ https://substack.com/redirect/5e179f28-4398-4fa4-a42d-481d56e06c34?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ], co-founder and CEO of Luvu [ https://substack.com/redirect/3d3d8648-fe88-49b5-be4d-8e1d31e2ed47?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]:
Fake-presenting in front of our bathroom mirror 50 times.
>From Philipp Wehn [ https://substack.com/redirect/c58dab21-6b76-4f44-ac97-2f767ab158bd?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ], co-founder and CEO of Nexxa.ai [ https://substack.com/redirect/da53312e-98bb-430f-98a1-21c35ec2e94d?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]:
We really wanted to share why this matters front and center. Second, we focused on showing why we are winning and will dominate this market. It all started old school with pen and paper—yep, real paper! After the story was done we designed the deck and started pitch practice
Progress Day Pitch - Taylor Offer (Atrios)
>From Taylor Offer [ https://substack.com/redirect/d41df952-aecc-4763-8b8c-f6af375c3737?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ], founder and CEO of Atrios [ https://substack.com/redirect/6be2ee78-9c06-44e4-a8cb-84e050a8f735?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] (see full pitch above):
Whenever I’m communicating to anyone, whether it’s chatting to a friend, writing a social media post or giving a pitch on stage, I tend to think in three parts.
1. Hook (You need someone to be interested enough to listen)
2. Meat (This is where you deliver the info, must match the hook)
3. Payoff (What can they walk away with).
>From Maxwell Wang [ https://substack.com/redirect/c2196318-f728-464b-8cad-5290e7074ea3?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ], co-founder of Dispatcher:
We ideated on the content for a long time but only put the deck together the week before. Nick Clouse [ https://substack.com/redirect/7adb84fe-b22b-43a1-bc50-293552eb81c4?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] helped me practice delivery and condense the first draft into something more succinct. Then just read the speech over and over again probably 100 times, while sending recorded attempts to Troy and Nick for review.
Question 2: What advice would you offer to other early-stage founders putting together their own 2-min pitch?
>From the co-founders of Anchr [ https://substack.com/redirect/49f2abd2-df6b-43ff-b689-792856e44007?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] (currently in stealth):
Boil it down to four one-sentence pillars: the problem, the solution, the founders, and the traction. String them together, check the timing, and recognize that everything else is just execution.
>From Philipp Wehn, co-founder and CEO of Nexxa.ai:
Less is more. Instead of thinking how much can I share in two minutes, the approach for Demo Day will be: what is the absolute best thing about us that has to be shared within one minute. That approach will 10x the quality of the pitch. Ask yourself: what are the 1-3 things that spike interest in this company?
>From the co-founders of Dispatcher (currently in stealth):
The best advice that people gave during Progress Day, and that I agree with, is that people will only take away one or two things from your presentation. So focus your presentation on those ideas.
>From Lior Strugach [ https://substack.com/redirect/45c311a4-f6b5-4abd-9f1a-aab923ca706f?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ], co-founder and CTO of Argu [ https://substack.com/redirect/46bbeb88-3e61-405f-a537-e1d8974ad6bc?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]:
Emphasizing our traction metrics was crucial. This is one of the most important aspects that helped us nail the pitch and can help others as well.
Progress Day Pitch - Alexis Sursock (Luvu)
>From Alexis Sursock, co-founder and CEO of Luvu (see full pitch above):
Pick the one or two sentences you want investors to write down about your pitch. Everything you say should support that. If you’re early, that’s usually about what makes your product and/or team experience stand out. 
If you’re later, it’s traction (users, retention, revenue). Investors hear endless pitches for similar products from similar teams. 
Your pitch needs to be succinctly memorable (In our case, we're going for: "Unhinged marshmallow cursed at lazy people. Retentive Accountability Partner by Duolingo + AI experts")
>From Fearn’s Han Kim:
Get iterative feedback from other founders. We’re surrounded by a bunch of brilliant and decent folks at a16z speedrun. Find those people, get their opinions, and rinse and repeat. When you’ve thought about something too hard and long (so every founder and their company), it’s easy to miss the forest for the trees. A two minute pitch is a “find the forest” exercise. A separate pair of eyes is very useful for finding the forest.
>From Kevin Jiang, co-founder and CEO of Azimov:
The thing I think most people don’t realize—or that they do realize but don’t truly internalize—is that at the end of the day your entire startup gets distilled down into a one-liner in someone else’s brain. Usually it’s something like “oh the AI recruiter app with decent traction” or “the guys making the drones, I think they're pre traction but have a good team.” For us, I tried to imagine what would be the ideal distilled one-liner people extract from us. I wanted ours to be “the guys with insane traction making AI companions.” If the day ends and every single person can walk away with this one-liner about us in their minds, then we did our job. If any slides helped craft that impression, they stayed. If not, that slide got cut.
>From Leo Korsunsky [ https://substack.com/redirect/a2e7c34a-7a49-4887-b753-b7b78a188c01?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ], co-founder of Oleum:
Just practicing the pitch while timing yourself. Our pitch ran slightly over so this is still something we have to work on.
Question 3: What are your plans between now and Demo Day?
>From Fearn’s Han Kim:
There’s not much time between now and Demo Day. We’re doing what we’ve always done: improving our models, building our software, and selling our product. But we’re also starting to think about fundraising, in earnest: who out there understands our vision, and understands why we’re taking the approach we’re taking? Fortunately, we trust the a16z speedrun community to help us navigate that world.
>From Lior Strugach, co-founder of Argu:
We’re refactoring our entire deck! Planning to hire a professional designer to align everything with our new branding.
>From Alexis Sursock, co-founder and CEO of Luvu:
Make a splash: Go all in on getting users (and making them sweat).
>From Taylor Offer, founder and CEO of Atrios:
Double our revenue, tighten the pitch, take over the world.
>From the co-founders of Anchr [ https://substack.com/redirect/49f2abd2-df6b-43ff-b689-792856e44007?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] (currently in stealth):
Close deals — and rock and roll.
That’s it for this week! Thanks to all the founders who shared their experience and thoughts with us.
For more weekly dives into the world of early stage startups, subscribe to our newsletter below.
Get the a16z speedrun newsletter weekly 👇

subject: Call preparation Cusp Capital  - Technical Defensability Research
body_text:
Technical defensibility:

Executive Summary

Verdict: Moderate Technical Defensibility with High Execution Risk
Calibrion AI positions itself as a "Human-Agent-Native" platform, aiming to solve the cost and scalability bottlenecks of data annotation by orchestrating LLM agents alongside human experts. While its architecture addresses a critical market need (efficiently training GenAI), its core features—agent orchestration, expert matching, and privacy—are rapidly becoming standard capabilities among well-funded incumbents like Scale AI and Labelbox.

To achieve true "technical defensibility" (a moat), Calibrion must prove its agent-human collaboration is not just a workflow feature, but a proprietary "System of Intelligence" that generates unique data on how agents fail, creating a self-reinforcing quality loop that competitors cannot replicate simply by plugging in the same open-source LLMs.

1. Defining "Technical Defensibility" in Venture Capital

In the modern VC environment (post-2023 AI boom), technical defensibility is no longer defined by simply having a better algorithm, as foundational models (like GPT-4 or Claude) are becoming commodities. Instead, VCs define defensibility through "Moats"—structural barriers that protect margins and market share.

Core Definitions & Frameworks:

The "Thin Wrapper" Risk: VCs (e.g., a16z, Greylock) are wary of startups that are merely "thin wrappers" around public APIs. If a feature can be replicated by OpenAI or a competitor in a weekend, it is not defensible.

Systems of Intelligence (Greylock): A defensible product doesn't just use AI; it combines multiple data sources and workflows to create a unique record of intelligence that gets smarter with use.

The Data Flywheel: True defensibility comes when every user interaction generates proprietary data that improves the product, making it impossible for a new entrant to catch up even with the same code.

Workflow Lock-in (Switching Costs): Deep integration into a customer's daily operations (e.g., project management, compliance) creates a moat because ripping it out is too painful.

2. Technical Defensibility Analysis: Calibrion AI

This analysis evaluates Calibrion AI’s claims against the VC frameworks defined above.

A. The "Human-Agent-Native" Architecture

The Claim: Calibrion uses "Annotation Agents" (LLMs) to collaborate with humans, reducing costs and speeding up timelines.

Defensibility Score: Low to Medium

Analysis: "Agentic workflows" are the current industry standard. Major competitors like Labelbox have already launched "Agent" support (using protocols like MCP) to allow humans to supervise and correct agent tool usage. Scale AI similarly uses a "Data Engine" where AI pre-labels data and humans correct it. <https://ubiai.tools/the-complete-guide-to-data-annotation-tools/>​

Risk: If Calibrion relies on off-the-shelf LLMs (e.g., "choose several agents... from open-source to proprietary"), its core engine is replicable. The defensibility lies only if Calibrion has a proprietary routing algorithm that knows exactly which agent (or human) is best for a specific micro-task better than anyone else.

B. Privacy & "No-Access" Infrastructure

The Claim: "We do not access or use your datasets... they remain securely on your specified encrypted storage."

Defensibility Score: Medium

Analysis: For enterprise customers (banks, healthcare), this is a critical selling point, but arguably a feature, not a moat. Many "Bring Your Own Cloud" (BYOC) architectures exist. However, if Calibrion has built a unique federated learning or privacy-preserving computelayer that allows agents to work on data they can't "see" (e.g., using secure enclaves), that would be highly defensible. Without that, it is a policy/infrastructure decision that competitors can copy.

C. Expert Matching & Quality Control

The Claim: Matching tasks to specific experts and observing "agreement" between agents and humans.

Defensibility Score: High (Potential)

Analysis: This is Calibrion's strongest potential moat. If Calibrion can capture granular data on "human-agent disagreement"—specifically where an agent fails and why a specific type of human expert corrected it—they can build a proprietary evaluation dataset.

The Moat: Over time, Calibrion could predict "This legal contract clause is too complex for GPT-4 but easy for a senior paralegal," automatically routing the task. This proprietary routing logic is a "System of Intelligence" that creates a cost/quality advantage no competitor can match without having processed the same volume of hybrid tasks.

D. Workflow & Project Management

The Claim: Reimagining annotation as "well-defined structured projects" with hierarchies and roles.

Defensibility Score: Low

Analysis: Project management interfaces are notoriously hard to defend. They rely on "workflow lock-in," which takes years to build. Incumbents with established enterprise contracts (Scale, Labelbox, Snorkel) already have deep integration into customer MLOps pipelines.

3. Strategic Recommendations for Defensibility

To maximize its valuation and defensibility, Calibrion should emphasize:

The "Router" Moat: Shift messaging from "we use agents" to "we have the world's best algorithm for orchestrating agents vs. humans."

Proprietary Evaluation Data: Highlight any unique datasets they possess regarding agent error rates across specific domains (e.g., "We know exactly where Llama-3 fails in medical triage").

Vertical Integration: Focus on a specific complex vertical (e.g., Legal, Medical) where generic "human-in-the-loop" platforms struggle with expert sourcing.

4. List of Top Venture Capital Blogs on This Topic

The following VC firms frequently publish deep-dive analysis on AI infrastructure, moats, and technical defensibility.



VC Firm	Blog / Publication URL	Focus Area
Greylock	https://greylock.com/greymatter/	Originated the concept of "Systems of Intelligence" vs. "Systems of Record." Deep focus on AI application layers.
Andreessen Horowitz (a16z)	https://a16z.com/ai/	Extensive writing on "The New Business of AI," economic moats in GenAI, and infrastructure vs. apps.
Sequoia Capital	https://sequoiacap.com/stories/	Focuses on "The Arc" of company building, product-market fit frameworks, and enduring business models.
Redpoint Ventures	https://www.redpoint.com/memory-leak/	Often covers "Infrastructure as Code," data engineering, and the modern data stack (MDS).
Benchmark	https://www.benchmark.com/	(Less frequent blogging, but partners often speak on podcasts/interviews about "network effects" and bottom-up adoption).
Insight Partners	https://www.insightpartners.com/ideas/	Focuses on scaling SaaS metrics, efficiency, and enterprise AI adoption patterns.
Battery Ventures	https://www.battery.com/blog/	Covers enterprise infrastructure, DevOps, and data platforms.

subject: Follow-up: Calibrion AI – Seed Round & Product Walkthrough
body_text:
Dear team at Yaday Venture Capital,

I hope this note finds you well. I’m following up on my earlier introduction of Calibrion AI and our EUR 3M seed round.

We’re building the first data annotation platform designed natively for LLMs, solving one of the toughest challenges in generative AI: enabling enterprises to turn proprietary data into high-quality, domain-specific training sets. This is the critical layer that allows companies to move from general-purpose models to production-grade, business-ready LLMs.

With Calibrion, enterprises achieve:

Up to 35% cost savings and weeks faster project delivery

Superior dataset quality through proprietary QC and automation workflows

Early traction – pilots already underway with a publicly traded German online retailer and a $150M revenue US apparel brand

A clear position in a $1–1.4B obtainable market by 2028 for LLM-specific tooling

Given Yaday’s focus on early-stage ventures with strong defensibility and global potential, we believe our mission aligns well with your investment thesis. I’d love to share our pitch deck and arrange a short call or product demo to dive deeper.

Would you have 30 minutes next week for an initial chat? I’d be happy to adjust to your schedule.

Looking forward to your feedback.

Best regards,
Nils

--
Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 ・ nils@calibrion.ai <mailto:nils@calibrion.ai>
Französische Str. 20・10117 Berlin・Germany
www.calibrion.ai <http://www.calibrion.ai/>
￼￼ ￼


> On 1. Aug 2025, at 09:55, Nils Erbach <nils@calibrion.ai> wrote:
> 
> Dear team at Yaday Venture Capital,
> 
> I hope this note finds you well.
> 
> I’m following up on my initial introduction of Calibrion AI and our EUR 3 million seed round. We’re encouraged by the early traction with pilot-stage interest from both European and US companies and would love to discuss how we see this opportunity scaling with the right partners on board.
> 
> As mentioned, our platform sits at the critical intersection of LLM deployment and proprietary data, helping enterprises unlock the full value of generative AI by tailoring models to their unique domains — an area that we believe will define the next wave of enterprise AI adoption.
> 
> Given Yaday’s focus on early-stage ventures with strong defensibility and global potential, I think there could be great alignment here. I’d be glad to share our pitch deck and arrange a short call or product walkthrough to dive deeper.
> 
> Would you have 30 minutes next week for an initial conversation? I’m flexible and can adjust to your preferred time.
> 
> Looking forward to your feedback and hoping we can connect soon.
> 
> Best regards,
> Nils
>

🎯 The AI Point of No Return: When Billion-Dollar Bets, Silent Panics, and Global Laws Collide 🌍⚡
body_text:
View this post on the web at https://aibuzz.news/p/the-ai-point-of-no-return-when-billion

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
Would you like to be featured in our newsletter🔥 and get noticed, QUICKLY 🚀 (57,000+ subscribers)? Simply reply to this email or send an email to editor@digitalhealthbuzz.com, and we can take it from there.
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
The moment we’ve all been anticipating has arrived—and according to Nvidia’s CEO, there’s no turning back. This week, we’re seeing three simultaneous signals that AI has crossed from experimental technology into permanent infrastructure: Jensen Huang is declaring that AI has achieved a self-sustaining “virtuous cycle” that will only accelerate from here. Game developers are discovering that AI has already infiltrated their industry far deeper than anyone publicly admits, creating an atmosphere of quiet panic about what comes next. And halfway around the world, Kazakhstan just became one of the first nations to pass comprehensive AI legislation, drawing legal lines around who owns what in an AI-generated future. These aren’t isolated incidents—they’re three sides of the same transformation. The speculation phase is over. The institutionalization phase has begun. Let’s dive into what happens when AI stops being a bet and starts being the foundation.
The AI Money Machine: Jensen Huang Says We’ve Hit the Point of No Return [ https://substack.com/redirect/f9ee8477-6957-47c9-bbb2-7cb574a75e6d?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang just made a declaration at the APEC CEO Summit that should change how we think about AI’s trajectory: “AI has now achieved the virtuous cycle.” What does that mean? Better AI attracts more users, more users generate more revenue, more revenue funds bigger infrastructure, and bigger infrastructure creates even better AI. It’s a self-reinforcing loop that accelerates without outside intervention. Huang isn’t predicting a future tipping point—he’s declaring we’ve already crossed it. This is why global capital expenditure in AI is exploding at unprecedented rates. The companies aren’t placing bets anymore; they’re responding to a demand cycle that feeds itself. The avalanche isn’t coming—it’s already here, gaining speed, and according to the man whose chips power most of the AI revolution, there’s no mechanism to slow it down.
The Silent Revolution: Why Game Developers Are Quietly Panicking About AI [ https://substack.com/redirect/29a5209a-0f89-45cf-ad9e-d8abfbec3dc2?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
The gaming industry’s worst-kept secret: AI is already everywhere, and most players have no idea. Research shows roughly one in five Steam games this year used generative AI during development—but that’s only counting studios that disclosed it. AI is dubbing dialogue, generating placeholder art, and debugging code across major studios. Game designer Mike Cook confirms AI integration runs far deeper than public admissions suggest. But here’s where the panic sets in: developers speaking off the record paint a very different picture from corporate optimism. Every productivity gain from AI creates pressure to do more with less, and every studio that adopts these tools forces competitors to follow or fall behind. The revolution isn’t loud because it doesn’t need to be. It’s happening in thousands of small optimizations, each barely noticeable, all of them collectively reshaping an entire industry while everyone pretends everything is fine.
Kazakhstan Takes a Bold Step Into the AI Future: What Their New Law Means for Creators and Tech [ https://substack.com/redirect/a9ce0b4b-eec0-4b93-8ea6-f846662824d1?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
While the world debates AI regulation in theoretical terms, Kazakhstan just passed actual legislation with clear answers. In September 2025, their parliament approved comprehensive AI laws tackling the thorniest question: who owns AI-generated content? Their answer is surprisingly nuanced. If AI creates content entirely on its own with no human input, it gets zero copyright protection—it’s public domain. But the creative prompts you craft to instruct AI tools might actually be protected as intellectual property. The output isn’t yours, but your instructions could be. What makes this significant is the timing—Kazakhstan isn’t waiting for global consensus or following Western frameworks. They’re planting their flag while much larger nations are still forming committees. For creators and tech companies, Kazakhstan just created a potential blueprint for how copyright adapts to AI. Whether others follow or chart different paths, the conversation just shifted from “what should we do?” to “here’s what one country actually did.”
Thanks for reading! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work.

Unsubscribe https://substack..tGh-L9dpw9jukcbLDSZhScdISLAgROkdj1-_1vqZYaI?

subject: B2B SaaS – Product ready - Zalando SE paid pilot secured – Seed Round EUR 600K; Calibrion.ai - AI Infrastructure / Data annotation
body_text:
Dear Team at Seed VC, dear Ralf-Peter, dear Ismet, dear Christian,

We are building Calibrion, an agent-native data annotation platform adressing one of the largest barriers to organisational GenAI adoption: enabling proprietary LLM training through high-quality, rapid annotation. 

Calibrion delivers: 
- Agentic data annotation that accelerates annotation up to 100x (from months to hours)
- Cost savings of around 90%
- Built-in proprietary quality control to ensure accuracy at scale 
- 35% faster human-in-the-loop through research driven optimized workflows

We recently signed a 6 months paid pilot with Zalando SE, secured a verbal commitment from Siemens Energy AG E-Commerce division, and are negotiating pilots with Cotopaxi (US sustainable outdoor brand) and LILT (AI-driven translation leader). 

We are seeking EUR 600,000 in seed funding to expand our team, onboard additional clients, and implement product-led-growth.

Our founder and CEO, Meghdad, Holds a PhD in Computer Science with a specialization in Natural Language Processing, and has over eight years of applied AI product experience. 

Given your investment focus, we think Calibrion could be a strong fit. You’ll find our one-pager, pitch-deck, and product demos attached. We’d be glad to arrange a 30-minute call to walk you through the product and discuss and our upcoming milestones.

Best regards,
Nils 


Nils Erbach

Calibrion AI

body_text:
Hi Sara,

Thank you very much!

No worries at all. I completely understand and I appreciate your efforts in
accommodating my schedule.

Have a great week ahead as well.

subject: Re: Calibrion opportunity / use case (Cotopaxi introduction)
body_text:
Hi Sara,

I hope you're doing well!

I just wanted to follow up on my email from a few days ago regarding
rescheduling our meeting.

Just to recap, the proposed time of May 29th at 6:15 pm CEST had a slight
conflict with my schedule, and I was wondering if any of the following
alternatives might work for you instead:
Friday, May 30th , Tuesday, June 3rd , or Wednesday, June 4th, starting
anytime between 5 and 6:30 pm CEST.

If none of these work on your side, please feel free to suggest alternative
time slots that work best for you.

Looking forward to hearing from you.

Best regards,
Meghdad

subject: Re: Calibrion opportunity / use case (Cotopaxi introduction)
body_text:
Lindsay,

It's a pleasure to meet you and the team!

Jacob, it's great to hear from you, and thank you for the introduction!

Interestingly, regarding this use case involving AI to help generate images
for your "Del Dia" collection, we've recently supported a similar project
for a large online retailer here in Europe, assisting them with fine-tuning
an AI model for image generation for their diverse clothing collections.

I am looking forward to connecting and exploring how Calibrion might be
able to help Cotopaxi with this initiative.

Thanks,
Meghdad

subject: Calibrion opportunity / use case (Cotopaxi introduction)
body_text:
Meghdad,

Hope you're doing well.  I'm copying in Lindsay Shumlas, the CEO of our portfolio company Cotopaxi.

Lindsay has a specific use case need where we think there could be an AI opportunity to help with photographic images that go onto the Cotopaxi website.

I'll let her or another member of the Cotopaxi team share more, but the short version is we have a collection called "Del Dia", which is all remnant material and thus nearly unlimited color combinations.  See examples here:

https://www.cotopaxi.com/products/allpa-35l-travel-pack-del-dia-1?variant=42280405368893

We individually photography each pack as it arrives, but this is timely (and employ multiple photographers to do this).  We are thinking there is a faster/easier way to use AI with some prompts to generate images of each bag.

I know with Calibrion you're supporting other enterprises with AI use cases so thought the introduction to Cotopaxi might also be relevant.

I defer to you both on setting up a call to explore more (Meghdad in Berlin; Lindsay splits time between Utah and California).  Let me know how I can help!

Jacob





Jacob Donnelly

Partner

________________________________

Meghdad, mein letzter Anstoß
body_text:
Hi Meghdad,

wollte dir ein letztes Mal schreiben, bevor ich deinen Posteingang wieder verlasse. Ich bin immer noch überzeugt davon, dass du mit dem Video-Feed auf LinkedIn eine enorme Reichweite generieren könntest.

Ich nehme aber an, dass es für dich 2025 nicht auf der Agenda steht, deine Personal-Brand aufzubauen.

Falls du doch Lust auf einen Austausch hast, melde dich einfach, und wir können in einem kurzen Gespräch die Details besprechen.

Viele Grüße

Jan Reuther

subject: Welcome to Antler | Kick-off & To-dos
body_text:
Dear Meghdad,

Welcome to Antler! We're thrilled to have you on board for this exciting
journey. As we gear up for the residency, let's dive into the Pre-Residency
Kick-Off. This initial phase has now officially started, paving the way for
an inspiring and productive journey ahead!

*Pre-Residency Structure:*

Our Pre-Residency is designed to get you informed, inspired, and prepared.
Expect reading material, opportunities to discuss your ideas with the
Antler team, and a chance to connect with the cohort across our three
locations. Furthermore, we suggest you to join our weekly Welcome +
Ideation calls to get further information and the chance to ask questions
and gather tips on maximizing the time until the *31st of March*. You'll
find these calls, along with all other sessions, in our Google Calendar
<https://djW2rd04.eu1.hs-sales-engage.com/Ctc/2P+23284/djW2rd04/Jl24YGXpW7lCGcx6lZ3nXW8JspJ632MN9jW62gCb882fXv6VMq2vm3S7HTfW4S_KjF2lMlQmW1j1X0R8PR2x2W689m8C49kC7bW1HL7-d2_H2x-N6zBTs54jDcqW6t_dn48x3SYGW9d5P387ZYj-dW75QG308s14BMW2Y8n4w2Vchf-W8nCxjv4bGKLqN3HZhDQmV8g_W3DsJdd6k4B6fW2MTM1K1dbT7WW1Q2-Wh10v-LqW1p4Krx5QFf_6W5ThbNr1lRmNcW1chDhk9gmVlZW8pr2rq9k-dmbW6dWzjr3XCh1HW11CWCk3_TqJTW342ncR2yzD-yW1Q3fVH8_YZ64W6zvCfk5-t0bdW4q1yGf54KnSxW8NjRFh8f0Gc8W6cp-YV4CsZZtVTTyhG8YYVmRW26rb3c5V3xfbW9gZ1kg2KcTKHW9bqqkD8zJb3YVfgHbD2qw5PXW3WPm-W7Wfrc-W5BQJCs5SCjvsW3t62DB1HXlgkW2M3XTN2kmdpfW1Qf2P37JLt5XVmrNvM3TQftSf3WkwT804>

با احترام، جناب دکتر قبل از واریزی بنده نمیتونم این رو امضا کنم. لطفاً به
همکارتون بگید که برام واریز کنن نهایتاً تا فردا پولمو لازم دارم. پنجشنبه هم
۱۳ ساعت کار کردم نه ۱۰ ساعت.
 در غیر این صورت پسفردا میرم و نظام صنفی رایانه ای شکایت میکنم.
با تشکر.
میرزایی

subject:
[Product Update] Data protection insights for Gmail available
 starting Oct 20, 2025
body_text:
Google Workspace logo



Get advanced insights into how sensitive data is being shared via Gmail.



Dear administrator,

We're writing to inform you that starting October 20, 2025, we're launching  
Data Protection Insights for Gmail. This is an extension of the existing  
Insights functionality for Google Drive and is designed to give you a  
better understanding of your organization's data security.

What this means for your organization

Data protection insights for Gmail uses metadata from your existing Google  
Workspace Data Loss Prevention (DLP) rules to generate high-level,  
aggregate statistics. These insights can help you:


Identify trends in sensitive data sharing via Gmail
Visualize potential risk areas within your organization
Evaluate the effectiveness of your current DLP policies

This feature provides valuable analytics without enforcing any new rules or  
impacting end users. It's an informational tool that gives you a clearer,  
more strategic view of potential data exfiltration via email messages.

What you need to do

Data protection insights for Gmail will be enabled by default for your  
organization on October 20, 2025.

You can opt out at any time by completing the following steps:


In your Google Admin console, go to Security > Access and data control >  
Data protection
Click the Data insights scanning and report card
Turn Off the Enable Gmail Insights button

We're here to help

Review DLP Data protection insights report to learn more about Data  
protection insights for Gmail.

Thanks for choosing Google Workspace.

– The Google Workspace Team




Was this information helpful?

A smiling face A neutral face A sad face


© 2025 Google LLC 1600 Amphitheatre Parkway, Mountain View, CA 94043

You have received this important update about your Google Workspace account  
because you designated this email address as a primary or secondary contact  
for mandatory service communications in your Google Admin Console profile.

Martina

subject: 🚀💰 From Dorm Rooms to Billions: When AI Markets Go Parabolic (and One Legend Says It's Time to Exit)
body_text:
View this post on the web at https://aibuzz.news/p/from-dorm-rooms-to-billions-when

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
Would you like to be featured in our newsletter🔥 and get noticed, QUICKLY 🚀 (56,000+ subscribers)? Simply reply to this email or send an email to editor@digitalhealthbuzz.com, and we can take it from there.
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
🎯 Cursor’s $29.3B Rocket Ship: MIT Friends Become Billionaires in 24 Months
Four MIT graduates turned a side project into a $1 billion revenue business faster than anyone thought possible. Cursor, the AI coding assistant, just raised $2.3 billion at a $29.3 billion valuation — achieving in two years what took Slack seven years and Zoom five years. With a team of fewer than 20 people and zero marketing spend, they’ve rewritten the playbook on startup scaling. The timeline is genuinely absurd: five months ago they were valued at $9.9 billion. This week? Nearly tripled.
Read the full story → [ https://substack.com/redirect/bd7c0ce2-6498-482e-aba1-90ed9f9d9c86?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
Thanks for reading! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work.
🤝 Microsoft’s Chess Move: Let OpenAI Build the Chips, We’ll Take the Blueprints
Microsoft just announced full access to OpenAI’s custom chip designs — essentially outsourcing its semiconductor R&D to its closest AI partner. Instead of competing in the brutal chip development race, Satya Nadella’s team gets access to OpenAI’s entire innovation pipeline (built with Broadcom), then customizes it for Azure. Brilliant pragmatism or quiet admission of defeat? Either way, OpenAI looks less like a portfolio investment and more like Microsoft’s in-house chip lab.
Read the full story → [ https://substack.com/redirect/e12ac730-9ff1-4251-bdab-b10d7290bcce?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
⚠️ The Big Short Investor Folds His Cards: Michael Burry Exits the Market
When the man who predicted the 2008 crash decides to close his hedge fund and return all investor capital, it’s worth paying attention. Michael Burry is shutting down Scion Asset Management by year’s end, citing markets that have “lost their mind.” His blunt assessment: “My estimation of value in securities is not now, and has not been for some time, in sync with the markets.” With AI enthusiasm pushing tech valuations to dizzying heights, Burry is cashing out. The question: what does he see that we don’t?
Read the full story → [ https://substack.com/redirect/f392a021-742d-41bd-b001-a00c1bbc8039?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
Thanks for reading! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work.

Unsubscribe https://substack..ms6tEWer5ViiTjeW2xfP31VAD21Zxcj7O-ZkH4bVV8c?

subject: Following up on Calibrion AI – Seed Round & Next Steps
body_text:
Dear team at First Momentum,

I hope you are doing well.

I wanted to follow up on my earlier introduction of Calibrion AI and our EUR 3 million seed round. We continue to see strong early interest from European and US companies as we help enterprises unlock the value of their proprietary data through fine-tuning workflows for LLMs.

Given First Momentum’s focus on backing outstanding technical founders and early-stage deep tech, I believe there’s a great fit with what we’re building at Calibrion. As mentioned, our platform is fully developed, already in pilot stages, and uniquely positioned to address a key gap in enterprise AI adoption.

I would be glad to share our deck and run you through a short product walkthrough to show you where we are today and how we plan to grow. Would you have 30 minutes next week for a quick conversation? I’m flexible and happy to work around your availability.

Looking forward to your feedback and hoping we can connect soon.

Best regards,
Nils

--
Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 ・ nils@calibrion.ai <>
Französische Str. 20・10117 Berlin・Germany
www.calibrion.ai <http://www.calibrion.ai/>
￼￼

> On 22. Jul 2025, at 15:01, Nils Erbach <nils@calibrion.ai> wrote:
> 
> Dear team at First Momentum,
> 
> I am reaching out to introduce Calibrion AI, a data annotation platform enabling enterprises and organisations to fine-tune LLMs to their proprietary data. We built a working product and already reached pilot-stage interest from European and US companies in the eCom vertical.
> 
> While most LLM infra and dev platforms focus on model architecture and LLMOps, we focus on unlocking enterprise adoption through (proprietary) data and fine-tuning workflows.
> 
> Calibrion envisions a future where businesses harness their proprietary data and domain expertise to build specialized LLMs, turning generative AI from a threat into a strategic asset for both internal and market-facing applications.
> 
> Our mission is to become a market-leading platform for LLM customization and domain adaptation, setting the standard for performance and usability in the enterprise AI space.
> 
> To underscore the scale of this opportunity and the importance of what Calibrion does: In June 2025, Meta acquired a 49% stake in Scale AI (a company also focused on LLM data annotation and founded in 2016) for $14.8 billion at a $30 billion valuation. 
> 
> We are currently offering subscription for our EUR 3 m seed investment round to: 
> - expand our engineering and data science team
> - Accelerate go-to-market efforts across verticals 
> - Further develop our AI capabilities and integrate new LLM-driven features.
> 
> To accelerate the company's development, we assigned significant equity to an option pool for key employees and aim to be cash-flow positive within 18 months.
> 
> We would be happy to share our pitch deck and schedule a call to walk you through the live product. The product is fully developed and currently undergoing regular testing and stabilization.
> 
> Please find attached a letter outlining our fund-raising goals as well as a one-pager on Calibrion.
> 
> We look forward to hearing from you and would be glad to schedule a conversation at your convenience.
> 
> Best regards,
> Nils
> 
> -- 
> Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
> Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 <tel:+49+(0)30+478+662+25> ・ nils@calibrion.ai <mailto:meghdad@calibrion.ai>
> Französische Str. 20・10117 Berlin・Germany
> www.calibrion.ai <http://www.calibrion.ai/>

subject: Fwd: Calibrion AI <> bmp Ventures
body_text:
> Begin forwarded message:
> 
> From: thaake@bmp.com
> Subject: Re: Calibrion AI <> bmp Ventures
> Date: 12. November 2025 at 10:20:48 CET
> To: nils@calibrion.ai
> 
> Hallo Nils,
> 
> zunächst einmal vielen Dank für Dein Interesse an einer Zusammenarbeit mit bmp Ventures.
> 
> Von den von bmp Ventures verwalteten Fonds kommen derzeit lediglich die IBG Fonds des Landes Sachsen-Anhalt für ein etwaiges Investment unsererseits infrage. Diese Fonds erlauben uns Investitionen ausschließlich in Unternehmen mit Hauptsitz in Sachsen-Anhalt oder solche, die dort eine Betriebsstätte haben – für die wir dann Kapital zur Verfügung stellen können. 
> 
> Wir haben bereits Startups aus Städten wie Berlin, Hamburg und München erfolgreich nach Sachsen-Anhalt umgesiedelt, zum Beispiel nach Magdeburg oder Halle. Das Bundesland bietet großartige Unterstützung für Gründer, die an einer Umsiedlung interessiert sind.
> 
> Wenn eine Verlagerung nach Sachsen-Anhalt für Euch interessant klingt, würden wir Euren Case gerne genauer prüfen.
> 
> Ich freue mich darauf, von Dir zu hören! 
> 
> 
> Mit freundlichen Grüßen / With regards 
> 
> Tom Haake 
> Junior Investment Analyst 
> 
￼
> p:	+49 391 53281-40 I +49 30 20305-0
> a:	Am Alten Theater 4 • D-39104 Magdeburg | Schlüterstraße 38 • D-10629 Berlin
> e:	thaake@bmp.com I w: www.bmp.com <http://www.bmp.com/>
> b:	Oliver Borrmann (chair), Dr. Jan Alberti, Andreas van Bon
> s:	Dr. Torsten Poeck (chair supervisory board)
> t:	bmp Ventures AG I Amtsgericht Berlin-Charlottenburg HRB 96662B
> 
> 
> 
> 
> Ihr bmp Ventures Pitch Deck Upload Nachricht: 
> 
> Dear Team at bmp Ventures, We are building Calibrion, an agent-native data annotation platform adressing one of the largest barriers to organisational GenAI adoption: enabling proprietary LLM training through high-quality, rapid annotation.  Calibrion delivers:  – Agentic data annotation that accelerates annotation up to 100x (from months to hours) – Cost savings of around 90% – Built-in proprietary quality control to ensure accuracy at scale  – 35% faster human-in-the-loop through research driven optimized workflows We recently signed a 6 months paid pilot with Zalando SE, secured a verbal commitment from Siemens Energy AG E-Commerce division, and are negotiating pilots with Cotopaxi (US sustainable outdoor brand) and LILT (AI-driven translation leader).  We are seeking EUR 600,000 in seed funding to expand our team, onboard additional clients, and implement product-led-growth. Our founder and CEO, Meghdad, holds a PhD in Computer Science with a specialization in Natural Language Processing, and has over eight years of applied AI product experience.  Given your investment focus, we think Calibrion could be a strong fit. You’ll find our one-pager, pitch-deck, and product demos attached. We’d be glad to arrange a 30-minute call to walk you through the product and discuss and our upcoming milestones or just connect via phone (number attached). 
> 
> Name: Nils Erbach 
> E-Mail-Adresse: nils@calibrion.ai 
> Telefon: +4917662205065 
> Firma: Calibrion AI 
> Website:  <https://www.calibrion.ai/>https://www.calibrion.ai <https://www.calibrion.ai/> 
>
