Good afternoon Meghdad,

I am really pleased to read you.

Also i realise i completely forgot to send you our corporate presentation. We need to put a simple nda in place (as all our past clients are in) but that is easy. I will do that as soon as i am back from the children easter holidays in probably 10 days (i hope this is ol for you).

The deal i mentioned to you during our last call. You have a great memory… well i actually advised my clients 2/3 weeks ago to put things on hold as the private equity buyer was too aggressive in the nego of the legal parameters and i was fearing the trump tarriff things/war… and i was not too wrong and my client is happy with the way i am managing the process (well i have to admit that i was not happy with the negotiation not in favour of my client, hen e why i wanted to slowdown - and given the mess since last week, we are very lucky to have slowdown things 🤣). Anyway all good and i am sure it will close later this year either with this fund or maybe with a strategic buyer (as in reality i never put things on hold and i am always hunting for new offers and alternatives).

I agree with you given your situation that it is better to delay further your process. Have you had any chance with a few business angels in Berlin? Or a german crowdfunding platform? Now that people are withdrawing money from the stockmarket, maybe more BA could be interested to invest 200/300k in your firm. That is what i wish you anyway for shortterm…

Bear in mind that a process takes in average 4/6 months when we push and the environment is normal. If we have more time to search for alternatives, then it is better. But linked with your email, our reduced engagement retainer of £10k still needs to be paid so you need to have financial bandwidth for sure. No rush. I like what you do and your philosophy as a businessman and hopefully we will work together at some points.

I will get back to you soon on the nda and our corporate presentation.

Thanks and kind regards
Louisarmand

subject: Follow-up: Calibrion AI – Seed Round & Live Demo
body_text:
Dear team at Iron Wolf Capital,

I hope you’re doing well. I’m following up on my earlier introduction of Calibrion AI and our EUR 3M seed round.

We’re building the first platform designed natively for LLM-specific data annotation and fine-tuning, solving the core bottleneck that enterprises face when adapting LLMs to their proprietary data. Unlike legacy annotation tools, Calibrion allows companies to:

Realize up to 35% cost savings and accelerate project delivery by weeks

Achieve superior data quality with proprietary QC and automation workflows

Transform proprietary data into strategic, domain-specific training sets — the foundation for production-grade LLMs

Already demonstrate traction, with pilots secured with a publicly traded German online retailer and a $150M-revenue US apparel brand

The recent Meta–Scale AI $14.3B deal underscores how critical this data layer has become for generative AI. With a $1–1.4B obtainable market by 2028 for LLM-specific tooling, we see significant near-term growth ahead.

Given Iron Wolf Capital’s focus on bold early-stage founders building transformative technologies, we believe there’s clear alignment. I’d be glad to share our deck in detail and walk you through a live demo of the product.

Would you have 30 minutes next week for an initial conversation? I’m happy to adjust to your schedule.

Looking forward to your thoughts.

Best regards,
Nils

--
Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 ・ nils@calibrion.ai <mailto:nils@calibrion.ai>
Französische Str. 20・10117 Berlin・Germany
www.calibrion.ai <http://www.calibrion.ai/>
￼
￼ ￼

> On 1. Aug 2025, at 09:56, Nils Erbach <nils@calibrion.ai> wrote:
> 
> Dear team at Iron Wolf Capital,
> 
> I hope you are doing well.
> 
> I wanted to follow up on my introduction of Calibrion AI and our EUR 3 million seed round. We’re seeing promising traction, with early pilot interest from European and US companies, and we believe this is an ideal moment to discuss how we can scale this momentum with the right partners.
> 
> As mentioned, our focus is on helping enterprises unlock the real value of LLMs by fine-tuning them with proprietary data — an approach we believe will define the next phase of practical AI adoption in business. With the recent Scale AI–Meta deal highlighting how critical this layer is, we see significant growth ahead for this segment.
> 
> Given Iron Wolf Capital’s focus on supporting ambitious early-stage founders building transformative technologies, we’d love to explore how Calibrion could fit into your portfolio.
> 
> I’d be glad to share our detailed deck and walk you through a live demo of the product. Would you have 30 minutes next week for an initial conversation? I’m happy to adjust to your schedule.
> 
> Looking forward to your thoughts and hoping we can connect soon.
> 
> Best regards,
> Nils
> 
> --
> Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
> Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 ・ nils@calibrion.ai <>
> Französische Str. 20・10117 Berlin・Germany
> www.calibrion.ai <http://www.calibrion.ai/>
> 
> <250719_OnePager Calibrion.pdf>
> 
> <250722_Iron_Wolf_Capital_Investor Letter.pdf>
> 
> 
>> On 22. Jul 2025, at 16:47, Nils Erbach <nils@calibrion.ai> wrote:
>> 
>> Dear team at Iron Wolf Capital,
>> 
>> I am reaching out to introduce Calibrion AI, a data annotation platform enabling enterprises and organisations to fine-tune LLMs to their proprietary data. We built a working product and already reached pilot-stage interest from European and US companies in the eCom vertical.
>> 
>> While most LLM infra and dev platforms focus on model architecture and LLMOps, we focus on unlocking enterprise adoption through (proprietary) data and fine-tuning workflows.
>> 
>> Calibrion envisions a future where businesses harness their proprietary data and domain expertise to build specialized LLMs, turning generative AI from a threat into a strategic asset for both internal and market-facing applications.
>> 
>> Our mission is to become a market-leading platform for LLM customization and domain adaptation, setting the standard for performance and usability in the enterprise AI space.
>> 
>> To underscore the scale of this opportunity and the importance of what Calibrion does: In June 2025, Meta acquired a 49% stake in Scale AI (a company also focused on LLM data annotation and founded in 2016) for $14.8 billion at a $30 billion valuation. 
>> 
>> We are currently offering subscription for our EUR 3 m seed investment round to: 
>> - expand our engineering and data science team
>> - Accelerate go-to-market efforts across verticals 
>> - Further develop our AI capabilities and integrate new LLM-driven features.
>> 
>> To accelerate the company's development, we assigned significant equity to an option pool for key employees and aim to be cash-flow positive within 18 months.
>> 
>> We would be happy to share our pitch deck and schedule a call to walk you through the live product. The product is fully developed and currently undergoing regular testing and stabilization.
>> 
>> Please find attached a letter outlining our fund-raising goals as well as a one-pager on Calibrion.
>> 
>> We look forward to hearing from you and would be glad to schedule a conversation at your convenience.
>> 
>> Best regards,
>> Nils
>> 
>> -- 
>> Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
>> Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 <tel:+49+(0)30+478+662+25> ・ nils@calibrion.ai <mailto:meghdad@calibrion.ai>

>> www.calibrion.ai <http://www.calibrion.ai/>

subject: RE: Connection - Private Placement Calibrion - Confidential
body_text:
Hi Meghdad,

I did send you our large corporate presentation back in April 2025. In it you have the full detailed process that we went through over the phone – slides 23 to 38, with a detailed and average timetable on page 35.
I do attach this book again 😉

Easy to discuss over the phone than via email/iphone…

We can book Wednesday the 7th of January at 10am UK times / 11am your time. Let’s see closer to the date, as I am on 3 deals at the minutes and once is gonna close in January and with lawyers, calls do change and appear last minute all the time. But let’s have this slot blocked. Then we can discuss and see if you are ready to engage us…

Wishing you a great holiday season!
Merry Christmas and happy new year 2026!

Kind regards,

Louis-Armand Weisheimer
CEO & Managing Director
Direct line: +44 (0) 1202 359255
Mobile: +44 (0) 7908 369768
Email: louis-armand@weissknightcorporate.com<mailto:louis-armand@weissknightcorporate.com>
Weissknight Corporate Finance

The information in this e-mail and any attachment is confidential. If you are not the named addressee or if you receive this e-mail in error then any distribution, copying or use of this communication or the information in it is strictly prohibited. Please notify us immediately by e-mail at contact@weissknight.com and then delete the message from your computer.
Weissknight Corporate Ltd is registered in England (Company Number: 12438912), 34 Oaks Drive, Ringwood, BH24 2QT – UK.

From: Meghdad Farahmand <meghdad@calibrion.ai>
Sent: 18 December 2025 17:35
To: Louis-Armand Weisheimer - WEISSKNIGHT Corporate Finance <louis-armand@weissknightcorporate.com>
Subject: Re: Connection - Private Placement Calibrion - Confidential

Thanks for your detailed response, Louisarmand.

If you agree, let's schedule a call early January.
For instance, how about the 7th, 8th of January 10 am 11 am  or 9th of January 10 am CET?

If you could in the meantime, also give me a very brief description of the process. That would be great.
I am also interested in knowing that with you, how soon we would be able to pitch to the right people (partners / decision makers) in the right VC firms (reputable, relevant fields).
Not being able to get to the right people has been a main issue when we were looking for angels and investment.

Have a nice holiday, Merry Christmas and Happy New Year to you in advance too!

Meghdad

Thanks for your update and email.

Glad to hear the positive developments. Glad customers are excited as this is what matters in real. BA unfortunately are difficult, no surprise. And you will see that VCs are slow also anyway, especially in recession times.

If we are serious and honest, no VC funds will close a potential funding round in 3 months. It takes a good 5/6 months in average (plus summer or christmas holidays). Not much i can if i am honnest….

The sooner we start, the better you will be but there is nothing magical. Our process does allow you to systematically open all doors on an international basis in parallel, but investors take their time as they want to follow companies and minimse that way their risk…

I am on holidays tomorrow till early january. If you think a call could help you decide when to engage us, then it will be my pleasure. But i follow and respect your timing - no pressure.

Merry christmas and happy new Year!
Louisarmand

Hi Louis-Armand,

I hope you are doing well!

I am writing to follow up on our previous conversation (sorry it took too long).

We didn't have much luck with finding angels or pre-seed funding in Germany yet. As far as we experienced, most angel tickets we came across were very small (€20k-€70k) and a real risk averseness is clouding the investment scene in Germany.

But we are having a good run with customers. We closed and signed a pilot with Zalando (to start in Feb), Siemens Energy and are negotiating with two other enterprises.

I see massive potential in the market we are building. Current market dynamics enable rapid revenue growth (€100M - 1B ARR reported in 2024/2025 within 12–24 months) for similar data annotation platforms, underscoring the current demand for high-quality AI data.

Most potential customers I speak with are highly interested. We have such a time advantage right now. But we need to close an investment (€1M  - €4M) in the next quarter if we are to use this advantage and stay ahead and hyper-grow. If we start collaborating on closing an investment deal, do you think the timeline is feasible?

A friend who is a finance controller and analyst has joined Calibrion and created a comprehensive financial planning to help inform product decisions and implementation of revenue channels. Does this facilitate your work if we are to collaborate?

Let me know if you would like to check out the pitch and supporting contents too.

Looking forward to hearing back from you.

Best,
Meghdad

subject: Re: Connection - Private Placement Calibrion - Confidential
body_text:
Good afternoon Meghdad,

Thanks for your update and email.

Glad to hear the positive developments. Glad customers are excited as this is what matters in real. BA unfortunately are difficult, no surprise. And you will see that VCs are slow also anyway, especially in recession times.

If we are serious and honest, no VC funds will close a potential funding round in 3 months. It takes a good 5/6 months in average (plus summer or christmas holidays). Not much i can if i am honnest….

The sooner we start, the better you will be but there is nothing magical. Our process does allow you to systematically open all doors on an international basis in parallel, but investors take their time as they want to follow companies and minimse that way their risk…

I am on holidays tomorrow till early january. If you think a call could help you decide when to engage us, then it will be my pleasure. But i follow and respect your timing - no pressure.

Merry christmas and happy new Year!
Louisarmand



Sent from Outlook for iOS<https://aka.ms/o0ukef>
________________________________
From: Meghdad Farahmand <meghdad@calibrion.ai>
Sent: Thursday, December 18, 2025 12:00 pm
To: Louis-Armand Weisheimer - WEISSKNIGHT Corporate Finance <louis-armand@weissknightcorporate.com>
Subject: Re: Connection - Private Placement Calibrion - Confidential

Hi Louis-Armand,

I hope you are doing well!

I am writing to follow up on our previous conversation (sorry it took too long).

We didn't have much luck with finding angels or pre-seed funding in Germany yet. As far as we experienced, most angel tickets we came across were very small (€20k-€70k) and a real risk averseness is clouding the investment scene in Germany.

But we are having a good run with customers. We closed and signed a pilot with Zalando (to start in Feb), Siemens Energy and are negotiating with two other enterprises.

I see massive potential in the market we are building. Current market dynamics enable rapid revenue growth (€100M - 1B ARR reported in 2024/2025 within 12–24 months) for similar data annotation platforms, underscoring the current demand for high-quality AI data.

Most potential customers I speak with are highly interested. We have such a time advantage right now. But we need to close an investment (€1M  - €4M) in the next quarter if we are to use this advantage and stay ahead and hyper-grow. If we start collaborating on closing an investment deal, do you think the timeline is feasible?

A friend who is a finance controller and analyst has joined Calibrion and created a comprehensive financial planning to help inform product decisions and implementation of revenue channels. Does this facilitate your work if we are to collaborate?

Let me know if you would like to check out the pitch and supporting contents too.

Looking forward to hearing back from you.

Best,
Meghdad

subject: RE: Connection - Private Placement Calibrion - Confidential
body_text:
Good morning Meghdad,

Thanks for that.

Attached our confidential corporate presentation, I have left both chapters about our crossborder fundraising process, and sell-side process (in case you might be interested to raise money from a strategic player via a minority sell).

Next step, when you are ready to engage us, would be for me to prepare the Engagement Letter. And when the small admin step will be done, then we can start the preparation phase (so InfoMemo, Teaser, Excel and Pitchdeck). I know you are not ready and no rush. Ball is on your side, I remain on a waiting mode with your case.

I am about to onboard a very large FinTech sells-side mandate (US/UK) and a small pharmatech fundraising mandate (UK/Europe) anyway – both of them using AI (well everybody does nowdays anyway), so will be busy this month anyway 😉

I hope things are progressing well on your end and I am ready to jump on your case when needed.
Kind regards,lo


Louis-Armand Weisheimer
CEO & Managing Director

Mobile: +44 (0) 7908 369768

Dear Louis-Armand,

Thank you for your email!

The NDA after the Easter holidays is perfectly fine with me.

Thank you for sharing this information about your recent placement. That sounds like a very thoughtful decision and quite helpful towards your client.

From my end, I thought given the duration of the process, it does not make any sense if we start the process, and after 3/4 months I have to drop off to find short-term financing/projects etc. to secure my runway.

I have started reaching out to a few BAs. Also an EX-CEO friend who has a good BA network in Berlin started to help me out. I think it looks good. I am looking for 250K, and with that, I can secure the runway for a year, add several interesting additions to the product, and build quite a bit of traction.
This I believe not only gives us time to find the right VC, but also if my traction plans work out, we can go for a higher round and valuation. I'll keep you posted.

Best regards,
Meghdad



On Mon, Apr 7, 2025 at 5:46 PM Louis-Armand Weisheimer - WEISSKNIGHT Corporate Finance <louis-armand@weissknightcorporate.com<mailto:louis-armand@weissknightcorporate.com>> wrote:
Good afternoon Meghdad,

I am really pleased to read you.

Also i realise i completely forgot to send you our corporate presentation. We need to put a simple nda in place (as all our past clients are in) but that is easy. I will do that as soon as i am back from the children easter holidays in probably 10 days (i hope this is ol for you).

The deal i mentioned to you during our last call. You have a great memory… well i actually advised my clients 2/3 weeks ago to put things on hold as the private equity buyer was too aggressive in the nego of the legal parameters and i was fearing the trump tarriff things/war… and i was not too wrong and my client is happy with the way i am managing the process (well i have to admit that i was not happy with the negotiation not in favour of my client, hen e why i wanted to slowdown - and given the mess since last week, we are very lucky to have slowdown things 🤣). Anyway all good and i am sure it will close later this year either with this fund or maybe with a strategic buyer (as in reality i never put things on hold and i am always hunting for new offers and alternatives).

I agree with you given your situation that it is better to delay further your process. Have you had any chance with a few business angels in Berlin? Or a german crowdfunding platform? Now that people are withdrawing money from the stockmarket, maybe more BA could be interested to invest 200/300k in your firm. That is what i wish you anyway for shortterm…

Bear in mind that a process takes in average 4/6 months when we push and the environment is normal. If we have more time to search for alternatives, then it is better. But linked with your email, our reduced engagement retainer of £10k still needs to be paid so you need to have financial bandwidth for sure. No rush. I like what you do and your philosophy as a businessman and hopefully we will work together at some points.

I will get back to you soon on the nda and our corporate presentation.

Thanks and kind regards
Louisarmand

subject: You just made a payment
Fwd: Capnamic x Calibrion
Event Reminder I Data + AI Summit starts today
Follow-up: Calibrion AI – Seed Round & Product Walkthrough
body_text:
Dear team at First Momentum,

I hope you’re doing well. I’m following up on my earlier introduction of Calibrion AI and our ongoing EUR 3M seed round.

We’re tackling one of the hardest challenges in enterprise AI adoption: enabling organizations to unlock the value of their proprietary data through LLM fine‑tuning workflows. Unlike traditional annotation platforms, Calibrion is built natively for LLMs, delivering:

Up to 35% lower costs and weeks faster project execution

Superior data quality powered by proprietary QC and workflow automation

Early traction – with pilots already secured with a $150M US apparel brand and a publicly traded German online retailer

A defined role in a $1–1.4B obtainable market by 2028 for LLM‑specific training data tools

With a technical founder (PhD in NLP, ex‑AI/ML architect) and a team deeply experienced in applied AI, our platform is already product-ready and in pilot stages, positioning us to scale quickly.

Given First Momentum’s focus on outstanding technical founders and early‑stage deep tech, we believe there’s a strong fit. I’d be glad to share our deck and walk you through a short product demo. Would you have 30 minutes next week for a quick call? I’m flexible and happy to work around your schedule.

Looking forward to your thoughts.

Best regards,
Nils

--
Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 ・ nils@calibrion.ai <mailto:nils@calibrion.ai>
Französische Str. 20・10117 Berlin・Germany
www.calibrion.ai <http://www.calibrion.ai/>
￼
￼

￼

> On 1. Aug 2025, at 09:57, Nils Erbach <nils@calibrion.ai> wrote:
> 
> Dear team at First Momentum,
> 
> I hope you are doing well.
> 
> I wanted to follow up on my earlier introduction of Calibrion AI and our EUR 3 million seed round. We continue to see strong early interest from European and US companies as we help enterprises unlock the value of their proprietary data through fine-tuning workflows for LLMs.
> 
> Given First Momentum’s focus on backing outstanding technical founders and early-stage deep tech, I believe there’s a great fit with what we’re building at Calibrion. As mentioned, our platform is fully developed, already in pilot stages, and uniquely positioned to address a key gap in enterprise AI adoption.
> 
> I would be glad to share our deck and run you through a short product walkthrough to show you where we are today and how we plan to grow. Would you have 30 minutes next week for a quick conversation? I’m flexible and happy to work around your availability.
> 
> Looking forward to your feedback and hoping we can connect soon.
> 
> Best regards,
> Nils
> 
> --
> Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
> Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 ・ nils@calibrion.ai <>
> Französische Str. 20・10117 Berlin・Germany
> www.calibrion.ai <http://www.calibrion.ai/>
> 
> <250719_OnePager Calibrion.pdf>
> 
> <250722_First_Momentum_Investor Letter.pdf>
> 
> 
>> On 22. Jul 2025, at 15:01, Nils Erbach <nils@calibrion.ai> wrote:
>> 
>> Dear team at First Momentum,
>> 
>> I am reaching out to introduce Calibrion AI, a data annotation platform enabling enterprises and organisations to fine-tune LLMs to their proprietary data. We built a working product and already reached pilot-stage interest from European and US companies in the eCom vertical.
>> 
>> While most LLM infra and dev platforms focus on model architecture and LLMOps, we focus on unlocking enterprise adoption through (proprietary) data and fine-tuning workflows.
>> 
>> Calibrion envisions a future where businesses harness their proprietary data and domain expertise to build specialized LLMs, turning generative AI from a threat into a strategic asset for both internal and market-facing applications.
>> 
>> Our mission is to become a market-leading platform for LLM customization and domain adaptation, setting the standard for performance and usability in the enterprise AI space.
>> 
>> To underscore the scale of this opportunity and the importance of what Calibrion does: In June 2025, Meta acquired a 49% stake in Scale AI (a company also focused on LLM data annotation and founded in 2016) for $14.8 billion at a $30 billion valuation. 
>> 
>> We are currently offering subscription for our EUR 3 m seed investment round to: 
>> - expand our engineering and data science team
>> - Accelerate go-to-market efforts across verticals 
>> - Further develop our AI capabilities and integrate new LLM-driven features.
>> 
>> To accelerate the company's development, we assigned significant equity to an option pool for key employees and aim to be cash-flow positive within 18 months.
>> 
>> We would be happy to share our pitch deck and schedule a call to walk you through the live product. The product is fully developed and currently undergoing regular testing and stabilization.
>> 
>> Please find attached a letter outlining our fund-raising goals as well as a one-pager on Calibrion.
>> 
>> We look forward to hearing from you and would be glad to schedule a conversation at your convenience.
>> 
>> Best regards,
>> Nils

subject:
VC Call Prep - Anticipated FAQs / Improved Go-to-Market Strategy /
 Competitive Advantage Analysis & Platform Comparison
body_text:
1. Problem -> Product / Tech -> Solution [ Meghdad ]:

- Currently, data annotation is manual, labor intensive and possibly entails various quality pitfalls 
- We build Calibrion to solve this bottleneck

2. Financial Roadmap [ Nils ] - Base: Build-Up Business Plan 

- Market: Currently, only about 6% of companies in Germany have a dedicated data science department / employ data experts [ Huge runway in adaption ] - 66% say they pursue a digitalization strategy 
- Cost structure and burn rate: 

i.) Direct personel espenses: 

￼

ii.) Indirect personel expsnes: 

￼

iii.) Costs of running the company:

￼ 


3. Go-To-Market Strategy / Milestones and how to achieve them [ Meghdad, Nils ]:

KPIs & Metrics tracking: 

i.) Primary: 

- Monthly New ARR 
- Customer Acquisition Cost by Channel 
- Customer Lifetime-Value / CAC Ratio [ max 4:1 ]
- CAC Payback Period [ max. 3-4 months ] 

ii.) Product-Led: 

- Free trial signups 
- Product-Qualified Leads generated [ PQLs ] 
- Free to paid conversion rate [ Target: 15-25% ] 
- Time to first value [ <10 Mins ] 

iii.) Marketing: 

- Pipeline velocity [ days from MQL to close ] 
- Win rate by ICP segment 
- Avg. deal size by tier 
- Pilot to paid conversion rate [ Target: >75% ] 

iv.) Tracking Infrastructure: 

- CRM: HubSpot / Salesforce [ Custom PQL ] 
- Product analytics [ Amplitude or mixpanel -> Usage tracking ] 
- Attribution: Bizible / HockeyStack [ Multi-touch Attribution ] 
- Data warehouse: Snowflake / BigQuery / Databricks [ Unified reporting ] 

a. Pilots

- Onboard pilots -> They need to give written testimonials / case studies; Track success metrics -> work as accelerator for onboarding additional customers / spread the word 
- Weekly check-ins with pilot stakeholders -> Accelerate time to value 
- Document quantifyable results and testimonials [ Display on website ]
- Negotiate contracted commitments before pilot end dates 

Phase 1 - Road to product-red growth

- Freemium Tier design: Offer limited free tier for up to 1,000 data points [ etc. ] per month - Display clear upgrade prompts when user hits limits
- Self-serve onboarding:  Creation of a 5-minute „Quick start“ flow -> Get users to first annotation within 10 minutes 
- Product qualified scoring system [ product usage system; buying intend signal; customer fit signal ] -> If certain threshold is reached, sales becomes involved 

Phase 2 - Content-driven inbound machine [ building educational content ] -> Grow organic traffic to 5,000-8,000 / month; generate 150-200 MQLs; CAC @ USD 80-100

- Pillar one: Data annotation for AI 
- Pillar two: Ai Agent workflow
- Pillar three: Comparison & alternative content
- Bottom-up funnel content 
- Content production targets
- Build and execute webinars

Phase 3 - Community-led growth:

i.) Developer & Data Scientist Community [ Slack / Discord / GitHub ]
ii.) User generated content & referral program [ Build & promote ] 


Phase 4 - Strategic outbound & account-based marketing [ Based on ideal Customer Profile (ICP) definition ]:
- Developing ICPs: i.) E-Commerce and Retail Brands; ii.) AI/ML Platform companies; iii.) Software & SaaS Companies
- Account based marketing (ABM) Strategy
- 

Phase 5- Partnership & Channel 

- LLM Infrastructure Platforms 
- ML Ops & Data Platforms 
- 

i.) Integration Partnerships 
- Developer & data scientist community 
- User-generated content & referral program 

ii.) Referral partnerships
- AI Consulting Firms & ML Agencies 
- Technology Resellers 

iii.) Co-Marketing Partnerships
- AI Industry Events & Communities 

Phase 6 - Paid acquisition [ Monthly budget - USD 21,000 - 34,000 ]: 

- Paid search (Google Ads)
- LinkedIn Ads 
- YouTube & Programmatic Display 

￼


4. Expected questions [ Andreas ]: 

- How does Calibrion technically differentiate from companies like Snorkel AI; Scale AI; Encord; Surge AI? 
- What is your path to ARR growth and how defensible is your revenue model? 
- How do you ensure data quality at scale? 
- What challenges do you anticipate in expanding enterprise adoption? 
- What are your plans for Series A/B, given potential revenue targets? 
- Who are your main competitors and potential acquisition targets? 

5. Competitive Advantage: 

- Current solutions: Manual annotation [ Mayor data annotation platforms generate data with ~1,000,000 people in the loop ]
- Calibrion solution: Agentic [ human / AI Integration ] data annotaiton -> 100x speed at uncompromised quality 

i.) Quality control [ Not available / minimal version ]
ii.) LLM jury [ Competitors: Not available / minimal version ]
iii.) Agents [ COmpetitors: Not available / minimal version ] 

i.) Comprehensive Competitive Advantage Comparison: 

￼

￼

subject: Following up on Calibrion AI – Seed Round & Product Walkthrough
body_text:
Dear team at Cathay Innovation,

I hope you are doing well.

I wanted to follow up on my earlier introduction of Calibrion AI and our EUR 3 million seed round. We’re seeing encouraging momentum with early pilot interest from European and US companies, and we believe this is a strong moment to deepen the conversation with you.

As mentioned, our focus is on unlocking enterprise adoption of LLMs through proprietary data annotation and fine-tuning — a layer that is essential for turning general models into business-ready, domain-specific solutions. Given Cathay Innovation’s track record in scaling globally minded, transformative tech companies, we feel there is a great strategic alignment.

I would be glad to share our pitch deck and arrange a short call or product walkthrough to explore the opportunity further and hear your perspective.

Would you have 30 minutes next week for a quick conversation? I’m flexible and happy to adjust to your schedule.

Looking forward to your thoughts and hoping we can connect soon.

Best regards,
Nils

--
Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 ・ nils@calibrion.ai <>
Französische Str. 20・10117 Berlin・Germany
www.calibrion.ai <http://www.calibrion.ai/>

￼￼

> On 22. Jul 2025, at 17:56, Nils Erbach <nils@calibrion.ai> wrote:
> 
> Dear team at Cathay Innovation,
> 
> I am reaching out to introduce Calibrion AI, a data annotation platform enabling enterprises and organisations to fine-tune LLMs to their proprietary data. We built a working product and already reached pilot-stage interest from European and US companies in the eCom vertical.
> 
> While most LLM infra and dev platforms focus on model architecture and LLMOps, we focus on unlocking enterprise adoption through (proprietary) data and fine-tuning workflows.
> 
> Calibrion envisions a future where businesses harness their proprietary data and domain expertise to build specialized LLMs, turning generative AI from a threat into a strategic asset for both internal and market-facing applications.
> 
> Our mission is to become a market-leading platform for LLM customization and domain adaptation, setting the standard for performance and usability in the enterprise AI space.
> 
> To underscore the scale of this opportunity and the importance of what Calibrion does: In June 2025, Meta acquired a 49% stake in Scale AI (a company also focused on LLM data annotation and founded in 2016) for $14.8 billion at a $30 billion valuation. 
> 
> We are currently offering subscription for our EUR 3 m seed investment round to: 
> - expand our engineering and data science team
> - Accelerate go-to-market efforts across verticals 
> - Further develop our AI capabilities and integrate new LLM-driven features.
> 
> To accelerate the company's development, we assigned significant equity to an option pool for key employees and aim to be cash-flow positive within 18 months.
> 
> We would be happy to share our pitch deck and schedule a call to walk you through the live product. The product is fully developed and currently undergoing regular testing and stabilization.
> 
> Please find attached a letter outlining our fund-raising goals as well as a one-pager on Calibrion.
> 
> We look forward to hearing from you and would be glad to schedule a conversation at your convenience.
> 
> Best regards,
> Nils
> 
> -- 
> Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
> Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 <tel:+49+(0)30+478+662+25> ・ nils@calibrion.ai <mailto:meghdad@calibrion.ai>
> Französische Str. 20・10117 Berlin・Germany
> www.calibrion.ai <http://www.calibrion.ai/>

subject: Re: Calibrion - Updated Deck
body_text:
Hi Meghdad

Sorry about the delay, I was traveling most of the week.

We discussed Calibrion in our investment meeting. Unfortunately we came to the conclusion that the current stage of your company is not where we could be the best partner for you. We typically are the best fit at a first seven-figure round where we can help with our network and expertise for a first growth spurt. A good recent example is Neptune.ai<http://neptune.ai/>, a Polish company where we led the seed round and that got just acquired<https://neptune.ai/blog/we-are-joining-openai> by OpenAI.

I hope for your understanding and would love to stay in touch regarding your future funding needs!

Best,
Andreas

subject: Re: EU Grant Funding Consultation
body_text:
Hi Meghdad,

The FFplus Track 2 is open for applications.
€200K per applicant for a 10-month project duration.
Goal: to use AI or ML to develop software solutions, train models, and
solve business challenges with these technologies.
The deadline is in February, but it may close earlier.

Should we get this for you?

Let me know if you’d like to contact our references.

subject: Re: EU Grant Funding Consultation
body_text:
Hi Meghdad,

AI Safety Grant: The deadline for submission is the end of September.
Evaluation takes 8 weeks. Funding at the beginning of January. - With
similar programs, we achieve about a 60% success rate.
FFplus: Deadline 26th of November. Results at the end of January. Funding
at the beginning of March. - Last year, we submitted one application (Kodex
AI) and we succeeded, but of course, we cannot give predictions from one
applicant. So I would also count it as a similar program and say about a
60% success rate.
EIC Accelerator: Stage 1 submission at any time. Evaluation 8 weeks. Stage
2 preparation + evaluation takes 2-3 months. Stage 3 - one month. You could
expect funding in April. - This one is very competitive. The general
success rate is about 5-7%. Professionals can achieve about 20% success
rate.

subject: Your Antler Application – Submission Confirmed
body_text:
We’re excited to inform you that we’ve received your application to join Antler. Thank you for taking this step with us!

Kindly submit your application to only one Antler location at a time. We will prioritize processing your first application.

Our team will review your application and aim to respond within a few weeks. Please be aware that during periods when the start date of our next cohort is a bit further out, it could take us a bit longer to get back to you.

For a glimpse into the potential future with Antler, take a moment to explore our portfolio (https://content.antler.co/e3t/Ctc/2P+113/djW2rd04/VWGW566FXWYbW1_DLmx7MxDYlW3wqskQ5t39NpN3V8l8KcdhRjW69vqxv6lZ3kLVXFz0l3j_6kZN3HpZwnM33DyW4gCxzm141V_rW9jlRrH7-YXfRW2pSxNs9hNrmzW2BGqYS3HC_D3W3FsZm89hFjq1W5s8fM43xrwFHN8Tq4Gq6y1nkW3v60xQ1sGwsMW5yPq961FVF_VW4kXwFK4HpXWXW1D82Kp7sWYvjW9jWSCr3bkvkBW4YPQ5K2gLLcbW99sPfx91WX3nW2n0WSw8WLDdLW55fJKv72mvWwW1KGB7T53nlRFW2-FpjY15zS7RW7Jx90T7_fV7fW3nCPs612szcNW3QkyS_6Rjc8FW7v1JCg3F2Bt6W7RwFLk1z7nDYW9btRls5_LJBJW5h3X7t1nX0h7W2ZDDHC7NCGdqW65QWGV7w0V5fVPMpG53cJRKNW44_yJT9cqVGRW38BF102Pf1t0V9xsk_48ZhMmW4VKZVF8RTj-tW50n_3C54_74MW5R8_fr16WFmrW1wgqFH1jxThqW9h8g8Z2_DDZsW5bDFT-7xHk4GW7rPpTm90KpHVW1MHPj38JzJ0hW8Fc8862RcDVZW5vLdwJ8YHn2YW41kqbT8vzZTcW6DHt6j68XpbqW3dmk6R7LnGnXW4RSj6J5Sscp7W990b2_82tHmLW6XBPXt45YqkPW99zdBt5B1BvkW2y-YzY49wkGcW5BXK9W6QwfznW8xbpNt4yMVrCVFhRcQ25rVJ2W82M3YW4d4bjZN97WHtHMHt4FW5Lfc_n1Vk_lSW42Mll_73g77GW296HlG2CPcTnW46TzFZ1KGX4lVr-1Qh6_WpxtW5GDgLN7SV51zW5qTm7Q8p-DZ9N1QYQ5JR0JKFW7_qXm53G2b9xW7RbPph6gL5tHW7Jjz__5T3cC-W4dLLD267mp0cW2mQnsd4QRT9PN1__WKpNwTqZW2cvQ5613ydsGW2Xfslc34gTDJLk3qMWW4GlW1ZJFLS1FttX2W5WtZ218z9DYMW7YqN5K30RCN3W3KznF8686nQwW1kp8b044DVP8W2PztFz4txpChW5TdPyw2zH8bQW3G0Trv4Ws7kGW7h82B37BbHKWW1BYyv31d67sqW23_gBK4jjvR_f7cl8CC04 ) .

subject: Make ARR Useful Again
body_text:
View this post on the web at https://speedrun.substack.com/p/make-arr-useful-again

💡  This Week’s Big Ideas
🏘️  Madeline and Ben are building a reverse social network to restore the feeling of being part of a village. This is their story [ https://substack.com/redirect/a7c7d9cb-7d4e-4675-9d50-c2dcf7b2d546?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]. 
🔋  Jon Lai argues that “founder energy” can be a learned skill [ https://substack.com/redirect/9b7c695b-2e86-41c1-ba1b-8b5ae1c97ddc?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
💰  Andrew Chen says it’s a miracle VC works at all [ https://substack.com/redirect/f4695c66-5597-4e4c-b49f-53f4db78b25d?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
📢  Bella Nazzari had a job opp for people who want to work in a sugar factory [ https://substack.com/redirect/72d1bd02-ddad-43be-baec-561338360da7?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
💼  Join our talent network [ https://substack.com/redirect/681f2a98-c50c-4621-8f4a-7063ca82abff?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] for more opportunities. If we see a fit for you, we'll intro you to relevant founders in the portfolio.
As a founder, your ARR (annual recurring revenue) and its monthly cousin MRR are among the most important metrics you’ll use to measure the health of your business. Investors and media care about the figure too, so founders can sometimes be tempted to adopt creative definitions and calculation methods when communicating ARR.
The most common sins of ARR calculation include:
annualizing a spiky month
counting usage surges as if they’ll recur forever
or sneaking one-time, non-predictable, or non-contractual fees into a “recurring” bucket.
The best definition of ARR isn’t the one that makes your fundraising deck look good. It’s the one that gives you the most insight into the current state of your company. Relying on overly creative definitions of ARR means your numbers are lying to you.
The best definition of ARR is one that helps you operate. If your ARR ticks up, can you explain which lever you pulled (pricing, packaging, activation, expansion) and predict next month’s number? That’s the only bar that matters. You maximize the business, not the metric.
With that in mind, it’s worth being definitive on these terms.
What revenue is, and what MRR and ARR are
GAAP revenue is the rule-bound number stamped and approved by your accountant. It’s pretty simple, you have revenue when you deliver the service. This is done ratably for classic subscriptions and by consumption for usage models.
MRR, by contrast, is a management metric. This is a point-in-time monthly value of recurring revenue you expect to keep happening. It’s tempting to say “ARR = 12 × MRR” and stop there. But that identity is only useful if “recurring” really means recurring—meaning you exclude one-offs (implementation, training, hardware) and you’re disciplined about usage. The clean approach is simple to state and powerful in practice. You should treat subscription and contracted minimums as MRR. Truly variable usage is its own line item and only when it proves stable or becomes contractually committed you can graduate it into MRR and ARR.
Still, there is lots of nuance that occurs, even in how public market companies, subject to stringent reporting requirements, treat ARR. An analysis by Mostly Metrics and Virtua Research [ https://substack.com/redirect/6ec44586-502d-4108-a959-07614bef268a?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] found that there’s huge variation in the ways that top tech companies calculate it.
Among these definitions, we have no strong opinion which is best. And that’s the whole point! ARR is for you first and investors second. These examples are less commandments and more guardrails. Your MRR and ARR should reconcile, with a sensible lead/lag, to the way your model actually becomes GAAP revenue.
a16z speedrun General Manager Josh Lu says that founders should be aware that when you see large companies calculating ARR as MRRx12, their MRR is a lot more stable compared to startups. “Startups have spikier MRR,” Lu says. “MRR and MRR growth is all that matters, and reporting ARR as a brand new startup is kinda dumb.”
As Investment Partner Troy Kirwin points out [ https://substack.com/redirect/711629b6-b0df-4b6b-92c9-13dde30e107a?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ], there’s also an unfortunate naming schema in the discourse where Annual Recurring Revenue (“ARR”) shares the same acronym as Annual Run Rate (Revenue), or as some founders mistakenly describe it, ‘ARR.’
“It’s important for founders to be VERY clear with how they are calculating ‘ARR’ in their decks or memos,” Kirwin says. “Unintentionally misleading investors with a blanket ‘ARR’ definition can lead to a breakage of trust.”
The AI wrinkle
AI inference takes every MRR bad habit and amplifies them. Demand is often bursty (launch weeks, seasonal spikes), units are tiny (per 1,000 tokens, per request), and capacity can be either provisioned or opportunistic. That means you need an MRR policy that respects how the infrastructure is priced.
A founder-practical way to handle this without twelve sub-metrics is to:
Keep MRR for the part of your AI product that is contracted with items like fixed subscription fees, platform access, support tiers, and committed throughput minimums. If a customer signs for a model unit per month or a token floor, that is MRR.
Track Measured Usage separately for everything else: pay-as-you-go tokens, burst capacity, overages. Report it every month with a trailing average. If usage stabilizes for, say, six months, you can promote it into MRR with a straight face.
Allow us to offer two addenda that will save you from heartache and audits from Deloitte:
First, resist the urge to smuggle multi-year step-ups into today’s topline. Calling exit-year contracted revenue your “MRR” makes you look bigger right now but won’t track to GAAP or cash, and sophisticated investors will haircut it anyway. Second, you should have a strong mental model for “MRR + measured usage” that translates into GAAP revenue each quarter.
Ultimately, ginning up investor hype using shaky metrics won’t make your company great. Define your MRR/ARR in such a way you can make smart choices, faster.

subject: B2B SaaS – AI Infrastructure / Data - Early Stage Product Ready - Early Enterprise Traction – Seed Round - Calibrion AI
body_text:
Dear Team at D11Z. Ventures,

We are building Calibrion, an agent-native SaaS platform that removes one of the biggest bottlenecks in today's AI development: creating and validating training datasets at scale.

Current data annotation market dynamics enable rapid revenue growth (€100M - 1B ARR reported in 2024/2025 within 12–24 months) for data annotation platforms with strong technology and product–market fit, underscoring the current demand for high-quality AI data.

Calibrion positions itself as the first human-agent-native solution, approaching the problem with a new perspective and value proposition, delivering:
- Agentic data annotation that accelerates annotation up to ~100x (from months to hours)
- Radical cost savings
- Proprietary three-layer quality control to ensure accuracy at scale
- 35% faster human-in-the-loop through research driven optimized workflows

Our moat compounds across:
- Years of world-class AI, LLM, and agentic expertise
- Patentable processes
- Early distribution into some of the world’s leading enterprises.
- The inherent complexity of the problem we solve
- A fast, high-performing team

We recently signed paid pilots with Zalando, Siemens Energy, and are negotiating additional deals with Cotopaxi (US sustainable outdoor brand) and LILT (US AI-driven enterprise translation leader). 

We are seeking EUR 1,200,000 in seed funding to expand our team, onboard additional clients, and implement product-led-growth within the next 9 - 12 months.

Our founder and CEO, Meghdad, holds a PhD in CS with a specialization in NLP, and has over nine years of applied AI product experience.

Given your investment focus, we believe Calibrion could be a strong fit. I’ve attached our one-pager and pitch deck, and included a link to our product demos here <https://docs.google.com/document/d/1bMC1AW6Wu-eTxOIhEkro05LAr8QYiMcfU7WgXCZe4vQ/edit?usp=sharing>.

We’d be happy to walk you through the product and share what we’re building toward.

Nils Erbach

Calibrion AI
Tel: +49 1 <tel:+49+(0)30+478+662+25>7662205065 ・ nils@calibrion.ai <mailto:meghdad@calibrion.ai>
Kolonnenstr. 8・10827 Berlin・Germany
www.calibrion.ai <https://www.calibrion.ai/>

￼

￼

subject: Receipt for subscription payment Nov 28, 2025
Following up on Calibrion AI – Seed Round & Product Demo
body_text:
Dear team at Yaday Venture Capital,

I hope this note finds you well.

I’m following up on my initial introduction of Calibrion AI and our EUR 3 million seed round. We’re encouraged by the early traction with pilot-stage interest from both European and US companies and would love to discuss how we see this opportunity scaling with the right partners on board.

As mentioned, our platform sits at the critical intersection of LLM deployment and proprietary data, helping enterprises unlock the full value of generative AI by tailoring models to their unique domains — an area that we believe will define the next wave of enterprise AI adoption.

Given Yaday’s focus on early-stage ventures with strong defensibility and global potential, I think there could be great alignment here. I’d be glad to share our pitch deck and arrange a short call or product walkthrough to dive deeper.

Would you have 30 minutes next week for an initial conversation? I’m flexible and can adjust to your preferred time.

Looking forward to your feedback and hoping we can connect soon.

Best regards,
Nils

--
Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 ・ nils@calibrion.ai <>
Französische Str. 20・10117 Berlin・Germany
www.calibrion.ai <http://www.calibrion.ai/>
￼￼


> On 22. Jul 2025, at 17:13, Nils Erbach <nils@calibrion.ai> wrote:
> 
> Dear team at Yaday Venture Capital,
> 
> I am reaching out to introduce Calibrion AI, a data annotation platform enabling enterprises and organisations to fine-tune LLMs to their proprietary data. We built a working product and already reached pilot-stage interest from European and US companies in the eCom vertical.
> 
> While most LLM infra and dev platforms focus on model architecture and LLMOps, we focus on unlocking enterprise adoption through (proprietary) data and fine-tuning workflows.
> 
> Calibrion envisions a future where businesses harness their proprietary data and domain expertise to build specialized LLMs, turning generative AI from a threat into a strategic asset for both internal and market-facing applications.
> 
> Our mission is to become a market-leading platform for LLM customization and domain adaptation, setting the standard for performance and usability in the enterprise AI space.
> 
> To underscore the scale of this opportunity and the importance of what Calibrion does: In June 2025, Meta acquired a 49% stake in Scale AI (a company also focused on LLM data annotation and founded in 2016) for $14.8 billion at a $30 billion valuation. 
> 
> We are currently offering subscription for our EUR 3 m seed investment round to: 
> - expand our engineering and data science team
> - Accelerate go-to-market efforts across verticals 
> - Further develop our AI capabilities and integrate new LLM-driven features.
> 
> To accelerate the company's development, we assigned significant equity to an option pool for key employees and aim to be cash-flow positive within 18 months.
> 
> We would be happy to share our pitch deck and schedule a call to walk you through the live product. The product is fully developed and currently undergoing regular testing and stabilization.
> 
> Please find attached a letter outlining our fund-raising goals as well as a one-pager on Calibrion.
> 
> We look forward to hearing from you and would be glad to schedule a conversation at your convenience.
> 
> Best regards,
> Nils
> 
> -- 
> Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
> Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 <tel:+49+(0)30+478+662+25> ・ nils@calibrion.ai <mailto:meghdad@calibrion.ai>

subject: When Your AI Needs Its Own Nuclear Reactor
body_text:
### Run ads IRL with AdQuick

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/56e2a130-f390-4395-b9b3-a8b71bb22dd2/AdQuick_Newsletter_Hero_2025_2.png?t=1738376710)
Follow image link: (https://www.AdQuick.com/?utm_campaign=VPSLCCALRN&utm_source=beehiiv&_bhiiv=opp_794dc9b9-030f-4b25-99d6-81bf2f791ad4_c33e6721&bhcl_id=a1c37a73-e9aa-4b0d-838f-e91831b6f38a_bd74a657-278f-4ddb-833c-61e078d26306_70dc14dc-168a-41ff-81d7-6d2f0075867e)
Caption: 

With [AdQuick](https://www.AdQuick.com/?utm_campaign=VPSLCCALRN&utm_source=beehiiv&_bhiiv=opp_794dc9b9-030f-4b25-99d6-81bf2f791ad4_c33e6721&bhcl_id=a1c37a73-e9aa-4b0d-838f-e91831b6f38a_bd74a657-278f-4ddb-833c-61e078d26306_70dc14dc-168a-41ff-81d7-6d2f0075867e), you can now easily plan, deploy and measure campaigns just as easily as digital ads, making them a no-brainer to add to your team’s toolbox.

You can learn more at [www.AdQuick.com](https://www.AdQuick.com/?utm_campaign=VPSLCCALRN&utm_source=beehiiv&_bhiiv=opp_794dc9b9-030f-4b25-99d6-81bf2f791ad4_c33e6721&bhcl_id=a1c37a73-e9aa-4b0d-838f-e91831b6f38a_bd74a657-278f-4ddb-833c-61e078d26306_70dc14dc-168a-41ff-81d7-6d2f0075867e)

# 

Listen, I need to tell you about something wild that just happened on the Joe Rogan podcast. NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang—the guy whose chips power basically every AI system you've heard of—just casually dropped that tech companies are going to be building their own nuclear reactors within six or seven years. Not looking into it. Not exploring options. _Building them._

Here's the thing: this isn't some futurist fantasy. This is happening because the alternative is that AI development just... stops.

## The Math That Breaks Everything

The numbers here are absurd. A single NVIDIA Blackwell GPU pulls 1,200 watts. Modern AI racks are hitting 240 kilowatts—enough to power 200 American homes from one rack. A serious AI training cluster needs 500 megawatts running 24/7. That's a mid-sized city's worth of continuous power.

By 2030, US data centers will jump from 4% to potentially 12% of national electricity consumption. The deployed H100 chips alone will consume as much electricity as the entire country of Georgia. The entire country.

And here's the kicker: the grid can't deliver it. Not won't—_can't_. There's plenty of power generation out there, but the distribution infrastructure is maxed out. Upgrading transmission lines takes five to ten years. AI companies need power now.

## So They're Just... Building Reactors?

Yeah. And they're already signing the checks.

Microsoft is reopening Three Mile Island. Google signed a deal with Kairos Power for 500 megawatts of molten salt reactors by 2035, with the first one online in 2030. Amazon dropped $650 million to buy a data center campus right next to a nuclear plant, securing access to 960 megawatts. Oracle's Larry Ellison is planning a one-gigawatt data center powered by three small modular reactors.

This is real money moving right now. The technology exists—NuScale just got regulatory approval for their 77-megawatt design in May 2025. They're the only SMR vendor with full NRC approval, and they're on track for deployment by 2030.

## What This Actually Means

Here's what Huang understood that makes this whole thing click: companies will "build as much power as you need, and you can contribute back to the grid." Tech giants are becoming energy companies. They're not just consumers anymore—they're producers who can sell excess capacity back to utilities.

This privatizes what used to be public infrastructure. When Microsoft owns a nuclear plant and Google operates a fleet of reactors, that's a different world than everyone sharing the same electrical grid. It's about energy independence, sure, but it's also about competitive advantage. If your AI training runs on reliable nuclear baseload and your competitor is fighting for grid capacity, you win.

The geopolitical angle matters too. Huang explicitly tied this to US competitiveness: "If the United States doesn't grow, we will have no prosperity." China is building 150 new reactors by 2035. This is an arms race where the ammunition is electricity.

## The Wildcard Nobody's Talking About

There's one problem: uranium. Global production only meets 80-90% of current reactor demand. The US needs 50 million pounds annually but produces maybe 5 million domestically. Industry projections show a potential one billion pound deficit over the next 15 years.

So while tech companies are racing to build reactors, they might be racing into a supply bottleneck that nobody's priced in yet.

Six to seven years. That's the timeline. By 2032, your cloud services might be powered by a reactor that Google built. Wild times.


———

You are reading a plain text version of this post. For the best experience, copy and paste this link in your browser to view the post online:
https://www.aiweekly.com/p/when-your-ai-needs-its-own-nuclear-reactor

subject: Loud Isn't Enough
body_text:
View this post on the web at https://speedrun.substack.com/p/loud-isnt-enough

💡  This Week’s Big Ideas
✨  Jordan Mazer has curated a list of 19 companies that are hiring now [ https://substack.com/redirect/37560fc8-a524-4eb0-a995-4f600baa991a?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] after recently raising rounds
🤖  Box CEO Aaron Levie [ https://substack.com/redirect/2855ed88-5765-4ba5-a106-fcd87ca15a31?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] tells us that “Founders who understand what AI agents unlock will lead the next decade of startups.”
🌁  This Thanksgiving, Jon Lai is thankful [ https://substack.com/redirect/b06e6c40-db76-4f7a-9a0a-1e3e91f62afe?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] for the people that make up Silicon Valley
💼  Join our talent network [ https://substack.com/redirect/1d4247e8-dd5d-4071-bd6d-5752acb8cdc2?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] for more opportunities. If we see a fit for you, we'll intro you to relevant founders in the portfolio.
Today’s essay is a guest post by Evan Armstrong [ https://substack.com/redirect/d702d971-6a7a-481f-8809-ed13631774b5?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ], founder and CEO of The Leverage [ https://substack.com/redirect/32aa81a9-34fa-448f-9838-38629d6f8e7c?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ], a newsletter about shifts in the tech market. For more from Evan, be sure to subscribe. 
Evan argues that having a clear message makes all the difference when you’re trying to build awareness for your startup. We hope you enjoy it. —the a16z speedrun team
In normal times, startup marketing can often be genteel and orderly. When a platform shift is underway and there are billions on the line, however, things tend to become a bit more… aggressive. Pitches become battlecries as founders strive to stand out from the crowd of contenders.
Brash messaging and stunt marketing doesn’t always work, but the best founders use a more aggressive approach to draw attention to the product and the problem it’s solving, not merely to the company itself.
Marc Benioff understood this better than anyone.
It was February 22, 2000, and marchers in crimson t-shirts were pacing the sidewalks outside a San Francisco convention center, bellowing about software’s imminent demise. They waved signs, they chanted slogans. The scene was ridiculous, but that was precisely the idea. This circus was a Salesforce marketing operation, a stunt to promote Salesforce’s cloud-native CRM as an alternative to traditional on-premise software like the kind used by their competitors.
The target of Salesforce’s antics was competing CRM provider Siebel Systems, which had rented out the convention center for a customer conference.
Benioff had hired 25 actors, dressed them in bright red t-shirts emblazoned with “death to software,” and had them march with signs shouting slogans like [ https://substack.com/redirect/4dbe891a-3818-4850-932a-03ed2c8a451c?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] “Red rover, red rover, software is over.” He even hired a fake news network called “Channel 22” to show up in a news truck and interview the protesters like it was a real political movement.
Benioff even considered hiring an armored tank driven by someone dressed as General Patton but decided that might be “too outlandish [ https://substack.com/redirect/fb502aa7-c0f8-41f8-86f6-c3dba192407d?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ].”
When Siebel tried to respond by parking three semi-trucks in front of the center to block the protest, they just ended up blocking their own signage. Next, they called the police. This was a catastrophic error. Suddenly the tiny startup that nobody had heard of was all over the Wall Street Journal, Forbes, Business Week, and the New York Times. Business Week called Salesforce “the ant at the picnic”—which was exactly the underdog narrative Benioff wanted.
But Benioff wasn’t done. Later that same month, Salesforce threw their “End of Software” launch party at San Francisco’s Regency Theater with 1,500 attendees and a concert by the B-52s. To get in, guests had to bring old software discs to dump into overflowing trash bins.  [ https://substack.com/redirect/4dbe891a-3818-4850-932a-03ed2c8a451c?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]The party had “Software-Free” zones and games themed around the death of traditional software. It was theatrical, absurd, and once again, impossible to ignore.
Then came the Cannes taxi heist. Siebel held their most exclusive event in Cannes, France. Most executives flew into Nice and took a 45-minute taxi ride to the venue. So Salesforce rented every single airport taxi. They plastered the “No Software” logo on the cabs, hired drivers trained to pitch Salesforce, and turned a captive audience into a sales opportunity. Imagine being a Siebel executive stepping off your flight, looking for any cab, and discovering that every single one has been hijacked by your scrappiest competitor. Siebel called the police. Again.
In San Diego, at another Siebel event, they ran the same play with bike rickshaws.  [ https://substack.com/redirect/1c49c625-a03e-4934-b361-b3868c77cafa?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]Over and over again, Salesforce was making it physically impossible to attend a Siebel event without encountering their message.
The genius was in the message behind the stunts, not the stunts themselves. Salesforce wasn’t saying “our CRM has better features than Siebel.” They declared the entire category of installed software obsolete. By pointing to the flaws of on-prem software, they made Siebel look like they were selling typewriters in the age of computers. Every IT executive who had suffered through a months-long Siebel implementation, every sales team dealing with buggy on-premise software, heard “The End of Software” and thought: yes, please, for the love of God, make it stop.
The “No Software” logo—the crossed-out software box that looked like the Ghostbusters symbol—became their calling card. Simple, memorable, instantly understandable. You didn’t need a PowerPoint deck to know what Salesforce stood for.
And the positioning was lethal because Benioff cast Salesforce as the underdog fighting the stodgy, bloated giant. When you’re David against Goliath, people root for you. When Goliath calls the cops on your peaceful (if theatrical) protest, people root for you even harder. Every time Siebel tried to shut them down, they amplified the message.
And it worked. The ant at the picnic became a $240+ billion company while Siebel ended up being acquired by Oracle. The scrappy startup that threw fake protests became the establishment they once fought against.
What Founders Can Actually Learn From This
Some founders will read this and think: “That’s a fun story, but it wouldn’t work today.” Benioff benefited from the relative peace and calm before we invented short-form video and memes. However, that attitude is missing the point. Benioff one because he correctly predicted the benefits of the shift to cloud, and then aligned his marketing to those ideas. In AI today, too many early stage founders just say some variation of “[INSERT ADJECTIVE] intelligence,” tell customers they will make them 10x more efficient, and get back to coding. Remarkably, this has worked for the last few years, but as AI starts to saturate it will take deliberate positioning to stand out once again. Salesforce’s story has five takeaways:
Create a villain, not just a competitor. Salesforce could’ve just gone with a “here’s why the cloud is better” pitch. Instead, they made Siebel represent everything broken about the old world..  When you have a villain, you have a story, and stories spread. Feature comparison charts don’t.
Make it visceral and physical. The red shirts, the crossed-out software logo, the fake protests, the hijacked taxis were things people could see, touch, photograph, and talk about. It was dead simple and inherently viral (even pre social media!).
Attack the category, not just the leader. Salesforce didn’t try to be “better software.” They declared software itself dead. By making the problem existential, it made their product more important.
Relentless beats clever. One protest was memorable. But the protest, then the party, then the Cannes taxis, then the San Diego rickshaws—that’s a campaign. Most founders do one creative stunt and then retreat to safety and easy Google Ads. Don’t be lazy or simply bask in the success of your first idea. This has to be a team or skillset you build up internally so that the motion never slows down.
Always punch up, never down. This playbook only works when you’re smaller than your target. The instant Salesforce became the incumbent, these tactics would look desperate and bullying. Know your position in the power dynamic and act accordingly.
The uncomfortable truth is that most B2B founders are too scared to do anything remotely like this. They’ll read this essay, think it’s entertaining, and then go back to optimizing their conversion funnels. They’ll worry about being “professional” or “appropriate” or “too aggressive.” They’ll convince themselves that stunts don’t work in their specific vertical. And that’s fine—that’s why there’s still opportunity.
The founders who actually have the guts to identify a clear villain, position against an entire category, and relentlessly show up where their competitors are the ones who build the next Salesforce. Everyone else is just renting market share until someone braver shows up.
Like this post? Forward it to your team! And be sure to subscribe to Evan Armstrong’s newsletter, The Leverage [ https://substack.com/redirect/d6a25784-c06f-4f1c-b96b-fdda32c474c4?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ].
For more weekly dives into the world of early stage startups, subscribe to our newsletter below.

subject: Re: B2B SaaS – Product ready - Early mayor enterprise traction – Seed Round EUR 1.25M; Calibrion.ai - AI Infrastructure / Data annotation
body_text:
Dear Team at ABC Capital Partners,

We are building Calibrion, an agent-native data annotation platform that removes one of the largest barriers to building AI models at the scale and speed required today: creating high-quality training datasets.

Current data annotation market dynamics enable rapid revenue growth (>€100M ARR within 12–24 months) for data annotation platforms with strong technology and product–market fit, underscoring the current demand for high-quality AI data.

Calibrion position itself as the first human-agent-native solution, approaching the problem with a new perspective and value proposition, delivering:

- Agentic data annotation that accelerates annotation up to ~100x (from months to hours)
- Radical cost savings
- Proprietary three-layer quality control to ensure accuracy at scale
- 35% faster human-in-the-loop through research driven optimized workflows

Our moat compounds across:
- Years of world-class AI, LLM, and agentic expertise
- Patentable processes
- Early distribution into some of the world’s leading enterprises.
- The inherent complexity of the problem we solve
- A fast, high-performing team

We recently signed paid pilots with Zalando, Siemens Energy, and are negotiating additional deals with Cotopaxi (US sustainable outdoor brand) and LILT (US AI-driven enterprise translation leader). 

We are seeking EUR 1,200,000 in seed funding to expand our team, onboard additional clients, and implement product-led-growth within the next 9 - 12 months.

Our founder and CEO, Meghdad, Holds a PhD in CS with a specialization in NLP, and has over nine years of applied AI product experience.

subject: B2B SaaS – Product ready - Early mayor enterprise traction – Seed Round EUR 1.25M; Calibrion.ai - AI Infrastructure / Data annotation
body_text:
Dear Team at ABC Capital Partners,

We are building Calibrion, an agent-native data annotation platform adressing one of the largest barriers to organisational GenAI adoption: enabling proprietary LLM training through high-quality, rapid data annotation -> creating training data for LLMs. 

Current market dynamics allow for very quick (>EUR 100M ARR within 12-24 months) revenue growth rates at excellent technological solutions and product-market-fit, underscoring industry-wide demand for superior data-annotation solutions.

Calibrion as the first human-agent-native solution delivers: 
- Agentic data annotation that accelerates annotation up to 100x (from months to hours)
- Cost savings of around 90%
- Built-in proprietary quality control to ensure accuracy at scale 
- 35% faster human-in-the-loop through research driven optimized workflows

Our moat compounds across: 
- Years of world-class AI, LLM, and agentic expertise
- Patentable processes
- The inherent complexity of the problem we solve
- A fast, high-performing team
- Early distribution into some of the world’s leading enterprises.

We recently signed pilots with Zalando and Siemens Energy AG E-Commerce division, and are negotiating pilots with Cotopaxi (US sustainable outdoor brand) and LILT (AI-driven translation leader). 

We are seeking EUR 1,200,000 in seed funding to expand our team, onboard additional clients, and implement product-led-growth within the next 6-9 months.

Our founder and CEO, Meghdad, Holds a PhD in CS with a specialization in NLP, and has over nine years of applied AI product experience. 

Given your investment focus, we think Calibrion could be a strong fit. You’ll find our one-pager and pitch-deck attached.Here is a link to our product demos <https://docs.google.com/document/d/1bMC1AW6Wu-eTxOIhEkro05LAr8QYiMcfU7WgXCZe4vQ/edit?usp=sharing>.

We’d be happy to walk you through the product and share what we are building towards.

Best regards,
Nils 


Nils Erbach

Calibrion AI
Tel: +49 1 <tel:+49+(0)30+478+662+25>7662205065 ・ nils@calibrion.ai <mailto:meghdad@calibrion.ai>
Kolonnenstr. 8・10827 Berlin・Germany
www.calibrion.ai <https://www.calibrion.ai/>

We are building Calibrion, an agent-native data annotation platform that
removes one of the largest barriers to building AI models at the scale and
speed required today: creating high-quality training datasets.

Current data annotation market dynamics enable rapid revenue growth (>€100M
ARR within 12–24 months) for data annotation platforms with strong
technology and product–market fit, underscoring the current demand for
high-quality AI data.

Calibrion position itself as the first human-agent-native solution,
approaching the problem with a new perspective and value proposition,
delivering:

- Agentic data annotation that accelerates annotation up to ~100x (from
months to hours)
- Radical cost savings
- Proprietary three-layer quality control to ensure accuracy at scale
- 35% faster human-in-the-loop through research driven optimized workflows

Our moat compounds across:
- Years of world-class AI, LLM, and agentic expertise
- Patentable processes
- Early distribution into some of the world’s leading enterprises.
- The inherent complexity of the problem we solve
- A fast, high-performing team

We recently signed paid pilots with Zalando, Siemens Energy, and are
negotiating additional deals with Cotopaxi (US sustainable outdoor brand)
and LILT (US AI-driven enterprise translation leader).

We are seeking EUR 1,200,000 in seed funding to expand our team, onboard
additional clients, and implement product-led-growth within the next 9 - 12
months.

Our founder and CEO, Meghdad, Holds a PhD in CS with a specialization in
NLP, and has over nine years of applied AI product experience.

Given your investment focus, we believe Calibrion could be a strong fit.
I’ve attached our one-pager and pitch deck, and included a link to our
product demos here
<https://docs.google.com/document/d/1bMC1AW6Wu-eTxOIhEkro05LAr8QYiMcfU7WgXCZe4vQ/edit?usp=sharing>
.

If this is of interest, we’d be happy to walk you through the product and
share what we’re building toward.

subject: Re: B2B SaaS – Product ready - Early mayor enterprise traction – Seed Round EUR 1.25M; Calibrion.ai - AI Infrastructure / Data annotation
body_text:
We are building Calibrion, an agent-native data annotation platform that
removes one of the largest barriers to building AI models at today's
required scale and speed: creating training data for AI.

The data annotation market is rapidly consolidating at an unprecedented
scale: leading players like Turing, Scale AI, and Surge AI are generating
hundreds of millions in annual revenue, with recent deals valuing companies
between $15–30B. Even late entrants and pivots are reaching $100M+ ARR
within months, underscoring how critical AI data has become.

Calibrion as the first human-agent-native solution approaches this problem
with a new perspective and value proposition:
- Agentic data annotation that accelerates annotation up to ~100x (from
months to hours)
- Radical cost savings
- Proprietary three-layer quality control to ensure accuracy at scale
- 35% faster human-in-the-loop through research driven optimized workflows

Our moat compounds across patentable processes, inherent complexity of the
problem we are solving, years of relevant academic and applied
experience, early
distribution into some of the world’s leading enterprises, and more.

We recently signed paid pilots with Zalando, Siemens Energy AG's E-Commerce
division, and are negotiating additional deals with Cotopaxi (US
sustainable outdoor brand) and LILT (AI-driven translation leader).

We are seeking EUR 1,200,000 in seed funding to expand our team, onboard
additional clients, and implement product-led-growth within the next 6-9
months.

Our founder and CEO, Meghdad, holds a PhD in CS with a specialization in
NLP, and has over nine years of applied AI product experience.

Given your investment focus, we think Calibrion could be a strong fit.
You’ll find our one-pager, pitch-deck, and here is a link to our product
demos
<https://docs.google.com/document/d/1bMC1AW6Wu-eTxOIhEkro05LAr8QYiMcfU7WgXCZe4vQ/edit?usp=sharing>
.

We’d be glad to arrange a 30-minute call to walk you through the product
and discuss our upcoming milestones.

subject: B2B SaaS – Product ready - Early mayor enterprise traction – Seed Round EUR 1.25M; Calibrion.ai - AI Infrastructure / Data annotation
body_text:
Dear Team at ABC Capital Partners,

We are building Calibrion, an agent-native data annotation platform adressing one of the largest barriers to organisational GenAI adoption: enabling proprietary LLM training through high-quality, rapid annotation. 

Calibrion delivers: 
- Agentic data annotation that accelerates annotation up to 100x (from months to hours)
- Cost savings of around 90%
- Built-in proprietary quality control to ensure accuracy at scale 
- 35% faster human-in-the-loop through research driven optimized workflows

Our moat compounds across: 
- Years of world-class AI, LLM, and agentic expertise
- Patentable processes
- The inherent complexity of the problem we solve
- A fast, high-performing team
- Early distribution into some of the world’s leading enterprises.

We recently signed a 6 months paid pilot with a mayor German e-commerce retailer, secured a verbal commitment from Siemens Energy AG E-Commerce division, and are negotiating pilots with Cotopaxi (US sustainable outdoor brand) and LILT (AI-driven translation leader). 

We are seeking EUR 1,250,000 in seed funding to expand our team, onboard additional clients, and implement product-led-growth within the next 6-9 months.

Our founder and CEO, Meghdad, Holds a PhD in Computer Science with a specialization in Natural Language Processing, and has over eight years of applied AI product experience. 

Given your investment focus, we think Calibrion could be a strong fit. You’ll find our one-pager, pitch-deck, and product demos attached. We’d be glad to arrange a 30-minute call to walk you through the product and discuss and our upcoming milestones.

Best regards,
Nils 


Nils Erbach

Calibrion AI
Tel: +49 1 <tel:+49+(0)30+478+662+25>7662205065 ・ nils@calibrion.ai <mailto:meghdad@calibrion.ai>
Kolonnenstr. 8・10827 Berlin・Germany
www.calibrion.ai <https://www.calibrion.ai/>
