subject: How to Find Your Wedge
body_text:
View this post on the web at https://speedrun.substack.com/p/how-to-find-your-wedge

💡  This Week’s Big Ideas
👩🏻‍💻  Do you prefer working at “BigCos” or startups? Jordan Carver has a poll for you. [ https://substack.com/redirect/c6abc21e-bb36-45c5-bd55-d28d31eeb13e?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
🐶  Macy Mills shamelessly farmed her foster dog for clout on LinkedIn [ https://substack.com/redirect/5bd6641e-d096-48fa-a790-120fff1e00a3?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
📈  Troy Kirwin says people underestimate the magic of a strong cold email [ https://substack.com/redirect/a2068a5c-3ee5-4422-8b2f-eaee0fc319cb?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
📢  Shrikala Kashyap shares how founders are using events like Tech Week as a marketing channel [ https://substack.com/redirect/76df1720-3b22-4447-b5f3-0e9c3a47f657?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
💼  Join our talent network [ https://substack.com/redirect/bc53e483-8c6b-4932-98c8-610830e0b351?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] for more opportunities. If we see a fit for you, we'll intro you to relevant founders in the portfolio.
Today’s newsletter is guest authored by a16z speedrun General Partner Jon Lai. Be sure to follow him on X [ https://substack.com/redirect/b5026811-107a-4fb2-ad25-173ad02fd96c?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] and LinkedIn [ https://substack.com/redirect/42b87b6e-8290-493b-ada4-7dc89c6e5662?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] for more.
A mistake we see many founders make is tackling too large of a product vision starting off.
You hear the truism that good start-ups tackle large markets, and so you start building a Maximum Viable Product that solves multiple problems at once or replaces an entire incumbent. For example, setting out to build a “Figma killer” or a “LinkedIn replacement.”
But the larger the vision, the longer it takes to build, and the more time and money you’ll burn before you can tell whether your product theory is correct.
In general, I advise start-up founders to avoid accidentally building Maximum Viable Products, and to instead start with a tightly focused initial product wedge that they can tackle with their current team size.
Right-Size Problems For Right-Size Teams
We’ve met many teams who spent over a year building their theory of the perfect product, only to discover that users ultimately wanted something very different or that the market had evolved past them in that time period.
Instead, I’m a fan of the “right size problem for the right size team” approach. Ask yourself: What’s an appropriately scoped problem that a team with your (limited) resources can execute on in a first-class way? This is what we call a product “wedge.”
Take for example Amazon’s books-only marketplace or Facebook’s college photo sharing feature. Both were tightly scoped products that a small team could ship and maintain, and then as they succeeded they “earned” the right to scale up the team to tackle more ambitious, larger problems. Over time, Amazon became the everything marketplace, and Facebook became a global social network for everyone—but in the beginning they started life as wedges that a small team could execute to perfection.
For a pre-seed team that is bootstrapping or has raised <$500k, you should consider picking a product wedge for which you can quickly build a serviceable MVP in just 1-3 months. It can be hard to keep a pre-seed team together at low salaries for more than a year without some market validation, so every month you spend building raises the stakes. Once you have some early signal on PMF and have raised a seed round (say $2-3M in the bank and 2+ years runway), then you can grow the team and swing for a larger product vision.
Earned Secrets and Startup Speed
When it comes to picking your product wedge, ideally find a problem space for which your founding team has a unique insight. That could come from experiencing that pain point personally, or from being in environments where you saw a solution that hasn’t yet been brought to the world.
Maybe you were a PM at a large tech company and built your team a new collaboration tool to communicate better between engineers and designers and it improved productivity 10x—but no one outside your immediate team knew about it. Maybe you traveled abroad and saw how young folks in Japan were using AI to remix video in a novel storytelling format that hasn’t yet made its way to the rest of the world. We love these kinds of insights—“earned secrets”—and the beautiful thing is they can come from anywhere and anyone.
Once you’ve picked a product wedge based on an earned secret, the next step is building and testing your hypotheses quickly. Early on, speed is the main advantage start-ups have over large companies. You move faster, ship faster, and iterate faster. By going after bite-sized problems that you know well, you can leverage your speed to nail them quickly, establish dominant market share, and then expand to adjacent use-cases.
Ultimately, your first product wedge may not be the right one, and it’s likely that you’ll need to iterate and maybe even pivot your way to success. The important thing is your learning rate: how quickly are you not only shipping but also learning from user feedback, what the market wants, and adapting your product or business? Early on, making decisions to maximize learning is usually the right decision.
Having said that, there are certain product categories for which the product does need to hit a certain minimum quality bar to satisfy user requirements. Design tools, for example, usually need a certain set of features to be useful for product or visual designers who tend to be picky about their tools. Games, as entertainment products that compete with many free alternatives, usually need to hit a certain quality bar to sustain player engagement. In categories like these, a more robust set of launch features could make sense, but as start-ups we should still generally rotate toward speed over comprehensiveness where we can.
Wedges and Spears: Your Fundraising Story
But wait, some founders might ask: “Won’t I have trouble raising VC money if my initial market is too small?” My advice for fundraising is that you want to articulate a crisp story on how your initial product wedge is only the tip of the spear, and there is a much larger vision you plan to tackle over time.
One rule of thumb is to focus the first half of a pitch on your initial wedge. What are you doing over the next 12 months? And then spend the second half on “what if it works.” Show the broader vision for what happens after you’ve nailed your wedge, and think through a roadmap for how to get there.
Done well, most investors should understand the strategy—and if they don’t, they probably just aren’t the right fit!

subject: How to Find Your Wedge
body_text:
View this post on the web at https://speedrun.substack.com/p/how-to-find-your-wedge

💡  This Week’s Big Ideas
👩🏻‍💻  Do you prefer working at “BigCos” or startups? Jordan Carver has a poll for you. [ https://substack.com/redirect/c6abc21e-bb36-45c5-bd55-d28d31eeb13e?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
🐶  Macy Mills shamelessly farmed her foster dog for clout on LinkedIn [ https://substack.com/redirect/5bd6641e-d096-48fa-a790-120fff1e00a3?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
📈  Troy Kirwin says people underestimate the magic of a strong cold email [ https://substack.com/redirect/a2068a5c-3ee5-4422-8b2f-eaee0fc319cb?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
📢  Shrikala Kashyap shares how founders are using events like Tech Week as a marketing channel [ https://substack.com/redirect/76df1720-3b22-4447-b5f3-0e9c3a47f657?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ]
💼  Join our talent network [ https://substack.com/redirect/bc53e483-8c6b-4932-98c8-610830e0b351?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] for more opportunities. If we see a fit for you, we'll intro you to relevant founders in the portfolio.
Today’s newsletter is guest authored by a16z speedrun General Partner Jon Lai. Be sure to follow him on X [ https://substack.com/redirect/b5026811-107a-4fb2-ad25-173ad02fd96c?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] and LinkedIn [ https://substack.com/redirect/42b87b6e-8290-493b-ada4-7dc89c6e5662?j=eyJ1IjoiNTRjbDB4In0.woIuZ60MffrlGDsdpn_3Gb8spbUfXffYo0aHcFuwcjU ] for more.
A mistake we see many founders make is tackling too large of a product vision starting off.
You hear the truism that good start-ups tackle large markets, and so you start building a Maximum Viable Product that solves multiple problems at once or replaces an entire incumbent. For example, setting out to build a “Figma killer” or a “LinkedIn replacement.”
But the larger the vision, the longer it takes to build, and the more time and money you’ll burn before you can tell whether your product theory is correct.
In general, I advise start-up founders to avoid accidentally building Maximum Viable Products, and to instead start with a tightly focused initial product wedge that they can tackle with their current team size.
Right-Size Problems For Right-Size Teams
We’ve met many teams who spent over a year building their theory of the perfect product, only to discover that users ultimately wanted something very different or that the market had evolved past them in that time period.
Instead, I’m a fan of the “right size problem for the right size team” approach. Ask yourself: What’s an appropriately scoped problem that a team with your (limited) resources can execute on in a first-class way? This is what we call a product “wedge.”
Take for example Amazon’s books-only marketplace or Facebook’s college photo sharing feature. Both were tightly scoped products that a small team could ship and maintain, and then as they succeeded they “earned” the right to scale up the team to tackle more ambitious, larger problems. Over time, Amazon became the everything marketplace, and Facebook became a global social network for everyone—but in the beginning they started life as wedges that a small team could execute to perfection.
For a pre-seed team that is bootstrapping or has raised <$500k, you should consider picking a product wedge for which you can quickly build a serviceable MVP in just 1-3 months. It can be hard to keep a pre-seed team together at low salaries for more than a year without some market validation, so every month you spend building raises the stakes. Once you have some early signal on PMF and have raised a seed round (say $2-3M in the bank and 2+ years runway), then you can grow the team and swing for a larger product vision.
Earned Secrets and Startup Speed
When it comes to picking your product wedge, ideally find a problem space for which your founding team has a unique insight. That could come from experiencing that pain point personally, or from being in environments where you saw a solution that hasn’t yet been brought to the world.
Maybe you were a PM at a large tech company and built your team a new collaboration tool to communicate better between engineers and designers and it improved productivity 10x—but no one outside your immediate team knew about it. Maybe you traveled abroad and saw how young folks in Japan were using AI to remix video in a novel storytelling format that hasn’t yet made its way to the rest of the world. We love these kinds of insights—“earned secrets”—and the beautiful thing is they can come from anywhere and anyone.
Once you’ve picked a product wedge based on an earned secret, the next step is building and testing your hypotheses quickly. Early on, speed is the main advantage start-ups have over large companies. You move faster, ship faster, and iterate faster. By going after bite-sized problems that you know well, you can leverage your speed to nail them quickly, establish dominant market share, and then expand to adjacent use-cases.
Ultimately, your first product wedge may not be the right one, and it’s likely that you’ll need to iterate and maybe even pivot your way to success. The important thing is your learning rate: how quickly are you not only shipping but also learning from user feedback, what the market wants, and adapting your product or business? Early on, making decisions to maximize learning is usually the right decision.
Having said that, there are certain product categories for which the product does need to hit a certain minimum quality bar to satisfy user requirements. Design tools, for example, usually need a certain set of features to be useful for product or visual designers who tend to be picky about their tools. Games, as entertainment products that compete with many free alternatives, usually need to hit a certain quality bar to sustain player engagement. In categories like these, a more robust set of launch features could make sense, but as start-ups we should still generally rotate toward speed over comprehensiveness where we can.
Wedges and Spears: Your Fundraising Story
But wait, some founders might ask: “Won’t I have trouble raising VC money if my initial market is too small?” My advice for fundraising is that you want to articulate a crisp story on how your initial product wedge is only the tip of the spear, and there is a much larger vision you plan to tackle over time.
One rule of thumb is to focus the first half of a pitch on your initial wedge. What are you doing over the next 12 months? And then spend the second half on “what if it works.” Show the broader vision for what happens after you’ve nailed your wedge, and think through a roadmap for how to get there.
Done well, most investors should understand the strategy—and if they don’t, they probably just aren’t the right fit!

subject: New features - Woodpecker 2025 recap
body_text:
Margaret Sikora [https://woodpecker.co/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/margaret_sikora_photo.png] 
Margaret Sikora
CEO @ Woodpecker.co
LinkedIn [https://www.linkedin.com/in/margaretsikora/] 
Woodpecker Logo [https://woodpecker.co/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/wp_logo.png] [https://woodpecker.co?utm_source=newsletter&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=monthly_update_1] 



Here’s a recap of all the new features rolled out in 2025 to help you make the most out of Woodpecker.




Woodpecker’s new features:


1.LinkedIn automation [https://app.getresponse.com/email_marketing_click.html?x=a62b&co=Itcat2&lc=h7VJOd&mc=Jg&s=BosBXMv&u=CI4vv&z=EFl5VVg&]: Automate LinkedIn DMs, connection requests and profile visits right in Woodpecker
 * Your LinkedIn campaigns don’t stop when your computer is off.The automation is cloud-based (not browser-based like other LinkedIn automations), so your campaigns keep running when you log off for the day.
   
 * Your LinkedIn account stays safe.The number of actions is set to not exceed the limits imposed by LinkedIn on the type of account you have (free or paid).
   

Pricing:

subject:
Data Labeling Process Snorkel AI; Scale AI; Labelbox - Calibrion
 shows advantage with Agent labeling
body_text:
Data Labeling Processes: Scale AI, Snorkel AI, and Labelbox
The data labeling landscape features three distinct approaches to creating high-quality training data for machine learning models. Scale AI emphasizes human-in-the-loop annotation with a global workforce, Snorkel AI pioneered programmatic labeling through weak supervision, and Labelbox provides a comprehensive platform combining software with labeling services. Each platform addresses the data labeling bottleneck through different methodologies and workflows.

Scale AI Data Labeling Process

Scale AI operates on a human-centric annotation model that combines a global workforce with machine learning assistance to deliver production-quality labeled data. <https://www.exgenex.com/article/scale-ai-data-labeling>​

Data Collection and Preparation

The Scale AI workflow begins with uploading raw data to their platform from various sources including cloud storage or client databases. Data undergoes preprocessing steps such as resizing images, cleaning audio, or organizing text to standardize it for annotation. Users can upload datasets through Scale Rapid (self-serve platform) or Scale Studio (for in-house teams). <https://www.smartsight.in/technology/what-to-know-about-data-labeling-and-scale-ai/>​

Project Setup and Configuration

After data upload, users define their annotation taxonomy and create detailed labeling instructions. The process involves specifying annotation types, setting quality benchmarks, and determining the number of reviews needed per task. Users establish standard operating procedures and workflows for their specific data labeling project. <https://labelyourdata.com/articles/scale-ai-review>​

Calibration Batch Phase

Before full-scale production, Scale AI implements a calibration batch system. This smaller batch is sent to Scale AI's workforce to test the taxonomy setup and labeling instructions. Labelers provide feedback on instruction clarity and deliver initial task responses, allowing users to identify discrepancies and refine their guidelines. The calibration batch undergoes fewer quality controls and serves primarily to fine-tune the labeling process. <https://research.contrary.com/company/scale>​

Production Batch Execution

Once calibration is complete, projects move to production batches with larger volumes. This phase includes rigorous onboarding, training, and periodic performance checks for labelers. Scale AI's platform supports three types of annotation pipelines: <https://www.gocodeo.com/post/scale-ai-explained-how-it-became-the-backbone-of-high-quality-data-labeling-for-enterprise-ai>​

Standard Pipeline: A single annotator performs the task followed by review

Consensus Pipeline: Multiple annotators label the same data, and the platform chooses the consensus result through agreement analysis

Collection Pipeline: Varying review attempts with different quality control mechanisms

Human-in-the-Loop Quality Control

Scale AI employs multiple quality assurance mechanisms throughout the annotation process. The platform uses automated quality assurance that programmatically checks for common errors and enforces project-specific rules. A consensus system sends identical data to multiple annotators, with the final label determined by agreement among them, automatically filtering out low-quality or outlier annotations. <https://www.systemdesignhandbook.com/guides/scale-ai-system-design-interview/>​

The Quality Lab allows administrators to evaluate tasker accuracy and view task-related statistics. Tasks can be audited individually or within date ranges, and training tasks help onboard annotators onto projects. Scale AI reviews labeled data several times before submitting the final version. <https://www.exgenex.com/article/scale-ai-data-labeling>​

Model-Assisted Labeling

For large datasets with well-known objects, Scale AI leverages machine learning models for automated or semi-automated labeling. The platform employs model inference for pre-labeling, filtering noisy tasks, ranking responses, and using embeddings for search capabilities. This approach balances inference cost, latency, and throughput while maintaining quality standards. <https://scale.com/guides/data-labeling-annotation-guide>​

Output and Delivery

Users download the final annotated output from Scale AI through their API or platform interface. The labeled data undergoes final validation before delivery, ensuring it meets the quality benchmarks established at project inception. <https://www.smartsight.in/technology/what-to-know-about-data-labeling-and-scale-ai/>​

Snorkel AI Data Labeling Process

Snorkel AI revolutionized data labeling through programmatic labeling and weak supervision, enabling users to create training data 10-100x faster than manual methods. <https://snorkel.ai/data-labeling-and-data-annotation/>​

Data Ingestion and Preparation

The Snorkel Flow process begins with uploading datasets to the platform. Data is stored in a context hierarchy consisting of context types connected by parent/child relationships in a relational database. This data model enables subject matter experts (SMEs) to write labeling functions over complex, unstructured data. <https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7075849/>​

Application and Dataset Creation

Users create applications in Snorkel Flow that string together data processing logic, programmatic labeling, and model training into a unified interface. Applications use a Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) structure where individual nodes are either operators (data transformations) or model nodes (environments for training set development and model training). <https://docs.snorkel.ai/docs/25.2/user-guide/sdk/quick_start>​

Labeling Function Development

The core of Snorkel's approach involves writing labeling functions (LFs) rather than manually labeling individual data points. Labeling functions are pieces of code that encode domain expertise and weak supervision sources: <https://snorkel.ai/data-centric-ai/programmatic-labeling/>​

Keyword-based rules: Functions that match specific patterns or keywords in text data <https://www.snorkel.org/get-started/>​

External knowledge bases: Integration with existing databases or dictionaries <https://snorkel.ai/blog/snorkel-labeling-functions-use-cases/>​

Weak classifiers: Using existing models that may be insufficient alone but provide useful signals <https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5951191/>​

Heuristic rules: Domain-specific logic that captures business rules <https://www.jpmorgan.com/technology/technology-blog/snorkeling>​

Embeddings-based functions: Using clustering and similarity measures to identify patterns <https://docs.snorkel.ai/docs/0.93/user-guide/best-practices/creating-good-labeling-functions/>​

Snorkel Flow provides multiple ways to create labeling functions including no-code templates in the GUI, custom code LFs in integrated notebooks, and auto-generation based on small labeled samples. <https://snorkel.ai/data-centric-ai/programmatic-labeling/>​

Warm Start with LLMs

Snorkel Flow's Warm Start feature allows users to quickly label entire datasets by prompting foundation models such as OpenAI GPT, Google Gemini, and Meta Llama. The platform can improve label accuracy by prompting multiple LLMs and choosing the best response. <https://snorkel.ai/data-labeling-and-data-annotation/>​

Generative Label Model

The distinguishing feature of Snorkel is its generative label model that automatically learns to estimate the accuracies and correlations of labeling functions. This process uses no ground-truth data, learning instead from agreements and disagreements among labeling functions. <https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7075849/>​

The label model treats the true class label as a latent variable in a probabilistic model. In the simplest case, it models each labeling function as a noisy "voter" with independent errors. More sophisticated versions account for correlations between labeling functions. <https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5951191/>​

Snorkel Flow selects from a suite of label aggregation algorithms based on the dataset's properties. The platform's label model offers superior performance compared to simple majority voting, improving end predictive performance by 5.81%. When tested across 10+ datasets, the Snorkel Flow label model demonstrated significant improvements in performance, speed, and memory efficiency. <https://snorkel.ai/blog/how-does-the-snorkel-flow-label-model-work/>​

Label Aggregation and Training Set Creation

Each labeling function votes or abstains on each data point. The label model evaluates these signals, weighting labeling functions based on their precision on ground truth data and their coverage. Functions that apply to fewer examples but score higher precision receive stronger votes. The model produces probabilistic labels for the training dataset. <https://snorkel.ai/data-centric-ai/weak-supervision/>​

Guided Error Analysis

Snorkel Flow includes visual error analysis tools that highlight label confidence, conflicts between predicted labels and ground truth, and recommendations for creating or updating labeling functions. The Clarity Matrix shows how model predictions compare to labels from labeling functions at a high level. <https://docs.snorkel.ai/docs/0.93/user-guide/best-practices/creating-good-labeling-functions/>​

Model Training and Deployment

Once the training set is created, users train machine learning models using the programmatically labeled data. Snorkel Flow provides one-click training with out-of-the-box industry standard models or allows integration of custom models via Python SDK. The platform supports AutoML searches over hyperparameters and advanced training options. <https://docs.snorkel.ai/docs/25.2/user-guide/sdk/quick_start>​

After model training, users can deploy models as MLFlow packages for use in external data pipelines. The platform enables monitoring of model performance overall and on specific dataset slices, with easy adaptation to new requirements or data distribution shifts by adjusting labeling functions and regenerating training sets in minutes. <https://snorkel.ai/data-centric-ai/programmatic-labeling/>​

Iterative Development Cycle

Snorkel emphasizes an iterative workflow where users continuously refine labeling functions based on model performance. Users page through examples, assign ground truth labels, identify model errors through error analysis, and create new labeling functions targeting low-coverage or low-confidence areas. The platform executes labeling functions at scale using Spark integration, enabling efficient rerunning as users refine their functions. <https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7075849/>​

Labelbox Data Labeling Process

Labelbox provides a comprehensive data-centric AI platform that combines annotation software, quality control tools, and managed labeling services. <https://labelbox.com/services/labeling/>​

Ontology Design and Setup

The Labelbox process begins with creating ontologies—structured schemas that define the labeling task. Ontologies consist of schema nodes (structure and hierarchy) and feature schemas (reusable components like bounding boxes, classifications, or relationships). <https://labelbox.com/blog/ontology-management-with-labelbox/>​

Users can create ontologies from scratch, reuse ontologies from previous projects, or clone existing ontologies. The platform supports advanced ontologies with features like nested classifications, relationships, and custom attributes. Each feature schema contains information for rendering specific annotation types including class names, colors, and tool types. <https://docs.labelbox.com/docs/labelbox-ontology>​

Project Creation and Data Import

After defining the ontology, users create projects and import data from local storage, cloud storage platforms, or direct uploads. Labelbox supports multiple data modalities including images, videos, text, PDFs, geospatial data, medical imagery, and audio. <https://smartone.ai/blog/getting-started-with-labelbox-comprehensive-guide/>​

Workflow Configuration

Labelbox allows users to set up customized multi-step review pipelines through the Workflows feature. Users can configure up to 10 review tasks with different routing logic. For example, specific annotation types can be routed to expert reviewers, or a percentage of data can undergo additional quality checks. <https://labelbox.com/guides/how-to-create-a-quality-strategy-for-your-data-labeling-project/>​

The platform enables creating different user groups, customizing queues and tasks, and establishing step-by-step review processes that drive efficiency and automation. <https://labelbox.com/product/platform/>​

Annotation Execution

Labelers use Labelbox's editor interface to create annotations using various tools (bounding boxes, polygons, keypoints, segmentation masks, text classifications, etc.). The platform provides real-time collaboration capabilities where internal and external labelers can simultaneously work on projects and communicate through issues and comments.​ <https://smartone.ai/blog/getting-started-with-labelbox-comprehensive-guide/>​

AI-Assisted Labeling

Labelbox incorporates multiple AI automation features to accelerate labeling: <https://labelbox.com/product/platform/>​

Model-assisted labeling: Pre-loaded frontier models or custom models provide initial labels that humans approve or reject <https://smartone.ai/blog/getting-started-with-labelbox-comprehensive-guide/>​

Built-in code and grammar critics: Automated reviews of annotations from AI trainers ensuring accuracy and speed <https://labelbox.com/product/platform/>​

LLM-as-a-judge: AI models assess annotation quality for text-based tasks <https://labelbox.com/blog/inside-the-data-factory-how-labelbox-produces-the-highest-quality-data-at-scale/>​

Auto-QA: Automated quality checks flagging inconsistencies and common mistakes <https://labelbox.com/product/platform/>​

Quality Assurance Mechanisms

Labelbox implements comprehensive quality control through multiple mechanisms: <https://docs.labelbox.com/docs/consensus>​

Benchmark System: Ground truth data is mixed with unlabeled data to evaluate annotator accuracy. Annotators have no indication which data is ground truth, allowing objective quality measurement. Reviewers compare annotations to the gold standard. <https://www.labellerr.com/blog/automated-qc/>​

Consensus Scoring: Multiple labelers annotate the same data rows, and the platform calculates real-time agreement scores. Consensus scoring compares annotations from different labelers to determine a consensus winner. Agreement scores range from 0 (no agreement) to 1 (complete agreement). Reviewers can reassign consensus to different annotation sets once multiple labels exist. <https://docs.labelbox.com/docs/quality-analysis>​

Multi-Step Review and Rework: Labelbox employs processes inspired by scientific methods like double-entry. Initial labeling is performed by two independent labelers, results are automatically compared to identify discrepancies, expert reviewers examine discrepancies and make final determinations, and tasks are sent back for rework with specific feedback when necessary. <https://labelbox.com/blog/inside-the-data-factory-how-labelbox-produces-the-highest-quality-data-at-scale/>​

Real-Time Analytics: The platform provides real-time statistical analysis using precision and accuracy as foundational pillars. Labelbox Monitor offers a centralized dashboard to visualize metrics on projects and team members across workspaces. Users can track labeling throughput, annotation activity volume, review actions, time metrics, and average agreement scores. <https://labelbox.com/guides/data-labeling/>​

Quality Control Features: Random spot checks by senior AI trainers, periodic recalibration sessions ensuring consistency, automated checks for logical inconsistencies or outliers, and LLM-assisted quality control for text tasks. <https://labelbox.com/blog/inside-the-data-factory-how-labelbox-produces-the-highest-quality-data-at-scale/>​

Data Export and Model Training

Once labeling is complete, users export annotations in various formats (JSON, CSV, COCO, Pascal VOC, etc.) through the UI or API. The export includes data rows, annotations, labels, metadata, project details, performance metrics, and workflow history. <https://docs.labelbox.com/reference/export-overview>​

Labelbox Model enables users to upload and version datasets, train models using exported data, visualize model predictions against ground truth, view auto-generated metrics (F1, precision, recall, IoU, confusion matrices), and perform error analysis to identify model weaknesses. <https://labelbox.com/guides/how-to-train-evaluate-and-improve-your-ml-models/>​​

Iterative Improvement Cycle

The platform supports iterative data-centric AI workflows:​ <https://labelbox.com/guides/data-labeling/>​

Using models to find labeling errors by comparing high-confidence predictions against annotations​

Identifying model errors by analyzing metrics and finding data rows where models struggle​

Active learning to curate high-value unlabeled data based on model uncertainty​

Finding similar data using embeddings and similarity search to expand training sets​

Sending problematic data rows back to labeling projects for correction or additional annotation​

Managed Labeling Services

Labelbox offers fully-managed labeling services powered by the Alignerr community of expert annotators. The service includes project scoping with dedicated teams, assessment and calibration to train labelers on project specifics, production launch with lightning-fast labels at scale, and continuous collaboration and iteration with real-time feedback. <https://docs.labelbox.com/>​

The managed service excels in GenAI tasks including RLHF, supervised fine-tuning, multimodal LLM evaluation, preference ranking, LLM chat arenas, red teaming, and coding tasks. <https://docs.labelbox.com/>​

Comparative Analysis

Approach Philosophy: Scale AI focuses on human expertise augmented by machine learning, Snorkel AI emphasizes programmatic automation over manual labeling, and Labelbox provides a hybrid platform combining software tools with optional managed services. <https://labelbox.com/services/labeling/>​

Speed and Scalability: Snorkel achieves the fastest acceleration (10-100x faster than manual labeling) through programmatic labeling functions that label entire datasets at once. Scale AI optimizes human annotation speed through workforce management and consensus mechanisms. Labelbox balances speed through AI-assisted tools and managed services. <https://www.exgenex.com/article/scale-ai-data-labeling>​

Quality Control Methods: Scale AI uses multi-annotator consensus and automated QA checks. Snorkel employs a generative label model that denoises and aggregates noisy labeling function outputs. Labelbox combines benchmarks, consensus scoring, multi-step review processes, and LLM-assisted quality control. <https://www.gocodeo.com/post/scale-ai-explained-how-it-became-the-backbone-of-high-quality-data-labeling-for-enterprise-ai>​

Adaptability: Snorkel excels in adaptability—adjusting labeling functions and regenerating training sets takes minutes rather than weeks. Scale AI requires recalibrating human annotators for major changes. Labelbox allows iterative refinement through its error analysis and active learning workflows. <https://research.contrary.com/company/scale>​​

Technical Expertise Required: Snorkel requires the most technical expertise, as users write Python code for labeling functions. Scale AI requires the least technical expertise—users primarily write instructions for human annotators. Labelbox sits in the middle, offering both no-code interfaces and advanced SDK capabilities. <https://labelyourdata.com/articles/scale-ai-review>​

Cost Structure: Scale AI involves per-task costs for human annotation plus platform fees. Snorkel operates on a platform subscription model with minimal marginal costs for additional labeling once functions are written. Labelbox offers both self-service software and managed service options with flexible pricing. <https://snorkel.ai/data-labeling-and-data-annotation/>​

Best Use Cases: Scale AI excels for complex, nuanced tasks requiring human judgment such as autonomous vehicle perception, content moderation, and RLHF for LLMs. Snorkel is optimal for high-volume text classification, entity extraction, and scenarios where domain expertise can be encoded as rules. Labelbox serves diverse needs from computer vision to GenAI with flexibility for teams wanting full control or managed services. <https://labelbox.com/guides/data-labeling/>​

Each platform addresses the data labeling bottleneck through fundamentally different methodologies, making the choice dependent on project requirements, team capabilities, budget constraints, and the nature of the data being labeled.

subject: Important Information Regarding Your WeWork WeJingle Party Registration
body_text:
Hey there,

Thank you for registering for our WeWork Berlin annual holiday party! We
are delighted to welcome you and are busy preparing for a festive evening.

For the security and safety of all attendees, please ensure the following
details have been fully entered in your RSVP:

   - Your full first and last name
   - A contact email address for you and any guests

*Important Safety & Security Reminder:*
All attendees are required to present a valid photo ID upon arrival to gain
access to the event. Please ensure all guests you have registered are aware
of this requirement.
If you are bringing a guest, we kindly ask that you arrive together and
greet them at the entrance. As the host who registered the reservation, you
are responsible for your guests during the event.

*If your plans change and you are no longer able to attend, please update
your RSVP immediately by replying to this email.* This will allow us to
offer your place to others who are on the waiting list.

We look forward to JINGLE with you,
Your Berlin WeWork Community Team

subject: Re: image and color sheet
body_text:
Hi Eugene,

I hope you’ve had a good week so far.

I have discussed this project internally with our team and our computer
vision expert and have formulated a solution that addresses the discussed
image generation challenge. From a time and resource perspective, we’re
able to dedicate the effort needed to carry it out.

We can start with a proof of concept to demonstrate the solution, assess
its feasibility, and incorporate your feedback. Once validated, we can then
move it into production together with your team.

Does any of next Wednesday, Thursday, or Friday work for you to have a
quick call about the details?

Best regards,

Hope to have a great event!

Luc & The AI Builders Team

>>> early-stage company, we are agile and adaptable, and our potential
>>> customers are globally distributed, so location is not a limiting factor.
>>>
>>> Looking forward to your thoughts and next steps.
>>>
>>> Best regards,
>>> Meghdad
>>>
>>> ---------------------------------------
>>> *PS: Our Elevator Pitch:*
>>>
>>> Large Language Models (LLMs) –the technology behind ChatGPT, Claude, and
>>> generative AI in general– have sparked a worldwide technological revolution.
>>>
>>> The problem is most LLMs are built on public data, leaving enterprises
>>> and companies struggling to adapt them for domain-specific internal
>>> processes and user-facing applications.
>>>
>>> Companies are aware of this. In mid-2024, Databricks conducted a survey
>>> revealing that 70% of enterprise executives are eager to train their own
>>> LLMs. But another 2024 survey conducted by Snorkel AI found that almost 40%
>>> of enterprises have 0 LLM in production, while nearly 40% have only 1-2,
>>> and only 13% have productized 5 or more LLMs.
>>>
>>> With the recent availability and abundance of LLM customization and
>>> fine-tuning platforms, open/closed-source LLMs, and required software and
>>> hardware, the biggest roadblock remains training/fine-tuning data —without
>>> it, adapting and fine-tuning LLMs is impossible.
>>>
>>> But transforming raw data into fine-tuning data is a complex process
>>> involving restructuring, annotation, preference adaptation, quality
>>> assurance, and many more steps.
>>>
>>> A challenging, time-consuming, and expensive process, making it one of
>>> the most critical barriers to fine-tuning LLMs to domain-specific needs.
>>>
>>> Our software Calibrion streamlines the transfer of raw enterprise data
>>> into LLMs, addressing all these issues and unlocking enterprise use of LLMs
>>> for domain-specific applications.
>>>
>>> We are one of the first movers into this market size of 63B (by 2028)
>>> with a SAM of 37B and an estimated market share of $1 - 1.4B by 2028, if we
>>> move fast.
>>>
>>> We are seeking $3,700,000 in investment to grow our team, scale sales,
>>> refine our product, introduce key new features, and secure a significant
>>> market share within 18–24 months.
>>>
>>> ---------------------------------------
>>>
>>> Best regards,

subject: Re: Investment Pitch & Product Overview – Calibrion AI
body_text:
Hi Behdad,

Thanks so much for the introduction!

Pejman, great to meet you. I’m familiar with Pear VC and have followed some
of your impressive investments.

Our product, Calibrion (https://www.calibrion.ai), tackles one of the
biggest needs in AI right now: fine-tuning LLMs to specific use cases.
After repeatedly facing the data annotation bottleneck when fine-tuning AI
models in various applied AI projects, I realized existing and traditional
tools weren’t enough. This led me to start building Calibrion.

Since I last spoke with Behdad, we’ve made some additional progress in
pilot client acquisition, product improvements, and financial planning.
Please find the updated pitch attached (under
calibrion_investment_pitch_updated.pdf).

We are seeking investment to close and support our high-potential
enterprise leads and strengthen infrastructure for scale.

I would love to schedule a call. Let me know what works best.

Best regards,
Meghdad

subject: Re: Investment Pitch & Product Overview – Calibrion AI
body_text:
Meghdad

Sorry for the late reply.  Introducing you to my good friend and one of the best venture capitalists in Silicon Valley. Pejman Nojzad


On Feb 27, 2025, at 8:25 AM, Meghdad Farahmand <meghdad@calibrion.ai> wrote:

﻿


CAUTION: This email originated from outside our organization - meghdad@calibrion.ai

Dear Behdad,

I’ve attached our investment pitch and product overview.

Our company, Calibrion AI (www.calibrion.ai<https://urldefense.com/v3/__http://www.calibrion.ai__;!!I-gnmhOqOw!UvDzlqm5fZ4VGyBQH6M6toDN9SjtZQjViZOVYYZuAIZrYueNGAc1VVgSmfoa5Fz-AQnAOwY7tUYdAqvrII4$>), launched its product in November 2024, and we are currently in negotiations with multiple international and European enterprises as our first customers.

Regarding location, we are open to relocating to the US. As an early-stage company, we are agile and adaptable, and our potential customers are globally distributed, so location is not a limiting factor.

Looking forward to your thoughts and next steps.

Best regards,
Meghdad

---------------------------------------
PS: Our Elevator Pitch:

Large Language Models (LLMs) –the technology behind ChatGPT, Claude, and generative AI in general– have sparked a worldwide technological revolution.

The problem is most LLMs are built on public data, leaving enterprises and companies struggling to adapt them for domain-specific internal processes and user-facing applications.

Companies are aware of this. In mid-2024, Databricks conducted a survey revealing that 70% of enterprise executives are eager to train their own LLMs. But another 2024 survey conducted by Snorkel AI found that almost 40% of enterprises have 0 LLM in production, while nearly 40% have only 1-2, and only 13% have productized 5 or more LLMs.

With the recent availability and abundance of LLM customization and fine-tuning platforms, open/closed-source LLMs, and required software and hardware, the biggest roadblock remains training/fine-tuning data —without it, adapting and fine-tuning LLMs is impossible.

But transforming raw data into fine-tuning data is a complex process involving restructuring, annotation, preference adaptation, quality assurance, and many more steps.

A challenging, time-consuming, and expensive process, making it one of the most critical barriers to fine-tuning LLMs to domain-specific needs.

Our software Calibrion streamlines the transfer of raw enterprise data into LLMs, addressing all these issues and unlocking enterprise use of LLMs for domain-specific applications.

We are one of the first movers into this market size of 63B (by 2028) with a SAM of 37B and an estimated market share of $1 - 1.4B by 2028, if we move fast.

We are seeking $3,700,000 in investment to grow our team, scale sales, refine our product, introduce key new features, and secure a significant market share within 18–24 months.

---------------------------------------

Best regards,
--

Meghdad Farahmand PhD

Founder ・ Calibrion AI

Follow-up: Calibrion AI – Seed Round & Live Demo
body_text:
Dear team at QuantumLight Capital,

I hope this note finds you well. I’m following up on my earlier introduction of Calibrion AI and our ongoing EUR 3M seed round.

At Calibrion, we address one of the hardest challenges in enterprise AI adoption: high-quality dataset creation and annotation for LLMs. Our platform is the first built natively for LLM workflows, helping enterprises turn proprietary data into a strategic advantage. Compared to traditional ML annotation tools, Calibrion delivers:

Up to 35% cost savings and weeks faster project turnaround

Superior data quality with proprietary QC automation

Early traction – pilots underway with a $150M US apparel brand and a publicly traded German online retailer

Strong positioning in a $1–1.4B LLM-specific tooling market by 2028

With QuantumLight’s focus on AI and enterprise infrastructure breakthroughs, we believe this is a compelling fit. We are delighted to share our full deck and walk you through the live product demo.

Would you have availability next week for a short introductory call? I’ll be happy to adjust to your schedule.

Looking forward to your thoughts.

Best regards,
Nils

-- 
Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 <tel:+49+(0)30+478+662+25> ・ nils@calibrion.ai <mailto:meghdad@calibrion.ai>
Französische Str. 20・10117 Berlin・Germany
www.calibrion.ai <http://www.calibrion.ai/>

Gerne kannst Du uns auch anrufen / You are also welcome to call us.

+4930235935570

Happy CoWoS 😊

Geschäftsbedingungen

Allgemeine Geschäftsbedingungen (Stand: Januar 2025)

Diese Allgemeinen Geschäftsbedingungen (AGB) gelten für sämtliche Leistungen der Valuedfriends Workspaces GmbH, Kolonnenstraße 8, 10827 Berlin („CoWoS“). Entgegenstehende oder ergänzende Geschäftsbedingungen des Vertragspartners („Kunde“) finden nur Anwendung, wenn CoWoS deren Geltung ausdrücklich und schriftlich zustimmt.

I.	Geltungsbereich und Leistungsumfang

CoWoS erbringt die vereinbarten Leistungen innerhalb der Geschäftszeiten (Montag bis Freitag, 9:00 bis 17:00 Uhr, ausgenommen gesetzliche Feiertage am Standort). Unterbrechungen infolge höherer Gewalt oder technischer Störungen sind hiervon ausgenommen.

II.	Vertragsgegenstand

1. CoWoS stellt entgeltlich Büroarbeitsplätze (fest zugewiesen oder flexibel), Postempfangsservices sowie Internetzugang über (W)LAN zur Verfügung. Je nach Bedarf können zusätzlich Meetingräume gebucht werden. Art und Umfang der Leistungen ergeben sich aus dem gewählten Tarif; dort sind auch die Preise ersichtlich.

2. An den Standorten befindet sich jeweils ein Bistro, das Getränke gegen Entgelt anbietet. Die jeweils gültigen Preise sind vor Ort ausgewiesen.

3. Der Vertrag wird persönlich mit dem Kunden geschlossen und ist nicht auf Dritte übertragbar.

III.	Rechte und Pflichten des Kunden

1. Je nach Tarif darf der Kunde die Anschrift des gewählten CoWoS-Standorts als Geschäftsadresse bzw. Firmensitz führen.

2. Kunden dürfen sich während der Geschäftszeiten in den CoWoS-Räumlichkeiten aufhalten und das Bistro nutzen. Arbeitsplätze stehen nur Kunden mit entsprechendem Tarif zur Verfügung. Bei Buchung eines Fix Desk oder Team Office erhält der Kunde einen Schlüssel und darf die Räume auch außerhalb der Geschäftszeiten nutzen.

3. Eingehende Post wird in einer verschließbaren Box hinterlegt.

4. Kunden mit Flex Desk-Tarif verpflichten sich, den Arbeitsplatz am Ende jedes Nutzungstags vollständig zu räumen.

5. Die Hausordnung ist bindend und hängt vor Ort aus. In sämtlichen Innenräumen – auch auf Balkonen und Innenhöfen – ist das Rauchen (inkl. E-Zigaretten) untersagt.

6. Die überlassenen Einrichtungsgegenstände und sonstige Gegenstände sind pfleglich zu behandeln; etwaige Schäden sind unverzüglich zu melden.

7. Die Nutzung der Geschäftsräume und Infrastruktur ist für rechtswidrige, sittenwidrige oder betrügerische Zwecke untersagt.

8. Der Kunde hat sicherzustellen, dass seine Geräte hinreichend gegen Viren und andere Sicherheitsrisiken geschützt sind, um Schäden an der Infrastruktur oder anderen Nutzern zu vermeiden.

9. CoWoS verpflichtet sich zur Vertraulichkeit. Informationen werden nur auf ausdrückliche Weisung des Kunden weitergegeben.

10. CoWoS ist nicht verpflichtet, Einschreiben oder Pakete mit Zusatzkosten entgegenzunehmen.

11. Für Inhalte von durch CoWoS im Auftrag versandten Nachrichten (Briefe, Faxe etc.) haftet ausschließlich der Kunde. Dieser stellt CoWoS von etwaigen Ansprüchen Dritter frei.

12. Nach Vertragsende sind sämtliche überlassenen Räume, Gegenstände, Schlüssel und Zugangskarten vollständig und im ordnungsgemäßen Zustand zurückzugeben.

IV.	Vertragsabschluss, Laufzeit und Kündigung

1. Verträge können per E-Mail, über das Online-Buchungstool von CoWoS oder vor Ort geschlossen werden.

2. Verträge können unbefristet oder mit einjähriger Laufzeit abgeschlossen werden.

3. Unbefristete Verträge sind jederzeit mit Monatsfrist kündbar. Jahresverträge können mit einer Frist von zwei Monaten zum Laufzeitende gekündigt werden. Erfolgt keine fristgerechte Kündigung, verlängert sich der Vertrag automatisch um ein weiteres Jahr.

4. CoWoS kann das Vertragsverhältnis fristlos kündigen, wenn ein wichtiger Grund vorliegt – insbesondere bei:

o Zahlungsverzug von über vier Wochen

o Nichtzahlung der Sicherheitsleistung

o schwerwiegenden Verstößen gegen die Hausordnung

o vertragswidrigem Verhalten oder Überlassung an Dritte

o strafrechtlich relevanter Geschäftstätigkeit

o grober Verletzung von Nebenpflichten

o Insolvenzeröffnung oder deren Ablehnung mangels Masse

5. Kündigungen können auch in Textform (z. B. per E-Mail) erfolgen. Eine Unterschrift in Papierform ist nicht erforderlich.

6. Bei fristloser Kündigung kann CoWoS dem Kunden den Zutritt zu den Räumlichkeiten untersagen, sofern dies zur Räumungsabwicklung nicht erforderlich ist.

V. Preisanpassung

1. CoWoS ist berechtigt, Preise anzupassen, wenn sich wesentliche Kostenfaktoren wie Energiepreise, Löhne oder behördliche Abgaben verändern.

2. Preisanpassungen müssen verhältnismäßig sein und dürfen den Kunden nicht unangemessen benachteiligen.

3. Änderungen werden mindestens vier Wochen vor Inkrafttreten in Textform mitgeteilt. Erfolgt kein Widerspruch innerhalb von zwei Wochen, gilt die Anpassung als genehmigt. Der Kunde wird in der Mitteilung auf das Widerspruchsrecht sowie die Folgen des Schweigens ausdrücklich hingewiesen.

VI. Zahlungsmodalitäten, Gebühren und Sicherheitsleistung

1. Zahlungen erfolgen per Kreditkarte oder SEPA-Lastschrift.

2. Bei Rücklastschriften werden neben Bankgebühren zusätzlich 10 € Bearbeitungsgebühr berechnet.

3. Bei Verlust oder nicht rechtzeitiger Rückgabe von Schlüsseln trägt der Kunde die Kosten für den Austausch der betroffenen Schlösser und ggf. Schließanlagen.

4. Die monatliche Servicemiete ist im Voraus zum Ersten eines jeden Monats fällig. Nichtnutzung der Leistung entbindet nicht von der Zahlungspflicht.

5. Zusatzleistungen werden separat abgerechnet und zusammen mit der Miete des Folgemonats fällig. Einwendungen sind binnen 7 Tagen nach Rechnungserhalt zu erheben.

6. Bei Zahlungsverzug verfallen gewährte Rabatte. Es gelten dann die regulären Tarife.

VII.	Haftung

1. Der Kunde haftet für alle Schäden, die durch ihn, seine Mitarbeiter oder Lieferanten verursacht werden – insbesondere bei Schäden durch unsichere technische Geräte, die das CoWoS-Netzwerk beeinträchtigen. In solchen Fällen stellt der Kunde CoWoS von etwaigen Forderungen Dritter frei.

2. CoWoS haftet bei Vorsatz oder grober Fahrlässigkeit – uneingeschränkt bei Schäden an Leben, Körper oder Gesundheit.

3. Ansprüche gegen CoWoS verjähren, wenn sie nicht innerhalb von drei Monaten nach Ablehnung durch CoWoS oder dessen Versicherung gerichtlich geltend gemacht werden.

VIII.	Änderungen beim Kunden

1. Änderungen der Rechtsform, Handelsregistereinträge oder Vertretungsverhältnisse sind CoWoS unverzüglich mitzuteilen.

2. Bei Wechsel in eine haftungsbeschränkte Rechtsform bleibt die persönliche Haftung des ursprünglichen Vertragspartners bestehen, sofern keine gesonderte Vereinbarung getroffen wird.

IX.	Vorsteuerabzug

Der Kunde sichert zu, zum vollen Vorsteuerabzug berechtigt zu sein. Sollte sich dies ändern, verpflichtet er sich, CoWoS unverzüglich zu informieren und etwaige daraus entstehende steuerliche Nachteile auszugleichen. In diesem Fall gilt die Miete als Bruttobetrag ohne separaten Umsatzsteuerausweis.

X.	Schriftform, AGB-Änderung und salvatorische Klausel

1. Änderungen dieses Vertrags bedürfen der Schriftform. Nebenabreden oder Ergänzungen sind nur wirksam, wenn CoWoS sie schriftlich bestätigt.

2. CoWoS behält sich vor, die AGB jederzeit zu ändern. Änderungen werden dem Kunden spätestens zwei Wochen vor Inkrafttreten in Textform mitgeteilt. Widerspricht der Kunde nicht fristgerecht, gelten die Änderungen als angenommen. Bei Widerspruch besteht der Vertrag unter den alten AGB fort; CoWoS kann jedoch mit Monatsfrist kündigen.

3. Sollte eine Bestimmung unwirksam sein oder werden, bleiben die übrigen Regelungen unberührt. Die Parteien verpflichten sich, eine der unwirksamen Regelung wirtschaftlich gleichwertige Ersatzregelung zu vereinbaren.

XI.	Gerichtsstand und anwendbares Recht

1. Gerichtsstand ist Berlin, sofern der Kunde Kaufmann ist.

2. Es gilt das Recht der Bundesrepublik Deutschland.

Du erhältst diese E-Mail, weil du dich für eine Mitgliedschaft bei CoWoS Berlin angemeldet hast.

CoWoS - eine Marke der Valuedfriends Workspaces GmbH

Kolonnenstr. 8
10827 Berlin

subject: Following up on Calibrion AI – Seed Round & Product Demo
body_text:
Dear team at Iron Wolf Capital,

I hope you are doing well.

I wanted to follow up on my introduction of Calibrion AI and our EUR 3 million seed round. We’re seeing promising traction, with early pilot interest from European and US companies, and we believe this is an ideal moment to discuss how we can scale this momentum with the right partners.

As mentioned, our focus is on helping enterprises unlock the real value of LLMs by fine-tuning them with proprietary data — an approach we believe will define the next phase of practical AI adoption in business. With the recent Scale AI–Meta deal highlighting how critical this layer is, we see significant growth ahead for this segment.

Given Iron Wolf Capital’s focus on supporting ambitious early-stage founders building transformative technologies, we’d love to explore how Calibrion could fit into your portfolio.

I’d be glad to share our detailed deck and walk you through a live demo of the product. Would you have 30 minutes next week for an initial conversation? I’m happy to adjust to your schedule.

Looking forward to your thoughts and hoping we can connect soon.

Best regards,
Nils

--
Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 ・ nils@calibrion.ai <>
Französische Str. 20・10117 Berlin・Germany
www.calibrion.ai <http://www.calibrion.ai/>
￼￼

> On 22. Jul 2025, at 16:47, Nils Erbach <nils@calibrion.ai> wrote:
> 
> Dear team at Iron Wolf Capital,
> 
> I am reaching out to introduce Calibrion AI, a data annotation platform enabling enterprises and organisations to fine-tune LLMs to their proprietary data. We built a working product and already reached pilot-stage interest from European and US companies in the eCom vertical.
> 
> While most LLM infra and dev platforms focus on model architecture and LLMOps, we focus on unlocking enterprise adoption through (proprietary) data and fine-tuning workflows.
> 
> Calibrion envisions a future where businesses harness their proprietary data and domain expertise to build specialized LLMs, turning generative AI from a threat into a strategic asset for both internal and market-facing applications.
> 
> Our mission is to become a market-leading platform for LLM customization and domain adaptation, setting the standard for performance and usability in the enterprise AI space.
> 
> To underscore the scale of this opportunity and the importance of what Calibrion does: In June 2025, Meta acquired a 49% stake in Scale AI (a company also focused on LLM data annotation and founded in 2016) for $14.8 billion at a $30 billion valuation. 
> 
> We are currently offering subscription for our EUR 3 m seed investment round to: 
> - expand our engineering and data science team
> - Accelerate go-to-market efforts across verticals 
> - Further develop our AI capabilities and integrate new LLM-driven features.
> 
> To accelerate the company's development, we assigned significant equity to an option pool for key employees and aim to be cash-flow positive within 18 months.
> 
> We would be happy to share our pitch deck and schedule a call to walk you through the live product. The product is fully developed and currently undergoing regular testing and stabilization.
> 
> Please find attached a letter outlining our fund-raising goals as well as a one-pager on Calibrion.
> 
> We look forward to hearing from you and would be glad to schedule a conversation at your convenience.
> 
> Best regards,
> Nils
> 
> -- 
> Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
> Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 <tel:+49+(0)30+478+662+25> ・ nils@calibrion.ai <mailto:meghdad@calibrion.ai>
> Französische Str. 20・10117 Berlin・Germany
> www.calibrion.ai <http://www.calibrion.ai/>

subject: New features and cold email tips - July 2025
body_text:
Margaret Sikora [https://woodpecker.co/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/margaret_sikora_photo.png] 
Margaret Sikora
CEO @ Woodpecker.co
LinkedIn [https://www.linkedin.com/in/margaretsikora/] 
Woodpecker Logo [https://woodpecker.co/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/wp_logo.png] [https://woodpecker.co?utm_source=newsletter&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=monthly_update_1] 



The new Woodpecker features help you get more conversions from your outreach. Plus, I’m sharing two bonus cold email tips at the end. Let’s go!




Woodpecker’s new features:




1. LinkedIn automation [https://app.getresponse.com/click.html?x=a62b&co=Itcat2&lc=hctT5d&mc=rK&s=BosBXMv&u=CI4vv&z=Eyc8hX3&]: Automate LinkedIn DMs, connection requests and profile visits right in Woodpecker


🔹Your LinkedIn campaigns don’t stop when your computer is off.The automation is cloud-based (not browser-based like other LinkedIn automations), so your campaigns keep running when you log off for the day.


🔹 Your LinkedIn account stays safe.The number of actions is set to not exceed the limits imposed by LinkedIn on the type of account you have (free or paid).


Pricing:
$29/mo per 1 connected LinkedIn account


(That’s at least 50% cheaper than most LinkedIn automation tools!)




[https://us-ms.gr-cdn.com/getresponse-CI4vv/photos/29d3e68e-2768-4f12-abd2-6a02b28a0539.png]


2. Google and Microsoft mailboxes: [https://app.getresponse.com/click.html?x=a62b&co=Itcat2&lc=hctTYe&mc=rK&s=BosBXMv&u=CI4vv&z=EMSKIau&]Buy Google & Microsoft domains and mailboxes directly in Woodpecker


🔹 You get mailboxes with all the DNS records (SPF, DKIM and DMARC) already set up= you don’t need to spend time doing it yourself


🔹 You can start from 1 mailbox= no need to buy in bulk


🔹 You can buy them without leaving Woodpecker= again, you save time


Pricing (the same for Google and Microsoft):
 * $1/month per domain (purchased annually)
   
 * $6/month per mailbox
   





[https://us-ms.gr-cdn.com/getresponse-CI4vv/photos/90401bf1-420d-4117-8825-a2405b953bf5.png]


3. Email Service Provider matching [https://app.getresponse.com/click.html?x=a62b&co=Itcat2&lc=hctTxG&mc=rK&s=BosBXMv&u=CI4vv&z=ESV89DT&]:Get extra deliverability by sending emails from the same email provider as your prospects


🔹 Google mailboxes send to Google mailboxes. Microsoft mailboxes send to Microsoft mailboxes.If your prospect uses a different provider, they’ll get categorized as ‘Other’ – and your email will be sent from one of your other providers, too.



🔹 Benefits:
 * better sender reputation
   
 * better inbox placement (=more emails delivered to the primary inbox)
   
 * better campaign performance (better deliverability means more emails seen and replied)
   
 * it’s easy: switch the feature on with 1 click and it runs automatically
   



[https://us-ms.gr-cdn.com/getresponse-CI4vv/photos/9a2793f1-a484-48a6-b15d-acb62e9ea7c1.png]


4. MCP server: [https://app.getresponse.com/click.html?x=a62b&co=Itcat2&lc=hctTTx&mc=rK&s=BosBXMv&u=CI4vv&z=EG16zt0&]Prompt your AI agent (using natural language) to perform an action in Woodpecker or show you data from your Woodpecker campaigns


🔹 Benefits:
 * you can create and manage campaigns with a prompt
   
 * it’s personalized to your market and region
   
 * you can get improvement suggestions based on your past campaign results
   
 * you don’t need technical knowledge to run the actions
   
   

🔹You can use AI prompts to:
 * create your first campaign
   
 * add prospects in bulk, update prospect data
   
 * manage campaigns (create, run, update, pause and delete)
   
 * get analytics and reporting (campaign stats and performance metrics)
   
 * create multistep email sequences with A/B tests
   
 * assign email accounts to campaigns
   
 * adjust delivery schedules, timezone settings and GDPR compliance
   



[https://us-ms.gr-cdn.com/getresponse-CI4vv/photos/0e3ba2b2-ef43-43ed-bc74-e9311fa71309.png]


5. New blacklisting options: [https://app.getresponse.com/click.html?x=a62b&co=Itcat2&lc=hctTpl&mc=rK&s=BosBXMv&u=CI4vv&z=EhHII1J&]Never send to excluded emails or domains again


🔹 When you change a prospect’s status to BLACKLISTED, you can also add their email address and domain to a blacklist. Additionally, agency users can add it to a global blacklist from a client account.


🔹 You can delete data of a BLACKLISTED prospect from your account – but still keep their email address/domain blacklisted on your account.

🔹 You can export blacklists to a CSV file.




[https://us-ms.gr-cdn.com/getresponse-CI4vv/photos/96834cd9-1490-4e0e-86aa-01ee6d4c9ea8.png]


6. New Woodpecker API documentation [https://app.getresponse.com/click.html?x=a62b&co=Itcat2&lc=hctTsD&mc=rK&s=BosBXMv&u=CI4vv&z=EIp8BmV&]: It’s clear, beautiful and a pleasure to use


🔹 Access it here [https://app.getresponse.com/click.html?x=a62b&co=Itcat2&lc=hctTbm&mc=rK&s=BosBXMv&u=CI4vv&z=E9IcSgm&]


🔹 Examples of what you can do with Woodpecker API:
 * build an integration with your CRM: export and add prospects to and from your CRM
   
 * build custom analytics for your campaigns: get reports from Woodpecker and send them automatically to your analytics platform
   
 * automate managing your campaigns: create new campaigns without logging in to Woodpecker
   
 * sync prospect statuses between Woodpecker and other sales tools
   
 * agencies:integrate your client management system with Woodpecker – creating a client in the system automatically creates a client account in Woodpecker
   
 * agencies:blacklist domains and emails for all the companies that you manage
   
   

All users with the Integrations add-on can access these 👆




[https://us-ms.gr-cdn.com/getresponse-CI4vv/photos/4734e826-2b6f-4942-ab8f-20cf626468f8.png]


Cold email tip #1 [https://app.getresponse.com/click.html?x=a62b&co=Itcat2&lc=hctTNM&mc=rK&s=BosBXMv&u=CI4vv&z=ES46Imw&]: Micro-campaigns get more replies


 1. Pick a narrow group - same job/pain/objections/goals/value proposition (e.g. “B2B marketers struggling with launching on LinkedIn” not “B2B marketers”)
    
 2. Write one message that fits everyone on the list - the more homogeneous the group, the better
    
 3. Skip fake personalization - ‘I see we both love dogs’
    
 4. Build the list to match the message - not the other way around
    
 5. If your message doesn’t work for all of them as-is, it’s not a micro-campaign
    


Cold email tip #2 [https://app.getresponse.com/click.html?x=a62b&co=Itcat2&lc=hctT7s&mc=rK&s=BosBXMv&u=CI4vv&z=EIK0SQL&]: Your cold email might land in spam because of your signature
ESP filters love minimalism.


Here’s what NOT to put in your email signature:
 * Images (especially profile pics or logos)
   
 * 3-5 links to socials, booking tools, websites
   
 * Fancy formatting, bold colors, or HTML signatures copied from Gmail
   


Thanks for reading and see you next time!



Profile photo [https://ci3.googleusercontent.com/meips/ADKq_NZEbjt1hLHRpJUm-T03r9RSh0pwx0-vUDdosLWVAC69H2jIjrgMytC4Kslr2Uk4rI0Ukx8mixCWrEHpRLnwbN4SiDXZcZ90QdmtPtQboLN18kRmrqArVzSKbnOYqhiJ=s0-d-e1-ft#https://i.woodpeckerhq.com/sign/c316b4b4-504a-42e6-b119-b8facaa7b4cc.jpeg]
Best Regards,
Margaret Sikora
CEO @Woodpecker.co



LinkedIn [https://i.woodpeckerhq.com/sign/linkedin.png] [https://www.linkedin.com/in/margaretsikora/].


You may unsubscribe [https://app.getresponse.com/unsubscribe.html?x=a62b&co=Itcat2&m=B8I43x&mc=rK&s=BosBXMv&u=CI4vv&z=ESqqtlB&] or change your contact details [https://app.getresponse.com/change_details.html?x=a62b&co=Itcat2&m=B8I43x&s=BosBXMv&u=CI4vv&z=E94cC9r&] at any time.

Thank you for being a valued Deepgram customer! We’re reaching out to remind you of an upcoming change to streaming speech-to-text rate limits, effective on March 17th 2025.

To ensure optimal performance and reliability for all users, we will be adjusting the rate limits as follows:

- New Rate Limit: Maximum of 50 concurrent requests per project
- Previous Limit: Maximum of 100 concurrent requests per project

Please reach out to danny.kim@deepgram.com (mailto:danny.kim@deepgram.com) to discuss upgrading options.

If you need support related to this update, feel free to join us on Discord (https://e.deepgram.com/e3t/Ctc/5D+113/cQMRG04/VVM0Qr6lGZ3jW5TXY2m8yJTPdW1CR2895sPBrcN4bXfJn3l5QzW69sMD-6lZ3nKW5BGxWG26tC3FW5zY7cD4M11THW1s-3TF3K2xQQW3YJn6_2553FfW3Hw1S344kvBKVYL74v7HLGrjW2Yy4Dz8n7c-pW67LxrD2rxYx6W660VBw2L6w0ZW86xbsr5y9N1SW6l0Nzk7wt63TW5QKwrQ6NtH_NW3fP1qt6X4YdNW6SMMVX7nb2M9W3q_QdC1JpJ40W2-7qJc1VwxxgW4zbw5P2BS0w7N57pzdG906vJW6NlLxt4HS3QCW6TxnyF4bDgXdf1dchD604 ) for additional resources.

Best regards,

subject: Re: Calibrion x Cotopaxi – Pilot Project & Next Steps
Calibrion x Cotopaxi – Pilot Project & Next Steps
body_text:
Hi all,

Thank you again for the great conversation on Wednesday.
I really enjoyed learning more about Cotopaxi and we are excited about the
potential to collaborate on this pilot project.

As discussed, we’ll be sharing a draft contract and offer for your review.
I’ve looped in:
- *Nils*, who manages legal and finance at Calibrion and will coordinate
the contract.
- *Mina*, who oversees sales and client relationships and will support you
with any questions related to the offer or next steps.

They’ll follow up shortly with the documents and are happy to set up a
quick call if any clarifications would be helpful.

@Blair, I’ve used the email address that appeared in our meeting under your
name in the invite (stacey.howe@cotopaxi.com) please feel free to correct
me or add the correct email if this is not the right one.

Looking forward to taking the next steps together.

Best regards,
Meghdad

-- 

Meghdad Farahmand PhD

Founder ・ Calibrion AI

Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 <+49+(0)30+478+662+25> ・ meghdad@calibrion.ai

Französische Str. 20・10117 Berlin・Germany

www.calibrion.ai

subject: Calibrion Investment Sync Prep
body_text:
Hi Meghdad

Please find attached the following details to be send out to Venture Capital Companies. Also attached a list of 50 VCs matching Calibrion’s business Case as a file. 

I suggest the following text while approaching the VCs: 

"Dear Team at ABC Capital,

We are building Calibrion, an agent-native data annotation platform which solves the bottleneck in organisational GenAI adaptation: Data annotation to enable organisations to train LLMs [ and further level GenAI models ] on their proprietary data. 

Compared to previous solutions in this space, Calibrion increases annotation time by ~170x [ From months to hours ]; Cuts annotation cost by ~90% and increases annotation quality with built-in quality control. Human intervention by manual annotation and review time is cut by ~35%, allowing for less human resources deployed. 

We were able to sign a 6 month paid pilot with a large listed e-commerce retail company in Germany and seek EUR 350,000 in seed financing to build a teamweich blends onboarding of further clients and iterative product development to speed up time towards product led growth.

Given ABC’s strong track record of backing early-stage deep tech ventures, we believe Calibrion aligns closely with your investment thesis. Please find attached our pitch deck and a one-pager on further information. We would greatly apprechiate some 20-30 mins for a call including a product-walk-through according to your schedule.

Thanks for getting back to us and best,
Nils“ 


In terms of attached files I would suggest sending over [ updated ] pitch-deck, one-pager.

Please let me know your thoughts on this.

Best wishes
Nils

BIG NEWS:

A keynote speaker with a truly global perspective

We are delighted to announce that Chris Stephens, VP and Field CTO at Groq, will join the keynote line-up at World Summit AI Amsterdam 2025.

GET TICKETS NOW
(https://email.worldsummit.ai/e3t/Ctc/5B+113/cvlSS04/VWdXQP4WFw09W5Bhq335ct952W6phD5r5DjLFcN6Q02rx3l5QzW69sMD-6lZ3krW41TQfk1QCGnvW586B9M4h7dBnW6CjpM76nXbjpW1Q_vf260NW6SW7T8bnG7-70FHVXhQxR7NpxBVN7V02F97yJnyW7gRfdC90C_d9W7n-Sxy9dVvLlVkwFnf2BRwyNVSmzV07lpvrmW1rS_X26Ys8mtN840zPFtz-_gW7Ypbyc431CdVW4pHF2g6hglvnW5mWGqg1vbxhtW1rT3nl6WJh7RW202_hQ4fxn-cW2bzr-y7khsthW7PxD9M49CF42f19gmtg04 )

As the Field Chief Technology Officer at Groq, Chris drives the adoption of cutting-edge AI solutions. He will be joining us to talk about Delivering Sovereign AI exploring the emerging architecture of Sovereign AI—the fusion of compute sovereignty, trusted data pipelines, and national-scale model deployment.

Chris Stephens, Field CTO at Groq, outlines the playbook for delivering deterministic, high-throughput inference at sovereign scale. From the economics of “tokens per citizen” to the regulatory frameworks guiding national AI infrastructure, this session shows how countries can build AI systems that are fast, safe, and self-reliant—balancing innovation with independence in the age of intelligent infrastructure.

For our AI creatives at World Summit AI, we are excited to welcome Jeremy Davis, Founding Member of Grammy Award Winning band Paramore.

Since departing Paramore Davis has expanded his focus to address the evolving challenges artists face in the digital age. He is currently collaborating with Monarrch, a pioneering company at the forefront of the generative AI revolution.

Monarrch is dedicated to ensuring that creators are recognized and compensated for their contributions in the digital age. Their technology is groundbreaking for creators, AI companies, and policymakers navigating the complexities of intellectual property in the AI era.

Davis will be joined by music technology pioneers Ken Gay and John Pisciotta from Monarrch to showcase this new technology and discuss its impact for AI creatives.

Stay tuned for more exciting final speaker line-up announcement later today!

See you in Amsterdam! 👋

- WSAI

InspiredMinds (https://email.worldsummit.ai/e3t/Ctc/5B+113/cvlSS04/VWdXQP4WFw09W5Bhq335ct952W6phD5r5DjLFcN6Q02rx3l5QzW69sMD-6lZ3p4W6Ymtwr392TQTW7cyyQd1_zgyyW3nqCJt2Jw9pxW4gBQKM3Y1Q_9W69Qjs18ckstDW2CTWNb8jRgJ0W1tTjJ08x-NYyW8dWwSL3T86vDW1JpY5d3zswR_W1LGCP62l4qfkW2f5wGh4c6fn5W1Yp8XH4b_cG3W87_j7Y3zTrTxW5S2gPh4shTV-MzBBBdr0XjZW8ttbjV1dCjmQVn6jXZ6L7SxJW3Z_KxD7Ln9NYW12b2pS4JkzxfW8_G-Qr5tNYPHf5KrW8M04 )
