>>>>>>>>> On Sat, Nov 8, 2025 at 12:32 AM Kel Commins <kel@lilt.com> wrote:
>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> hi Meghdad --
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> Getting back to this...thanks for your patience.
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> Overall, seems like the right direction/approach, especially
>>>>>>>>>> because it seems like there would be the ability to configure for different
>>>>>>>>>> customer requirements.  And I think there is a lot of potential for using
>>>>>>>>>> the jury/judge capabilities to automate further (and focus any human
>>>>>>>>>> annotation/labeling).
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> About the open questions:
>>>>>>>>>> -- question 1 - so far, we've defined decently strict manifests
>>>>>>>>>> simply because that has put us in a position to guide the content
>>>>>>>>>> generation more specifically (which has been necessary, otherwise we get
>>>>>>>>>> too-unpredictable responses from the content generation prompts); we are
>>>>>>>>>> now using pydantic models to define the output format and merging that into
>>>>>>>>>> a jinja template used for the prompt
>>>>>>>>>> -- question 2 - see attached (I can explain this further in a
>>>>>>>>>> follow-up call); we would generate between 20-30K of these per week, each
>>>>>>>>>> of which would feed the 'content generation' process
>>>>>>>>>> -- question 3 - ideally we have confidence in a jury/judge
>>>>>>>>>> agent(s) and would only human-review those that show low-confidence or
>>>>>>>>>> wide-range out of the jury/judge evaluation
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> Also, it is probably worth it at some point for us to talk about
>>>>>>>>>> funding/costs.  I want to make sure the efforts of you and your team are
>>>>>>>>>> worthwhile.  I'm not in a position to make commitments, but it would
>>>>>>>>>> probably make sense to hear from you how you would charge for the types of
>>>>>>>>>> capabilities/functionality we are talking about.
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> I'm around next week if you'd like to reconvene to talk about
>>>>>>>>>> any/all of the above.  Thanks again for considering these
>>>>>>>>>> content-generation needs that we are starting to see.
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> -- Kel
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> On Wed, Nov 5, 2025 at 2:48 PM Kel Commins <kel@lilt.com> wrote:
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>> hi Meghdad --
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>> Thanks for the follow-up.  I'm just acknowledging that this is
>>>>>>>>>>> well-received and that I'll spend some time on it tomorrow/Friday and get
>>>>>>>>>>> back to you.
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>> -- Kel
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>> On Wed, Nov 5, 2025 at 10:20 AM Meghdad Farahmand <
>>>>>>>>>>> meghdad@calibrion.ai> wrote:
>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>> Hi Kel,
>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>> I hope you are doing well!
>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>> Thanks again for the great meeting last Wednesday.
>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>> We reviewed your use cases with the team, placing particular
>>>>>>>>>>>> focus on the group chat/message scenario.
>>>>>>>>>>>> We can offer you a tailored workflow to support your needs. At
>>>>>>>>>>>> a high level, it would look as follows:
>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>>    1. *Dataset description*: The user can describe the dataset
>>>>>>>>>>>>    in natural language, semi-structured form, or as a fully structured
>>>>>>>>>>>>    specification.
>>>>>>>>>>>>    2. *Manifest generator agent assignment:* The user assigns
>>>>>>>>>>>>    a manifest generator agent from a list of Calibrion's
>>>>>>>>>>>>    pre-existing or their organization's custom-defined agents
>>>>>>>>>>>>    <https://screen.studio/share/BKhY8U5U>.
>>>>>>>>>>>>    3. *Manifest preview:* The user previews the manifest
>>>>>>>>>>>>    generated by the above agent (with the possibility of editing if necessary).
>>>>>>>>>>>>    4. *Dataset generator agent assignment:* The user selects a
>>>>>>>>>>>>    dataset generator agent (from a list of Calibrion's pre-existing or their
>>>>>>>>>>>>    organization's custom-defined agents).
>>>>>>>>>>>>       - The user is presented with estimated time and cost for
>>>>>>>>>>>>       dataset generation according to the chosen agent.
>>>>>>>>>>>>       - Can confirm or change agents.
>>>>>>>>>>>>    5. *Quality control (LLM jury assignment)*
>>>>>>>>>>>>       - The user selects agents for their LLM jury (from a
>>>>>>>>>>>>       list of Calibrion's pre-existing or their organization's custom-defined
>>>>>>>>>>>>       agents).
>>>>>>>>>>>>       - Configures jury's decision type (accept/reject, rank,
>>>>>>>>>>>>       natural_language_description).
>>>>>>>>>>>>       - Configures jury's strictness (mild, moderate, strict).
>>>>>>>>>>>>    6. *Preview details and finalize: *The user is presented
>>>>>>>>>>>>    with a summary of the above configuration on the finalization page and can
>>>>>>>>>>>>    go back to any step to edit it. When confirmed, the project starts.
>>>>>>>>>>>>    7. *Project dashboard: *When the project starts, the user
>>>>>>>>>>>>    can access the project dashboard.
>>>>>>>>>>>>       - While the generation is in progress, the user can
>>>>>>>>>>>>       access selected dashboard panels (e.g., statistics, member agents,
>>>>>>>>>>>>       manifest) for real-time visibility.
>>>>>>>>>>>>       - Once the project is complete, the user can view the
>>>>>>>>>>>>       jury evaluation results, filter the data based on jury (or manual review)
>>>>>>>>>>>>       decisions, and export the final dataset.
>>>>>>>>>>>>       - In the final export, the user can include
>>>>>>>>>>>>       comprehensive metadata related to the dataset generation, such as the
>>>>>>>>>>>>       manifest, initial configuration, dataset description, participating agents,
>>>>>>>>>>>>       and human reviewers. The user can select which metadata to include in the
>>>>>>>>>>>>       export.
>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>> *Open questions:*
>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>>    - *Regarding step 1:* Would you prefer the user to define
>>>>>>>>>>>>    the configuration entirely in natural language, strictly through a
>>>>>>>>>>>>    structured format (e.g., by uploading a JSON file), or with a semi-flexible
>>>>>>>>>>>>    approach that mixes both (e.g., specifying size as an integer while
>>>>>>>>>>>>    describing other elements in natural language)?
>>>>>>>>>>>>    - *Manifest: *Do you have a sample/mock manifest you could
>>>>>>>>>>>>    share with us?
>>>>>>>>>>>>    - *Manual review: *In addition to the LLM-based jury, would
>>>>>>>>>>>>    you also like to include a manual review process? If so, how would you
>>>>>>>>>>>>    envision this working? At present, we can offer the following options:
>>>>>>>>>>>>       - Human experts review the full generated dataset (or a
>>>>>>>>>>>>       configurable sample of it). They can accept, reject, or edit the
>>>>>>>>>>>>       generations.
>>>>>>>>>>>>       - Human experts review the full (or a configurable
>>>>>>>>>>>>       sample of) LLM jury decisions.
>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>> *Other use cases:*
>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>>    - We already have a span-detection workflow in progress. If
>>>>>>>>>>>>    this is of interest, we can make it available for your PII-redaction use
>>>>>>>>>>>>    case.
>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>> *Next steps: *If our proposal is interesting to you, I suggest
>>>>>>>>>>>> scheduling a follow-up meeting to walk through the details and clarify any
>>>>>>>>>>>> open questions. Following that, we can move to an initial prototype that
>>>>>>>>>>>> enables us to discuss, adjust, and finalize the workflow with respect to
>>>>>>>>>>>> your needs and preferences. We can then proceed to implementation and
>>>>>>>>>>>> deployment.
>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>> Feel free to revisit some of Calibrion's workflows I presented
>>>>>>>>>>>> during our initial demo, which you can find here
>>>>>>>>>>>> <https://docs.google.com/document/d/1bMC1AW6Wu-eTxOIhEkro05LAr8QYiMcfU7WgXCZe4vQ/edit?tab=t.0>
>>>>>>>>>>>> .
>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>> I look forward to your thoughts and would be excited to work
>>>>>>>>>>>> together on this project if you decide to proceed.
>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>> Best regards,
>>>>>>>>>>>> Meghdad
>>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>> --

>>>>>>>>>>>    in natural language, semi-structured form, or as a fully structured

>>>>>>>>>>>       dataset generation according to the chosen agent.

>>>>>>>>>>>       metadata related to the dataset generation, such as the manifest, initial
>>>>>>>>>>>       configuration, dataset description, participating agents, and human
>>>>>>>>>>>       reviewers. The user can select which metadata to include in the export.

>>>>>>>>>>>    the configuration entirely in natural language, strictly through a
>>>>>>>>>>>    structured format (e.g., by uploading a JSON file), or with a semi-flexible
>>>>>>>>>>>    approach that mixes both (e.g., specifying size as an integer while

>>>>>>>>>>>    - *Manual review: *In addition to the LLM-based jury, would
>>>>>>>>>>>    you also like to include a manual review process? If so, how would you

>>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>>    - We already have a span-detection workflow in progress. If
>>>>>>>>>>>    this is of interest, we can make it available for your PII-redaction use
>>>>>>>>>>>    case.

>>>>>>>>>>       selected dashboard panels (e.g., statistics, member agents, manifest) for
>>>>>>>>>>       real-time visibility.
>>>>>>>>>>       - Once the project is complete, the user can view the jury
>>>>>>>>>>       evaluation results, filter the data based on jury (or manual review)
>>>>>>>>>>       decisions, and export the final dataset.
>>>>>>>>>>       - In the final export, the user can include comprehensive
>>>>>>>>>>       metadata related to the dataset generation, such as the manifest, initial
>>>>>>>>>>       configuration, dataset description, participating agents, and human
>>>>>>>>>>       reviewers. The user can select which metadata to include in the export.
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> *Open questions:*
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>    - *Regarding step 1:* Would you prefer the user to define the
>>>>>>>>>>    configuration entirely in natural language, strictly through a structured
>>>>>>>>>>    format (e.g., by uploading a JSON file), or with a semi-flexible approach
>>>>>>>>>>    that mixes both (e.g., specifying size as an integer while describing other
>>>>>>>>>>    elements in natural language)?
>>>>>>>>>>    - *Manifest: *Do you have a sample/mock manifest you could
>>>>>>>>>>    share with us?
>>>>>>>>>>    - *Manual review: *In addition to the LLM-based jury, would
>>>>>>>>>>    you also like to include a manual review process? If so, how would you
>>>>>>>>>>    envision this working? At present, we can offer the following options:
>>>>>>>>>>       - Human experts review the full generated dataset (or a
>>>>>>>>>>       configurable sample of it). They can accept, reject, or edit the
>>>>>>>>>>       generations.
>>>>>>>>>>       - Human experts review the full (or a configurable sample
>>>>>>>>>>       of) LLM jury decisions.
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> *Other use cases:*
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>>    - We already have a span-detection workflow in progress. If
>>>>>>>>>>    this is of interest, we can make it available for your PII-redaction use
>>>>>>>>>>    case.
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> *Next steps: *If our proposal is interesting to you, I suggest
>>>>>>>>>> scheduling a follow-up meeting to walk through the details and clarify any
>>>>>>>>>> open questions. Following that, we can move to an initial prototype that
>>>>>>>>>> enables us to discuss, adjust, and finalize the workflow with respect to
>>>>>>>>>> your needs and preferences. We can then proceed to implementation and
>>>>>>>>>> deployment.
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> Feel free to revisit some of Calibrion's workflows I presented
>>>>>>>>>> during our initial demo, which you can find here
>>>>>>>>>> <https://docs.google.com/document/d/1bMC1AW6Wu-eTxOIhEkro05LAr8QYiMcfU7WgXCZe4vQ/edit?tab=t.0>
>>>>>>>>>> .
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> I look forward to your thoughts and would be excited to work
>>>>>>>>>> together on this project if you decide to proceed.
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> Best regards,
>>>>>>>>>> Meghdad
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> --
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> Meghdad Farahmand PhD
>>>>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>>>> Founder ・ Calibrion AI
>>>>>>>>>>

>>>>>>>>>> meghdad@calibrion.ai

subject: Re: Data generation with Calibrion
body_text:
hi Meghdad --

My apologies for being silent the past few weeks...just got caught up
between customer commitments, holidays, and internal planning
[additionally, we've had some new people join the team focusing on AI Data
so we are working on growing our team, also].  This week we are starting an
internal effort to determine what AI Data offerings we are going to focus
on.  This is part of a broader initiative for LILT where we are
increasing our investments/focus on AI Data, so overall the timing is still
good but I wasn't in a position in the past couple/few weeks to indicate
that your Q1 roadmap ought to consider the generation requirements that we
spoke about.

I think it would make sense for us to reconvene early/mid next month since
that will allow time for the internal planning activities that we are
kicking off to come to some conclusions and at that point I ought to have a
much more informed response to you about the priority of generation
offerings (that your platform would clearly help facilitate).

Does that sound ok?  (Again, apologies for the silence).

-- Kel

>>>>>

>>>>> it seems like there would be the ability to configure for different
>>>>> customer requirements.  And I think there is a lot of potential for using
>>>>> the jury/judge capabilities to automate further (and focus any human
>>>>> annotation/labeling).
>>>>>

>>>>> -- question 1 - so far, we've defined decently strict manifests simply
>>>>> because that has put us in a position to guide the content generation more
>>>>> specifically (which has been necessary, otherwise we get too-unpredictable
>>>>> responses from the content generation prompts); we are now using pydantic
>>>>> models to define the output format and merging that into a jinja template
>>>>> used for the prompt
>>>>> -- question 2 - see attached (I can explain this further in a
>>>>> follow-up call); we would generate between 20-30K of these per week, each
>>>>> of which would feed the 'content generation' process
>>>>> -- question 3 - ideally we have confidence in a jury/judge agent(s)
>>>>> and would only human-review those that show low-confidence or wide-range
>>>>> out of the jury/judge evaluation
>>>>>
>>>>> Also, it is probably worth it at some point for us to talk about
>>>>> funding/costs.  I want to make sure the efforts of you and your team are

>>>>> probably make sense to hear from you how you would charge for the types of
>>>>> capabilities/functionality we are talking about.

>>>>> of the above.  Thanks again for considering these content-generation needs
>>>>> that we are starting to see.

>>>>>

>>>>>> On Wed, Nov 5, 2025 at 10:20 AM Meghdad Farahmand <
>>>>>> meghdad@calibrion.ai> wrote:
>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Hi Kel,
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> I hope you are doing well!
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Thanks again for the great meeting last Wednesday.
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> We reviewed your use cases with the team, placing particular focus
>>>>>>> on the group chat/message scenario.
>>>>>>> We can offer you a tailored workflow to support your needs. At a
>>>>>>> high level, it would look as follows:
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>    1. *Dataset description*: The user can describe the dataset in
>>>>>>>    natural language, semi-structured form, or as a fully structured
>>>>>>>    specification.
>>>>>>>    2. *Manifest generator agent assignment:* The user assigns a
>>>>>>>    manifest generator agent from a list of Calibrion's pre-existing
>>>>>>>    or their organization's custom-defined agents
>>>>>>>    <https://screen.studio/share/BKhY8U5U>.
>>>>>>>    3. *Manifest preview:* The user previews the manifest generated
>>>>>>>    by the above agent (with the possibility of editing if necessary).
>>>>>>>    4. *Dataset generator agent assignment:* The user selects a
>>>>>>>    dataset generator agent (from a list of Calibrion's pre-existing or their
>>>>>>>    organization's custom-defined agents).
>>>>>>>       - The user is presented with estimated time and cost for
>>>>>>>       dataset generation according to the chosen agent.
>>>>>>>       - Can confirm or change agents.
>>>>>>>    5. *Quality control (LLM jury assignment)*
>>>>>>>       - The user selects agents for their LLM jury (from a list of
>>>>>>>       Calibrion's pre-existing or their organization's custom-defined agents).
>>>>>>>       - Configures jury's decision type (accept/reject, rank,
>>>>>>>       natural_language_description).
>>>>>>>       - Configures jury's strictness (mild, moderate, strict).
>>>>>>>    6. *Preview details and finalize: *The user is presented with a
>>>>>>>    summary of the above configuration on the finalization page and can go back
>>>>>>>    to any step to edit it. When confirmed, the project starts.
>>>>>>>    7. *Project dashboard: *When the project starts, the user can
>>>>>>>    access the project dashboard.
>>>>>>>       - While the generation is in progress, the user can access
>>>>>>>       selected dashboard panels (e.g., statistics, member agents, manifest) for
>>>>>>>       real-time visibility.
>>>>>>>       - Once the project is complete, the user can view the jury
>>>>>>>       evaluation results, filter the data based on jury (or manual review)
>>>>>>>       decisions, and export the final dataset.
>>>>>>>       - In the final export, the user can include comprehensive
>>>>>>>       metadata related to the dataset generation, such as the manifest, initial
>>>>>>>       configuration, dataset description, participating agents, and human
>>>>>>>       reviewers. The user can select which metadata to include in the export.
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> *Open questions:*
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>    - *Regarding step 1:* Would you prefer the user to define the
>>>>>>>    configuration entirely in natural language, strictly through a structured
>>>>>>>    format (e.g., by uploading a JSON file), or with a semi-flexible approach
>>>>>>>    that mixes both (e.g., specifying size as an integer while describing other
>>>>>>>    elements in natural language)?
>>>>>>>    - *Manifest: *Do you have a sample/mock manifest you could share
>>>>>>>    with us?
>>>>>>>    - *Manual review: *In addition to the LLM-based jury, would you
>>>>>>>    also like to include a manual review process? If so, how would you envision
>>>>>>>    this working? At present, we can offer the following options:
>>>>>>>       - Human experts review the full generated dataset (or a
>>>>>>>       configurable sample of it). They can accept, reject, or edit the
>>>>>>>       generations.
>>>>>>>       - Human experts review the full (or a configurable sample of)
>>>>>>>       LLM jury decisions.
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> *Other use cases:*
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>>    - We already have a span-detection workflow in progress. If this
>>>>>>>    is of interest, we can make it available for your PII-redaction use case.
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> *Next steps: *If our proposal is interesting to you, I suggest
>>>>>>> scheduling a follow-up meeting to walk through the details and clarify any
>>>>>>> open questions. Following that, we can move to an initial prototype that
>>>>>>> enables us to discuss, adjust, and finalize the workflow with respect to
>>>>>>> your needs and preferences. We can then proceed to implementation and
>>>>>>> deployment.
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Feel free to revisit some of Calibrion's workflows I presented
>>>>>>> during our initial demo, which you can find here
>>>>>>> <https://docs.google.com/document/d/1bMC1AW6Wu-eTxOIhEkro05LAr8QYiMcfU7WgXCZe4vQ/edit?tab=t.0>
>>>>>>> .
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> I look forward to your thoughts and would be excited to work
>>>>>>> together on this project if you decide to proceed.
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Best regards,
>>>>>>> Meghdad
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> --
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Meghdad Farahmand PhD
>>>>>>>
>>>>>>> Founder ・ Calibrion AI

Hi Kel,

I hope you’re doing well.

I just wanted to check in one last time as we’re planning the development
of our next set of features for Q1. If you’re still interested in what we
discussed, a quick note in the next few days would be great. Otherwise, If
the timing isn’t right on your end, that’s absolutely fine, I’ll go ahead
and close this on our end for now.

If you’d like to revisit it later, just let me know anytime.

In the meantime, wishing you a great start to the holiday season!

Warm regards,
Meghdad

>>>>>    organization's custom-defined agents).
>>>>>       - The user is presented with estimated time and cost for
>>>>>       dataset generation according to the chosen agent.
>>>>>       - Can confirm or change agents.
>>>>>    5. *Quality control (LLM jury assignment)*
>>>>>       - The user selects agents for their LLM jury (from a list of
>>>>>       Calibrion's pre-existing or their organization's custom-defined agents).
>>>>>       - Configures jury's decision type (accept/reject, rank,
>>>>>       natural_language_description).
>>>>>       - Configures jury's strictness (mild, moderate, strict).
>>>>>    6. *Preview details and finalize: *The user is presented with a
>>>>>    summary of the above configuration on the finalization page and can go back
>>>>>    to any step to edit it. When confirmed, the project starts.
>>>>>    7. *Project dashboard: *When the project starts, the user can
>>>>>    access the project dashboard.
>>>>>       - While the generation is in progress, the user can access
>>>>>       selected dashboard panels (e.g., statistics, member agents, manifest) for
>>>>>       real-time visibility.
>>>>>       - Once the project is complete, the user can view the jury
>>>>>       evaluation results, filter the data based on jury (or manual review)
>>>>>       decisions, and export the final dataset.
>>>>>       - In the final export, the user can include comprehensive
>>>>>       metadata related to the dataset generation, such as the manifest, initial
>>>>>       configuration, dataset description, participating agents, and human
>>>>>       reviewers. The user can select which metadata to include in the export.
>>>>>
>>>>> *Open questions:*
>>>>>
>>>>>    - *Regarding step 1:* Would you prefer the user to define the
>>>>>    configuration entirely in natural language, strictly through a structured
>>>>>    format (e.g., by uploading a JSON file), or with a semi-flexible approach
>>>>>    that mixes both (e.g., specifying size as an integer while describing other
>>>>>    elements in natural language)?
>>>>>    - *Manifest: *Do you have a sample/mock manifest you could share
>>>>>    with us?
>>>>>    - *Manual review: *In addition to the LLM-based jury, would you
>>>>>    also like to include a manual review process? If so, how would you envision
>>>>>    this working? At present, we can offer the following options:
>>>>>       - Human experts review the full generated dataset (or a
>>>>>       configurable sample of it). They can accept, reject, or edit the
>>>>>       generations.
>>>>>       - Human experts review the full (or a configurable sample of)
>>>>>       LLM jury decisions.
>>>>>
>>>>> *Other use cases:*
>>>>>
>>>>>    - We already have a span-detection workflow in progress. If this
>>>>>    is of interest, we can make it available for your PII-redaction use case.
>>>>>
>>>>> *Next steps: *If our proposal is interesting to you, I suggest
>>>>> scheduling a follow-up meeting to walk through the details and clarify any
>>>>> open questions. Following that, we can move to an initial prototype that
>>>>> enables us to discuss, adjust, and finalize the workflow with respect to
>>>>> your needs and preferences. We can then proceed to implementation and
>>>>> deployment.
>>>>>
>>>>> Feel free to revisit some of Calibrion's workflows I presented during
>>>>> our initial demo, which you can find here
>>>>> <https://docs.google.com/document/d/1bMC1AW6Wu-eTxOIhEkro05LAr8QYiMcfU7WgXCZe4vQ/edit?tab=t.0>
>>>>> .
>>>>>
>>>>> I look forward to your thoughts and would be excited to work together
>>>>> on this project if you decide to proceed.
>>>>>
>>>>> Best regards,
>>>>> Meghdad
>>>>>
>>>>> --
>>>>>

subject: Re: Data generation with Calibrion
body_text:
hi Meghdad --

Getting back to this...thanks for your patience.

Overall, seems like the right direction/approach, especially because it
seems like there would be the ability to configure for different customer
requirements.  And I think there is a lot of potential for using the
jury/judge capabilities to automate further (and focus any human
annotation/labeling).

About the open questions:
-- question 1 - so far, we've defined decently strict manifests simply
because that has put us in a position to guide the content generation more
specifically (which has been necessary, otherwise we get too-unpredictable
responses from the content generation prompts); we are now using pydantic
models to define the output format and merging that into a jinja template
used for the prompt
-- question 2 - see attached (I can explain this further in a follow-up
call); we would generate between 20-30K of these per week, each of which
would feed the 'content generation' process
-- question 3 - ideally we have confidence in a jury/judge agent(s) and
would only human-review those that show low-confidence or wide-range out of
the jury/judge evaluation

Also, it is probably worth it at some point for us to talk about
funding/costs.  I want to make sure the efforts of you and your team are
worthwhile.  I'm not in a position to make commitments, but it would
probably make sense to hear from you how you would charge for the types of
capabilities/functionality we are talking about.

I'm around next week if you'd like to reconvene to talk about any/all of
the above.  Thanks again for considering these content-generation needs
that we are starting to see.

-- Kel

subject: Data generation with Calibrion
body_text:
Hi Kel,

I hope you are doing well!

Thanks again for the great meeting last Wednesday.

We reviewed your use cases with the team, placing particular focus on the
group chat/message scenario.
We can offer you a tailored workflow to support your needs. At a high
level, it would look as follows:

   1. *Dataset description*: The user can describe the dataset in natural
   language, semi-structured form, or as a fully structured specification.
   2. *Manifest generator agent assignment:* The user assigns a manifest
   generator agent from a list of Calibrion's pre-existing or their
   organization's custom-defined agents
   <https://screen.studio/share/BKhY8U5U>.
   3. *Manifest preview:* The user previews the manifest generated by the
   above agent (with the possibility of editing if necessary).
   4. *Dataset generator agent assignment:* The user selects a dataset
   generator agent (from a list of Calibrion's pre-existing or their
   organization's custom-defined agents).
      - The user is presented with estimated time and cost for dataset
      generation according to the chosen agent.
      - Can confirm or change agents.
   5. *Quality control (LLM jury assignment)*
      - The user selects agents for their LLM jury (from a list of
      Calibrion's pre-existing or their organization's custom-defined agents).
      - Configures jury's decision type (accept/reject, rank,
      natural_language_description).
      - Configures jury's strictness (mild, moderate, strict).
   6. *Preview details and finalize: *The user is presented with a summary
   of the above configuration on the finalization page and can go back to any
   step to edit it. When confirmed, the project starts.
   7. *Project dashboard: *When the project starts, the user can access the
   project dashboard.
      - While the generation is in progress, the user can access selected
      dashboard panels (e.g., statistics, member agents, manifest) for
real-time
      visibility.
      - Once the project is complete, the user can view the jury evaluation
      results, filter the data based on jury (or manual review) decisions, and
      export the final dataset.
      - In the final export, the user can include comprehensive metadata
      related to the dataset generation, such as the manifest, initial
      configuration, dataset description, participating agents, and human
      reviewers. The user can select which metadata to include in the export.

*Open questions:*

   - *Regarding step 1:* Would you prefer the user to define the
   configuration entirely in natural language, strictly through a structured
   format (e.g., by uploading a JSON file), or with a semi-flexible approach
   that mixes both (e.g., specifying size as an integer while describing other
   elements in natural language)?
   - *Manifest: *Do you have a sample/mock manifest you could share with us?
   - *Manual review: *In addition to the LLM-based jury, would you also
   like to include a manual review process? If so, how would you envision this
   working? At present, we can offer the following options:
      - Human experts review the full generated dataset (or a configurable
      sample of it). They can accept, reject, or edit the generations.
      - Human experts review the full (or a configurable sample of) LLM
      jury decisions.

*Other use cases:*

   - We already have a span-detection workflow in progress. If this is of
   interest, we can make it available for your PII-redaction use case.

*Next steps: *If our proposal is interesting to you, I suggest scheduling a
follow-up meeting to walk through the details and clarify any open
questions. Following that, we can move to an initial prototype that enables
us to discuss, adjust, and finalize the workflow with respect to your needs
and preferences. We can then proceed to implementation and deployment.

Feel free to revisit some of Calibrion's workflows I presented during our
initial demo, which you can find here
<https://docs.google.com/document/d/1bMC1AW6Wu-eTxOIhEkro05LAr8QYiMcfU7WgXCZe4vQ/edit?tab=t.0>
.

I look forward to your thoughts and would be excited to work together on
this project if you decide to proceed.

Best regards,
Meghdad

-- 

Meghdad Farahmand PhD

Founder ・ Calibrion AI

Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 <+49+(0)30+478+662+25> ・ meghdad@calibrion.ai

Kolonnenstr. 8・10827 Berlin・Germany

www.calibrion.ai

I was able to push some work on the financial model, which data-wise is outlined as follows:

Dashboard 
P_P&L

Revenue: IN_Scenario request; IN_OperatingModel provides data 
Cost of Goods Sold: C_COGS
R&D Expenses: C_Payroll
Sales & Marketing: C-Payroll minus IN_Scenario request; C_OpEX provides Data
General & Administrative: C_Payroll; C_OpEX
Depreciation & Amortization: C_COGS
Interest Expenses: P_CashFLow
Other Expenses [ esp. Currency Gains / Losses ] 
Cash KPI’s:

Cash Balance: P_CashFlow
Key Business KPIs:
IN_OperatingModel [ Enterprise; Business; Team - already extended]
[ … ] To Customize
ARR Development:
Beginning ARR: 
P_CashFlow 
Operating Cash-Flow
Inflows: IN_OperatingModel; Based additionally on IN_Scenarios
Outflows: Direct Costs [ C_COGS ] + OpEx [ C_Payroll ] 
Investing Cash-Flow 
Acquisition of Assets [ C_CapEx ] 
Sale of Assets -  Manually here
Financing Cash-Flow
Proceeds from Issuance of Equity [ SAFE Notes ] - Manually here
Proceeds from Issuance of Debt or Loans received - Manually here
Repayments of Debt - Manually here
Payments of Dividende - Manually here
Repurchase of Company Stares - Manually here
Payment of Lease Liabilities - Manually here
Financing Cost - Manually here
P_BalanceSheet
Assets: 
Current Assets:
Cash & Equivalents - P_CashFLow
Short-term investments - Manually here
Accounts receivable - P_P&L; Manually in terms of percentage payments delayed 
Inventory - Manually here
Short-term interperiod  settlements - Open
Non-Current Assets: 
Property Plant & Equipment  - C_CapEx
Long-term investments - Manually here
Intangible Assets - C_CapEx
Long-term receivables - Manually here 
Long-term interperiod settlements - Manually here
Liabilities: 
Current liabilities 
Accounts payable - Manually here / Assumptio: Everythin paid for before YE 
Short-term debt - Manually here / Assumption: Bankability depends on corporate development [ 3-5 yrs plus ] 
Acccrued liabilities - P_P&L; P_CashFlow 
Other short-term liabilities - Manually here / Assumption: None 
Short-term deferred revenue - Manually here 
Non-Current Liabilities
Long-term Debt - Manually here / Assumption: None 
Indebted Securities - Manually here / Assumption: None 
Non-current provisions - Manually here / Assumption: None 
Other long-term liabilities  - Manually here / Assumption: None
Equity: 
Common Stock - P_CashFlow
Retained earnings / Losses - P_P&L

IN_Plans - Manually here
IN_Costs - Manually here

C_CapEX - IN_Costs;

P_Export_UnitEconomics - C_COGS; IN_OperatingModel

subject: B2B SaaS – Product ready - Zalando SE paid pilot secured – Seed Round kEUR 600; Calibrion.ai - AI Infrastructure / Data annotation
body_text:
Dear team at Coparion Venture Capital,

We are building Calibrion, an agent-native data annotation platform adressing one of the largest barriers to organisational GenAI adoption: enabling proprietary LLM training through high-quality, rapid annotation. 

Calibrion delivers: 
- Agentic data annotation that accelerates annotation up to 100x (from months to hours)
- Cost savings of around 90%
- Built-in proprietary quality control to ensure accuracy at scale 
- 35% faster human-in-the-loop through research driven optimized workflows

We recently signed a 6 months paid pilot with Zalando SE, secured a verbal commitment from Siemens Energy AG E-Commerce division, and are negotiating pilots with Cotopaxi (US sustainable outdoor brand) and LILT (AI-driven translation leader). 

We are seeking EUR 600,000 in seed funding to expand our team, onboard additional clients, and implement product-led-growth.

Our founder and CEO, Meghdad, holds a PhD in Computer Science with a specialization in Natural Language Processing, and has over eight years of applied AI product experience. 

Given your investment focus, we think Calibrion could be a strong fit. You’ll find our one-pager, pitch-deck, and product demos attached. We’d be glad to arrange a 30-minute call to walk you through the product and discuss and our upcoming milestones or just connect via phone (number attached).


Best regards,
Nils 


Nils Erbach

Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
Tel: +49 1 <tel:+49+(0)30+478+662+25>7662205065 ・ nils@calibrion.ai <mailto:meghdad@calibrion.ai>
Kolonnenstr. 8・10827 Berlin・Germany
www.calibrion.ai <https://www.calibrion.ai/>
￼

subject: Steve Jobs’ Vision of AI (1983),Elon’s Grok 3 Strikes in the AI Wars, andZuckerberg’s Facebook posts used to train AI
body_text:
Welcome (back) to AI Minds, a newsletter about the brainy and sometimes zany world of AI, brought to you by the Deepgram editorial team.

### In this edition:

* 🎥 Grok 3 Strikes: Is it the new AI King?

* 🔍 Using Deep Learning to Detect AI Images

* 🔊 VocalCrypy: A Novel Active Defense Against Deepfake Voices

* 🗣️How Synthetic Voice Works - Voice Cloning and more

* 🚗 The 7 Best arXiv Papers to Learn how LLMs Work

* 🐦 Social Media Buzz: Training AI on Zuckerberg’s Facebook Posts (and more!)

* 📲 Three new, trending AI Apps for you!

* 🎙️ AI Minds Podcast with Vaibhav Saxena, Co-Founder at Infer

* 🔭 Steve Jobs’ Vision of AI (1983 Talk)

* 🍷 Why Can’t ChatGPT Draw a Full Glass of Wine?

* 🐇 The tests that tricked GPT-4o

* 📚 Glossary Entry: All You Need to Know about Augmented Intelligence

Thanks for letting us crash your inbox; let’s party. 🎉

----------
**Deepgram recently released Nova-3, extending our leadership in Voice AI for Enterprise Use Cases. Check it out **[here](https://deepgram.com/learn/introducing-nova-3-speech-to-text-api)**!**


--------------------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/18c135d7-112b-4588-8b9f-659e512b4066/div-2.gif?t=1700246279)
Caption: 


----------# 🎥 Is Elon’s Grok 3 the new AI King?

Youtube: Is Elon’s Grok 3 the new AI king? (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=b0XI-cbel1U)

Last week’s edition came out a few hours after Grok 3. That is, we weren’t able to give it a proper review, since the world was still testing it out by the time readers received our newsletter. However, the world has now had Grok 3 in its hands for seven full days. Here’s a brief review from none other than the legendary YouTuber, Fireship.

Description: “Take a first look at Elon Musk's Grok 3 deep thinking AI model. Let's find out how Grok 3 compares to OpenAI o3, Google Gemini, and Claude.”

----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/18c135d7-112b-4588-8b9f-659e512b4066/div-2.gif?t=1700246279)
Caption: 


----------# 🔍  Using Deep Learning to Detect AI Images and Prevent AI Voice Deepfakes

View image: (https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXeVRbgScVDoUZVJgEDKaXJxSnul4mZvcg85BaDEvwlkrRq8E4tHJcnUqRODsn4_cYPdIa947LyR2Oeu414Hyls_ppfS320gqCUnM2C_BcJYZ6F6NbZs0KyiYDnrG8CCOw9jDjRTsQ?key=raU-ZzXcgp9WaoTh2C-8DVa7)
Follow image link: (https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.08821)
Caption: 

[DejAIvu: Identifying and Explaining AI Art on the Web in Real-Time with Saliency Maps](https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.08821) - This paper introduces DejAIvu. Using an ONNX-optimized deep learning model, DejAIvu automatically analyzes images on websites, identifies AI-generated content using model inference, and overlays a saliency heatmap to highlight AI-related artifacts.

———————————————————————————

View image: (https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXdEpqIGBmosYx_jjGOCmxCT-MtayQnXbhcA5w14d5xOSwMjVqE-tQQ7l0hP79UJjhvllCzE3W15cRTccqLx0MGh3Yf5MGZszH6AhHT1B_5jVo544o2I9KyRI12hM-tvzWIBWjBLTg?key=raU-ZzXcgp9WaoTh2C-8DVa7)
Follow image link: (https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.10329)
Caption: 

[VocalCrypt: Novel Active Defense Against Deepfake Voice Based on Masking Effect](https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.10329) - The authors of this paper propose a novel active defense method, VocalCrypt, which embeds pseudo-timbre (jamming information) based on SFS into audio segments that are imperceptible to the human ear, thereby forming systematic fragments to prevent voice cloning.

----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/18c135d7-112b-4588-8b9f-659e512b4066/div-2.gif?t=1700246279)
Caption: 


----------# ⚡ Exposing Voice Cloning and the 7 best arXiv papers to learn how LLMs work

View image: (https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXdV4Fkgtz_IoFZ7tSxdhJ4NwzyZfPHSbHquLR-0bV6vli5mu8sdqqtfJRB1NFCLbGTFPNob49F1eJeuhwcP65vZFyJim7C0t_0A3cmnoXX-Nmo9o6E6biAW5it4f9S70569Gj0OAQ?key=raU-ZzXcgp9WaoTh2C-8DVa7)
Follow image link: (https://deepgram.com/learn/voice-cloning-synthetic-voices)
Caption: 

[Exposing Voice Cloning: How Synthetic Voices Shape Futures](https://deepgram.com/learn/voice-cloning-synthetic-voices)** - **In this article, we will delve into the fascinating world of voice cloning, exploring the cutting-edge techniques and model architectures that make it possible.

———————————————————————————

View image: (https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXc4AcxFeNwv45WZuB8bZbSc_AoO9js4f3MooMogB4n0h8GjgXg1dUI4Z9Mw5TGHaupJW-xaEdXoBC4JV8vuwe6_HJtzn1UQxrllTaWgQM_bggHQXNQA0Q2I1TITwJsswR5X0vUFUA?key=raU-ZzXcgp9WaoTh2C-8DVa7)
Follow image link: (https://deepgram.com/learn/the-7-best-arxiv-papers-to-learn-how-llms-work)
Caption: 

[The 7 best arXiv papers to learn how LLMs work](https://deepgram.com/learn/the-7-best-arxiv-papers-to-learn-how-llms-work) - These papers cover various aspects of LLMs, including their architectures, pre-training methods, scaling properties, few-shot learning capabilities, and applications in tasks like reasoning and transfer learning.

----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/18c135d7-112b-4588-8b9f-659e512b4066/div-2.gif?t=1700246279)
Caption: 


----------# 🐝 Social Media Buzz: Grok 3 as a Coding Assistant and training an AI on Zuckerberg’s Facebook Posts

Tweet:  (https://twitter.com/dr_cintas/status/1893703881806250412)

———————————————————————————

Tweet:  (https://twitter.com/minimaxir/status/1120434509302915073)

———————————————————————————

Tweet:  (https://twitter.com/aaditsh/status/1892205010999730270)

----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/18c135d7-112b-4588-8b9f-659e512b4066/div-2.gif?t=1700246279)
Caption: 


----------# 📲 Three new, trending AI Apps for you!

View image: (https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXdIglAk7bmJZ2pZ_x0v0CXjDovdV-baPdgTK6oXtgiAY3k54fjwIni61r0vSb3fNhlkY8qJfOockQW3_zQFRo0SjEdO0J1ugNBuqLGaSuePgJb6LzkYWyricQgZ3zVu9gsb3Kq7?key=raU-ZzXcgp9WaoTh2C-8DVa7)
Follow image link: (https://deepgram.com/ai-apps/songr)
Caption: 

[SongR](https://deepgram.com/ai-apps/songr) is an AI-powered songwriting assistant that helps you create original songs in various genres. It allows you to enter lyrics and then generates accompanying music to match the lyrics and genre you select.

———————————————————————————

View image: (https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXecKvLEbMRhLZGj5t3lwsiF-tTqHWL8DX3YryVAYNZ8jK4MQ3xjM_sMFlma2OzfG_dxbAS5RftUsFqhdKR1u6CoCPpSFWrkDxgNOkdZzNOoKoAJY2CtFRw_3h2EpYc70QDvHRyQ-A?key=raU-ZzXcgp9WaoTh2C-8DVa7)
Follow image link: (https://deepgram.com/ai-apps/free-use-score)
Caption: 

[Free Use Score](https://deepgram.com/ai-apps/free-use-score) analyzes customer service calls using AI to provide actionable insights in just 15 minutes. The platform simplifies call evaluation into three steps: uploading recordings, AI-driven analysis, and receiving detailed reports.

———————————————————————————

View image: (https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXdDYBo2dx9C-ueghqZ3XaQuco0GDN0-UJgA4erCGOJ73_o7NnROLqka7QbEXdgvhAmu6uRxtoxl47DnXOOUz53n4fjqAExXy1jIPrTgtZRZeIP4WSHX9E9tGuo29KtEl3Ofss7j?key=raU-ZzXcgp9WaoTh2C-8DVa7)
Follow image link: (https://deepgram.com/ai-apps/hello-history)
Caption: 

[Hello History](https://deepgram.com/ai-apps/hello-history) enables realistic AI-driven conversations with over 400 historical figures. The app serves educational purposes by providing interactive tools for teachers and students. Users can explore diverse topics like science and philosophy through engaging dialogues.

----------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/18c135d7-112b-4588-8b9f-659e512b4066/div-2.gif?t=1700246279)
Caption: 


----------# 🎤 The AI Minds Podcast

Youtube: Vaibhav Saxena, Co-Founder at Infer (Part 2) | AI Minds #050 (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WHiUHIl2Jmo&t=1s)

Vaibhav Saxena, Co-Founder at Infer, is our first guest to make a second appearance on the AI Minds Podcast. Infer are a company which accelerates revenue growth with AI voice bots automating phone conversations and executing tasks like humans for insurance and lending.

Vaibhav explains how tailoring voice agents to user preferences—such as tone, pitch, and cadence—can significantly enhance engagement and conversion rates.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/18c135d7-112b-4588-8b9f-659e512b4066/div-2.gif?t=1700246279)
Caption: 

# 🤖 Bonus Bits and Bytes

If you’ve scrolled down this far, here’s some extra bonus content for you!

Youtube: Steve Jobs Vision of AI (5 min) (https://youtu.be/cHuqhQmc4ok?si=JezpUUGBldoQjKLQ)

* 🧠** **[Steve Jobs Vision of AI](https://youtu.be/cHuqhQmc4ok?si=JezpUUGBldoQjKLQ)** **- Prepping for AI Bootcamp for UX Teams, the people at STRAT came across this 5 min video that proves that Steve Jobs envisioned AI experiences in 1983 that we just now beginning to realize in tangible ways. 

* 🎞️ [Why Can’t ChatGPT Draw a Full Glass of Wine?](https://youtu.be/160F8F8mXlo?si=xWnF3xtegxwekVDG)** **- While the answer to this question may be obvious to those of us who work in AI, it’s quite interesting to see ChatGPT’s shortcomings in action.

* 🐇 [The Tests that Tricked GPT-4o](https://deepgram.com/learn/the-tests-that-tricked-gpt-4o) - While GPT-4o excels in some areas, it continues to face challenges in others. However, these are not just hurdles but opportunities.

* 📚 [Glossary Entry: Augmented Intelligence](https://deepgram.com/ai-glossary/augmented-intelligence) - This article dives deep into the realm of augmented intelligence, offering a comprehensive exploration of its foundational principles, practical applications, and its profound impact on enhancing human decision-making and productivity.


———

You are reading a plain text version of this post. For the best experience, copy and paste this link in your browser to view the post online:
https://www.aiminds.com/p/steve-jobs-vision-of-ai-grok-3-strikes

subject: Urgent Job Opportunity Senior Architect – Data Lake, Microsoft Technologies & AI - Cologne, Germany / Onsite
body_text:
EXTERNAL EMAIL: DO NOT CLICK links or attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe. </p

Hi Meghdad,

Greetings,

I am Girish writing from Zealogics IT Solutions.

I was trying to reach you to discuss regarding one of our openings for a Software Engineer –  Senior Architect – Data Lake, Microsoft Technologies & AI  - role
I found your profile to be good match. Kindly review the below job description and let me know if you'd like to proceed with an application. Also please revert back with the below requested details for completion of the process.
Job Title: Senior Architect – Data Lake, Microsoft Technologies & AI
Location: Cologne, Germany (Hybrid / Onsite)
Employment Type:Full-time
Client: TUV

Senior Architect – Data Lake, Microsoft Technologies & AI

Overview

Zealogics is seeking an experienced Senior Architect with strong expertise in Data Lake architecture, Microsoft technologies, and AI integration to design and lead cutting-edge data and analytics solutions for enterprise clients. The ideal candidate will combine deep technical knowledge with strategic thinking to architect scalable data ecosystems that power AI-driven insights and digital transformation.

Key Responsibilities
  •  Design and implement Data Lake and AI platforms using Microsoft Azure technologies (Azure Data Lake, Synapse, Microsoft Fabric, Databricks, Cognitive Services, OpenAI).
  •  Define data architecture frameworks covering ingestion, transformation, storage, governance, and AI model integration.
  •  Build and optimize end-to-end data pipelines across structured and unstructured data sources.
  •  Collaborate with data scientists to operationalize AI/ML models for predictive analytics and automation.
  •  Establish best practices for data security, compliance (GDPR), and performance optimization.
  •  Drive innovation by integrating Generative AI, Cognitive Services, and Machine Learning capabilities into enterprise applications.
  •  Mentor teams on modern Microsoft Data & AI tools and architecture standards.

Qualifications
  •  Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related discipline.
  •  10+ years of experience in data architecture with a focus on Microsoft Azure ecosystems.
  •  Expertise in Azure Data Lake, Synapse Analytics, ADF, Power BI, Microsoft Fabric, and Databricks.
  •  Proven experience integrating AI/ML pipelines using Azure Machine Learning, Cognitive Services, or Azure OpenAI.
  •  Strong background in ETL/ELT design, data modeling, and MLOps.
  •  Proficiency in Python, PySpark, SQL, and REST APIs.
  •  Excellent communication skills in English; German proficiency is a plus.

Preferred
  •  Experience implementing AI-powered analytics or LLM-based data solutions.
  •  Familiarity with RAG architecture, vector databases, or prompt engineering.
  •  Microsoft Certified: Azure Solutions Architect Expert / Data Engineer Associate preferred.

Kindly share me the below details
Full Legal Name :
Contact number:
Current email id:
Current Location :
Visa status :
Rate :
Availability:
LinkedIn Profile URL :


Thanks,
Girish V
Senior Associate - Talent Acquisition
[cid:cad5c96d-79f8-4cd4-b269-ebacb91948f4]
USA | UK | Canada | India | UAE | Netherlands | Taiwan

subject: Re: AI Builders
body_text:
Hi Luc,

My apologies for the late reply.
We have had a few pitches this past few weeks which were extremely
demanding.
We have a few more such meetings planned until the end of January, and
hence, unfortunately I have to pass on your generous offer.

I think at least until the end of fundraising for this round, I won't be
able to make this commitment.

Let's stay in touch for sure.

And maybe we will see each other soon in one of your upcoming events.

With best wishes for the season

Best regards,
Meghdad

subject: Re: AI Builders
body_text:
Hi Luc,

Thank you so much for your kind words!
I really appreciated the opportunity and I’m glad to hear you enjoyed my
talk.

It was great to meet you in person and get to know Andrew as well.

I did notice that the audience fields and experience level were a bit
different than I expected, but I still genuinely enjoyed speaking, and your
transparency means a lot.

I’d love to take you up on the offer and join the January event. Do you
have the date already?

Kind regards,
Meghdad

I was also just thinking with Andrew, we actually want to offer you a spot
for free.

In all honesty I did not like how the Berlin event was run. This was the
first time that I had been there in person and I did not like a lot of
things...

I felt really bad that I had you sponsor for such an event.

This is my attempt to make it up to you. Let me know if you are interested.

Warm regards,

Luc Guimberteau

body_text:
Hi Meghdad,

Unfortunately we did not record this one...

Andrew and I were just raving about you. We really appreciated how down to
earth and accepting you were, I personally also just really liked your
vibe. We would really like you to be a part of the cohort we are hosting in
January.

The normal price is 499€ but we want to offer it to you for whatever price
you think is reasonable. Does that sound like something you would be
interested in? If you want more information Andrew and I are happy to hop
on a call and explain more.

https://lunacal.ai/luc-guimberteau/

Warm regards,

Luc Guimberteau

subject: Re: AI Builders
body_text:
Hi Luc,

Thank you so much for the follow-up.

Here is the title and description of my talk (I am completely open to
feedback if you have any suggestions for improvement).

*Human–Agent & Multi-Agent Workflows to Create, Scale and Assure the
Quality of Training Data for AI Models*

*Description: *
In this talk, Meghdad Farahmand, founder of Calibrion AI --an agentic
platform for Data Annotation and Dataset Creation,
talks about how AI agents are employed to reduce data annotation time from
months to hours. He discusses how Calibrion
pioneers the use of agreement methods and LLM juries to assure quality at
the same pace as agentic dataset creation.

I just scheduled a call with you.

Looking forward to speaking with you and to this event!

Best regards,
Meghdad

🚀 Launch Edition

Welcome to the very first edition of Voice of AI - yes, that’s the name, and yes, we’re a voice AI company. We had to.

Name puns aside, this biweekly digest delivers what’s new across the Deepgram platform: product updates, dev tutorials, customer stories, and ideas worth stealing. Let’s dive in.

PRODUCT UPDATES

🌍 Deepgram Goes Global: New Deployment Options Now Available

Voice AI is going global, and so is Deepgram. To support enterprise teams scaling across regions, we’ve launched two major infrastructure expansions:

- Deepgram Dedicated - a fully managed, single-tenant deployment option | Request access (https://e.deepgram.com/e3t/Ctc/5D+113/cQMRG04/VW0f0f6jKbB0W3Wy8Dh5-QNpsW6D6W6y5zGJbMN3X6VHv3prCCW7lCdLW6lZ3pLW31y6R38kjyJcW6s6q6b4FKw7xW6jVF4p5Y3yT2W6YhzMb5VxMnwLZwsw8y3FvW5XQwgN1D7SSLN3xncH1Ny5cVW1K8wKw17ylrKVv1RMf5rBGxYW6GWs1C6nQ4DQW5QzVXn8fKqQRW4HhLbF68lK4vW2WRT0y5PQcqkW5V15fV5CzTHPW3Ky41l6ng_rjW17Cc_m66mDtgW6q98sh82hffVW7ByxQ51Dyf_qW8rn09l86_srZW1mB1_X5Q6WQmW6HfcvP3wL8P2W5Vvvr74XgRdPW5gp7CH7bTXDpW98RPCg7SzTbMf2q560j04 )

subject: Re: Meghdad Farahmand and Antler
body_text:
Dear Christoph and Alan,

Thank you so much for the engaging and insightful conversations and for
offering me the opportunity to join Antler.

After careful consideration, I’ve decided to respectfully decline. At this
stage, I require a larger pre-seed round, and I am focusing on product
development to launch for our existing warm leads, and the freemium version
right after. Given this focus and timeline, joining Antler isn’t the right
fit for me at the moment.

I truly appreciate your time, insights, and the work you do for the founder
community in Berlin, and I hope our paths cross again.

Warm regards,
Meghdad

subject: Re: Seed Round Subscription Opportunity Calibrion AI
body_text:
Dear team at Bosch Ventures,

I hope you’re doing well. I wanted to kindly follow up on my earlier email about Calibrion AI and our ongoing seed investment round.

At Calibrion, we’re addressing one of the hardest bottlenecks in building and customizing large language models: data annotation and high-quality training datasets. Our platform is built natively for LLMs, not adapted from legacy ML tooling, which allows us to deliver:

Significant time and cost savings – up to 35% lower annotation costs and weeks faster turnaround for enterprises.

Unmatched data quality – powered by proprietary quality-control workflows.

Early traction – pilot projects secured with a publicly traded German online retailer and a $150M-revenue US outdoor apparel brand.

Huge market opportunity – with a serviceable obtainable market of ~$1–1.4B by 2028, driven by enterprises moving toward domain-specific LLM customization.

Given Bosch Ventures’ focus on transformative, enabling technologies, we believe there’s a strong strategic fit here.

We’d be delighted to walk you through our live product demo and discuss how Calibrion is positioning itself to be the market leader in LLM-specific data annotation. Would you have availability next week for a short introductory call? I’d be more than happy to accommodate your schedule.

Thank you for considering, and I look forward to the chance to connect.

Best regards,
Nils
—

Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 <tel:+49+(0)30+478+662+25> ・ nils@calibrion.ai <mailto:meghdad@calibrion.ai>
Französische Str. 20・10117 Berlin・Germany
www.calibrion.ai <http://www.calibrion.ai/>

￼￼ ￼

Given Bosch Ventures’ track record in backing transformative technologies, we believe this could be a great fit for your portfolio.
> 
> If you’re interested, I’d be glad to share our detailed pitch deck and arrange a call or demo session at your convenience.
>

>

> Nils

>> 
>> I am reaching out to introduce Calibrion AI, a data annotation platform enabling enterprises and organisations to fine-tune LLMs to their proprietary data. We built a working product and already reached pilot-stage interest from European and US companies in the eCom vertical.
>> 
>> While most LLM infra and dev platforms focus on model architecture and LLMOps, we focus on unlocking enterprise adoption through (proprietary) data and fine-tuning workflows.
>> 
>> Calibrion envisions a future where businesses harness their proprietary data and domain expertise to build specialized LLMs, turning generative AI from a threat into a strategic asset for both internal and market-facing applications.
>> 
>> Our mission is to become a market-leading platform for LLM customization and domain adaptation, setting the standard for performance and usability in the enterprise AI space.
>> 
>> To underscore the scale of this opportunity and the importance of what Calibrion does: In June 2025, Meta acquired a 49% stake in Scale AI (a company also focused on LLM data annotation and founded in 2016) for $14.8 billion at a $30 billion valuation. 
>> 
>> We are currently offering subscription for our EUR 3 m seed investment round to: 
>> - expand our engineering and data science team
>> - Accelerate go-to-market efforts across verticals 
>> - Further develop our AI capabilities and integrate new LLM-driven features.
>> 
>> To accelerate the company's development, we assigned significant equity to an option pool for key employees and aim to be cash-flow positive within 18 months.

subject: Re: Seed Round Subscription Opportunity Calibrion AI
body_text:
Dear team at Seed Capital,

I hope you are doing well.

I wanted to follow up on my recent introduction of Calibrion AI and our ongoing EUR 3 million seed round. We are seeing strong early traction and pilot-stage interest from European and US companies, and we believe this is an exciting time for you to get an in-depth look at what we are building.

As mentioned, our platform is focused on unlocking enterprise adoption of LLMs through proprietary data annotation and fine-tuning workflows — a crucial piece often overlooked by LLM infrastructure players today. With the recent Meta–Scale AI deal underscoring the importance of this space, we see a significant opportunity ahead.

We would be glad to share our full pitch deck and walk you through the live product in a short call.

Would you have 30 minutes next week for an introduction and demo? I’m happy to adjust to your schedule.

Looking forward to your thoughts and hoping to connect soon.

Best regards,
Nils

--
Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 ・ nils@calibrion.ai <>
Französische Str. 20・10117 Berlin・Germany
www.calibrion.ai <http://www.calibrion.ai/>
￼￼

> On 22. Jul 2025, at 19:28, Nils Erbach <nils@calibrion.ai> wrote:
> 
> Dear team at Seed Capital,
> 
> I am reaching out to introduce Calibrion AI, a data annotation platform enabling enterprises and organisations to fine-tune LLMs to their proprietary data. We built a working product and already reached pilot-stage interest from European and US companies in the eCom vertical.
> 
> While most LLM infra and dev platforms focus on model architecture and LLMOps, we focus on unlocking enterprise adoption through (proprietary) data and fine-tuning workflows.
> 
> Calibrion envisions a future where businesses harness their proprietary data and domain expertise to build specialized LLMs, turning generative AI from a threat into a strategic asset for both internal and market-facing applications.
> 
> Our mission is to become a market-leading platform for LLM customization and domain adaptation, setting the standard for performance and usability in the enterprise AI space.
> 
> To underscore the scale of this opportunity and the importance of what Calibrion does: In June 2025, Meta acquired a 49% stake in Scale AI (a company also focused on LLM data annotation and founded in 2016) for $14.8 billion at a $30 billion valuation. 
> 
> We are currently offering subscription for our EUR 3 m seed investment round to: 
> - expand our engineering and data science team
> - Accelerate go-to-market efforts across verticals 
> - Further develop our AI capabilities and integrate new LLM-driven features.
> 
> To accelerate the company's development, we assigned significant equity to an option pool for key employees and aim to be cash-flow positive within 18 months.
> 
> We would be happy to share our pitch deck and schedule a call to walk you through the live product. The product is fully developed and currently undergoing regular testing and stabilization.
> 
> Please find attached a letter outlining our fund-raising goals as well as a one-pager on Calibrion.
> 
> We look forward to hearing from you and would be glad to schedule a conversation at your convenience.
> 
> Best regards,
> Nils
> 
> --

> Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 <tel:+49+(0)30+478+662+25> ・ nils@calibrion.ai <mailto:meghdad@calibrion.ai>

subject: Re: Seed Round Subscription Opportunity Calibrion AI
body_text:
Dear team at Bosch Ventures,

I hope this message finds you well.

I wanted to follow up on my previous email regarding Calibrion AI and our ongoing seed investment round. We’re excited about the interest we’ve received so far and would love the opportunity to present our live product to you in more detail.

As mentioned, we’re building a leading platform for LLM customization and domain adaptation, with strong early traction and pilot-stage engagements in the EU and US. Given Bosch Ventures’ track record in backing transformative technologies, we believe this could be a great fit for your portfolio.

If you’re interested, I’d be glad to share our detailed pitch deck and arrange a call or demo session at your convenience.

Do you have availability next week for a brief conversation? I’m happy to accommodate your schedule.

Looking forward to your feedback and hoping we can connect soon.

Best regards,
Nils

subject: Seed Round Subscription Opportunity Calibrion AI
body_text:
Dear team at Volution Ventures,

I am reaching out to introduce Calibrion AI, a data annotation platform enabling enterprises and organisations to fine-tune LLMs to their proprietary data. We built a working product and already reached pilot-stage interest from European and US companies in the eCom vertical.

While most LLM infra and dev platforms focus on model architecture and LLMOps, we focus on unlocking enterprise adoption through (proprietary) data and fine-tuning workflows.

Calibrion envisions a future where businesses harness their proprietary data and domain expertise to build specialized LLMs, turning generative AI from a threat into a strategic asset for both internal and market-facing applications.

Our mission is to become a market-leading platform for LLM customization and domain adaptation, setting the standard for performance and usability in the enterprise AI space.

To underscore the scale of this opportunity and the importance of what Calibrion does: In June 2025, Meta acquired a 49% stake in Scale AI (a company also focused on LLM data annotation and founded in 2016) for $14.8 billion at a $30 billion valuation. 

We are currently offering subscription for our EUR 3 m seed investment round to: 
- expand our engineering and data science team
- Accelerate go-to-market efforts across verticals 
- Further develop our AI capabilities and integrate new LLM-driven features.

To accelerate the company's development, we assigned significant equity to an option pool for key employees and aim to be cash-flow positive within 18 months.

We would be happy to share our pitch deck and schedule a call to walk you through the live product. The product is fully developed and currently undergoing regular testing and stabilization.

Please find attached a letter outlining our fund-raising goals as well as a one-pager on Calibrion.

We look forward to hearing from you and would be glad to schedule a conversation at your convenience.

Best regards,
Nils

-- 
Finance & Legal ・ Calibrion AI
Tel: +49 (0)30 478 662 25 <tel:+49+(0)30+478+662+25> ・ nils@calibrion.ai <mailto:meghdad@calibrion.ai>
Französische Str. 20・10117 Berlin・Germany
www.calibrion.ai <http://www.calibrion.ai/>

￼ ￼
